headline: Morovan Recalls Gel Nail Polish Remover (15ml) Containing Banned Hazardous Chemicals Methylene Chloride and Chloroform
description: Morovan recalls Gel Nail Polish Remover (15ml) due to prohibited methylene chloride and chloroform found in the product. No injuries reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Morovan has issued a recall for its Gel Nail Polish Remover due to the presence of methylene chloride and chloroform, both hazardous substances prohibited in cosmetic products. Methylene chloride has been linked to cancer in animal studies and is not allowed in cosmetics by FDA regulations at any concentration.

No injuries or incidents related to this product recall have been reported to date. The recalled product was sold online at Amazon.com between August 2025 and January 2026.

Product NameSizePackagingProduction DateExpiration DateUnits AffectedRetailerManufactured In
Gel Nail Polish Remover 15ml (0.5 fl.oz) Square turquoise bottle with white cap, green packaging box 02-07-2024 02-07-2027 About 4000 units Amazon.com (sold by HONGKONG YIIXIN TRADING LIMITED, Nails Studio) China

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Morovan Gel Nail Polish Remover (15ml) should stop using the product immediately, mark the bottle and packaging with “Recalled,” dispose of it safely following local guidelines, then take a photo of the discarded product and email it to customer-service@morovan.com for verification.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: customer-service@morovan.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on April 13, 2026.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.

headline: Blueroot Health Expands Voluntary Recall of Vital Nutrients Aller-C Dietary Supplement Due to Undeclared Egg, Hazelnut, and Soy Allergens
description: Blueroot Health expands recall of Vital Nutrients Aller-C dietary supplement for undeclared egg, hazelnut, and soy allergens. No reported adverse health events.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On April 10, 2026, Blueroot Health of Middletown, Connecticut, announced an expansion of its voluntary recall of Vital Nutrients Aller-C dietary supplements. The recall was initiated due to the potential presence of undeclared allergens including egg, hazelnut, and soy in the affected products. This expansion now covers all products made from lot 25E04, in addition to the previously recalled lots 25E04-A and 25E04-B.

Consumers with allergies or severe sensitivities to egg, hazelnut, or soy may face serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume the recalled supplements. The Aller-C dietary supplement was distributed nationwide through VitalNutrients.co and other online retailers.

The products impacted by this recall are packaged in white plastic bottles containing either 100 or 200 capsules. All recalled lots carry an expiration date of 05/27 on the bottle. The affected lots were distributed between September 2025 and March 2026. No adverse health events have been reported to date related to this issue.

Product Count UPC Lot Numbers Expiration Date
Vital Nutrients Aller-C 200 capsules 693465524213, 693465000090 25E04-B 05/27
Vital Nutrients Aller-C 100 capsules 693465524114, 693465000083 25E04, 25E04-A, 25E04-B 05/27

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Vital Nutrients Aller-C dietary supplements should check the lot number and product details and return any affected products to Blueroot Health for replacement or refund. For assistance, contact Blueroot Health at (888) 328-9992 or email support@vitalnutrients.co.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Customer Service | (888) 328-9992 | support@vitalnutrients.co

headline: Blaine Labs Recalls Revitaderm® and Tridergel™ Antiseptic Wound Care Gels Contaminated with Lysinibacillus fusiformis

image.caption: Blaine Labs, Inc Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Wound Care Gel Products Due to Microbial Contamination

description: Blaine Labs issues a voluntary recall of Revitaderm and Tridergel wound care gels due to contamination with Lysinibacillus fusiformis in specific lots.
keywords: Antiseptic wound care gel bottles and tubes
Products were found to contain Lysinibacillus fusiformis, an environmental organism

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Blaine Labs, Inc. has announced a voluntary nationwide recall of certain lots of its Revitaderm® and Tridergel™ wound care antiseptic gels. This recall affects both 1 oz. and 3 oz. bottles and tubes containing 0.1% Benzalkonium Chloride due to microbial contamination with Lysinibacillus fusiformis, an environmental bacterium.

The presence of Lysinibacillus fusiformis, a spore-forming organism generally considered to have low pathogenicity in healthy individuals, was detected in specific product lots. However, individuals with open wounds, compromised skin barriers, or weakened immune systems—including elderly, diabetic, and immunocompromised persons—may face increased risks of infection, skin irritation, or delayed wound healing. In severe cases, especially among vulnerable populations, infections could lead to serious complications such as endocarditis or central nervous system infection.

No adverse events related to this recall have been reported to Blaine Labs to date.

Brand Product Description Lot Number Expiration Date Packaging Details
Revitaderm® 1 oz. Wound Care Antiseptic Gel BL3608 07/01/28 Yellow ink on bottom of 1 oz. bottle, shorter twist cap
Revitaderm® 1 oz. Wound Care Antiseptic Gel BL3435 11/06/26 Black ink on bottom of 1 oz. bottle, long pointy cap
Revitaderm® 3 oz. Wound Care Antiseptic Gel BL3435 11/06/26 Imprinted on tube crimp
Revitaderm® 1 oz. Wound Care Antiseptic Gel BL3525 08/07/27 Black ink on bottom of 1 oz. bottle, long pointy cap
Revitaderm® 3 oz. Wound Care Antiseptic Gel BL3525 08/07/27 Imprinted on tube crimp
Tridergel™ 1 oz. Wound Care Gel BL3435 11/06/26 Black ink on bottom of 1 oz. bottle, long pointy cap

facts.product: Antiseptic wound care gel bottles and tubes
facts.reason: Products were found to contain Lysinibacillus fusiformis, an environmental organism

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check product lots against the recall list, immediately discontinue use of affected Revitaderm and Tridergel wound care gels, segregate remaining inventory, and contact Blaine Labs at (800) 307-8818 or Contact@blainelabs.com to arrange product return.

content.text: Consumer support: Blaine Labs, Inc | (800) 307-8818 | Contact@blainelabs.com

headline: Nalpac Recalls DTF Sexual Chocolate Nationwide for Presence of Undeclared Sildenafil and Tadalafil

image.caption: Nalpac Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of DTF Sexual Chocolate Due to the Presence of the Pharmaceutical Ingredient’s Sildenafil and Tadalafil

description: Nalpac recalls DTF Sexual Chocolate due to undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil, posing health risks with certain medications. UPC 757817783069 affected.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Nalpac is voluntarily recalling DTF Sexual Chocolate due to the presence of undeclared pharmaceutical ingredients sildenafil and tadalafil. These ingredients are the active compounds in Viagra and Cialis, respectively, prescription drugs used to treat erectile dysfunction. The product was purchased and resold by Nalpac; however, Nalpac is not the manufacturer.

The inclusion of sildenafil and tadalafil is concerning because these substances can dangerously interact with nitrates found in certain prescription medications, such as nitroglycerin, potentially causing a significant drop in blood pressure. This risk is especially critical for individuals using nitrate-based medicines. To date, Nalpac has not received any reports of adverse events related to this recall.

The recalled product is identifiable by the UPC 757817783069 and was sold in adult retail stores as well as online at shopsexology.com and royalsins.com.

Product UPC Packaging
DTF Sexual Chocolate 757817783069 Cases containing 20 retail units

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased DTF Sexual Chocolate with UPC 757817783069 should stop use immediately and return the product to the place of purchase or dispose of it. For questions, contact Nalpac at 248-541-1140 or sales@nalpac.com.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 248-541-1140 | sales@nalpac.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on April 07, 2026.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.
Review consumer support information on the company website.
Visit the company website for product details.

headline: Wawa Recalls 16-oz Iced Tea Lemon, Iced Tea Diet Lemon, Diet Lemonade, and Fruit Punch for Undeclared Milk Allergen Risk

image.caption: Wawa Issues Product Recall on 16-oz. Wawa Brand Iced Tea Lemon, Iced Tea Diet Lemon, Diet Lemonade and Fruit Punch Due to Possible Undeclared Milk Allergen

description: Wawa recalls select 16-oz beverages due to possible undeclared milk allergen, affecting stores in PA, DE, MD, NJ, and VA with no reported illnesses.
keywords: Iced Tea Lemon, Iced Tea Diet Lemon, Diet Lemonade and Fruit Punch
Potential or Undeclared Allergen – Undeclared Milk

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Wawa, based in Media, Pennsylvania, has announced a recall of select 16 oz pint bottles of its Wawa Brand beverages including Iced Tea Lemon, Iced Tea Diet Lemon, Diet Lemonade, and Fruit Punch. This recall affects products sold at a limited number of Wawa stores located in Pennsylvania, Delaware, Maryland, New Jersey, and Virginia. The recall is due to the potential presence of an undeclared milk allergen, which poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with milk allergies.

All impacted products have been removed from sale and disposed of at the affected store locations. No other Wawa-branded beverages or stores in states outside the listed regions are included in this recall. To date, no illnesses related to this issue have been reported.

The recall was initiated after Wawa identified and corrected a temporary equipment issue that may have caused the presence of the undeclared milk allergen in the affected beverages.

For more information, including a full list of impacted store addresses and product images, consumers may visit the Wawa recall alert page at www.wawa.com. Questions or refund requests can also be directed to Wawa’s 24-hour Customer Contact Center via their online contact form at wawa.com/iframe/contact or by phone at 1-800-444-9292.

Product Description UPC Code Date (Printed on top of bottle) Number of Stores/States Impacted
Wawa Iced Tea Lemon, 16 oz Pint bottle 726191018425 MAY 15, 2026 123 stores in DE, MD, NJ, PA, and VA
Wawa Iced Tea Diet Lemon, 16 oz Pint bottle 726191018548 MAY 18, 2026 8 stores in NJ and PA
Wawa Diet Lemonade, 16 oz Pint bottle 726191055901 MAY 18, 2026 12 stores in DE and NJ
Wawa Fruit Punch, 16 oz Pint bottle 726191018432 MAY 19, 2026 53 stores in DE, MD, NJ, PA, and VA

content.text: Consumers who have purchased the recalled 16-oz Wawa Iced Tea Lemon, Iced Tea Diet Lemon, Diet Lemonade, or Fruit Punch should immediately discard the products and contact Wawa’s Customer Contact Center for a refund, either through their online contact form at wawa.com/iframe/contact or by phone at 1-800-444-9292.

headline: Karns Foods Recalls Mini Dark Chocolate Raspberry Cups for Potential Undeclared Peanuts in Pennsylvania
description: Karns Foods recalls Mini Dark Chocolate Raspberry Cups due to undeclared peanut allergen, posing risks for individuals with peanut allergies in Pennsylvania.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Karns Foods has issued a voluntary recall of its Mini Dark Chocolate Raspberry Cups, approximately 8-ounce packages, due to the potential presence of undeclared peanuts. This product was distributed exclusively to Karns Foods locations in Pennsylvania. Individuals with peanut allergies are at risk of serious or life-threatening reactions if they consume these items.

The recalled product is marked with a pack date of September 18, 2025. About 26 to 42 packages were affected. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that a product containing peanuts was mistakenly packaged without this allergen being disclosed on the label. This issue resulted from a temporary breakdown in the packaging processes at Karns Foods.

The affected Mini Dark Chocolate Raspberry Cups were removed from shelves starting January 30, 2026, and notification efforts were made to registered rewards program shoppers who had purchased the product. No illnesses related to this recall have been reported at this time.

Product Package Size Pack Date Distribution Area Approximate Quantity Affected
Mini Dark Chocolate Raspberry Cups 8 ounces September 18, 2025 Karns Foods locations in Pennsylvania 26-42 packs

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased the recalled Mini Dark Chocolate Raspberry Cups should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Karns Foods at 717-766-6477 between 8 a.m. and 3 p.m. EST, Monday through Friday.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 717-766-6477

headline: Raw Farm Recalls RAW FARM Raw Milk Cheddar Cheese Shredded and Block Varieties Over Potential E. coli Risk
description: Raw Farm recalls specific batches of RAW FARM raw milk cheddar cheese, shredded and block, due to potential E. coli contamination. Check affected lots here.
keywords: Raw milk cheddar cheese, shredded and block

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Raw Farm has initiated a voluntary recall of certain batches of RAW FARM-brand raw milk cheddar cheese, both shredded and block varieties, due to potential contamination with E. coli. Although no pathogens have been detected in FDA or state Health Department samples, and the company contests the epidemiological evidence used by the FDA, Raw Farm is proceeding with the recall at the FDA's request to address public safety concerns.

This voluntary recall applies only to specific lots of RAW FARM cheddar cheese and includes any batches produced prior to the dates listed.

The FDA has issued an advisory regarding an outbreak investigation of E. coli O157:H7 linked to raw cheddar cheese but did not specify batch numbers or expiration dates. More detailed information about the FDA advisory can be found on their website.

Item Item Description Batch Expiration Date Barcode (UPC)

1060 80 oz Lightly Salted Cheddar Block 20251015-4 8/11/2026 835204001160
1075 16 oz Lightly Salted Cheddar Block 20251027-4 8/23/2026 835204000156
1076 80 oz Bag of Original Cheddar Shred 20260205 5/6/2026 835204000194
1078 16 oz Jalapeno Cheddar Block 20251128-1J 9/24/2026 835204000354
1080 8 oz Lightly Salted Cheddar Shred 20260212 5/13/2026 835204001184
1090 8 oz Jalapeno Cheddar Block 20251128-2J 9/24/2026 835204000330

facts.product: Raw milk cheddar cheese, shredded and block

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the product description, batch numbers, and expiration dates against the recalled lots listed. Do not consume any RAW FARM cheddar cheese from those batches or earlier. Return recalled products to the original place of purchase for a full refund. For further questions or concerns, contact Raw Farm directly through their official consumer support channels.

headline: Tops Recalls Christopher Ranch and Garland Peeled Garlic Due to Potential Botulism Contamination

image.caption: Tops Issues Recall of Christopher Ranch Peeled Garlic & Garland Fresh Peeled Garlic Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Tops recalls Christopher Ranch and Garland Peeled Garlic due to risk of Clostridium botulinum contamination linked to botulism illness.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tops Friendly Markets of Williamsville, NY, has recalled all lots of Christopher Ranch Peeled Garlic and Garland Fresh Peeled Garlic because of potential contamination with Clostridium botulinum, a bacterium that can cause the life-threatening illness botulism. This recall follows discovery that the products were stored at insufficient temperatures, possibly enabling bacterial growth.

The affected products are 6-ounce plastic bags of Christopher Ranch Peeled Garlic and Garland Fresh Peeled Garlic sold at Tops Markets in New York, Pennsylvania, and Vermont. Botulism symptoms may occur without visible spoilage of the garlic, so consumption of affected products is unsafe.

Symptoms of botulism poisoning include general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing difficulties, muscle weakness, abdominal swelling, and constipation. No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

Brand Product Package Size UPC Product Code Dates
Christopher Ranch Peeled Garlic 6 oz. plastic bags 74574-10852 All product code dates
Garland Fresh Peeled Garlic 6 oz. plastic bags 71894-00000 All product code dates
Garland Fresh Peeled Garlic 6 oz. plastic bags 68826-75340 All product code dates

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their packaging for affected lots of Christopher Ranch and Garland Peeled Garlic and return the recalled products to Tops for a full refund. For further assistance, contact Tops Consumer Affairs at 1-800-522-2522.

headline: Aphreseller Voluntarily Recalls Kian Pee Wan Dietary Supplement Capsules Containing Undeclared Prescription Drugs

image.caption: Aphreseller (Buy-herbal.com) Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Kian Pee Wan Capsules Due the Presence of Undeclared Dexamethasone and Cyproheptadine

description: Aphreseller recalls Kian Pee Wan capsules marketed for weight gain after FDA found undeclared dexamethasone and cyproheptadine, posing health risks.
keywords: Dietary supplement capsules marketed for treatment for gaining weight and stimulating appetite.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Aphreseller, operating under the eBay seller ID Buy-herbal.com, has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots of Kian Pee Wan capsules. This dietary supplement, marketed for weight gain and appetite stimulation, was found to contain undeclared drug ingredients dexamethasone and cyproheptadine during FDA analysis. These substances are not permitted in dietary supplements and render the product an unapproved drug.

Dexamethasone is a corticosteroid that can impair immune response and cause serious side effects including high blood sugar, muscle injury, psychiatric issues, cardiovascular events, and adrenal gland suppression with prolonged use or high doses. Abrupt discontinuation may also lead to withdrawal symptoms. Cyproheptadine is a prescription antihistamine which, when unlisted on product labels, may cause sedation, dizziness, fatigue, restlessness, dry mouth, nausea, gastrointestinal disturbances, urinary retention, and blurred vision.

Kian Pee Wan capsules are packaged in white bottles with beige labels containing 30 pills. They were distributed nationwide via online sales on eBay.com.

No specific injuries or adverse events associated with this product have been reported to date.

Product Name Form Packaging Distribution Recall Reason
Kian Pee Wan capsules Dietary supplement capsules White bottle with beige label, 30 pills Nationwide online via eBay Contains undeclared dexamethasone and cyproheptadine (unapproved drug ingredients)

Customers who purchased Kian Pee Wan capsules from Aphreseller may return the product to the following address for a refund:

Return Address Contact Phone Contact Email Return Hours
136-61 41st Ave, #602, Flushing, NY 11355 347-313-8605 greatremedy68@gmail.com Monday–Friday, 10 a.m.–6 p.m. ET

facts.product: Dietary supplement capsules marketed for treatment for gaining weight and stimulating appetite.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should stop using Kian Pee Wan capsules, verify product details, and return them to Aphreseller at 136-61 41st Ave, #602, Flushing, NY 11355 for a refund. Contact the company at 347-313-8605 or greatremedy68@gmail.com for assistance. Report any adverse reactions to FDA MedWatch.

title: Schreiber Foods Recalls Einstein Bros. Honey Almond Cream Cheese for Undeclared Almonds
headline: Schreiber Foods Recalls Einstein Bros. Honey Almond Cream Cheese Due to Undeclared Tree Nuts (Almonds)
description: Schreiber Foods recalls Einstein Bros. Honey Almond Cream Cheese due to undeclared almonds posing allergy risk. Distributed in CO, UT, NM, and WY.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Schreiber Foods, Inc. of Green Bay, WI, has announced a voluntary recall of 144 cases of Honey Almond Cream Cheese Spread due to the presence of undeclared almonds. This product poses a serious risk to individuals with almond allergies or severe sensitivities, as consumption may trigger a life-threatening allergic reaction. The affected product was distributed to Einstein Bros. Bagel locations in Colorado, Utah, New Mexico, and Wyoming.

The recalled item is a 6-ounce plastic cup labeled with the Einstein Bros. Bagels brand. The packaging features a Honey Almond lid paired with a Plain cup, with the lot code "Best If Used By Jul 21, 2026 LO" printed on the bottom. The lid accurately identifies the product as Honey Almond, but the cup incorrectly labels it as Plain, which does not indicate the presence of almonds.

This recall was initiated after the company discovered a packaging mismatch issue during shipment caused by a limited packaging staging error, which has since been corrected. There have been no reported illnesses linked to this product to date.

Product Size Lot Code / Expiration Date Distribution Area
Einstein Bros. Bagels Honey Almond Cream Cheese Spread (with mismatched Plain cup) 6-ounce plastic cup Best If Used By Jul 21, 2026 LO Colorado, Utah, New Mexico, Wyoming

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Einstein Bros. Honey Almond Cream Cheese Spread with the lot code "Best If Used By Jul 21, 2026 LO" and a Honey Almond lid paired with a Plain cup should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional questions, contact Schreiber Foods via their official website or the press contact provided.

title: Gear Isle Voluntarily Recalls Gold Lion and ilum Chocolate Products Due to Undeclared Sildenafil and Tadalafil
headline: Gear Isle Recalls Gold Lion Aphrodisiac and ilum Sex Chocolate Products Containing Undeclared Sildenafil and Tadalafil
description: Gear Isle recalls Gold Lion and ilum chocolate products containing undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil posing health risks, with no reported injuries to date.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gear Isle has initiated a voluntary nationwide recall of certain chocolate products due to the presence of undeclared active pharmaceutical ingredients, sildenafil and tadalafil. These ingredients are not listed on the product labels and pose potential health risks to consumers, especially those taking nitrates for cardiac conditions.

The recalled products include Gold Lion Aphrodisiac Chocolate Sachet and ilum Sex Chocolate. Both products are marketed as dietary supplements and were distributed nationwide via internet sales.

Undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil can cause serious side effects, such as a significant drop in blood pressure, which may be life-threatening for individuals using nitrate-based medications. Although Gear Isle has not received any reports of adverse events related to these products so far, consumers are advised to contact their healthcare provider if they experience any health issues after use.

content.text: Consumers who have purchased Gold Lion Aphrodisiac Chocolate Sachet or ilum Sex Chocolate from Gear Isle should immediately stop using the products and contact Gear Isle customer service at 888-387-4753 or via email at info@gearisle.com to request a refund.

title: Falcon Trading Company Recalls Organic Black Bean Products Due to Pesticide Residue
headline: Falcon Trading Company Recalls Black Beans Organic, Chili Bean Blend Organic, and Sunset Soup Mix Organic for Pesticide Residue Contamination
description: Falcon Trading Company recalls organic black bean products due to pesticide residue. Affected items include Black Beans Organic, Chili Bean Blend Organic, and Sunset Soup Mix Organic.
keywords: Black Beans Organic | Chili Bean Blend Organic | Sunset Soup Mix Organic
productDescription: Black Beans Organic | Chili Bean Blend Organic | Sunset Soup Mix Organic

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Falcon Trading Company, Inc. of Royal Oaks, California, has issued a recall for certain organic black bean products due to the detection of pesticide residue. The recall affects all lot numbers of the products listed below, as the items are sold in bulk and lot numbers may be mixed within sales bins.

The affected products include Black Beans Organic, Chili Bean Blend Organic, and Sunset Soup Mix Organic. These products were found to contain pesticide residue following routine testing of organic beans sourced from a certified organic handler.

To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with the consumption of these products. However, because the presence of pesticide residue violates organic product standards, Falcon Trading Company is taking precautionary measures by recalling all affected items.

Product Name Item Number UPC Package Size
Black Beans Organic 003040 086700930403 25 Pound sack
Chili Bean Blend Organic 003056 086700030561 15 Pound Box
Sunset Soup Mix Organic 013000 086700130001 25 Pound Box

facts.product: Black Beans Organic | Chili Bean Blend Organic | Sunset Soup Mix Organic

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the lot numbers on Falcon Trading Company’s Black Beans Organic, Chili Bean Blend Organic, and Sunset Soup Mix Organic products. If you have any affected items, return them to the place of purchase for a full refund or contact Falcon Trading Company at (831) 786-7000 or info@sunridgefarms.com for further instructions.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Falcon Trading Company, Inc. | (831) 786-7000 | info@sunridgefarms.com

title: Amneal Pharmaceuticals Recalls Magnesium Sulfate in Water for Injection, USP 4g/100mL Due to Product Mix-Up with Tranexamic Acid
headline: Amneal Pharmaceuticals Issues Nationwide Recall of Magnesium Sulfate in Water for Injection, USP 4g/100mL (NDC 70121-1720-3) for Tranexamic Acid Contamination
description: Amneal Pharmaceuticals recalls Magnesium Sulfate in Water for Injection, USP 4g/100mL IV bags after a lot was found to contain Tranexamic Acid, posing significant health risks.
keywords: Magnesium Sulfate in Water for Injection, USP, 4g/100mL, IV bag
A Magnesium Sulfate in Water for Injection pouch was found to contain an IV bag of Tranexamic Acid in 0.7% Sodium Chloride Injection, 10 mg/mL.
productDescription: Magnesium Sulfate in Water for Injection, USP, 4g/100mL, IV bag
reason: A Magnesium Sulfate in Water for Injection pouch was found to contain an IV bag of Tranexamic Acid in 0.7% Sodium Chloride Injection, 10 mg/mL.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Amneal Pharmaceuticals LLC has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of Magnesium Sulfate in Water for Injection, USP, 4g/100mL IV bags (NDC 70121-1720-3) due to a product mix-up. A pouch labeled as Magnesium Sulfate was found to contain an IV bag of Tranexamic Acid in 0.7% Sodium Chloride Injection, 10 mg/mL.

This mix-up poses significant risks, especially for pregnant individuals with preeclampsia or eclampsia who rely on magnesium therapy to prevent seizures. A delay in receiving correct magnesium treatment could lead to life-threatening conditions or long-term complications for both mother and preterm neonates. While Amneal has not received reports of adverse events linked to this recall, inadvertently administering Tranexamic Acid instead of magnesium sulfate could cause adverse effects such as blood clotting, seizures, hypersensitivity reactions, visual disturbances, and dizziness.

The affected product, lot AH250162, was distributed nationwide to wholesalers and distributors between December 22, 2025, and February 27, 2026. This product is typically supplied in cartons containing twelve 100mL pouches. Amneal has inspected 74% of the remaining recalled pouches and found no additional mix-ups.

Product Lot Number NDC Packaging Distribution Dates
Magnesium Sulfate in Water for Injection, USP, 4g/100mL IV bag AH250162 70121-1720-3 12 x 100mL pouches per carton 12/22/2025 to 2/27/2026

title: Cardinal Health Recalls Webcol™ Large Alcohol Prep Pads Due to Microbial Contamination
headline: Cardinal Health Voluntarily Recalls Webcol™ Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% Isopropyl Alcohol) Over Contamination Risk
description: Cardinal Health recalls Webcol Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% isopropyl alcohol) due to microbial contamination posing infection risks to vulnerable patients.
keywords: Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% isopropyl alcohol)
productDescription: Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% isopropyl alcohol)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Cardinal Health has voluntarily recalled select lots of Webcol™ Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% isopropyl alcohol) due to microbial contamination identified as Paenibacillus phoenicis. The recall affects products distributed in the United States, Puerto Rico, and Japan between September 2025 and February 2026. The contaminated prep pads pose a risk of local and systemic infections, particularly in at-risk populations such as patients with weakened immune systems, including those undergoing chemotherapy or with poorly controlled diabetes. These infections could be life-threatening, including bacteremia or central nervous system infections.

The Webcol™ Alcohol Prep Pads are intended for external use only and are commonly used for cleansing the skin. Cardinal Health informed customers of the recall by overnight mail on March 2, 2026.

If any adverse reactions or problems have occurred after using the product, consumers should consult their healthcare provider. Such events may also be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program.

This recall is being conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

Product Lot Number Distribution Period
Webcol™ Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% isopropyl alcohol) Various (select lots) September 2025 – February 2026

facts.product: Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% isopropyl alcohol)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their Webcol Large Alcohol Prep Pads are from affected lots and contact Cardinal Health at 800-292-9332 or via email at GMB-FieldCorrectiveAction@cardinalhealth.com for guidance and support.

title: Mama Grande Recalls Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar for Undeclared Wheat and Soy Allergens
headline: Mama Grande Recalls Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar Due to Undeclared Wheat and Soy Allergens
description: Mama Grande Tortilla Factory recalls Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar products due to undeclared wheat and soy allergens posing allergy risks.
keywords: Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar
productDescription: Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mama Grande Tortilla Factory of Mission, Texas, has issued a recall for two of its products, Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar, due to undeclared wheat and soy allergens. These allergens were not properly indicated on the product labels, posing a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with wheat or soy allergies or severe sensitivities.

The affected products were distributed between February 2, 2026, and March 5, 2026, to retail stores and wholesale customers within Texas. The products are packaged in plastic bags with the product name and corresponding UPC code clearly displayed on the front of the package.

Mama Grande Tortilla Factory has not received any reports of illnesses related to this recall.

Product Name UPC Expiration Dates
Gorditas de Azucar 860010238134 All Expiration Dates
Doraditas de Azucar 5901234123457 All Expiration Dates

facts.product: Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Gorditas de Azucar or Doraditas de Azucar from Mama Grande Tortilla Factory should not consume these products if allergic or sensitive to wheat or soy. Return the affected items to the place of purchase for a refund or dispose of them. Retailers must remove the recalled products from sale immediately and hold them for pickup. For questions or to arrange product return, contact Agustin Armendaiz at 956-905-8234, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. CST.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Agustin Armendaiz | 956-905-8234

authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

title: Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements Recalls Honey Dietary Supplements Due to Undeclared Sildenafil and Tadalafil
headline: Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC Voluntarily Recalls Boner Bear, Red Bull, and Blue Bull Honey Supplements Containing Undeclared Sildenafil and Tadalafil
description: Recall issued for Boner Bear, Red Bull, and Blue Bull honey supplements due to undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil posing serious health risks.
keywords: Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC
Boner Bear, Red Bull and Blue Bull
brandName: Boner Bear, Red Bull and Blue Bull
brandNames: Boner Bear, Red Bull and Blue Bull
companyName: Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC of Tampa, FL has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of certain honey dietary supplement products due to the presence of undeclared pharmaceutical ingredients. The affected products — marketed under the Boner Bear, Red Bull, and Blue Bull brand names — were found to contain sildenafil and tadalafil, active ingredients in the prescription medications Viagra and Cialis. These substances are not listed on the product labels and can pose serious health risks.

Sildenafil and tadalafil are used to treat erectile dysfunction under medical supervision. Their presence in these supplements without proper labeling may cause harmful interactions, especially for people taking nitrates for conditions such as diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease. Such interactions can lead to dangerously low blood pressure.

The recalled products are packaged in single-serving plastic tubes and identified by brand names and specific lot numbers and expiration dates.

Product Name Lot Number Expiration Date UPC Code
Boner Bears Honey BBH0128 01/28 788362191603
Red Bull Extreme ORB524 05/2029 9554100205595
Blue Bull Extreme N/A 02/2029 707443349917

facts.company: Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC
facts.brand: Boner Bear, Red Bull and Blue Bull

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have Boner Bear, Red Bull, or Blue Bull honey dietary supplements with the listed lot numbers and expiration dates, and return affected products to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions or further assistance, contact Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC at 1-800-362-4772 or sales@purevitaminsusa.com.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 1-800-362-4772 | sales@purevitaminsusa.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on March 14, 2026.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.
Review consumer support information on the company website.
Visit the company website for product details.

title: Insulet Recalls Certain Omnipod® 5 Pods Due to Insulin Leakage Risk
headline: Insulet Voluntarily Corrects Omnipod® 5 Pods with Internal Tubing Defect Causing Insulin Leakage
description: Insulet recalls specific Omnipod® 5 Pods due to a small tear in internal tubing that may cause insulin leakage and high blood glucose levels, including DKA risk.
keywords: Alternate controller enabled insulin infusion pump
Pods may have a small tear in the internal tubing that delivers insulin. This may leak inside the Pod instead of being fully infused into the body as intended.
productDescription: Alternate controller enabled insulin infusion pump
reason: Pods may have a small tear in the internal tubing that delivers insulin. This may leak inside the Pod instead of being fully infused into the body as intended.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Insulet Corporation has initiated a voluntary Medical Device Correction for certain lots of its Omnipod® 5 Pods distributed in the United States. This action follows the identification of a manufacturing issue involving a small tear in the internal tubing that delivers insulin inside the affected Pods. This defect may cause insulin to leak inside the Pod instead of being fully infused into the user's body as intended.

As a result, users of these affected Pods may experience high blood glucose levels due to under-delivery of insulin. In severe cases, this can lead to diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA), a serious condition requiring prompt medical attention. Insulet has received 18 reports of serious adverse events related to high blood glucose levels, including hospitalizations and cases of DKA, but no deaths have been reported. This issue does not affect continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) systems or their readings.

Affected Omnipod® 5 Pod Lot Numbers
Specific lot numbers can be checked online at omnipod.com/check-pods as they were not explicitly listed in the announcement.

facts.product: Alternate controller enabled insulin infusion pump

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should visit omnipod.com/check-pods to verify if their Omnipod® 5 Pods are part of the affected lots and request free replacements. If using a Pod from an affected lot, discontinue use immediately and switch to a Pod from an unaffected lot. For assistance, contact Insulet Product Support at 1-800-641-2049 (available 24/7) or use live chat at omnipod.com/current-podders.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: Insulet Product Support | 1-800-641-2049
Media contact: Cristal Downing | Chief Corporate Affairs Officer | | pr@insulet.com

title: Lidl US Recalls Favorina Chocolate Ladybugs German-Style Nougat for Undeclared Hazelnuts
headline: Lidl US Recalls Favorina Chocolate Ladybugs German-Style Nougat Candy Due to Undeclared Hazelnut Allergen
description: Lidl US recalls Favorina Chocolate Ladybugs - German-Style Nougat candy for undeclared hazelnuts, posing allergy risks to consumers in select states.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Lidl US has announced a recall of all lots of Favorina Chocolate Ladybugs - German-Style Nougat candy due to the presence of undeclared hazelnuts. This allergen was not indicated on the packaging, posing a potential risk to individuals with hazelnut allergies. Consuming the product could result in serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recalled product was distributed from January 28, 2026, through March 11, 2026, at Lidl store locations in Delaware, District of Columbia, Georgia, Maryland, New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, and Virginia. To date, there have been no reported illnesses linked to this product.

Product Size UPC Lot Numbers Distribution Dates States
Favorina Chocolate Ladybugs - German-Style Nougat 3.52 oz box 20304492 All lots 01/28/2026 – 03/11/2026 DE, DC, GA, MD, NJ, NY, NC, PA, SC, VA

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should not eat any Favorina Chocolate Ladybugs - German-Style Nougat candy and return the product to any Lidl US store for a full refund; no receipt is required. For questions, contact the Lidl US Customer Care Hotline at (844) 747-5435, Monday through Saturday, 8 am to 8 pm ET.

title: Primal Herbs Voluntarily Recalls Primal Herbs Volume Dietary Supplement Due to Undeclared Sildenafil
headline: Primal Supplements Group LLC Recalls Primal Herbs Volume Dietary Supplement for Undeclared Sildenafil Contamination
description: Primal Herbs recalls Primal Herbs Volume dietary supplement due to undeclared sildenafil, posing serious health risks. Recall affects purchases from July to September 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Primal Supplements Group LLC has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of Primal Herbs Volume dietary supplement due to the presence of undeclared sildenafil. This action follows FDA laboratory analysis that confirmed sildenafil, the active ingredient in the prescription drug Viagra, is present in the product but not listed on the label.

Sildenafil is used to treat erectile dysfunction under medical supervision. Its undisclosed inclusion in Primal Herbs Volume may pose serious health risks, especially for individuals taking nitrates for conditions such as diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease. The interaction can cause dangerously low blood pressure.

The recall applies to all Primal Herbs Volume orders placed between July 2, 2025, and September 19, 2025, purchased via the website primalherbs.com. The product is supplied in 8.5 oz round containers featuring green labels.

Individuals experiencing adverse effects related to this product should consult a healthcare provider.

Product NameOrder DatesPackagingWebsite Purchased From
Primal Herbs VolumeJuly 2, 2025 – September 19, 20258.5 oz round container with green labelprimalherbs.com

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Primal Herbs Volume between July 2, 2025, and September 19, 2025, should immediately discontinue use, check product details on the FDA website to confirm if their product is affected, and contact Primal Supplements Group LLC for a replacement or full store credit via email at hello@primalherbs.com or phone at +1 (856) 420-6117.

title: Frito-Lay Recalls Miss Vickie’s Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Frito-Lay Issues Voluntary Recall of Miss Vickie’s Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen
description: Frito-Lay recalls Miss Vickie’s Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips for undeclared milk allergen risk. Check affected lots distributed in six states from January 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Frito-Lay has issued a voluntary recall for select 8 oz. bags of Miss Vickie’s Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips due to the potential presence of undeclared milk allergens. The issue arises from the possible inclusion of jalapeño-flavored potato chips in the bags, which contain milk. Individuals with a milk allergy or severe sensitivity are at risk of a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction if they consume the recalled products.

The recalled products were distributed beginning January 15, 2026, through grocery, convenience, and drug stores, as well as local digital retailers across Arkansas, Louisiana, Mississippi, New Mexico, Oklahoma, and Texas. No other Miss Vickie’s products, including other flavors, sizes, or Variety Packs, are affected by this recall.

The issue was identified following a consumer report. To date, no allergic reactions have been reported in connection with this recall.

Product Size UPC "Guaranteed Fresh" Date Manufacturing Codes
Miss Vickie’s Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips 8 oz. 0 28400 76177 2 21 APR 2026 38U301414, 48U101514

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with milk allergies or sensitivities should not consume Miss Vickie’s Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips from the affected lots and should discard any remaining product. For assistance or questions, contact Frito-Lay consumer service at 1-877-984-2543, Monday through Friday, 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CST.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (877) 984-2543

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on March 04, 2026.

title: USA LESS Co. Recalls RHINO CHOCO VIP 10X Chocolate Due to Undeclared Tadalafil
headline: USA LESS Co. Recalls RHINO CHOCO VIP 10X Chocolate for Undeclared Prescription Drug Ingredient
description: USA LESS Co. is recalling RHINO CHOCO VIP 10X chocolate due to undeclared tadalafil, posing serious health risks for users of nitrate medications.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
USA LESS Co. has issued a recall for its product RHINO CHOCO VIP 10X due to the presence of undeclared tadalafil, a prescription drug ingredient used to treat erectile dysfunction. The chocolate product is packaged in 10 gram, 12-piece boxes with a black cardboard outer box and is marked with UPC Code 724087947668.

Tadalafil, the active ingredient in Cialis, is not listed on the product label. Its undisclosed presence poses health risks, especially for individuals taking prescription nitrates such as nitroglycerin. These interactions can cause a dangerous drop in blood pressure. This risk is particularly concerning for people with diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease who commonly use nitrate medications.

The recalled product was available for purchase both in retail stores and on various online platforms, including the company's website. The item has been removed from the online store pending resolution of the issue.

Product Package Size Quantity UPC Code Expiration Date
RHINO CHOCO VIP 10X 10 grams 12 pieces 724087947668 10/2027

No information is available regarding reported injuries or adverse events related to this product.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased RHINO CHOCO VIP 10X chocolate should stop using the product and return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For more information, contact USA LESS Co. at 1-800-872-5377 or via email at 409749@email4pr.com.

content.text: Consumer support: 1-800-872-5377 | 409749@email4pr.com

title: Savannah Bee Company Recalls Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard for Undeclared Wheat and Soy Allergens
headline: Savannah Bee Company Issues Recall of Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard Due to Undeclared Wheat and Soy Allergens
description: Savannah Bee Company recalls Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard due to undeclared wheat and soy allergens, posing risk of serious allergic reactions.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Savannah Bee Company has issued a recall for their Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard, 16 FL OZ, lot number B1L1360525, due to the presence of undeclared wheat and soy allergens. The product carries a best before date of 05/16/27 and UPC 8 50033 93758 9. Consumers with allergies or severe sensitivities to wheat or soy are at risk of potentially serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume this product.

The affected honey BBQ sauce was distributed nationwide to distribution centers, retail stores, and consumers between July 30, 2025, and February 26, 2026. The sauce is packaged in clear glass bottles with an orange Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard label. The lot number and best before date are etched on the neck of the bottle.

The recall was initiated after a customer reported that Honey BBQ Sauce-Sweet was mislabeled with a Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard label. Investigation revealed that the mislabeled product omitted wheat and soy allergens from the ingredient statement. No illnesses have been reported to date.

Product Lot Number Best Before Date UPC
Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard, 16 FL OZ B1L1360525 05/16/27 8 50033 93758 9

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately dispose of all Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard jars with lot number B1L1360525 and seek a full refund from the place of purchase. For questions, contact Savannah Bee Company customer service at 800-955-5080, Monday through Friday, 8 AM to 5 PM EST.

title: GO Raw LLC Expands Recall of Quest Cat Food Products Due to Low Thiamine Levels and Initiates Stop Sale
headline: GO Raw LLC Expands Voluntary Recall of Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Frozen and Enacts Stop Sale of All Quest Products
description: GO Raw LLC expands recall of Quest Cat Food due to low thiamine (Vitamin B1) levels; stop sale of all Quest products initiated nationwide.
keywords: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food
May contain low levels of thiamine (Vitamin B1)
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food
reason: May contain low levels of thiamine (Vitamin B1)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Go Raw LLC. has expanded its voluntary recall of Quest Diet Cat Food products due to potential low levels of thiamine (Vitamin B1). The recall now includes two lots of Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Frozen products, in addition to a previously recalled freeze-dried product. The company has also enacted a stop sale of all Quest products at all retailers until the thiamine content issue is resolved.

Thiamine is an essential vitamin for cats, and deficiency can lead to serious health issues. Symptoms of thiamine deficiency include decreased appetite, salivation, vomiting, failure to grow, weight loss, and, in advanced cases, neurological signs such as neck ventroflexion, mental dullness, vision changes, unsteady walking, circling, falling, and seizures. Prompt veterinary treatment can typically reverse the condition.

The recalled frozen products were distributed nationwide through retail stores in multiple states including Colorado, Utah, Washington, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Michigan, California, Texas, Illinois, Georgia, North Carolina, South Carolina, Florida, Minnesota, New York, Ohio, Wisconsin, Idaho, and Montana. The company has instructed retailers to remove the affected products from shelves immediately and is replacing all existing inventory with newly formulated products.

The source does not specify whether injuries or adverse events related to consumption of these products have been reported.

Product Lot Number Best Buy Date Description UPC
Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried C25288 10/15/2027 10 oz Bags Not specified
Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Frozen MCD25350 5/17/2027 Beige zip-lock 2 lb. package with purple stripe 6-91730-17104-9
Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Frozen MCC25321 6/16/2027 Beige zip-lock 2 lb. package with purple stripe 6-91730-17104-9

facts.productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop feeding the affected Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Frozen and freeze-dried products and return them to the place of purchase for a full refund or replacement. For questions, contact Go Raw LLC at cs@gorawllc.com or call 801-432-7478 (Monday through Friday, 9 am to 4 pm MST).

title: Saputo USA Recalls Great Value Cottage Cheese Because of Possible Health Risk
headline: Saputo USA Recalls Great Value Cottage Cheese Because of Possible Health Risk
description: Great Value announced a recall involving Cottage Cheese after issues linked to not fully pasteurized.. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Saputo Cheese USA Inc. is voluntarily recalling select Great Value cottage cheese products due to the possible use of liquid dairy ingredients that were not fully pasteurized according to state regulatory standards. Consuming these products may pose a significant health risk, particularly for young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems.

The issue was identified during pasteurizer troubleshooting in collaboration with the California Department of Food and Agriculture. The affected pasteurizer has since been restored to normal function, verified, and sealed by the Department. No other products from the facility are impacted by this recall.

Affected products were distributed to Walmart stores and distribution centers across multiple states between February 17 and February 20, 2026. Consumers who purchased any of the recalled cottage cheese products should not consume them and may return the items to the place of purchase for a full refund.

For further inquiries, customers can contact Saputo at 1-888-587-2423, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT. This recall is conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Product Name Packaging Size & Type UPC Expiration Dates Plant Code
Great Value Fat Free Small Curd Cottage Cheese – 0% Milkfat 24 oz. container 078742373393 BEST IF USED BY APR-01-26, APR-03-26 06-71
Great Value Lowfat Small Curd Cottage Cheese – 2% Milkfat 24 oz. container 078742116730 BEST IF USED BY APR-01-26, APR-02-26, APR-03-26 06-71
Great Value Small Curd Cottage Cheese – 4% Milkfat minimum 16 oz. container 078742372358 BEST IF USED BY APR-02-26 06-71
Great Value Small Curd Cottage Cheese – 4% Milkfat minimum 24 oz. container 078742372365 BEST IF USED BY APR-02-26, APR-03-26 06-71
Great Value Small Curd Cottage Cheese – 4% Milkfat minimum 3 lb. container 078742147970 BEST IF USED BY APR-01-26, APR-02-26 06-71

The recalled products were sold in white plastic tubs with white lids. The “Best If Used By” date and plant code (PLT# 06-71) are printed on the side of the container lid. The recall only applies to the states listed below where these products were distributed:

  • Alaska
  • Alabama
  • Arkansas
  • Arizona
  • California
  • Colorado
  • Georgia

title: Lockout Supplements Recalls Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup for Undeclared Sildenafil
headline: Lockout Supplements Announces Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup Due to Undeclared Sildenafil
description: Lockout Supplements recalls Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup nationwide due to undeclared sildenafil, posing health risks. No injuries reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Lockout Supplements has announced a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots of Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup due to the presence of undeclared sildenafil. Sildenafil is the active ingredient in the prescription drug Viagra, which is intended to be used only under the supervision of a licensed healthcare professional. Its inclusion in this product was not disclosed on the label.

The undeclared sildenafil in Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup poses a significant health risk, especially to individuals taking nitrates for conditions such as diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease. Combining sildenafil with nitrates can dangerously lower blood pressure.

Although no adverse events have been reported to Lockout Supplements so far, consumers are advised to stop using the product immediately.

The recall includes all lots of Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup distributed from January 1, 2025, through February 13, 2026. The product was sold nationwide online via lockoutsupplements.com. Lockout Supplements is notifying customers through its website and is offering refunds for returned products.

Product Name UPC Lot Dates
Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup 000856683570 01/01/25 - 02/13/26

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup should check the lot dates and discontinue use immediately. Return the product to the place of purchase for a refund or dispose of it. For questions, contact Lockout Supplements at 972-548-1988 or email glenn@lockoutforums.com.

content.text: Consumer support: Lockout Supplements | 972 548-1988 | glenn@lockoutforums.com

The undeclared sildenafil in Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup poses a significant health risk, especially to individuals taking nitrates for conditions such as diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease. Combining sildenafil with nitrates can dangerously lower blood pressure.

Although no adverse events have been reported to Lockout Supplements so far, consumers are advised to stop using the product immediately.

The recall includes all lots of Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup distributed from January 1, 2025, through February 13, 2026. The product was sold nationwide online via lockoutsupplements.com. Lockout Supplements is notifying customers through its website and is offering refunds for returned products.

Product Name UPC Lot Dates

title: Elite Treats LLC Recalls Single Lot of Chicken Chips Dog Treats Due to Salmonella Contamination
headline: Elite Treats LLC Issues Recall for Lot 24045 of Chicken Chips for Dogs Due to Potential Salmonella
description: Elite Treats recalls one lot of Chicken Chips dog treats contaminated with Salmonella, distributed in southeastern U.S. No illnesses reported. Check lot 24045.
keywords: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Elite Treats, LLC., based in Boca Raton, FL, has issued a recall for a single lot of its "Elite Treats Chicken Chips for Dogs" 6-ounce bags due to potential Salmonella contamination. The affected lot is marked with lot number 24045 and an expiration date of 04/2027. Salmonella contamination in pet food poses health risks to both pets and humans.

Pets consuming contaminated products may experience symptoms such as lethargy, diarrhea (possibly bloody), fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Some pets may be asymptomatic carriers, spreading the bacteria through feces and saliva, placing other pets and household members at risk.

Humans can be exposed to Salmonella through handling the product, contact with infected pets, or contact with contaminated surfaces. Symptoms in humans can include nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal pain, and fever, and in rare cases can lead to severe infections affecting various organs. Individuals who develop symptoms after exposure should seek medical advice promptly.

The recalled product was distributed to feed stores in Alabama, Florida, Georgia, North Carolina, and South Carolina via Florida Hardware, LLC. There have been no reported illnesses linked to this product as of this announcement.

Product Package Size Lot Number Expiration Date
Elite Treats Chicken Chips for Dogs 6-ounce bag 24045 04/2027

facts.productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Elite Treats Chicken Chips dog treats for lot number 24045 with expiration 04/2027. If you have this lot, safely dispose of the product and thoroughly clean all contacted utensils and surfaces. For questions or refunds, contact Elite Treats at 561-901-5310 or elite.treats@yahoo.com.

title: Made Fresh Salads, Inc. Recalls Assorted Cream Cheeses and Tofu Spread Due to Listeria Contamination
headline: Made Fresh Salads, Inc. Issues Recall on Various Cream Cheese Flavors and Tofu Spread for Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
description: Made Fresh Salads recalls assorted cream cheese flavors and tofu spread due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination, with no illnesses reported.
keywords: Various Cream Cheeses and Tofu Spread
productDescription: Various Cream Cheeses and Tofu Spread

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Made Fresh Salads, Inc., located in Bay Shore, NY, is recalling multiple flavors of its cream cheese products due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Pregnant women infected with Listeria risk miscarriage or stillbirth, while healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea.

The recalled cream cheese products were distributed in Brooklyn, Queens, the Bronx, and broader New York City areas via direct delivery to retail stores and distributors. The affected products come in 5-pound white plastic tubs labeled with Made Fresh Salads branding. All affected items have expiration dates through February 27, 2026, located on the bottom left corner of the label.

This recall was initiated after a routine company sampling program detected Listeria monocytogenes contamination within a mixer used for production. Made Fresh Salads has ceased using and removed the contaminated mixer from service. To date, no illnesses related to these products have been reported.

title: Go Raw LLC Voluntarily Recalls Single Lot of Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets for Low Thiamine Levels
headline: Go Raw LLC Issues Voluntary Recall of Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets, Lot C25288, Due to Low Thiamine (Vitamin B1)
description: Go Raw LLC recalls a single lot of Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets due to low thiamine levels that may cause feline health risks.
keywords: Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets, 10oz bag
Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food
May contain low levels of thiamine (Vitamin B1)
productDescription: Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets, 10oz bag
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food
reason: May contain low levels of thiamine (Vitamin B1)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Go Raw LLC has announced a voluntary recall of a single lot of Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets, 10 oz bag, due to potentially low levels of thiamine (Vitamin B1). This recall was initiated after one confirmed illness report linked to the product, with testing confirming thiamine levels below requirements for a feline diet.

Thiamine is an essential nutrient for cats, and inadequate intake over time can cause thiamine deficiency. Symptoms in cats may begin with gastrointestinal issues such as decreased appetite, salivation, vomiting, failure to grow, and weight loss. Advanced deficiency may result in neurological symptoms including neck bending towards the floor (ventroflexion), mental dullness, vision changes, unsteady walking, circling, falling, and seizures. Early veterinary intervention is important as thiamine deficiency is generally reversible with prompt treatment.

The recalled product was distributed nationwide in retail stores across Colorado, Utah, Washington, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Michigan, California, Texas, and Illinois.

Product Size Lot Code UPC Best Buy Date Distribution States
Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets 10 oz bag C25288 6-91730-18103-1 10/15/2027 CO, UT, WA, OR, PA, RI, MI, CA, TX, IL

The affected product is packaged in a beige zip-lock bag with a purple stripe, labeled with the lot code and best buy date on the front.

facts.product: Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets, 10oz bag
facts.productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should stop feeding the affected 10 oz bags of Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets, Lot C25288, and return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions or support, contact Go Raw LLC customer service at 801-432-7478 (Monday to Friday, 9 am to 4 pm Mountain Time) or by email at cs@gorawllc.com.

title: Shaman Botanicals, LLC Recalls Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein for Undeclared Excess 7-Hydroxymitragynine
headline: Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein by Shaman Botanicals, LLC Due to Excess 7-Hydroxymitragynine
description: Shaman Botanicals, LLC recalls Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein for undeclared 7-Hydroxymitragynine exceeding labeled amount, nationwide distribution affected.
keywords: Product contains 7-Hydroxymitragynine (7-OH) in an amount more than the declared value of 7.5 mg/tablet.
reason: Product contains 7-Hydroxymitragynine (7-OH) in an amount more than the declared value of 7.5 mg/tablet.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Shaman Botanicals, LLC has voluntarily recalled one lot of its Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein dietary supplement due to an undeclared concentration of 7-Hydroxymitragynine (7-OH) exceeding the labeled amount of 7.5 mg per tablet. This recall affects products distributed nationwide to wholesalers, retailers, and consumers through online sales.

Consumers using the affected product may ingest a higher dose of 7-Hydroxymitragynine than intended, which could potentially lead to adverse health effects. To date, Shaman Botanicals, LLC has not received any reports of adverse events related to the recalled lot.

The recall applies to Lot B# AAW.501.3 of Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein, regardless of expiration date. The product is sold in various packaging formats identified by their UPC codes and lot number printed on the package or bottle bottom.

Product Name Lot Number UPC Code
Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein (2-count bag) Lot B# AAW.501.3 810057763724
Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein (20-count bag) Lot B# AAW.501.3 810057763830
Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein (30-count bottle) Lot B# AAW.501.3 810057763779

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein Lot B# AAW.501.3 should stop use immediately and return the product to Shaman Botanicals, LLC for a full refund or replacement. Contact the company at 855-427-7386 or email Quality@CBDAmericanShaman.com. Visit https://recall.cbdamericanshaman.com/ for recall details and registration.

content.text: Consumer support: Vince Sanders | 855-427-7386 | Quality@CBDAmericanShaman.com

title: Ambrosia Brands, LLC Recalls Rosabella Moringa Capsules Because of Possible Health Risk
headline: Ambrosia Brands, LLC Recalls Rosabella Moringa Capsules Because of Possible Health Risk
description: Rosabella announced a recall involving Moringa capsules after issues linked to possible contamination with salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ambrosia Brands, LLC of New York, New York, is voluntarily recalling certain lots of its Rosabella Moringa Capsules due to possible contamination with Salmonella. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly people, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Healthy persons infected with Salmonella may experience symptoms such as fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, infection can lead to severe complications including bloodstream infections and arterial infections.

The recalled product, Rosabella Moringa Capsules in 60-count bottles, was sold nationwide in the United States via the company's direct-to-consumer website and TikTok Shop since February 2025. There is concern about unauthorized third-party sales through platforms such as eBay and Shein. Although the recalled lots have not been sold on Amazon.com, consumers who have purchased this product from Amazon are advised to verify their lot numbers.

To date, seven illnesses and three hospitalizations across the U.S. have been linked to Salmonella contamination possibly associated with these products. Ambrosia Brands has stopped purchasing the implicated raw moringa leaf powder and is working with the FDA and CDC to investigate the outbreak. Customers who have bottles from the affected lots should not consume, sell, or distribute the capsules and are instructed to dispose of them immediately.

Consumers with questions can contact Ambrosia Brands' customer service Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT at 914-768-1357.

title: Green Lumber Dietary Supplement Recall Due to Undeclared Prescription Drug Tadalafil
headline: Recall of Green Lumber Dietary Supplement Containing Undeclared Tadalafil in Counterfeit Products
description: Green Lumber recalls dietary supplements with undeclared prescription drug tadalafil found in counterfeit products. Confirm lot numbers and avoid affected items.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Green Lumber Holding, LLC has issued an updated consumer alert related to a recall of dietary supplements falsely marketed as Green Lumber products. The recall was originally announced on August 28, 2025, after the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) detected the presence of tadalafil—an undeclared prescription drug—in some products labeled as Green Lumber. Tadalafil is not an ingredient in any authentic Green Lumber product, and its presence poses significant health risks.

The FDA’s February 9, 2026 Enforcement Report referenced this recall but did not announce a new recall. Green Lumber’s internal investigation found that a former employee had misappropriated genuine packaging and customer information to distribute counterfeit or adulterated products. That individual has since been terminated. Consumers should be aware that only products bearing the lot number LOT308EXP03/28 are counterfeit and subject to the recall.

Authentic Green Lumber products have specific lot numbers that are not affected by the recall. Consumers are urged to verify the lot numbers on their packaging to ensure product authenticity. Using the counterfeit products may expose consumers to dangerous side effects, especially if they are taking medications for heart disease, blood pressure, or other health conditions due to the undeclared tadalafil. Anyone experiencing unexpected side effects after using these supplements should seek medical attention promptly.

Consumers are encouraged to report suspected counterfeit products both to Green Lumber and the FDA’s MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting Program. Green Lumber has strengthened its safeguards and cooperated with the FDA and law enforcement to protect customers from such risks.

Lot Number Expiration Date
LOTGL0130151EXP0926 09/26
GL01EXP09/27 09/27
GL110725EXP110727 11/07/27

The following lot number is the only one subject to recall due to being identified as counterfeit and containing undeclared tadalafil:

Lot Number Expiration Date
LOT308EXP03/28 03/28

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have Green Lumber dietary supplements should check if their product bears the recalled lot number LOT308EXP03/28, and if so, discontinue use and discard the product. For questions or to report suspected counterfeit products or adverse events, contact Green Lumber Holding, LLC at 949.426.8622 or consumer-safety@greenlumber.com.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Green Lumber Holding, LLC | 949.426.8622 | consumer-safety@greenlumber.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on February 13, 2026.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.
Visit the company website for product details.

title: IF Copack LLC dba Initiative Foods Recalls Tippy Toes Apple Pear Banana Fruit Puree Due to Elevated Patulin Levels
headline: IF Copack LLC dba Initiative Foods Recalls Tippy Toes Apple Pear Banana Fruit Puree for Elevated Patulin Contamination
description: IF Copack LLC dba Initiative Foods recalls Tippy Toes Apple Pear Banana fruit puree due to elevated patulin levels detected by FDA in 2026.
keywords: IF Copack, LLC. d.b.a. Initiative Foods
companyName: IF Copack, LLC. d.b.a. Initiative Foods

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
IF Copack LLC d.b.a. Initiative Foods is recalling one lot of the “Tippy Toes” Apple Pear Banana fruit puree due to elevated levels of patulin, a mycotoxin produced by mold that can grow on various fruits. Long-term exposure to patulin may cause immune suppression, nerve damage, headache, fever, and nausea. No illnesses or injuries have been reported so far.

The recalled product was distributed nationwide across the United States, excluding Alaska, and may have also reached Guam and Puerto Rico. The recall follows findings by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) during its Total Diet Study, which detected patulin levels higher than normally expected. Initiative Foods is cooperating with the FDA to ensure consumer safety by recalling the affected lot.

Product Name Packaging Format UPC Lot Number / Expiration Date Package Code Identifier
Tippy Toes Apple Pear Banana 2-pack plastic tubs, with product information sleeve 036800 265783 Lot # 07174 / Best By Date: BB 07/17/2026 INIA0120

facts.company: IF Copack, LLC. d.b.a. Initiative Foods

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Tippy Toes Apple Pear Banana fruit puree with Lot #07174 and Best By Date 07/17/2026 should stop using the product immediately and either dispose of it or return it to the place of purchase for a refund. For questions or further information, contact Initiative Foods directly at 1-855-215-5730.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 1-855-215-5730

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on February 13, 2026.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.

title: Junebar Recall of Chocolate Cherry and Peanut Butter Chocolate Chip Snack Bars for Undeclared Milk and Soy Allergens
headline: Junebar Recalls Specific Snack Bars Containing Undeclared Milk and Soy Allergens Posing Allergy Risks
description: Junebar is recalling Chocolate Cherry and Peanut Butter Chocolate Chip Snack Bars due to undeclared milk and soy allergens that may cause severe allergic reactions.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Juniper Granola, LLC., based in Rochester, NY, has issued a recall for select Junebar snack bars due to the presence of undeclared milk and soy allergens. The affected products are the Chocolate Cherry and Peanut Butter Chocolate Chip Junebars with batch labels L1300, L1300A, L1300B, and L1301A. These bars may pose a serious or life-threatening risk to individuals allergic or severely sensitive to milk or soy.

The impacted bars were distributed throughout New York State in January 2026 via grocery retailers and direct website orders. The undeclared allergens were identified after a customer experienced an allergic reaction to milk, prompting an investigation. The issue was traced to a sourcing error, where non-vegan chocolate chips containing milk and soy were mistakenly used during production instead of the usual dairy-free, soy-free chips.

Product Batch Labels Distribution Area Distribution Date
Chocolate Cherry Junebar L1300, L1300A, L1300B, L1301A New York State January 2026
Peanut Butter Chocolate Chip Junebar L1300, L1300A, L1300B, L1301A New York State January 2026

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Junebar snack bars for batch labels L1300, L1300A, L1300B, or L1301A. If affected, do not consume and return the bars to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Juniper Granola at 315-226-3339 or email contact@junebars.com.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (315) 226-3339 | contact@junebars.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on February 12, 2026.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.
Visit the company website for product details.

title: Slade Gorton & Co., Inc. Recalls Wellsley Farms Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon Due to Listeria Contamination
headline: Slade Gorton & Co., Inc. Issues Recall on Wellsley Farms Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon Sold at BJ’s Wholesale Club for Listeria Monocytogenes Risk
description: Slade Gorton & Co., Inc. recalls one lot of Wellsley Farms Atlantic Salmon sold at BJ’s Wholesale Club due to potential Listeria contamination. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Slade Gorton & Co., Inc. is recalling one lot of Wellsley Farms Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. The affected product was sold exclusively at BJ’s Wholesale Club stores in Delaware, Maryland, New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Virginia from January 31 through February 7, 2026.

The recalled salmon is packaged in 2-pound bags featuring the Wellsley Farms logo and the label "Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon." The product’s UPC code is 888670025963, and the specific lot number subject to recall is 3896. The suspected contamination was identified through a random sample collected by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

While healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea, Listeria infection poses a higher risk to pregnant women as it can lead to miscarriages and stillbirths. No illnesses related to this recall have been reported to date. Consumers who experience symptoms should seek medical advice promptly.

Product Lot Number UPC Code Package Size Retail Locations Sale Dates
Wellsley Farms Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon 3896 888670025963 2 lb bag BJ’s Wholesale Club stores in DE, MD, NJ, NY, NC, PA, VA January 31 – February 7, 2026

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Wellsley Farms Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon package for lot number 3896 and UPC 888670025963 purchased at BJ’s Wholesale Club between January 31 and February 7, 2026. If you have this product, stop use immediately and contact Slade Gorton & Co., Inc. at 1-888-628-0730 for refund and disposal instructions.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: (888) 628-0730
Media contact: | | Annie.tselikis@sladegorton.com | (781) 366-9251

content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on February 12, 2026.
