en.title: Slade Gorton & Co., Inc. Recalls Wellsley Farms Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon Due to Listeria Contamination
en.description: Slade Gorton & Co., Inc. recalls one lot of Wellsley Farms Atlantic Salmon sold at BJ’s Wholesale Club due to potential Listeria contamination. No illnesses reported.
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Slade Gorton & Co., Inc. is recalling one lot of Wellsley Farms Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. The affected product was sold exclusively at BJ’s Wholesale Club stores in Delaware, Maryland, New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Virginia from January 31 through February 7, 2026.

The recalled salmon is packaged in 2-pound bags featuring the Wellsley Farms logo and the label "Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon." The product’s UPC code is 888670025963, and the specific lot number subject to recall is 3896. The suspected contamination was identified through a random sample collected by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

While healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea, Listeria infection poses a higher risk to pregnant women as it can lead to miscarriages and stillbirths. No illnesses related to this recall have been reported to date. Consumers who experience symptoms should seek medical advice promptly.

Product Lot Number UPC Code Package Size Retail Locations Sale Dates
Wellsley Farms Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon 3896 888670025963 2 lb bag BJ’s Wholesale Club stores in DE, MD, NJ, NY, NC, PA, VA January 31 – February 7, 2026

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Wellsley Farms Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon package for lot number 3896 and UPC 888670025963 purchased at BJ’s Wholesale Club between January 31 and February 7, 2026. If you have this product, stop use immediately and contact Slade Gorton & Co., Inc. at 1-888-628-0730 for refund and disposal instructions.

title: Trividia Health Initiates Labeling Correction for TRUE METRIX Blood Glucose Monitoring Systems Due to E-5 Error Code Warning
headline: Labeling Correction Issued for TRUE METRIX Blood Glucose Monitoring Systems to Address E-5 Error Code Medical Warning
description: Trividia Health issues labeling correction for TRUE METRIX glucose monitors to instruct immediate medical attention for E-5 error code with symptoms.
keywords: As currently written, the Owner’s Booklets/System Instructions for Use fails to emphasize that users must seek medical attention immediately if they receive an E-5 error code and are experiencing symptoms of high glucose.
reason: As currently written, the Owner’s Booklets/System Instructions for Use fails to emphasize that users must seek medical attention immediately if they receive an E-5 error code and are experiencing symptoms of high glucose.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Trividia Health, Inc. has announced a labeling correction for all TRUE METRIX® Blood Glucose Monitoring Systems, including the TRUE METRIX, TRUE METRIX AIR, TRUE METRIX GO, and TRUE METRIX PRO models. This update affects devices distributed in the United States, United Kingdom, Mexico, Australia, and the Caribbean. The correction addresses the current Owner’s Booklets/System Instructions for Use, which do not sufficiently instruct users to seek immediate medical attention if they receive an E-5 error code and are experiencing symptoms of high blood glucose.

The E-5 error code signifies either a very high blood glucose reading (greater than 600 mg/dL) or a test strip error. Without appropriate warnings, users might delay necessary medical treatment, which can result in serious health consequences or death, particularly for those with extremely high glucose levels. Since the product launch in August 2014, there have been 114 reported serious injuries and one fatality linked to this error code.

Trividia Health has updated the instructions for users to retest with a new test strip and seek medical help immediately if symptoms such as fatigue, excessive urination, thirst, or blurry vision persist alongside an E-5 error. The company is distributing product notices to pharmacies, distributors, and mail order companies to inform end users and request the posting of these notices where products are stored or sold. The devices themselves do not require return or replacement, and users may continue their normal use.

For questions regarding the labeling update, users and customers can contact Trividia Health Customer Care or visit the company’s dedicated web page. Adverse events related to these products may be reported through the FDA’s MedWatch program.

Product Description Regions Affected Customer Contact Support Phone
TRUE METRIX, TRUE METRIX AIR, TRUE METRIX GO, TRUE METRIX PRO Blood Glucose Monitoring Systems requiring updated Owner’s Booklets/System Instructions for Use United States, United Kingdom, Mexico, Australia, Caribbean www.trividiahealth.com/E-5productnotice 1-888-835-2723 (Mon-Fri, 8AM-8PM EST)
1-800-803-6025 (Mon-Fri, 8AM-8PM EST for E-5 assistance)

Updated Instructions for E-5 Error Code for TRUE METRIX, TRUE METRIX AIR, TRUE METRIX GO, and TRUE METRIX PRO:

Display Reason Recommended Action
E-5 Error Code Very high blood glucose (>600 mg/dL) or test strip error Retest with a new test strip. If error persists and symptoms such as fatigue, excess urination, thirst, or blurry vision are present, seek medical attention immediately. If no symptoms, retest with a new strip again. If error continues without symptoms, call 1-800-803-6025 for assistance.

This labeling correction involves updates to the physical Owner’s Booklets provided with the devices as well as online instructions and help guides. Trividia Health is actively communicating with customers and regulatory bodies, including the U.S. FDA, to ensure safety and awareness.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Users of TRUE METRIX Blood Glucose Monitoring Systems should review the updated Owner’s Booklets and instructions available at www.trividiahealth.com/E-5productnotice. If an E-5 error code appears and symptoms such as fatigue, excessive urination, thirst, or blurry vision are present, retest with a new strip and seek medical attention immediately. For assistance, call 1-800-803-6025.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: Trividia Health Customer Care Department | 1-888-835-2723
Media contact: Annmarie Ramos | | ext. 3190 | (800) 342-7226

title: Prime Food Processing LLC Recalls Uneviscerated No Brand Dried Croaker Fish Due to Safety Risk
headline: Prime Food Processing LLC Voluntarily Recalls Uneviscerated No Brand Dried Croaker Fish Over Potential Bacterial Contamination
description: Prime Food Processing LLC recalls No Brand Dried Croaker Fish due to incomplete evisceration that may harbor harmful bacteria, posing health risks.
keywords: Product was not adequately eviscerated and may harbor harmful bacteria or toxins
reason: Product was not adequately eviscerated and may harbor harmful bacteria or toxins

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Prime Food Processing LLC has issued a voluntary recall of 80 cases of Dried Croaker Fish due to inadequate evisceration. The fish may contain internal organs that harbor harmful bacteria or toxins, posing a potential health risk to consumers. This action was taken in compliance with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) regulations after the issue was identified through routine testing by the New York Department of Agriculture.

The affected product was imported from Vietnam and packaged in clear packaging with green trim. The recall specifically concerns products distributed to Asian grocery stores across multiple states including AL, AZ, CA, FL, GA, HI, ID, MA, MD, MO, NJ, NV, NY, OH, OK, PA, TN, TX, UT, VA, WA, and WI.

So far, no illnesses or adverse reactions linked to this product have been reported.

Item Number Product Lot Code Expiration Date Packaging Description
AF1410 Dried Croaker Fish 25128 12-28-27 Clear Packaging with Green Trim

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased No Brand Dried Croaker Fish should verify the lot code and expiration date on the packaging and return any recalled product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further questions, contact Prime Food Processing LLC at 718-963-2323, Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM EST.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Prime Food Processing LLC | 718-963-2323

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on February 05, 2026.

title: Mondelēz Global LLC Expands Recall of CHIPS AHOY! Baked Bites Brookie Due to Choking Hazard in USA
headline: Expanded Recall of CHIPS AHOY! Baked Bites Brookie by Mondelēz Global LLC for Choking Hazard in the USA
description: Mondelēz Global LLC expands recall of CHIPS AHOY! Baked Bites Brookie for choking hazard from small starch clumps in the USA, no injuries reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mondelēz Global LLC has expanded its voluntary recall of CHIPS AHOY! Baked Bites Brookie products in the United States. This expansion includes an additional code date of 10MAY2026 and two more UPCs for product pouches within the recalled carton units. The recall addresses a product safety concern resulting from an incorrect mixing process that caused small corn starch clumps to form in the baked brownie bites.

These small starch clumps pose a choking hazard, particularly for vulnerable groups such as young children and the elderly. The recall is limited to CHIPS AHOY! Baked Bites Brookie products with specific "Best When Used By" dates listed below. Mondelēz Global LLC confirms that no other CHIPS AHOY! or company products are affected by this recall.

To date, no injuries or illnesses have been reported in connection with this product.

Product Description Retail UPC Case GTIN Best When Used By Dates

2.8 oz CA! BAKED BITES BROOKIE POUCH 4400008667 N/A 09MAY2026, 10MAY2026, 11MAY2026, 12MAY2026
7.0 oz CA! BAKED BITES BROOKIE 5CT 12 44000085650 10044000085657 10MAY2026, 12MAY2026, 18MAY2026
1.4 oz CA! BAKED BITES BROOKIE POUCH 4400008566 N/A 10MAY2026, 12MAY2026, 18MAY2026

content.text: Consumers should check the lot and product details to identify affected CHIPS AHOY! Baked Bites Brookie products and contact Mondelēz Global LLC at 1-855-535-5948 for assistance or to confirm product status.

title: Akkarco LLC Recalls Ashfiat Alharamain Honey Energy Support Due to Undeclared Tadalafil
headline: Akkarco LLC Voluntarily Recalls Ashfiat Alharamain Honey Product for Energy Support Containing Undeclared Tadalafil
description: Akkarco LLC recalls Ashfiat Alharamain honey energy supplement due to undeclared tadalafil, posing health risks. Distributed nationwide in the U.S.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Akkarco LLC of Lorton, Virginia, has initiated a voluntary recall of its product Ashfiat Alharamain Energy Support due to the presence of undeclared tadalafil. Tadalafil is a prescription drug approved by the FDA for treating male erectile dysfunction and belongs to the family of phosphodiesterase (PDE5) inhibitors. This substance is not permitted in dietary supplements and its safety and efficacy in this product have not been established.

The product, marketed as a honey-based energy support supplement, may pose health risks, especially to individuals with underlying medical conditions or those taking medications that could interact with tadalafil. Potential adverse effects include cardiovascular complications, changes in blood pressure, dizziness, and headache.

This recall affects products distributed nationwide in the United States, including through third-party e-commerce platforms such as Amazon.

To date, no confirmed adverse health events have been reported in connection with this recall.

Product Name UPC Batch Number Expiration Date
ASHFIAT ALHARAMAIN 1234561870003 ENGOT24 October 2028

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using the Ashfiat Alharamain Honey Product for Energy Support, check the product details and lot number, and follow return or disposal instructions provided by the seller or distributor. For questions or further information, contact Akkarco LLC at office@unitedlegalexperts.com.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: Akkarco LLC | office@unitedlegalexperts.com
Media contact: Shahzaib Amin Malik

title: Neogen Voluntarily Recalls Neogen®Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution Due to Microbial Contamination
headline: Neogen Announces Nationwide Recall of Neogen®Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution Over Contamination Risks
description: Neogen recalls Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution due to microbial contamination compromising sterility, posing infection risks in animals.
keywords: Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution
productDescription: Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Neogen Corporation has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots within expiry of Neogen®Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution. This product is intended for topical wound management in dogs, cats, and horses and is distributed to veterinarians and animal health distributors across the United States. The recall was initiated due to microbial contamination found in certain product lots, which compromises the sterility of the solution.

The contamination poses a risk of infection in animals, especially if the product is used in ways inconsistent with its labeled instructions, such as intraarticular injections. While adverse events have been reported in horses following such injections, no adverse events have been documented when the product is used as directed for topical wound care on surgical wounds, burns, ulcers, and autograft procedures. As a precautionary measure, all 2 mL/20 mg vials are included in the recall while the investigation continues.

Veterinarians who observe any adverse effects potentially related to this product are encouraged to report these instances to the FDA using Form FDA 1932a. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, and no other Neogen products are affected.

Product Variant Lot Numbers UPC Code
2 mL / 20 mg vials 0236735, 0236736, 0336746, 0336747, 0536760, 0536761, 0636768, 0636769, 0836792, 0836785, 0836788, 0836789, 0936794, 0936795, 1036801, 1036802, 1036803, 1036804, 1136807, 1136808, 0246837, 0246838, 4L001B, 4L002 726087089386
10 mL / 50 mg vials 0136731, 0736777, 0346843, 5A001 726087089393

facts.product: Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers and veterinarians should immediately discontinue use of recalled Neogen®Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution, quarantine any remaining product, and contact Neogen Animal Safety Customer Support at (859) 254-1221 or AnimalHealth@neogen.com for return instructions.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (859) 254-1221 | AnimalHealth@neogen.com

content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on January 29, 2026.

title: Why Not Natural Recalls Organic Moringa – Green Superfood Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination
headline: Why Not Natural Recalls Organic Moringa – Green Superfood for Possible Salmonella Contamination
description: Why Not Natural recalls Organic Moringa – Green Superfood capsules due to potential Salmonella risk; distributed nationwide July 2025 to January 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Why Not Natural, based in Houston, Texas, has initiated a voluntary recall of its Organic Moringa – Green Superfood due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella is a harmful bacterium that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. In healthy individuals, infection typically results in symptoms such as fever, diarrhea (which may include blood), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

The affected product consists of Moringa Capsules, which were distributed nationwide via mail orders through Why Not Natural’s own e-commerce platform as well as third-party online retailers, including Amazon.com. Sales occurred between July 2025 and January 2026. This recall follows observations by the FDA related to an outbreak investigation involving Salmonella contamination in other products containing moringa powder.

Production and distribution of the Moringa Capsules have been suspended while the FDA and Why Not Natural continue their investigation. The company has notified its customers about the recall. No specific injuries or adverse events linked to this product have been confirmed at this time.

ProductLot NumberExpiration Date
Why Not Natural Organic Moringa – Green Superfood (120 capsule bottle)A25G05107/2028

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Why Not Natural Organic Moringa – Green Superfood (lot number A25G051) should discontinue use immediately and return the product to Why Not Natural for a full refund. For questions or assistance, contact Why Not Natural customer care at care@whynotnatural.com.

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on January 28, 2026.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.

title: Koikoi Trading Inc. Recalls FU ZHOU Fish Balls for Undeclared Wheat and Sesame Allergens
headline: Koikoi Trading Inc. Announces Recall of FU ZHOU Fish Balls Due to Undeclared Wheat and Sesame Allergens
description: Koikoi Trading Inc. recalls FU ZHOU Fish Balls due to undeclared wheat and sesame allergens posing allergy risks. Distributed across multiple US states.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Koikoi Trading Inc. has issued a recall for certain lots of FU ZHOU FISH BALL products due to undeclared allergens, specifically wheat and sesame. These allergens are not listed on the product packaging, which poses a serious risk for individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to wheat and sesame. Consumption of these products could lead to serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The affected products were distributed through foodservice and e-commerce channels, potentially reaching consumers via restaurant deliveries, distributors, and online orders. Distribution areas include California, Washington, Oregon, New York, Florida, Texas, New Jersey, Illinois, Massachusetts, Georgia, Arizona, Nevada, and Colorado.

To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with these products. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that packaging and labeling failed to declare wheat and sesame in the ingredient and allergen statements. This action is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

Pack Size UPC Item # Lot Code Best By / Expiration Date Recall Scope
200g (Consumer pack) 81009389866 27010 08/12/2025 08/11/2027 All units within 24-month shelf life
400g (Consumer pack) 81009389131 27550 09/02/2025 09/01/2027 All units within 24-month shelf life
5LB (Foodservice pack, 4/5LB) 81009389132 27530 11/11/2025 11/12/2027 All units within 24-month shelf life

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check FU ZHOU Fish Balls packaging for undeclared wheat and sesame allergens and the recalled lot details. If affected, discontinue use and contact Koikoi Trading Inc. at 323-588-9888 or info@koikoitrading.com for return, credit, or replacement instructions.

title: Gerber Voluntary Recall of Limited Arrowroot Biscuits Batches for Potential Foreign Material Contamination
headline: Gerber Voluntarily Recalls Select Arrowroot Biscuits Batches Due to Potential Presence of Soft Plastic and Paper Pieces
description: Gerber recalls limited batches of Arrowroot Biscuits due to potential contamination with soft plastic or paper pieces. No injuries reported. Check affected batch codes.
keywords: potential presence of soft plastic and/or paper pieces
reason: potential presence of soft plastic and/or paper pieces

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gerber Products Company has voluntarily recalled limited batches of Gerber® Arrowroot Biscuits due to the potential presence of soft plastic and/or paper pieces. This precautionary measure follows a recall initiated by the arrowroot flour supplier. The company has ceased working with this supplier to ensure product safety.

The recall affects only Gerber® Arrowroot Biscuits in 5.5oz packaging produced between July 2025 and September 2025. This action applies nationwide within the United States, and no other Gerber® products are impacted. Consumers are advised to check the batch codes located on the back of the packaging before the best before date to identify affected products.

Although no illnesses or injuries have been reported, the recall is undertaken out of precaution. Gerber is cooperating fully with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and prioritizes the safety and quality of its products.

title: Navitas Organics Voluntarily Recalls Select Lots of 8oz Organic Chia Seeds Because of Possible Health Risk
headline: Navitas Organics Voluntarily Recalls Select Lots of 8oz Organic Chia Seeds Because of Possible Health Risk
description: Navitas Organice announced a recall involving Organic Chia Seeds after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - salmonella. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Navitas Organics is voluntarily recalling select lots of its 8oz Organic Chia Seeds due to potential Salmonella contamination. This precautionary recall follows a similar action by the company's chia seed supplier. The affected product was distributed nationwide through various retail stores including Whole Foods Market and Target, as well as online platforms such as Amazon, iHerb, and Vitacost.com.

The recall applies exclusively to the sealed stand-up pouches of the 8oz Organic Chia Seeds with specific lot codes and expiration dates as listed below. There have been no reports of Salmonella infections, illnesses, or adverse medical events linked to these products at this time. No other Navitas Organics products are impacted by this recall.

Salmonella infection can pose serious health risks, especially to young children, elderly individuals, and people with weakened immune systems. Symptoms often include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can lead to more severe complications if it spreads to the bloodstream.

Consumers who possess any of the affected chia seeds should not consume them. Instead, they are advised to discard the product and its packaging securely and return the product to the place of purchase for a refund. For further questions or to request a replacement, customers can contact Navitas Organics at 855-215-5702, Monday through Friday, from 8am to 8pm ET.

Product UPC Lot Code Best If Used By
Navitas Organics Organic Chia Seeds, 8oz 858847000284 W31025283 End APR 2027
Navitas Organics Organic Chia Seeds, 8oz 858847000284 W31025286 End APR 2027

Navitas Organics Organic Chia Seeds, 8oz 858847000284 W31025316 End MAY 2027
Navitas Organics Organic Chia Seeds, 8oz 858847000284 W31025317 End MAY 2027
  • Do not consume any 8oz Organic Chia Seeds with the above lot codes.
  • Open the bag, discard the chia seeds, and dispose of the packaging securely.
  • Return the product to the place of purchase for a refund.
  • Contact Navitas Organics with questions or for replacements at 855-215-5702, Monday–Friday, 8am–8pm ET.

Navitas Organics is cooperating with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) on this recall. Consumers are advised to take appropriate action to avoid potential health risks.

Consumers who possess any of the affected chia seeds should not consume them. Instead, they are advised to discard the product and its packaging securely and return the product to the place of purchase for a refund. For further questions or to request a replacement, customers can contact Navitas Organics at 855-215-5702, Monday through Friday, from 8am to 8pm ET.

title: IKM Recalls Metal Cookware Due to Lead Contamination Risk
headline: IKM Issues Recall on Metal Cookware Items Over Elevated Lead Levels
description: IKM recalls metal cookware items due to elevated lead contamination, posing health risks; products primarily distributed in California. No reported illnesses.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
IKM of San Jose, California, has announced a recall of several metal cookware items due to potential contamination with elevated levels of lead (Pb). Lead is a toxic metal that can leach into food from these products, posing a health risk to consumers of all ages. There is no known safe level of lead exposure, and even small amounts can cause serious health issues, particularly in children and fetuses.

The affected products include specific aluminum and brass cookware items. Lead exposure can lead to various health problems such as learning difficulties, reduced IQ, behavioral changes in children, and symptoms like fatigue, headaches, stomach pain, vomiting, and neurological changes in severe cases. The recall follows a routine FDA sampling program that detected unsafe lead levels in these finished products.

The contaminated items were distributed primarily to grocery stores in various cities across California, including Sunnyvale, Santa Clara, Fremont, Hayward, and others. IKM has stopped distribution of the affected cookware while investigating the source of contamination. No illnesses related to this issue have been reported to date.

Product Description Quantity Recalled
A-cook Aluminum Kadai size 5 Silver-toned wok-style vessel with a wide, curved basin and no long handle 43 pcs
Brass Tope Standard stock pot with a bright, golden finish 10 pcs
IKM 4-quart Pital Brass Pot Deep-bodied brass pot with a narrow mouth and a dull or matte golden appearance 9 pcs
IKM Aluminum Saucepan wooden handle 9” Silver aluminum body with a 9-inch diameter and wooden handle 56 pcs

This recall is conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their cookware matches the recalled products and return any affected items to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact IKM at 1-650-695-9009, Monday through Friday from 10:00 to 17:00 PST, excluding national holidays.

title: Superfoods Inc. dba Live it Up Recalls Live it Up Super Greens Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination
headline: Live it Up Recalls Super Greens Original and Wild Berry Pouches Over Potential Salmonella Health Risk
description: Live it Up recalls Super Greens Original and Wild Berry flavored pouches and stick packs due to Salmonella contamination risk, linked to illness and hospitalizations.
keywords: Superfoods, Inc. DBA as Live it Up
Super Greens Original and Wild Berry Flavored pouches and packs
productDescription: Super Greens Original and Wild Berry Flavored pouches and packs
companyName: Superfoods, Inc. DBA as Live it Up

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Superfoods, Inc., doing business as Live it Up, has issued a recall for certain batches of its Live it Up Super Greens dietary supplements due to potential contamination with Salmonella. The recall affects both Original and Wild Berry flavored products in pouch and stick pack forms. The company identified a possible health risk stemming from Salmonella, which can cause serious infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems.

Symptoms of Salmonella infection typically include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream causing more severe conditions such as arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis. As of this announcement, 45 illnesses and 12 hospitalizations in the United States have been reported in connection with this outbreak.

The recalled products were distributed nationwide in the U.S., including Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Virgin Islands, and have also been sold in the United Kingdom. Sales were through Live it Up’s direct-to-consumer website beginning September 2024 and Amazon.com starting July 30, 2025. Unauthorized resale through third-party platforms such as eBay and Walmart is also possible.

Product Net Weight UPC
Live it Up Super Greens Original (pouch) 8.5 oz (240g) 860013190804
Live it Up Super Greens Original, 30 - 0.28 oz (8g) stick packs 8.47 oz (240g) 850077468063
Live it Up Super Greens Wild Berry (pouch) 9.49 oz (269.16g) 860013190811
Live it Up Super Greens Wild Berry, 30 - 0.32 oz (9g) stick packs 9.52 oz (270g) 850077468070

The recalled products have green packaging with lot codes printed in black on the bottom back of the package. Original pouch lots begin with "A," and stick pack lots begin with "3." Expiration dates range from August 2026 to January 2028.

Production of the affected product has been suspended pending ongoing FDA and company investigation. No other Live it Up products are included in this recall.

facts.company: Superfoods, Inc. DBA as Live it Up
facts.product: Super Greens Original and Wild Berry Flavored pouches and packs

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using Live it Up Super Greens Original and Wild Berry flavored pouches and stick packs with lot codes starting with “A” or “3” and expiration dates from 08/2026 to 01/2028, dispose of them safely, and email recall@artofenso.com with their name, order number, and a photo of the lot code to request a refund.

title: Tri-Union Seafoods Expands Recall of Genova® Yellowfin Tuna Due to Potential Botulinum Contamination
headline: Tri-Union Seafoods Expands Recall of Genova® Yellowfin Tuna Products After Distribution of Previously Recalled Lots
description: Tri-Union Seafoods expands recall of Genova® Yellowfin Tuna due to potential Clostridium botulinum contamination linked to defective can lids.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tri-Union Seafoods has announced a recall of additional quantities of Genova® Yellowfin Tuna products due to the inadvertent distribution of previously recalled items. The initial recall, issued in February 2025, was prompted by a manufacturing defect in the “easy open” pull tab can lids that may compromise the product seal, increasing the risk of contamination with Clostridium botulinum, a potentially fatal cause of food poisoning.

Recently, Tri-Union Seafoods discovered that quarantined cases related to the initial recall were mistakenly shipped by a third-party distributor to limited retail stores in several states. Affected products were sold at Meijer locations in Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio, and Wisconsin; Giant Foods in Maryland and Virginia; and Safeway, Albertsons, Vons, and Pavilions stores in California.

Consumers are advised not to consume the recalled tuna products even if they appear normal, as the contamination risk remains regardless of appearance or smell. Anyone experiencing illness after consumption should seek immediate medical attention.

Description UPC Can Code Best if Used By Date
Genova Yellowfin Tuna in Olive Oil 5.0 oz 4 Pack 4800073265 S84N D2L 1/21/2028
Genova Yellowfin Tuna in Olive Oil 5.0 oz 4 Pack 4800073265 S84N D3L 1/24/2028
Genova Yellowfin Tuna in Extra Virgin Olive Oil with Sea Salt 5.0 oz 4800013275 S88N D1M 1/17/2028

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Genova Yellowfin Tuna products against the lot numbers and codes listed. If their product is affected, they should return it to the place of purchase for a full refund, dispose of it, or contact Tri-Union Seafoods at 833-374-0171 or support@thaiunionhelp.zendesk.com to obtain a retrieval kit and replacement coupon.

title: Olympus Expands Voluntary Recall of ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA Needles Due to Safety Defects
headline: Olympus Expands Voluntary Recall of All ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA Needles Over Component Detachment Risks
description: Olympus has expanded a voluntary recall of all ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA needles due to risks from component detachment and device defects.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Olympus Corporation has expanded its voluntary recall of the ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS -TBNA needles due to safety concerns. This recall now includes all lots of the device, following complaints about components ejecting or detaching during medical procedures. The initial recall in August 2025 addressed only certain lots, but subsequent investigations revealed issues affecting the entire product line.

The detachment of device components poses significant risks, including the possibility of unintended foreign objects in the tracheobronchial tree, which may require bronchoscopic extraction or surgical removal. Additionally, degradation of the device’s heat-shrink material can lead to complications such as difficulty in sample extraction, fluid leakage, impaired needle operation, or breakage.

The ViziShot 2 FLEX is used with ultrasound endoscopes for fine needle aspiration and biopsy of submucosal and extramural lesions of the tracheobronchial tree. Other EBUS-TBNA needles are not impacted by this recall.

Adverse events or quality issues related to this device can be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch program.

Product
ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS -TBNA needles (all lots)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Healthcare professionals should immediately stop using and quarantine all ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA needles. Affected devices must be returned to Olympus following recall instructions. Report adverse events or quality problems to FDA MedWatch online. For support, contact Olympus Technical Assistance Center at 1-800-848-9024 (Option 1) or email complaints@olympus.com.

content.text: Consumer support: Olympus Technical Assistance Center (TAC) | 1-800-848-9024 | complaints@olympus.com

title: Spring & Mulberry Expands Voluntary Recall of Select Chocolate Bars Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination
headline: Spring & Mulberry Expands Voluntary Recall of Multiple Chocolate Bar Flavors Over Salmonella Risk
description: Spring & Mulberry expands voluntary recall of select chocolate bars due to potential Salmonella contamination confirmed by routine testing.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Spring & Mulberry has announced an expansion of their voluntary recall of select chocolate bar flavors due to potential contamination with Salmonella. The recall, initially affecting only the Mint Leaf flavor, now includes additional flavors produced on the same equipment and during the same time period. The company first identified the risk after routine third-party testing revealed the presence of Salmonella in finished products.

Salmonella infection can cause serious health issues, especially for young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms typically include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection may spread to the bloodstream, leading to more severe conditions such as arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis. Individuals experiencing symptoms should seek medical attention promptly.

The affected products were available for purchase online and through select retail partners nationwide starting September 15, 2025. Consumers can identify recalled items by the brand name Spring & Mulberry, flavor name, specific lot codes, and box color. Lot codes are found on the back of the packaging and on the inner flow wrap.

To date, no confirmed illnesses or adverse health effects have been reported related to these products.

Flavor Lot Code Box Color
Mint Leaf ML1025 Green
Dark Chocolate Sea Salt DCSS1025 Blue
Hazelnut Crunch HC1025 Brown
Orange Zest OZ1025 Orange

en.title: Spring & Mulberry Expands Voluntary Recall of Select Chocolate Bars Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination
en.description: Spring & Mulberry expands voluntary recall of select chocolate bars due to potential Salmonella contamination confirmed by routine testing.
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness, Food & Beverages, Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Spring & Mulberry has announced an expansion of their voluntary recall of select chocolate bar flavors due to potential contamination with Salmonella. The recall, initially affecting only the Mint Leaf flavor, now includes additional flavors produced on the same equipment and during the same time period. The company first identified the risk after routine third-party testing revealed the presence of Salmonella in finished products.

Salmonella infection can cause serious health issues, especially for young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms typically include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection may spread to the bloodstream, leading to more severe conditions such as arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis. Individuals experiencing symptoms should seek medical attention promptly.

The affected products were available for purchase online and through select retail partners nationwide starting September 15, 2025. Consumers can identify recalled items by the brand name Spring & Mulberry, flavor name, specific lot codes, and box color. Lot codes are found on the back of the packaging and on the inner flow wrap.

To date, no confirmed illnesses or adverse health effects have been reported related to these products.

Mint Leaf ML1025 Green
Dark Chocolate Sea Salt DCSS1025 Blue
Hazelnut Crunch HC1025 Brown
Orange Zest OZ1025 Orange

title: Abbott Medical Device Correction for FreeStyle Libre 3 and 3 Plus Glucose Monitoring Sensors
headline: Abbott Issues Medical Device Correction for Certain FreeStyle Libre 3 and Libre 3 Plus Glucose Sensors Due to Potential Inaccurate Low Glucose Readings
description: Abbott recalls specific FreeStyle Libre 3 and 3 Plus sensors in the U.S. after internal testing found some sensors may give incorrect low glucose readings.
keywords: FreeStyle Libre 3 and 3 Plus Glucose monitoring sensors
Internal testing determined that some sensors may provide incorrect low glucose readings.
productDescription: FreeStyle Libre 3 and 3 Plus Glucose monitoring sensors
reason: Internal testing determined that some sensors may provide incorrect low glucose readings.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Abbott has initiated a medical device correction in the United States for certain FreeStyle Libre 3 and FreeStyle Libre 3 Plus glucose monitoring sensors. This action follows internal testing that identified an issue causing some sensors to provide incorrect low glucose readings. Incorrect readings may lead to improper treatment decisions, potentially resulting in serious health risks for people with diabetes.

The issue originates from one specific production line among several manufacturing Libre 3 and Libre 3 Plus sensors. Abbott has resolved the manufacturing problem and continues to produce these sensors to meet replacement and new order demands without expecting significant supply disruptions. This correction affects approximately 3 million sensors in the U.S., with about half already expired or used.

The correction does not affect other FreeStyle Libre sensors, readers, or apps, nor other Abbott biowearable products.

Impacted Product Details
FreeStyle Libre 3 Sensors Subset from one production line; approximately 3 million devices in the U.S.
FreeStyle Libre 3 Plus Sensors Subset from one production line; included in the approximately 3 million devices in the U.S.

facts.product: FreeStyle Libre 3 and 3 Plus Glucose monitoring sensors

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should visit www.FreeStyleCheck.com to verify if their sensor is included in the affected lots. If affected, discontinue use immediately, safely dispose of the sensor, and request a free replacement from Abbott. For questions or support, contact Abbott customer service at 1-833-815-4273 or use the 24/7 live chat at Abbott Support.

title: Ambriola Company Recalls Cheese Products Due to Listeria monocytogenes Contamination Risk
headline: Ambriola Company Recalls Multiple Cheese Brands Over Listeria monocytogenes Contamination Concern
description: The Ambriola Company recalls select cheese products including Ambriola, Locatelli, Member’s Mark, Pinna, and Boar’s Head due to potential Listeria contamination.
keywords: Ambriola, Locatelli, Member’s Mark, Pinna, and Boar’s Head
Potential to be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes
brandName: Ambriola, Locatelli, Member’s Mark, Pinna, and Boar’s Head
brandNames: Ambriola, Locatelli, Member’s Mark, Pinna, and Boar’s Head
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The Ambriola Company has issued a recall of select cheese products due to the detection of Listeria monocytogenes during routine testing. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. While healthy individuals may experience short-term symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea, pregnant women face risks of miscarriage and stillbirth from Listeria infection.

The recalled cheese products were distributed nationwide to retail stores and distributors between November 3, 2025, and November 20, 2025. Additional cheese products processed at the same West Caldwell, New Jersey facility are also included in the recall out of an abundance of caution. No illnesses have been reported to date.

The Ambriola Company is cooperating with the FDA and has suspended production and distribution of the affected products while undertaking a thorough review of sanitation and food safety protocols.

Brand Product Description Lot Numbers Expiration Dates UPC Distribution Dates
Ambriola Cheese (Retail Exact Weight) Varies by package Varies Variable Random Weights Nov 3, 2025 – Nov 20, 2025
Locatelli Cheese (Retail Exact Weight) Varies by package Varies Variable Random Weights Nov 3, 2025 – Nov 20, 2025
Member’s Mark Cheese (Retail Exact Weight) Varies by package Varies Variable Random Weights Nov 3, 2025 – Nov 20, 2025
Pinna Cheese (Retail Exact Weight) Varies by package Varies Variable Random Weights Nov 3, 2025 – Nov 20, 2025
Boar’s Head Cheese (Retail Exact Weight) Varies by package Varies Variable Random Weights Nov 3, 2025 – Nov 20, 2025
Various Bulk Cheese Products Varies Varies N/A Nov 3, 2025 – Nov 20, 2025

facts.brand: Ambriola, Locatelli, Member’s Mark, Pinna, and Boar’s Head

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their products for affected brands and lot numbers and promptly return recalled cheese products to the place of purchase for a full refund or properly dispose of them. If symptoms of listeriosis develop, contact a healthcare provider immediately. For more information or assistance, call Ambriola at 1-800-962-8224, Monday through Friday, 9:00 am to 4:00 pm ET.

title: Diva Fam Inc. Recalls True Sea Moss Gel Superfood 16 FL OZ Jars Due to Potential Botulism Risk
headline: Diva Fam Inc. Voluntarily Recalls True Sea Moss Gel Superfood Products Over Potential Botulism Hazard
description: Diva Fam Inc. recalls True Sea Moss Gel Superfood jars (16 FL OZ) due to possible botulism risk from inadequate regulatory controls and temperature monitoring.
keywords: Sea Moss Gel Superfood in various flavors (16 FL OZ glass jars)
productDescription: Sea Moss Gel Superfood in various flavors (16 FL OZ glass jars)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Diva Fam Inc. has announced a voluntary recall of all lots and flavors of its True Sea Moss Sea Moss Gel Superfood products packaged in 16 FL OZ (473 mL) glass jars. This action is due to the company's lack of required regulatory authorization and temperature monitoring records for pH-controlled food products, which may increase the risk of microbial growth. Such growth could include organisms producing toxins associated with botulism, a rare but serious illness affecting the nervous system.

Symptoms of botulism can include general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, and in severe cases, muscle weakness or difficulty breathing. To date, no illnesses or adverse effects have been reported related to the recalled products. The affected products were distributed nationwide through select retailers, online at truеsеamоss.cоm, and other channels.

The recall includes all flavors and batch numbers of True Sea Moss brand Sea Moss Gel Superfood manufactured before January 9, 2026. The manufacture date is indicated on the can lid in MM/YYYY format.

The recall follows a California Department of Public Health inspection questioning the regulatory status and production records of the products. Diva Fam is cooperating with regulatory authorities and conducting this recall with knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. For additional information, consumers can contact Diva Fam customer support.

title: Karison Foods Recalls Panjiri, Pinni, and Laddoo Products Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Karison Foods & Snacks Inc Recalls Panjiri, Alsi Pinni, Punjabi Pinni, Besan Laddoo, and No Sugar Added Besan Laddoo for Undeclared Milk Allergen
description: Karison Foods recalls Panjiri, pinni, and laddoo products in NY, NJ, and VA due to undeclared milk allergen; no illnesses reported. Check UPC codes and expiration dates.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Karison Foods & Snacks Inc, based in Port Washington, NY, has announced a recall of several of its products due to the presence of an undeclared milk allergen. The recall affects 12 oz containers of the following items: PANJIRI, ALSI PINNI, PUNJABI PINNI, BESAN LADDOO, and NO SUGAR ADDED BESAN LADDOO. These products were distributed through retail stores in New York, New Jersey, and Virginia.

The products come in clear plastic containers and include specific UPC codes and an expiration date of August 29, 2026. The issue was identified during a routine inspection, which revealed that the milk allergen was not declared on the product labels. No illnesses related to these products have been reported so far.

Consumers with milk allergies or severe milk sensitivities are at risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume these products.

title: Spring & Mulberry Voluntary Recall of Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar for Possible Salmonella Contamination
headline: Spring & Mulberry Recalls Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination
description: Spring & Mulberry recalls Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar lot #025255 for potential Salmonella contamination; no illnesses reported to date.
keywords: Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar
productDescription: Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Spring & Mulberry has announced a voluntary recall of lot #025255 of its Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar (2.1 oz) due to potential contamination with Salmonella. This recall follows routine testing by a third-party laboratory that identified the possible presence of this harmful bacterium. Salmonella infections can be serious, particularly for young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream and cause severe complications.

The affected Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar was sold online and through select retail partners nationwide starting September 15, 2025. The recalled product can be identified by the Spring & Mulberry brand, teal-colored box, Mint Leaf flavor, and the lot code #025255, which appears on both the back of the packaging and inner flow wrap. To date, no illnesses or adverse health effects have been reported in connection with this product.

Product Lot Number Flavor Weight Box Color
Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar 025255 Mint Leaf 2.1 oz Teal

facts.product: Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased the Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar lot #025255 should dispose of the product and request a refund by emailing recalls@springandmulberry.com with a photo of the lot code. For further assistance, contact Spring & Mulberry Customer Service at recalls@springandmulberry.com.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Spring & Mulberry Customer Service | recalls@springandmulberry.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on January 12, 2026.

title: VH Foods Inc. DBA Outside The Breadbox Recalls Bread Crumbs for Undeclared Egg and Milk Allergens
headline: VH Foods Inc. DBA Outside The Breadbox Issues Recall of Bread Crumbs Due to Undeclared Egg and Milk Allergens
description: VH Foods Inc. recalls 8 oz Bread Crumbs for undeclared egg and milk allergens, sold in Colorado and via mail order from Nov 24 to Dec 1, 2025.
keywords: VH Foods Inc. DBA Outside The Breadbox
Potential or Undeclared Allergen – Egg and Milk
companyName: VH Foods Inc. DBA Outside The Breadbox
reason: Potential or Undeclared Allergen – Egg and Milk

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
VH Foods Inc., operating as Outside The Breadbox in Colorado Springs, CO, has issued a recall of its 8 ounce packages of "Bread Crumbs" due to the potential presence of undeclared egg and milk allergens. This recall affects products distributed to retail stores in Colorado as well as those sold through mail orders via the company website between November 24, 2025, and December 1, 2025.

The affected bread crumbs are packaged in clear stand-up pouches, each weighing 8 ounces. The packages bear the UPC code 873899001554 and an expiration date of May 20, 2026, stamped on the bottom. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that the packaging failed to disclose the inclusion of egg and milk ingredients due to a temporary lapse in the company's production and packaging procedures. This issue appears to be limited to a single production run.

No illnesses related to the undeclared allergens have been reported to date.

Product Package Size UPC Expiration Date Distribution Period
Bread Crumbs 8 oz 873899001554 05/20/2026 11/24/2025 – 12/01/2025

facts.company: VH Foods Inc. DBA Outside The Breadbox

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Outside the Breadbox 8 oz Bread Crumbs with UPC 873899001554 and expiration date 05/20/2026, distributed from November 24 to December 1, 2025, should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund or contact VH Foods Inc. at 1-719-633-3434 for assistance.

title: HerbsForever LLC Recalls Gastro Care and Hingwastika Dietary Supplements for Undeclared Wheat Allergen
headline: HerbsForever LLC Recalls Gastro Care and Hingwastika Supplements Due to Undeclared Wheat Allergy Risk
description: HerbsForever LLC recalls Gastro Care and Hingwastika dietary supplements nationwide due to undeclared wheat posing allergy risks to consumers.
keywords: Gastro Care capsule and Hingwastika powder and capsule dietary supplements
productDescription: Gastro Care capsule and Hingwastika powder and capsule dietary supplements

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
HerbsForever LLC of Los Angeles, California, has issued a recall on certain dietary supplements due to the potential presence of undeclared wheat. The affected products include HerbsForever brand Hingwastik Churna Powder, Hingwastika Extract capsules, and Gastro Care capsules. These items were distributed nationwide through mail order.

Individuals with wheat allergies are at risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume these products. The recall was initiated after a routine FDA inspection at the manufacturing facility in India revealed that an ingredient called Hing (Ferula Asafoedita) is dried with flour that may contain wheat.

To date, no illnesses related to this issue have been reported. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Product Name Package Description UPC Batch Number Expiration Date
Hingwastik Churna Powder 100 gm amber PET Bottle 807814006224 622-2 June-2029
Hingwastika Extract 60 Veg capsules, 800 mg each 807814001335 133-14 April-2029
Gastro Care 90 Veg capsules, 800 mg each, white plastic bottle 807814001243 124-4 January-2029

facts.product: Gastro Care capsule and Hingwastika powder and capsule dietary supplements

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased HerbsForever Gastro Care capsules or Hingwastika powder and capsules should check packaging for the listed batch numbers and expiration dates to identify recalled products and contact HerbsForever LLC at contact@herbsforever.com for return instructions and a full refund.

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on January 09, 2026.

title: Nationwide Recall of Silintan Capsules by Anthony Trinh, 123Herbals LLC Due to Undeclared Meloxicam
headline: Anthony Trinh, 123Herbals LLC Recalls Silintan Capsules Containing Undeclared Meloxicam, an Unapproved Drug
description: 123Herbals LLC issues nationwide recall of Silintan capsules marketed for joint relief due to undeclared meloxicam, a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug not allowed in supplements.
keywords: Dietary supplement capsules marketed for relief of joint and body aches
Unapproved new drug found to contain undeclared meloxicam
productDescription: Dietary supplement capsules marketed for relief of joint and body aches
reason: Unapproved new drug found to contain undeclared meloxicam

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Anthony Trinh, 123Herbals LLC is issuing a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots of Silintan capsules, a dietary supplement marketed for relief of joint and body aches. The recall follows FDA analysis that found the product contains undeclared meloxicam, an approved nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) not permitted in dietary supplements.

Meloxicam is approved for osteoarthritis pain management but products containing it cannot be marketed as dietary supplements. The presence of meloxicam in Silintan capsules makes the product an unapproved new drug with unestablished safety and efficacy, leading to the recall.

Exposure to meloxicam above the recommended maximum daily dose of 15 mg may cause serious health risks including blood clots, heart attack, stroke, hypersensitivity reactions, gastrointestinal bleeding, ulceration, perforation, and acute kidney injury, especially in elderly patients. Dangerous drug interactions can occur in patients taking blood thinners or other NSAIDs, increasing bleeding risk.

No adverse events have been reported in relation to this recall.

Product Description Packaging Distribution
Silintan capsules Dietary supplement capsules marketed for relief of joint and body aches 25-count plastic bottles with blue labels Nationwide online distribution at 123herbals.com

For questions about this recall, consumers can contact Anthony Trinh, 123Herbals LLC by email at hello@123herbals.com or by phone at 1-626-656-3202 (Monday through Friday, 8am to 5pm Pacific Time). Customers who purchased directly from 123Herbals.com may return product to:

Return Address
300 West Valley Blvd., P.O. Box 1925, Alhambra, CA 91803

facts.product: Dietary supplement capsules marketed for relief of joint and body aches

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have Silintan capsules should stop use immediately and return the product to the place of purchase for a refund, discard it, or consult a healthcare provider. For direct purchases, return product to 300 West Valley Blvd., P.O. Box 1925, Alhambra, CA 91803, or contact Anthony Trinh, 123Herbals LLC by phone at 1-626-656-3202 or email hello@123herbals.com.

content.text: Consumer support: (626) 656-3202 | hello@123herbals.com

title: Modern Warrior Recalls 'Modern Warrior Ready' Supplement Due to Undeclared 1,4-DMAA, Aniracetam, and Tianeptine
headline: Modern Warrior Recalls 'Modern Warrior Ready' Dietary Supplement Containing Undeclared 1,4-DMAA, Aniracetam, and Unapproved Tianeptine
description: Modern Warrior recalls dietary supplement 'Modern Warrior Ready' for undeclared substances 1,4-DMAA, aniracetam, and unapproved tianeptine posing health risks.
keywords: Dietary supplement capsules marketed for metabolism moosting, improving brain function, and reducing cravings
Unapproved new drug found to contain undeclared 1,4-DMAA and aniracetam, and tianeptine
productDescription: Dietary supplement capsules marketed for metabolism moosting, improving brain function, and reducing cravings
reason: Unapproved new drug found to contain undeclared 1,4-DMAA and aniracetam, and tianeptine

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Modern Warrior has issued a voluntary recall for all lots of their dietary supplement "Modern Warrior Ready" following the detection of undeclared ingredients during regulatory testing. The supplement, marketed to boost metabolism, improve brain function, and reduce cravings, was found to contain unapproved substances including tianeptine, 1,4-DMAA, and aniracetam.

Tianeptine is associated with serious health risks, such as life-threatening events including suicidal ideation or behavior in individuals aged 25 years and younger. Overdose symptoms can include confusion, seizures, drowsiness, dry mouth, and shortness of breath, which may worsen with alcohol consumption. Additionally, tianeptine combined with monoamine oxidase inhibitor (MAOI) antidepressants may lead to severe complications including stroke and death.

Use of 1,4-DMAA may cause elevated blood pressure and cardiovascular problems such as heart attack, shortness of breath, and chest tightness. The product also contains synephrine, which can increase heart rate and blood pressure, especially when combined with caffeine, potentially causing adverse cardiovascular effects.

The recalled product was sold nationwide online from April 2022 through December 8, 2025. Modern Warrior has ceased distribution, removed the product from sale, and quarantined remaining stock to prevent further exposure.

Product Name Description Packaging Distribution Dates
Modern Warrior Ready Dietary supplement capsules for metabolism boosting, brain function improvement, and cravings reduction Black plastic bottle with black screw-top lid, black and gold tamper-evident shrink wrap, 60 capsules per bottle April 2022 – December 8, 2025

Consumers with questions or concerns about the recall should consult their healthcare providers.

facts.product: Dietary supplement capsules marketed for metabolism moosting, improving brain function, and reducing cravings

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately discontinue use of Modern Warrior Ready supplements, check their product packaging to confirm if it matches the recalled lots, and contact Modern Warrior via email at theviking@modernwarrior.life for support or further information.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (314)) 713-1984 | theviking@modernwarrior.life

title: Gusto Group Inc. Recalls Klong Kone Shrimp Paste (454 G and 910 G) Due to Elevated Lead Levels
headline: Gusto Group Inc. Recalls Klong Kone Shrimp Paste 454 G (1 LBS) and 910 G (2 LBS) Due to Potential Lead Contamination
description: Gusto Group Inc. recalls Klong Kone Shrimp Paste in 454 G and 910 G sizes after detecting elevated lead levels posing health risks. Distributed in IA, NJ.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gusto Group Inc. of Paterson, NJ, has issued a recall for two sizes of Klong Kone Shrimp Paste due to the potential presence of elevated levels of lead. The affected products include the ‘454 G. (1 LBS)’ and ‘910 G. (2 LBS)’ variants. Lead contamination poses health risks, particularly for children, who may suffer long-term neurological damage from prolonged exposure. Adults are also at risk of kidney problems, hypertension, and cognitive impairments from chronic lead exposure.

The recalled products were distributed between April 2023 and December 2023 to two retailers located in Iowa and New Jersey. No online sales were reported. The shrimp paste is packaged in a white plastic jar with a red cap and originates from Thailand, packed and distributed by P. Prateepthong 2000.

There has been one report involving four children who became ill after consuming the product; they experienced diarrhea and elevated blood lead levels. This incident prompted the recall and subsequent FDA lab testing, which confirmed the presence of unsafe lead levels. Gusto Group Inc. has ceased distribution of these items and is investigating the source of contamination.

title: Primavera Nueva Inc. Voluntarily Recalls Select 4-Count Tamales Due to Potential Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination
headline: Primavera Nueva Inc. Announces Voluntary Recall of Select 4-Count Tamales for Potential Listeria Contamination, Including Roasted Pumpkin & White Cheddar Seasonal Item
description: Primavera Nueva Inc. issues voluntary recall of select 4-count tamales distributed in California and Nevada due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Primavera Nueva Inc., based in Sonoma, CA, has issued a voluntary recall of select 4-count tamales due to the potential presence of Listeria monocytogenes, a harmful bacterial organism. This bacteria can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria risk miscarriages and stillbirths.

The recalled tamales were distributed to retail stores in California and Nevada. This action follows an FDA inspection on October 10, 2025, which found that internal cooking temperatures were not consistently verified during earlier production, raising concerns about controlling potential biological hazards like Listeria monocytogenes. Primavera Nueva Inc. has since implemented enhanced cooking temperature monitoring and verification starting October 11, 2025.

To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

Product Production Date Range
Roasted Green Chile & Jack Cheese 4-Count Tamales October 10, 2024 – October 10, 2025
Black Bean Bonanza & Jack Cheese 4-Count Tamales October 10, 2024 – October 10, 2025
Butternut Squash with Roasted Green Chiles & Cheese 4-Count Tamales October 10, 2024 – October 10, 2025
BBQ Chipotle Bean & White Cheddar 4-Count Tamales October 10, 2024 – October 10, 2025
Mushroom Spinach & Salsa with Two Cheeses 4-Count Tamales October 10, 2024 – October 10, 2025
Roasted Green Chile 4-Count Tamales October 10, 2024 – October 10, 2025
Black Bean Bonanza 4-Count Tamales October 10, 2024 – October 10, 2025
Butternut Squash with Roasted Green Chiles 4-Count Tamales October 10, 2024 – October 10, 2025
Mushroom Spinach & Salsa 4-Count Tamales October 10, 2024 – October 10, 2025
Roasted Pumpkin & White Cheddar 4-Count Tamales (Seasonal Item) October 10, 2024 – October 10, 2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should not consume any recalled tamales with date codes between October 22, 2024, and October 22, 2025. They should discard these products immediately and may contact Primavera Nueva Inc. at 707-939-9350 or primaveranueva@aol.com for further information.

title: Newway Import Inc. Recalls Preserved Mustard in Soybean Oil Due to Undeclared Wheat Allergen
headline: Recall Alert: Undeclared Wheat Found in Newway Import's Preserved Mustard in Soybean Oil
description: Newway Import recalls preserved mustard in soybean oil due to undeclared wheat, posing allergy risks. No illnesses reported. Available in California stores.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Newway Import Inc., located in La Puente, California, has issued a recall for the product "Preserved Mustard in Soybean Oil" under the Cai Hua Xiang brand due to the presence of undeclared wheat. This allergen is not listed on the product packaging, which poses a risk of serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with wheat allergies or severe sensitivities.

The recalled product was available for purchase in retail grocery stores throughout California from early July 2025 until early November 2025.

To date, no illnesses related to this recall have been reported. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that the product contained wheat but failed to declare it on the packaging, potentially endangering consumers with wheat allergies.

Product Name Brand Lot Code Best By Date Packaging Details
Preserved Mustard in Soybean Oil Cai Hua Xiang 505202008 May 20, 2026 Packaging details not specified; lot code and best by date found on package exterior

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should stop using the recalled product immediately and return it to the retailer for a full refund. For questions, contact Newway Import Inc. at (626) 766-3037, Monday through Friday, 10:00 a.m.–5:00 p.m. PST.

title: Gold Star Distribution Recalls All FDA-Regulated Products Due to Potential Salmonella and Contamination Risks
headline: Gold Star Distribution Issues Recall of All FDA-Regulated Drugs, Devices, Cosmetics, Food, and Pet Food from Contaminated Facility
description: Gold Star Distribution recalls all FDA-regulated products after rodent, avian contamination, and insanitary conditions risk Salmonella exposure and health hazards.
keywords: All FDA-regulated products held at facility including drugs, medical devices, cosmetics, dietary supplements, human food, and pet food
Animal & Veterinary Cosmetics Dietary Supplements Drugs Food & Beverages Medical Devices Foodborne Illness
Potential Salmonella contamination, presence of rodent and avian contamination and insanitary conditions during the storage process.
productDescription: All FDA-regulated products held at facility including drugs, medical devices, cosmetics, dietary supplements, human food, and pet food
productType: Animal & Veterinary Cosmetics Dietary Supplements Drugs Food & Beverages Medical Devices Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Cosmetics Dietary Supplements Drugs Food & Beverages Medical Devices Foodborne Illness
reason: Potential Salmonella contamination, presence of rodent and avian contamination and insanitary conditions during the storage process.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gold Star Distribution, Inc. has recalled all FDA-regulated products stored at its facility following the discovery of rodent and avian contamination and insanitary storage conditions that may expose products to bacterial contamination, including Salmonella. The recalled products include drugs, medical devices, cosmetics, dietary supplements, human food, and pet food.

Exposure to these products could cause serious illness or infection. Salmonella infection symptoms commonly include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Populations such as infants, elderly individuals, pregnant persons, and people with weakened immune systems are at increased risk of severe complications. The contamination may also affect animals consuming the recalled pet food products.

This recall applies to all relevant FDA-regulated products held at the Gold Star facility and excludes frozen or refrigerated items shipped directly to stores by distributors or manufacturers. As of now, no illnesses linked to these products have been reported.

facts.product: All FDA-regulated products held at facility including drugs, medical devices, cosmetics, dietary supplements, human food, and pet food
facts.productType: Animal & Veterinary Cosmetics Dietary Supplements Drugs Food & Beverages Medical Devices Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers and retailers should immediately stop using all FDA-regulated products from Gold Star Distribution, check for lot information, destroy the products, and provide verification of destruction to Gold Star Distribution at their Minneapolis address. Refunds are available upon request. For questions, contact Gold Star Distribution at 612-617-9800.

title: Atwater’s Recalls Cookie Tins Due to Undeclared Almond, Pecan, and Walnut Allergens
headline: Atwater’s Recalls Cookie Tins for Undeclared Tree Nut Allergens Including Almonds, Pecans, and Walnuts
description: Atwater’s recalls cookie tins distributed in Maryland for undeclared tree nut allergens posing allergy risks to consumers sensitive to almonds, pecans, and walnuts.
keywords: Undeclared tree nut allergens, almonds, pecans, and walnuts.
reason: Undeclared tree nut allergens, almonds, pecans, and walnuts.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Atwater’s, based in Baltimore, MD, has issued a recall for 197 cookie tins due to undeclared tree nut allergens. The affected products contain almonds, pecans, and walnuts that were not disclosed on the product labels, posing a serious risk to individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to these nuts.

The cookie tins were distributed between December 13, 2025, and December 22, 2025, in Baltimore, Towson, and Catonsville, MD. They were sold through retail stores and also gifted to three wholesalers. The tins are circular metal containers tied with a bow, marked with an “Atwater’s cookie tin” label on the bottom.

The recall was voluntarily initiated after the labeling issue was identified, which appeared to be caused by a temporary breakdown in Atwater’s labeling process. To date, no allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with an allergy or sensitivity to tree nuts should check for Atwater’s cookie tins purchased between December 13 and December 22, 2025, in Baltimore area locations, and return affected tins to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Atwater’s customer support at 410-644-3435.

content.text: Consumer support: 410-644-3435

The affected products contain almonds, pecans, and walnuts that were not disclosed on the product labels, posing a serious risk to individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to these nuts.

The cookie tins were distributed between December 13, 2025, and December 22, 2025, in Baltimore, Towson, and Catonsville, MD. They were sold through retail stores and also gifted to three wholesalers. The tins are circular metal containers tied with a bow, marked with an “Atwater’s cookie tin” label on the bottom.

The recall was voluntarily initiated after the labeling issue was identified, which appeared to be caused by a temporary breakdown in Atwater’s labeling process. To date, no allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with an allergy or sensitivity to tree nuts should check for Atwater’s cookie tins purchased between December 13 and December 22, 2025, in Baltimore area locations, and return affected tins to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Atwater’s customer support at 410-644-3435.

content.text: Consumer support: 410-644-3435

title: Gregory’s Foods Recalls Bag Full of Cookies White Chocolate Macadamia Nut Dough Due to Undeclared Peanuts
headline: Gregory’s Foods Recalls Bag Full of Cookies White Chocolate Macadamia Nut Frozen Cookie Dough Over Peanut Allergen Risk
description: Gregory’s Foods recalls Bag Full of Cookies White Chocolate Macadamia Nut Frozen Cookie Dough due to undeclared peanuts posing allergy risks in select regions.
keywords: Bag Full of Cookies White Chocolate Macadamia Nut Frozen Cookie Dough
productDescription: Bag Full of Cookies White Chocolate Macadamia Nut Frozen Cookie Dough

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gregory’s Foods, Inc., located in Eagan, MN, has issued a recall for certain 2-pound 8.5-ounce packages of its "Bag Full of Cookies" White Chocolate Macadamia Nut frozen cookie dough. The recall stems from the possibility that some packages labeled as White Chocolate Macadamia Nut may contain Frozen Monster Cookie Dough, which includes undeclared peanuts.

This mislabeling poses a serious risk for individuals with peanut allergies, as consuming these products could lead to life-threatening allergic reactions. The affected cookie dough was distributed in retail grocery stores and distribution centers across Minnesota, Wisconsin, and Nebraska.

The recalled product is packaged in a 2-pound 8.5-ounce plastic bag with a clear window and is marked with a "BEST BY 12/6/2026" date near the top front of the bag. Notably, the Frozen Monster Cookie Dough visible through the window contains chocolate chips and colored candy-coated pieces, while the White Chocolate Macadamia Nut dough does not.

To date, no illnesses have been reported related to this recall. The issue was isolated to certain units produced on one day, and the company has since corrected the problem.

Product Name Package Size Best By Date Distribution Area
Bag Full of Cookies White Chocolate Macadamia Nut Frozen Cookie Dough 2 lb 8.5 oz 12/6/2026 Minnesota, Wisconsin, Nebraska

title: Willy Pete’s Chocolate Company LLC Recalls Almond Despair Chocolate Bars Due to Undeclared Almond Allergen
headline: Willy Pete’s Chocolate Company LLC Recalls Almond Despair Chocolate Bars for Undeclared Almond Allergen in Ingredient Statement
description: Willy Pete’s Chocolate Company recalls Almond Despair chocolate bars due to undeclared almond allergen, posing risks for consumers with almond allergies.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Willy Pete’s Chocolate Company LLC of Harwinton has issued a recall for its Almond Despair chocolate bars due to the presence of almonds not declared on the ingredient label. The ingredient statement mistakenly lists macadamia nuts instead of almonds. This poses a risk for consumers with almond allergies or sensitivities, as ingestion could lead to serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recalled Almond Despair chocolate bars come in 2 oz purple wrappers, with UPC code 0 987261 5. These products were distributed to stores located in Connecticut, Massachusetts, and South Carolina.

To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

title: Handelnine Global Limited Recalls Virgo Rheumacare Capsules Nationwide for Elevated Lead Levels
headline: Handelnine Global Limited Initiates Nationwide Recall of Virgo Rheumacare Capsules Due to Unsafe Lead Contamination
description: Virgo Rheumacare Capsules recalled nationwide by Handelnine Global Limited for elevated lead levels posing health risks. Recall notice issued by FDA in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Handelnine Global Limited, operating as Navafresh, has announced a voluntary nationwide recall of Rheumacare Capsules manufactured by Virgo UAP Pharma Pvt. Ltd. (Virgo) due to elevated lead levels. Testing by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) revealed lead concentrations up to 11,100 ppm, which significantly exceed permissible limits, posing potential health risks.

The recalled product is intended for reducing joint pain and comes in white plastic containers labeled as Rheumacare Capsules. The affected lots are CAM040 and CALO79-N, distributed nationwide through the Navafresh website.

Exposure to high levels of lead can cause serious health problems including cognitive impairment, gastrointestinal symptoms, motor coordination issues, peripheral neuropathy, and anemia. Reproductive health risks include increased chance of miscarriage and reduced fertility. Acute lead poisoning may cause severe symptoms such as seizures, coma, or even death.

Consumers with concerns or who have experienced adverse effects should consult a healthcare professional promptly.

ProductLot Number
Rheumacare CapsulesCAM040
Rheumacare CapsulesCALO79-N

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their Rheumacare Capsules are from lot CAM040 or CALO79-N, stop using these products immediately, and contact Handelnine Global Limited at +1 442-223-0999 or legal@handelnine.com to request a refund or for further assistance.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Mr. Ankit Mota, Handelnine Global Limited | +1 442-223-0999 | legal@handelnine.com
