title: Direct Source Seafood LLC Recalls Frozen Raw Shrimp Due to Possible Cesium-137 Contamination
headline: Direct Source Seafood LLC Recalls Market 32 and Waterfront Bistro Frozen Raw Shrimp Potentially Contaminated with Cesium-137
description: Direct Source Seafood LLC recalls frozen raw shrimp sold under Market 32 and Waterfront Bistro brands due to possible contamination with cesium-137.
keywords: Product may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions whereby they may have become exposed to very low levels of cesium-137 (Cs-137)
reason: Product may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions whereby they may have become exposed to very low levels of cesium-137 (Cs-137)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Direct Source Seafood LLC, based in Bellevue, WA, announced a recall on December 19, 2025, involving approximately 83,800 bags of frozen raw shrimp imported from Indonesia. These products, sold under the Market 32 and Waterfront Bistro brands, may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions that exposed them to contamination by cesium-137 (Cs-137), a man-made radioisotope.

Cesium-137 can be present in the environment at varying levels, particularly in areas with environmental contamination. Prolonged, repeated low-dose exposure to Cs-137—such as through contaminated food or water—may increase the risk of cancer by causing DNA damage in living cells. The recalled products were distributed in multiple states and sold between mid-2025 and 2027.

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) is investigating reports of Cs-137 contamination linked to frozen shrimp products and shipping containers manufactured in Indonesia by PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods). According to the FDA, no contaminated products have been confirmed in the U.S. marketplace, and no illnesses have been reported so far.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Market 32 or Waterfront Bistro frozen raw shrimp from Direct Source Seafood LLC should not consume the product. They should dispose of it or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Direct Source Seafood LLC at 425-455-2291, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PST.

title: Olympus Voluntary Recall of Single-Use Ligating Device Due to Detachment Failure
headline: Olympus Initiates Voluntary Corrective Action for Single-Use Ligating Device with Detachment Issues
description: Olympus recalls Single-Use Ligating Device (Polyloop) due to risk of failure to detach, causing patient complications including bleeding and perforation.
keywords: PolyLoop device may fail to detach as expected and become anchored to patient anatomy
reason: PolyLoop device may fail to detach as expected and become anchored to patient anatomy

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Olympus Corporation has announced a voluntary corrective action for its Single-Use Ligating Device ("Polyloop") due to a potential safety concern. The Polyloop device is intended for use with Olympus endoscopes to deliver a nylon loop snare that helps prevent or control bleeding after polypectomy of pedunculated polyps.

This initiative follows reports of the device failing to release or detach as designed, causing the loop to become unintentionally anchored around patient anatomy during use. Although no deaths have been reported, the failure of the ligation loop to release can lead to procedural complications requiring emergency intervention. Potential risks include bleeding, mucosal injury, perforation, and in severe cases, surgical intervention or hospitalization.

In November 2025, Olympus notified customers of this corrective action and advised that all personnel review the supplemental illustrative guidance along with existing warnings, cautions, and emergency treatment procedures outlined in the Instructions for Use.

Adverse reactions or quality issues related to the Polyloop device can be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch program. For additional information or to report device concerns, Olympus provides a dedicated Technical Assistance Center.

Recall Details
Device: Single-Use Ligating Device ("Polyloop")
Issue: Device may fail to detach, anchoring loop to patient anatomy
Risks: Bleeding, mucosal injury, perforation, need for emergency intervention
Notification Date: November 2025
Recall Number: 0476

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should verify if their Single-Use Ligating Device is included in the recall by checking lot details at https://olympusamerica.com/recall using recall number "0476". For assistance or to report device issues, contact Olympus at 1-800-848-9024, Option 1, or email complaints@olympus.com.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: Olympus Technical Assistance Center | 1-800-848-9024 | complaints@olympus.com
Media contact: Susan Scerbo | | | sue.scerbo@olympus.com | (484) 505-0407

Issue: Device may fail to detach, anchoring loop to patient anatomy
Risks: Bleeding, mucosal injury, perforation, need for emergency intervention
Notification Date: November 2025
Recall Number: 0476

title: Primavera Nueva Inc. Voluntarily Recalls Select 4-Count Tamales for Potential Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination
headline: Primavera Nueva Inc. Issues Voluntary Recall of Select 4-Count Tamales Due to Possible Listeria Contamination
description: Primavera Nueva Inc. recalls select 4-count tamales distributed in California and Nevada for potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Primavera Nueva Inc., based in Sonoma, CA, has issued a voluntary recall of select 4-count tamale products due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Pregnant women are also at risk as infection can lead to miscarriages or stillbirths.

The recall affects tamales produced between October 10, 2024, and October 10, 2025, which were distributed by Primavera Nueva Inc. to retail stores in California and Nevada. No other products from Primavera Nueva are involved, and no illnesses have been reported so far. The concern arose after an FDA inspection on October 10, 2025, found gaps in verifying internal cooking temperatures needed to control biological hazards, including Listeria monocytogenes.

Primavera Nueva has since taken corrective actions, implementing enhanced cooking-temperature monitoring and verification starting October 11, 2025.

Product Variety
Roasted Green Chile & Jack Cheese
Black Bean Bonanza & Jack Cheese
Butternut Squash with Roasted Green Chiles & Cheese
BBQ Chipotle Bean & White Cheddar
Mushroom Spinach & Salsa with Two Cheeses
Roasted Green Chile
Black Bean Bonanza
Butternut Squash with Roasted Green Chiles
Mushroom Spinach & Salsa

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should not consume tamales with date codes between 10/22/2024 and 10/22/2025, discard affected products, and contact Primavera Nueva Inc. at 707-939-9350 or primaveranueva@aol.com for further information.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Araceli Santoyo | 707-939-9350 | primaveranueva@aol.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on December 17, 2025.

title: MediNatura Expands Nationwide Recall of ReBoost and ClearLife Nasal Sprays Due to Microbial Contamination
headline: MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. Expands Nationwide Recall of ReBoost and ClearLife Allergy Nasal Sprays for Microbial Contamination
description: MediNatura recalls ReBoost and ClearLife nasal sprays nationwide due to yeast, mold, and Achromobacter bacterial contamination above safety limits.
keywords: Product has been found to contain yeast/mold and microbial contamination with one species identified as Achromobacter, at levels above specifications.
reason: Product has been found to contain yeast/mold and microbial contamination with one species identified as Achromobacter, at levels above specifications.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. has expanded a voluntary nationwide recall involving all lots of its ReBoost Nasal Spray and ClearLife Allergy Nasal Spray. The recall is due to microbial contamination detected in both products, including yeast, mold, and the bacterium Achromobacter at levels above established specifications.

The ReBoost Nasal Spray is intended to temporarily relieve nasal congestion and related symptoms, while the ClearLife Allergy Nasal Spray is used for minor allergy symptom relief. Both products are sold in 20 mL bottles packaged in distinct cartons and were distributed nationwide through retail outlets and online at medinatura.com.

There is a reasonable risk that using these contaminated products could result in serious infections, especially in immunocompromised individuals. No injuries or adverse events have been reported to date.

Product NDC Number UPC Expiration Dates Package Description
ReBoost Nasal Spray 62795-4005-9 787647 10186 3 12/2022 to 12/2025 20 mL bottle in white and yellow carton
ClearLife Allergy Nasal Spray 62795-4006-9 787647 10188 7 12/2022 to 12/2025 20 mL bottle in white and green carton

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using recalled ReBoost and ClearLife nasal sprays, check product lot details, and contact MediNatura at 800-621-7644 or recall@medinatura.com for refunds or return the product to the place of purchase.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (800) 621-7644 | recall@medinatura.com

title: StuffbyNainax LLC Recalls MR.7 SUPER 700000 Dietary Supplement Due to Undeclared Sildenafil and Tadalafil
headline: StuffbyNainax LLC Initiates Nationwide Recall of MR.7 SUPER 700000 Dietary Supplement Containing Undeclared Sildenafil and Tadalafil
description: StuffbyNainax LLC recalls MR.7 SUPER 700000 male enhancement supplement for undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil, posing serious health risks.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
StuffbyNainax LLC is voluntarily recalling all lots of MR.7 SUPER 700000 dietary supplement capsules distributed nationwide in the United States. Testing by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) revealed that these products contain undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil, active pharmaceutical ingredients approved for prescription use to treat erectile dysfunction. Because of the presence of these substances, MR.7 SUPER 700000 capsules are considered unapproved new drugs and are subject to recall due to safety concerns.

Consumption of products containing sildenafil or tadalafil without appropriate medical supervision can pose serious health risks. These ingredients may interact dangerously with nitrates found in some prescription medications, potentially causing a significant drop in blood pressure that could be life-threatening. Individuals with diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease—conditions often treated with nitrates—are especially vulnerable.

MR.7 SUPER 700000 capsules are marketed as a dietary supplement for male enhancement but do not bear lot numbers or expiration dates. The product was sold online to customers across the United States between August 2025 and November 2025.

This recall is being conducted in coordination with the FDA to protect public health.

Product Name Brand Form Lot Number Distribution Period Distribution
MR.7 SUPER 700000 MR.7 Capsules Not available / unlisted August 2025 – November 2025 Sold online in the United States

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased MR.7 SUPER 700000 should check product labels for undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil, discontinue use immediately, and contact StuffbyNainax LLC at (346) 441-9470 or stuffbynainaxllc.info@gmail.com for further assistance.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Am Malik | (346) 441-9470 | stuffbynainaxllc.info@gmail.com

title: Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods Recalls Decorated Pound Cakes Due to Undeclared Soy Allergen
headline: Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods Recalls Decorated Pound Cakes Containing Undeclared Soy Allergen
description: Ukrop’s recalls decorated pound cakes with undeclared soy allergen, affecting products with Best By Date 12/15/2025 in VA and WV stores.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods, LLC, based in Richmond, VA, has announced a recall of four varieties of its Decorated Pound Cakes due to the presence of an undeclared soy allergen. The recall specifically affects products with a Best By Date of 12/15/2025. The issue arose because a cake releasing agent containing soy was used during the production of these cakes, and this ingredient was not disclosed on the product labels.

Consumers with soy allergies should be aware that exposure to soy can cause reactions ranging from mild symptoms such as hives and itching to severe anaphylaxis. No other Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods products are impacted by this recall.

The affected products were distributed primarily through Kroger Mid-Atlantic stores located in Virginia and West Virginia as well as Ukrop's Market Hall in Richmond, VA. All retail locations have been notified and are actively removing the recalled cakes from their shelves.

Product Best By Date Retailers
Decorated Pound Cakes (4 varieties) 12/15/2025 Kroger Mid-Atlantic (28 locations in VA and WV), Ukrop's Market Hall (Richmond, VA)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the Best By Date on their Ukrop’s Decorated Pound Cakes and if it matches 12/15/2025, return the product to the retailer for a full refund. For questions, contact Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods at 804-340-3050 Monday to Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. EST.

title: South Asian Food Inc. Recalls Bengal King Jhal Chanachur for Undeclared Peanut Allergen
headline: South Asian Food Inc. Recalls Bengal King Jhal Chanachur Food Treats Due to Undeclared Peanuts
description: South Asian Food Inc. recalls Bengal King Jhal Chanachur for undeclared peanuts, posing allergy risks. Distributed nationwide in 12.34 oz jars.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
South Asian Food Inc., located in Maspeth, Queens, NY, has issued a recall for its Bengal King Jhal Chanachur food treats due to the potential presence of undeclared peanuts. This poses a significant risk for individuals with peanut allergies, as consuming the product could cause serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recalled product is sold in 12.34 oz (350 gm) clear plastic jars and was distributed nationwide through retail stores and mail orders. The recall was prompted after routine testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets confirmed that packages without peanut labeling contained peanuts.

No reports of illnesses or allergic reactions related to this product have been received so far.

Product Weight Lot Number Expiration Date UPC Code
Bengal King Jhal Chanachur 12.34 oz (350 gm) B No.HCNCU1205/24 June-2026 8941153046243

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their product packaging for lot number B No.HCNCU1205/24 and expiration date June-2026 and return affected Bengal King Jhal Chanachur jars to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional information, contact South Asian Food Inc. at 1-718-894-2507.

content.text: Consumer support: (718) 894-2507

4 sizes of aluminum saucepans with capacities ranging from 1 quart to 3 quarts
Aluminum saucepans recalled because they have the potential to be contaminated with lead (Pb) which may leach into food.
productDescription: 4 sizes of aluminum saucepans with capacities ranging from 1 quart to 3 quarts
companyName: Town Food Service Equipment Co., Inc.
reason: Aluminum saucepans recalled because they have the potential to be contaminated with lead (Pb) which may leach into food.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Town Food Service Equipment Co., Inc. of Brooklyn, NY, is recalling certain aluminum saucepans due to potential lead (Pb) contamination, which may leach into food. Lead exposure poses serious health risks, particularly to children and fetuses, with no known safe exposure level. Even low levels can impair learning, IQ, and behavior, while higher levels may cause symptoms such as fatigue, headache, stomach pain, vomiting, or neurological changes.

The recall affects four sizes of aluminum saucepans with capacities ranging from 1 quart to 3 quarts. These saucepans feature indented interior graduations and a plated steel handle riveted to the body. They were distributed between January 1, 2023, and November 30, 2025, through authorized food service equipment dealers including retailers across various states in the U.S., Puerto Rico, and Quebec City, Canada.

The recall stems from FDA testing that detected leachable lead in the finished products. Town Food Service Equipment Co. has halted production and distribution of the affected saucepans as the investigation continues. No illnesses have been reported to date.

Item/SKU Number Description Capacity
SKU 101 Aluminum Saucepan with indented graduations and plated steel handle 1 Quart
SKU 102 Aluminum Saucepan with indented graduations and plated steel handle 1.5 Quarts
SKU 103 Aluminum Saucepan with indented graduations and plated steel handle 2 Quarts
SKU 104 Aluminum Saucepan with indented graduations and plated steel handle 3 Quarts

title: BAKKAVOR USA Recalls 16-Ounce Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: BAKKAVOR USA Recalls 16-Ounce Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen Risk
description: BAKKAVOR USA recalls 16-ounce Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup sold at H-E-B Texas stores for undeclared milk allergen, posing health risks to milk-sensitive consumers.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Bakkavor USA of Charlotte, North Carolina, has announced a recall of the 16-ounce Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup due to the presence of undeclared milk. This recall applies to products with a BEST BY date of January 11, 2026. The undeclared milk poses a risk to individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities, potentially causing serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The affected product was distributed between November 3, 2025, and December 8, 2025, and was sold exclusively at H-E-B retail stores in Texas. The soup is packaged in clear plastic cups with the BEST BY date printed on the bottom. To date, no allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

The recall resulted from a consumer report that the product labeled as Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup contained poultry and rice soup instead. An internal investigation found that the Meal Simple Turkey & Wild Rice Soup contains milk ingredients, while the Red Lentil Dal Soup does not list milk on its label. The cause of this labeling error is still under investigation.

Product Size Best By Date Retailer Distribution Dates
Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup 16 ounces 1/11/2026 H-E-B stores in Texas 11/3/2025 – 12/8/2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased the 16-ounce Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup with a BEST BY date of January 11, 2026, sold at H-E-B stores in Texas, should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund and contact H-E-B Customer Service at 1-855-432-4438 with any questions.

content.text: Consumer support: (855)-432-4438

title: Cargill Voluntarily Recalls Single Lot of Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn for Livestock Due to High Aflatoxin Levels
headline: Cargill Recalls Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn for Livestock Over Elevated Aflatoxin Concentrations
description: Cargill recalls one lot of Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn for livestock due to aflatoxin levels exceeding FDA safety limits for multiple animal types.
keywords: Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn for livestock, 50 lb. bag
Product may contain aflatoxin levels that exceed the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) action level for immature animals, wildlife, equines, small ruminants, and dairy animals.
productDescription: Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn for livestock, 50 lb. bag
reason: Product may contain aflatoxin levels that exceed the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) action level for immature animals, wildlife, equines, small ruminants, and dairy animals.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Cargill Animal Nutrition & Health has announced a voluntary recall of a single lot of Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn for livestock due to elevated levels of aflatoxin. The aflatoxin levels in this product exceed the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) action level for immature animals, wildlife, equines, small ruminants, and dairy animals. Exposure to high aflatoxin levels can cause serious health issues in livestock, including weight loss, reduced feed intake, liver damage, immune suppression, and in severe cases, cancerous tumors. Dairy animals may also pass aflatoxin into their milk.

The recalled product was distributed through retail stores in Texas. The recall was initiated after testing by the Office of the Texas State Chemist revealed aflatoxin concentration of 35 parts per billion (ppb) in a sample. Cargill’s retained sample from the same lot tested below 1 ppb, and the company has not yet received any reports of injury or illness linked to this product. Distribution of the affected lot has stopped as investigations continue.

Product Lot Code Manufacture Date Package Size Distribution Area
Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn for livestock Specific lot code (found on bottom right-hand side of label) Not specified in announcement 50 lb. bag Texas retail stores

facts.product: Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn for livestock, 50 lb. bag

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should stop using the affected Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn immediately, verify the lot code, and return any remaining product to the original point of purchase for a full refund or replacement. If animals show symptoms such as vomiting, diarrhea, decreased milk production, or other health issues, contact a veterinarian promptly. For more information, contact Cargill at 1 800-292-7694, Monday through Friday, 8:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. Central Time.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: (800) 292-7694
Media contact: media@cargill.com

title: MediNatura New Mexico Recalls ReBoost Nasal Spray 0.68 fl oz for Microbial Contamination
headline: MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. Announces Nationwide Recall of ReBoost Nasal Spray 0.68 fl oz Due to Yeast, Mold, and Achromobacter Contamination
description: MediNatura New Mexico recalls ReBoost Nasal Spray 0.68 fl oz due to microbial contamination with yeast, mold, and Achromobacter above safety limits.
keywords: Product has been found to contain yeast/mold and microbial contamination with one species identified as Achromobacter, at levels above specifications.
reason: Product has been found to contain yeast/mold and microbial contamination with one species identified as Achromobacter, at levels above specifications.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On December 10, 2025, MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. announced a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of ReBoost Nasal Spray, a homeopathic nasal spray used to temporarily relieve nasal congestion, sinus headache and pressure, postnasal drip, sneezing, runny nose, and nasal itching. The recall was initiated after microbial contamination was detected, including yeast, mold, and Achromobacter species at levels above established safety specifications.

There is a reasonable probability that using the affected product could cause adverse health consequences, including life-threatening infections, particularly in immunocompromised individuals. As of the announcement date, no adverse events related to this recalled product have been reported to the company.

The recalled nasal spray is packaged in a 20 mL bottle with a white and yellow carton. It was distributed nationwide through retail outlets and online sales via medinatura.com.

Product NameLot NumberExpiration DateNDC NumberUPCPackage Size
ReBoost Nasal Spray22426812/202762795-4005-9787647 10186 30.68 fl oz (20 mL)

This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased ReBoost Nasal Spray lot 224268 should immediately stop using it. Those who bought the product directly from MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. should contact the company at 800-621-7644 or recall@medinatura.com for a refund. Purchases from retail stores should be returned to the place of purchase.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (800) 621-7644 | recall@medinatura.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on December 10, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.
Review consumer support information on the company website.
Visit the company website for product details.

title: Lunds & Byerlys Recalls L&B Monster Cookies for Undeclared Peanut, Egg, and Soy Allergens
headline: Lunds & Byerlys Recalls L&B Monster Cookies After Undeclared Allergen Labeling Error
description: Lunds & Byerlys is recalling L&B Monster Cookies due to undeclared peanut, egg, and soy allergens posing serious health risks to allergic individuals.
keywords: May contain undeclared peanut, egg, and soy allergens
reason: May contain undeclared peanut, egg, and soy allergens

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Lunds & Byerlys is voluntarily recalling its L&B Monster Cookies due to incorrect labeling on the packaging. The affected products may contain undeclared allergens including peanuts, egg, and soy, which pose serious or potentially life-threatening risks to individuals with allergies to these ingredients.

The recalled cookies were sold between November 15, 2025, and December 9, 2025, at Lunds & Byerlys stores located throughout the Twin Cities and St. Cloud areas. To date, there have been no reported illnesses related to this recall.

The issue was identified by a store employee who alerted the company’s Quality Assurance team.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased L&B Monster Cookies from Lunds & Byerlys stores between November 15 and December 9, 2025, should return the product to any Lunds & Byerlys store for a full refund without a receipt. For additional information, contact Lunds & Byerlys Customer Service at 952-548-1400, Monday through Sunday from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: (952) 548-1400
Media contact: Aaron Sorenson | | | Aaron.Sorenson@lfhi.com | (952) 915-2694

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on December 10, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.

The recalled cookies were sold between November 15, 2025, and December 9, 2025, at Lunds & Byerlys stores located throughout the Twin Cities and St. Cloud areas. To date, there have been no reported illnesses related to this recall.

The issue was identified by a store employee who alerted the company’s Quality Assurance team.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased L&B Monster Cookies from Lunds & Byerlys stores between November 15 and December 9, 2025, should return the product to any Lunds & Byerlys store for a full refund without a receipt. For additional information, contact Lunds & Byerlys Customer Service at 952-548-1400, Monday through Sunday from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m.

content.text:
Consumer support: (952) 548-1400

title: Lil’ Turtles Recalls Grandma Belle’s Tomato Basil Soup Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Lil’ Turtles Voluntarily Recalls Grandma Belle’s Tomato Basil Soup for Undeclared Milk Allergen
description: Lil’ Turtles recalls Grandma Belle’s Tomato Basil Soup due to undeclared milk allergen, posing risk to individuals with milk allergies or sensitivities.
keywords: Potential or Undeclared Allergen – Undeclared Milk
reason: Potential or Undeclared Allergen – Undeclared Milk

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Lil’ Turtles has issued a voluntary recall of all lots of Grandma Belle’s Tomato Basil Soup due to undeclared milk on the product label. The recall is being conducted because the milk allergen was not listed, posing a risk to individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities.

The affected product is packaged in 17 oz glass jars and was sold between September 23, 2022, and December 3, 2025, at multiple retail locations across several states. The issue was identified during a routine inspection by the Ohio Department of Agriculture.

Although there have been no reported illnesses related to the consumption of this product, people allergic to milk could experience serious allergic reactions if they eat this soup.

title: TBC Distribution Corporate Recalls Lucky Foods 40g Cinnamon Powder Due to Elevated Lead Levels
headline: Recall of Lucky Foods Brand Cinnamon Powder 40g for Potential Lead Contamination
description: TBC Distribution Corporate recalls Lucky Foods Cinnamon Powder 40g with best before 15.09.2027 due to potential elevated lead levels posing health risks.
keywords: Lucky Foods brand Cinnamon Powder 40g, best before date 15.09.2027, packaged in a vacuum or modified atmosphere 40g pouch with a brown label
Potential to be contaminated with elevated levels of lead
productDescription: Lucky Foods brand Cinnamon Powder 40g, best before date 15.09.2027, packaged in a vacuum or modified atmosphere 40g pouch with a brown label
reason: Potential to be contaminated with elevated levels of lead

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
TBC Distribution Corporate of Brooklyn, NY, has issued a recall for Lucky Foods brand Cinnamon Powder 40g due to potential contamination with elevated levels of lead. The affected product has a best before date of 15.09.2027 and is packaged in a vacuum or modified atmosphere 40g pouch with a brown label.

Exposure to lead, even at low levels, can be harmful. While short-term exposure to very low levels may not cause symptoms, increased blood lead levels can be the only sign of exposure. Prolonged exposure, especially in children, may cause serious health issues including developmental defects and permanent damage to the central nervous system. Adults exposed chronically may experience kidney dysfunction, hypertension, and neurocognitive effects.

The recalled cinnamon powder was distributed to retail locations including grocery stores, convenience stores, supermarkets, delis, bakeries, and restaurants in multiple states between April 11, 2025, and September 1, 2025. The recall follows testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets, which detected elevated lead levels. No illnesses have been reported at this time.

title: Mellace Family Brands Recalls Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination
headline: Mellace Family Brands California, Inc. Issues Recall of Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted 34 oz & 11.5 oz Packages Over Salmonella Concern
description: Mellace Family Brands recalls Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted 34 oz tubs and 11.5 oz bags for potential Salmonella contamination; no illnesses reported.
keywords: Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted (34 oz tubs and 11.5 oz bags)
Product may be contaminated with Salmonella
productDescription: Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted (34 oz tubs and 11.5 oz bags)
reason: Product may be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mellace Family Brands California, Inc. has recalled Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted in 34 oz tubs and 11.5 oz bags due to possible Salmonella contamination. Salmonella is a harmful bacteria that can cause serious infections, especially in young children, elderly adults, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Symptoms often include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, and severe cases may lead to bloodstream infections and other complications.

The recalled products were sold at Wegmans stores in Connecticut, Delaware, Maryland, Massachusetts, North Carolina, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Virginia, and Washington, D.C. between November 3 and December 1, 2025. No illnesses have been reported in connection with these products to date.

The recall follows routine testing of a supplier’s raw pistachios that revealed a positive Salmonella result. These pistachios were used in the affected Deluxe Mixed Nuts.

Product Packaging UPC Lot Code Best By Date
Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted 34 oz (964g) Plastic tub 077890421314 58041 JUL 28, 2026
Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted 11.5 oz (326g) Plastic bag 077890421352 58171 AUG 10, 2026

facts.product: Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted (34 oz tubs and 11.5 oz bags)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted in 34 oz tubs or 11.5 oz bags with the listed lot codes and best by dates should not consume the product and return it to the store for a full refund. For more information, contact Wegmans Food Markets at 1-855-934-3663, available daily from 8:00 a.m. to 7:00 p.m. EST.

title: Mondelēz Global LLC Voluntary Recall of RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches Due to Potential Undeclared Peanut Allergen
headline: Mondelēz Global LLC Issues Limited Voluntary Recall of Select RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches in Eight U.S. States for Undeclared Peanut Allergen
description: Mondelēz Global LLC recalls select RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches in NY, NJ, PA, GA, AR, MO, OK, and AL due to potential undeclared peanut allergen risk.
keywords: Potential or Undeclared Allergen – Undeclared Peanut
reason: Potential or Undeclared Allergen – Undeclared Peanut

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mondelēz Global LLC has issued a limited voluntary recall of RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches sold in select U.S. states due to a potential undeclared peanut allergen. The recall affects 70 cases of one specific SKU with particular “Best When Used By” dates and a plant code, originally distributed in New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Georgia, Arkansas, Missouri, Oklahoma, and Alabama.

The issue involves individually wrapped packs inside the cartons which may be mislabeled as the Cheese variety, although the product inside is the Peanut Butter variety. This could pose a serious or life-threatening risk for individuals allergic or severely sensitive to peanuts. The outer cartons are correctly labeled and include an allergen advisory stating “contains peanuts.”

This recall is not an expansion of a previous recall from July but is a voluntary precautionary measure. No other Ritz products or other Mondelēz Global LLC products are impacted by this recall. To date, there have been no reports of injury or illness related to this product.

Product Description Retail UPC Best When Used By Dates Plant Code
27.6 oz. RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches - 20 Count (20 x 1.38-oz. 6-pack carton) 44000 07584 2 8 JAN 26, 15 JAN 26 “AE” (located on top of package)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with peanut allergies should not consume the affected 27.6 oz. RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches with UPC 44000 07584 2 and Best When Used By dates of 8 JAN 26 or 15 JAN 26. Dispose of any recalled product and contact Mondelēz Global LLC customer service at 1-844-366-1171, Monday through Friday, 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET for questions.

title: Vetoquinol USA Recalls Six Lots of FOLLTROPIN Injectable Kits for Cattle Due to Particulate Matter in Sterile Diluent Vial
headline: Vetoquinol USA Issues Recall of Six FOLLTROPIN Injectable Kit Lots for Cattle Over Particulate Matter Contamination in Diluent
description: Vetoquinol USA recalls six lots of FOLLTROPIN Injectable Kits for cattle due to particulate matter in sterile diluent vial; no adverse events reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Vetoquinol USA, Inc., based in Fort Worth, TX, has issued a recall for six lots of its FOLLTROPIN® Injectable Kits for cattle. The recall is due to the presence of particulate matter identified in the sterile diluent vial during routine examination of retained samples. The affected product is used for inducing superovulation in beef and dairy heifers and cows and is a prescription veterinary medicine in the U.S.

The presence of particulate matter in an injectable product may cause injection site reactions or hypersensitivity responses in treated animals. Possible adverse signs include pain, swelling, or wounds at the injection site, decreased activity, fever, and labored breathing. Veterinary attention should be sought immediately if any such signs occur after administration of the product. As of the recall announcement, no adverse events have been reported related to these lots.

The recall only concerns the sterile diluent vial included in the FOLLTROPIN Kit, which contains both the follicle stimulating hormone powder and a sterile bacteriostatic saline diluent. The FOLLTROPIN powder vials and the Dual Pack format (which does not contain diluent vials) are not affected by this recall. The affected products were distributed nationwide through veterinary distributors.

Recall Information
Product: FOLLTROPIN® Injectable Kits (porcine pituitary-derived FSH for injection)
Reason for Recall: Presence of particulate matter in sterile diluent vial
Impacted Product Format: FOLLTROPIN Kit (one vial of FSH powder + one vial of sterile diluent)
Unaffected Product Format: FOLLTROPIN Dual Pack (two vials of FSH powder, no diluent)
Distribution: Veterinary distributors nationwide, United States
Impacted Lots
Six lots of FOLLTROPIN sterile diluent vials (specific lot numbers were referenced in the announcement but not detailed here)

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with FOLLTROPIN Injectable Kits for Cattle from Vetoquinol USA should immediately stop using and quarantine the affected lots, then contact Vetoquinol USA at 1-800-267-5707 for instructions on product return or disposal.

title: Wegmans Recalls Locatelli Grated Pecorino Romano Cheese Due to Listeria Risk
headline: Wegmans Issues Recall for Locatelli Grated Pecorino Romano Cheese Potentially Contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes
description: Wegmans recalls Locatelli Grated Pecorino Romano Cheese sold between Nov 14-24, 2025 due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Wegmans Food Markets, Inc. has issued a recall for Locatelli Grated Pecorino Romano Cheese sold with Wegmans’ scale labels due to the potential presence of Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and potentially fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems.

The recalled product was available at Wegmans stores in Connecticut, Delaware, Maryland, Massachusetts, North Carolina, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Virginia, and Washington, D.C. between November 14 and November 24, 2025.

Symptoms of Listeria infection include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria risk miscarriage or stillbirth. No illnesses related to this product have been reported to date.

Product Description UPC Sale Dates
Locatelli Grated Pecorino Romano Cheese Packaged in plastic tub with Wegmans scale label 2-77580-XXXXX-7 (XXXXX varies by weight) November 14 – November 24, 2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check Locatelli Grated Pecorino Romano Cheese with Wegmans scale labels sold between November 14 and November 24, 2025, avoid consumption of affected lots, and return the product to Wegmans stores for a full refund. For questions, contact Wegmans Food Markets at 1-855-934-3663.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: Wegmans Food Markets | 1-855-934-3663
Media contact: Tracy Van Auker | | | tracy.vanauker@wegmans.com | 585-429-3826

title: Prairie Farms Recalls Prairie Farms Gallon Fat Free Milk Due to Potential Cleaning Agent Contamination
headline: Prairie Farms Recalls Gallon Fat Free Milk Distributed in Illinois and Wisconsin for Possible Contamination
description: Prairie Farms recalls select Gallon Fat Free Milk produced in Dubuque, Iowa due to possible contamination with food-grade cleaning agents.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Prairie Farms has announced a recall of select Prairie Farms Gallon Fat Free Milk due to potential contamination with food-grade cleaning agents. The affected milk was produced at the Dubuque, Iowa facility and distributed exclusively to Woodman’s stores in Illinois and Wisconsin. Consumption of this product may cause illness.

The recall specifically applies to Prairie Farms Gallon Fat Free Milk with a code date of DEC08 and plant code PLT19-145, produced during the timeframe of 17:51 to 21:23 on that date. Approximately 320 gallons of the affected milk were sold before the issue was identified on November 24, 2025. Prairie Farms promptly removed all remaining impacted product from store shelves and initiated this recall to ensure consumer safety.

Other Prairie Farms products are not affected by this recall.

Prairie Farms is cooperating with regulatory authorities and Woodman’s Markets to investigate the incident and manage the recall process. The affected product was distributed to multiple locations across Illinois and Wisconsin.

Product Code Date Plant Code Production Timeframe UPC Facility
Prairie Farms Gallon FAT FREE Milk DEC08 PLT19-145 17:51–21:23 7273023117 Dubuque, Iowa

Affected Woodman’s store locations include:

  • Illinois: Bloomingdale, Buffalo Grove, Carpentersville, Lakemoor, North Aurora, Rockford
  • Wisconsin: Appleton, Beloit, Green Bay, Janesville, Kenosha, Madison, Menomonee Falls, Oak Creek, Onalaska, Racine, Sun Prairie, Waukesha

For questions or further information, consumers may contact Prairie Farms Dairy via email at media@prairiefarms.com.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the code date DEC08 and plant code PLT19-145 on Prairie Farms Gallon Fat Free Milk purchased from Woodman’s stores in Illinois and Wisconsin. If you have the affected product, do not consume it; dispose of it safely or return it to the store for a refund. For assistance, contact Prairie Farms at media@prairiefarms.com.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: Prairie Farms Dairy | media@prairiefarms.com
Media contact: Prairie Farm’s Media Relations | | media@prairiefarms.com

title: MAMTAKIM Inc Recalls Belevini Dried Whole Salted Smelts (Korushka) Due to Clostridium botulinum Risk
headline: MAMTAKIM Inc Issues Recall for Belevini Dried Whole Salted Smelts (Korushka) Over Botulism Contamination Concern
description: MAMTAKIM Inc recalls Belevini Dried Whole Salted Smelts (Korushka) due to possible contamination with Clostridium botulinum, a serious health risk.
keywords: Dried Whole Salted Smelt, Mullet, Goby
Potential to be contaminated with Clostridium botulinum.
productDescription: Dried Whole Salted Smelt, Mullet, Goby
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Clostridium botulinum.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
MAMTAKIM Inc. has issued a recall for the product "Dried Whole Smelts Salted (Korushka)" under the Belevini brand due to a potential health risk of contamination with Clostridium botulinum. This bacterium can cause botulism, a serious and potentially fatal foodborne illness. The recalled product consists of dried, uneviscerated fish longer than 5 inches, which increases the risk of bacterial growth.

The product is packaged in a transparent vacuum-sealed plastic pouch with a net weight of 5.29 oz (150 g) and was stored refrigerated between 18°F and 68°F. It was sold exclusively to walk-in customers at the Cherry Hill Emmons retail store located at 1901 Emmons Ave, Brooklyn, NY 11235.

Symptoms of botulism can include general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, trouble breathing, muscle weakness, abdominal distension, and constipation. Anyone experiencing these symptoms should seek immediate medical attention. No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall to date.

The recall was initiated following an FDA inspection which found that the fish had not been properly eviscerated, allowing the potential growth of Clostridium botulinum. Consumers with questions can contact MAMTAKIM Inc. directly for further information.

Product Name Package Net Weight Brand Barcode (UPC) Storage Expiration Date Item Number
DRIED WHOLE SMELTS SALTED (KORUSHKA) Transparent vacuum-sealed plastic pouch 5.29 oz (150 g) BELEVINI 0754684898995 Refrigerated at 18°F–68°F 04/08/2026 LL0320

facts.product: Dried Whole Salted Smelt, Mullet, Goby

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should not consume Belevini Dried Whole Salted Smelts (Korushka), check for this product if purchased, and return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact MAMTAKIM Inc. at (718) 596-9577 or info@mamtakim.us.

title: Supreme Deli LLC Recalls Boar’s Head Chicken Caesar Salad and Wrap Due to Listeria Risk
headline: Supreme Deli LLC Voluntarily Recalls Boar’s Head Chicken Caesar Salad and Wrap for Possible Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination
description: Supreme Deli LLC recalls Boar’s Head Chicken Caesar Salad and Wrap products due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.
keywords: Due to the potential presence of Listeria monocytogenes
reason: Due to the potential presence of Listeria monocytogenes

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Supreme Service Solutions LLC (doing business as Supreme Deli) is participating in a recall linked to pecorino romano cheese supplied by Ambriola Company, which impacts certain Boar’s Head Brand products. The recall has been issued due to the potential presence of Listeria monocytogenes, a harmful bacteria that can pose serious health risks.

Ambriola Company initiated a Class I recall for specific pecorino romano cheese items following notification from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) about possible contamination. As a precaution, Boar’s Head has voluntarily withdrawn all products produced by Ambriola Company for Boar’s Head, including items that are not directly affected by the contamination.

The recalled products were distributed in Kroger retail stores located in Kentucky and Indiana. These products are packaged in clear plastic grab-and-go containers and include deli salads and wraps.

To date, no illnesses or consumer complaints related to this recall have been reported by Supreme Service Solutions.

Product Name Barcode UPC Best By Date
EverRoast Chicken Caesar Salad 850042244142 11/9/2025 - 11/22/2025
EverRoast Chicken Caesar Wrap 85004224455 11/9/2025 - 11/22/2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Boar’s Head Chicken Caesar Salad or Wrap from Kroger stores in Kentucky or Indiana within the best by dates 11/9/2025 to 11/22/2025 should not consume the products. They should discard any remaining items and contact Ambriola Company by emailing info@ambriola.com for further information.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: info@ambriola.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on November 24, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.
Visit the company website for product details.

title: Prime Food Processing LLC Recalls Lava Buns with Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Prime Food Processing LLC Issues Allergy Recall for Lava Buns with Salted Egg Yolk and Green Tea Flavor Due to Undeclared Milk
description: Prime Food recalls Lava Bun varieties due to undeclared milk allergen, posing risks to milk-allergic consumers. Products sold in 38 states in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Prime Food Processing LLC of Brooklyn, New York, has announced a voluntary recall of 2,243 cases of two varieties of dessert buns under the Prime Food brand due to undeclared milk allergens. The affected products contain milk derived from unsalted butter, which is not properly listed in the “Contains” allergen statement on the packaging as required by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

This recall poses a significant risk to individuals with milk allergies or severe milk sensitivities, as consuming these products could trigger a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction. The affected lava buns were distributed to Asian grocery stores in 38 states between April 2, 2025, and November 14, 2025.

No illnesses or allergic reactions have been reported to date. The recall was initiated after an internal product review identified the labeling issue, attributed to a temporary failure in the label review process at Prime Food Processing LLC.

Product Name UPC Size Item Number Packaging Description Lot Code Range Expiration Date Range
Prime Food Lava Bun with Salted Egg Yolk 97903705873 24 oz PD4188 Orange pouch approx. 10.25"W × 11.25"H 25092 - 25318 07/26 - 03/27
Prime Food Lava Bun with Green Tea Flavor 97803705883 24 oz PD4198 Bright lime green pouch approx. 10.25"W × 11.25"H 25092 - 25318 07/26 - 03/27

The recalled products were sold in the following states: Alabama, Arizona, California, Colorado, Connecticut, Florida, Georgia, Hawaii, Illinois, Indiana, Kansas, Louisiana, Massachusetts, Maryland, Maine, Michigan, Missouri, Mississippi, North Carolina, Nebraska, New Jersey, New York, Ohio, Oklahoma, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Virginia, Washington, and Wisconsin.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased the recalled Prime Food Lava Buns should check the packaging for affected lot codes and return the products with packaging to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional information, contact Prime Food Processing LLC at 718-963-2323, Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM EST.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Prime Food Processing LLC | 718-963-2323

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on November 20, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.

title: Shata Traders Inc Recalls Chef Brand Milk Pan 24cm Due to Lead Contamination Risk
headline: Shata Traders Inc Recalls Chef Brand Milk Pan 24cm Because of Potential Lead Contamination
description: Shata Traders Inc recalls Chef Brand Milk Pan 24cm due to potential lead contamination posing serious health risks. Sold in NY, NJ, CT, MD, VA, and MA.
keywords: Product has the potential to be contaminated with significant levels of lead (Pb) which may leach into food
reason: Product has the potential to be contaminated with significant levels of lead (Pb) which may leach into food

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Shata Traders Inc, based in Brooklyn, NY, is recalling the Chef Brand Milk Pan 24 cm due to potential contamination with significant levels of lead (Pb), which may leach into food during use. Lead is a toxic metal with no safe level of exposure and poses serious health risks, especially to children and fetuses.

Exposure to lead can cause severe health issues including learning difficulties, low IQ, behavioral problems in children, and symptoms such as fatigue, headache, stomach pain, vomiting, or neurological changes at higher levels. The recalled Milk Pan 24 cm was distributed in retailers across New York, New Jersey, Connecticut, Maryland, Virginia, and Massachusetts. The product is sold individually as part of a 6-piece box set.

No illnesses have been reported to date. The FDA identified the contamination after testing product samples collected at a retail store.

Product Size Distribution Areas Packaging
Chef Brand Milk Pan 24 cm NY, NJ, CT, MD, VA, MA Sold individually, 6 pieces per box

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using the Chef Brand Milk Pan 24 cm, check their product against the recall description, and return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Shata Traders Inc at (718) 369-7000 and ask for Mahboob Rahman.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Mahboob Rahman | (718) 369-7000

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on November 19, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.

title: Jeni’s Splendid Ice Cream Recalls Passion Fruit Dreamsicle Ice Cream Bars for Undeclared Wheat and Soy Allergens
headline: Jeni’s Splendid Ice Cream Initiates Voluntary Recall of Passion Fruit Dreamsicle Ice Cream Bars Due to Undeclared Wheat and Soy
description: Jeni’s recalls Passion Fruit Dreamsicle Ice Cream Bars over undeclared wheat and soy allergens, posing allergy risks. No injuries reported.
keywords: Passion Fruit Dreamsicle Ice Cream Bars
productDescription: Passion Fruit Dreamsicle Ice Cream Bars

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Jeni’s Splendid Ice Creams has announced a voluntary recall of one batch of its Passion Fruit Dreamsicle Ice Cream Bars due to the presence of undeclared wheat and soy allergens. This recall was initiated after it was discovered that a crunch topping containing wheat and soy was inadvertently mixed into the affected ice cream bars during production.

The recalled product poses a risk of serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions to individuals with soy or wheat allergies or severe sensitivities. To date, no illnesses or injuries related to this issue have been reported. The affected product was distributed nationwide through grocery stores.

Product Batch Code
Passion Fruit Dreamsicle Ice Cream Bars 25-210

facts.product: Passion Fruit Dreamsicle Ice Cream Bars

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the batch code on the top of the carton for 25-210 and return any affected Passion Fruit Dreamsicle Ice Cream Bars to the retailer for a full refund. For assistance, contact Jeni’s Splendid Ice Creams at 614-488-3224 (Monday to Friday, 9 a.m. – 5 p.m. ET) or email contact@jenis.com.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Jeni’s Splendid Ice Creams | 614-488-3224 | contact@jenis.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on November 18, 2025.

title: Mangalm LLC Recalls Tops Mixed Fruit Jam and Strawberry Jam Due to Undeclared Carmoisine
headline: Mangalm LLC Recalls Tops Mixed Fruit Jam and Tops Strawberry Jam for Undeclared Carmoisine Color Additive
description: Mangalm LLC recalls Tops Mixed Fruit Jam and Strawberry Jam due to undeclared carmoisine, distributed in several states. No confirmed illnesses reported.
keywords: Mixed Fruit Jam and Strawberry Jam
productDescription: Mixed Fruit Jam and Strawberry Jam

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mangalm LLC of Newark, California, has announced a recall of all batches of Tops Mixed Fruit Jam and Tops Strawberry Jam due to the presence of carmoisine, an unapproved color additive. The inclusion of this additive poses a potential health risk to consumers.

Both products affected are the 475g jars with batch number 4F1101 and a best before date of October 6, 2026.

The recalled products were distributed across several states including Utah, Oregon (Portland), Washington, and California. To date, no illnesses related to the consumption of these products have been confirmed. The recall action follows an FDA inspection at the manufacturing site, which detected the undeclared use of carmoisine in these jams.

Mangalm LLC has halted further distribution and is actively removing the affected products from retail shelves.

Product Name Weight Batch Number Best Before Date Distribution Areas
Tops Mixed Fruit Jam 475g 4F1101 10/06/2026 Utah, Portland (Oregon), Washington, California
Tops Strawberry Jam 475g 4F1101 10/06/2026 Utah, Portland (Oregon), Washington, California

facts.product: Mixed Fruit Jam and Strawberry Jam

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately check their products for the affected batch 4F1101 of Tops Mixed Fruit Jam and Tops Strawberry Jam, and discontinue use if they possess these items. For support or questions, contact Mangalm LLC at 925-319-7496 or manglamllc@gmail.com.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: 925-319-7496 | manglamllc@gmail.com

title: Africa Imports Voluntarily Recalls Organic Moringa Leaf Powder Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination
headline: Africa Imports Issues Voluntary Recall of Organic Moringa Leaf Powder for Potential Salmonella Risk
description: Africa Imports recalls Organic Moringa Leaf Powder sold after June 5, 2025, due to potential Salmonella contamination to protect consumer safety.
keywords: Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Africa Imports has issued a voluntary recall of its Organic Moringa Leaf Powder (1 kilogram box) due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella is a bacterium that can cause serious infections, particularly in young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals infected with Salmonella may experience symptoms such as fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare instances, the infection can lead to more severe health issues including arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The affected product was sold nationwide through Africa Imports' official website, africaimports.com, and is packaged in 1-kilogram boxes labeled "Organic Moringa Leaf Powder." All product purchased after June 5, 2025, is included in this recall following the detection of Salmonella in some samples from this batch.

This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). For additional information or assistance, customers can contact Wayne Kiltz, President of Africa Imports, at 201-457-1995 ext. 236 or via email at africa@africaimports.com. More details are also available through the FDA Outbreak Advisory.

Product Packaging Recall Details
Organic Moringa Leaf Powder 1 kilogram box All product purchased after June 5, 2025

As of this recall notice, there have been no reported illnesses or adverse events linked to this product.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should verify if their Organic Moringa Leaf Powder was purchased after June 5, 2025, discontinue its use immediately, and safely dispose of the product. Confirm disposal by replying to the recall notification email or contacting Africa Imports directly at 201-457-1995 ext. 236 or africa@africaimports.com. Distributors must inform their customers and share the recall notice.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (201) 457-1995 | africa@africaimports.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on November 12, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.
Visit the company website for product details.

disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.

title: ByHeart Voluntary Recall of All Batches of Whole Nutrition Infant Formula Due to Potential Clostridium botulinum Contamination
headline: ByHeart Updates Recall of Whole Nutrition Infant Formula in 24 oz Cans and 0.6 oz Packets Over Infant Botulism Risk
description: ByHeart recalls all batches of Whole Nutrition Infant Formula due to possible Clostridium botulinum contamination linked to infant botulism cases.
keywords: Whole Nutrition Infant formula 24 oz cans and 0.6oz packets
productDescription: Whole Nutrition Infant formula 24 oz cans and 0.6oz packets

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
ByHeart Inc. has updated the information regarding its voluntary recall of all batches of ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula in 24 oz cans and 0.6 oz Anywhere Pack™ packets due to the potential presence of Clostridium botulinum, a bacterium that can cause life-threatening illness or death. This recall follows an ongoing investigation related to a multistate outbreak of infant botulism potentially linked to the consumption of this formula.

Infant botulism is a rare but serious condition that occurs when Clostridium botulinum spores are ingested and produce neurotoxins in the immature intestinal tract of infants. Symptoms may include constipation, poor feeding, drooping eyelid, sluggish pupils, low muscle tone, difficulty sucking and swallowing, weak cry, generalized weakness, respiratory problems, and possibly respiratory arrest. Immediate medical attention should be sought if any of these symptoms appear.

The recall affects all batch codes and expiration dates of the affected ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula products distributed nationwide through retail and online channels.

The recall and investigation are being conducted in close coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the California Department of Public Health (CDPH), and other partners. As of November 19, 2025, 31 infants from 15 states with suspected or confirmed infant botulism and exposure to ByHeart formula have been reported, with all hospitalized and no deaths reported to date.

Product Size UPC Additional Info
ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula 24 oz can 5004496800 All batch codes and expiration dates included in recall
ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula Anywhere Pack™ 0.60 oz packets (14 packets per package) 5004496802 All batch codes and expiration dates included in recall

For further details and updates, visit the ByHeart website or the FDA website.

facts.product: Whole Nutrition Infant formula 24 oz cans and 0.6oz packets

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using all batches of ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula in 24 oz cans and 0.6 oz Anywhere Pack™ packets. Take a photo or record package information, keep the product labeled as “DO NOT USE,” and contact ByHeart customer support at 1 (833) 429-4327 or hello@byheart.com for assistance. If symptoms of infant botulism occur, seek immediate medical attention. Report any adverse events to the FDA’s MedWatch system.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: 1(833) 429-4327 | hello@byheart.com
Media contact: ByHeart Public Relations | | press@byheart.com

content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on November 11, 2025.

title: Moonlight Companies Recalls California-Grown Yellow and White Peaches Due to Listeria Contamination Risk
headline: Moonlight Companies Issues Voluntary Recall of Conventional Yellow and White Peaches Grown in California Over Potential Listeria Contamination
description: Moonlight Companies recalls California-grown yellow and white peaches for possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination, sold from Sept 16 to Oct 29, 2025.
keywords: Potential to be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes.
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Moonlight Companies has announced a voluntary recall of California-grown conventional yellow and white peaches due to a potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious infections, especially in young children, the elderly, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria face risks of miscarriage and stillbirth.

The recalled peaches were sold nationwide at retail stores from September 16, 2025, through October 29, 2025. These peaches were sold either as individual fruit bearing PLU stickers or in multi-packs. This recall does not include products labeled as “Washington” or “Organic.” The recall was initiated after Listeria monocytogenes was detected in the packing facility environment. No related illnesses have been reported at this time.

Product Description PLU Codes Dates Sold Notes
Conventional Yellow Peaches 3426 September 16, 2025 – October 29, 2025 Individual fruit with PLU sticker, not “Washington” or “Organic”
Conventional White Peaches 3427 September 16, 2025 – October 29, 2025 Individual fruit with PLU sticker, not “Washington” or “Organic”
Conventional Yellow and White Peaches Various (multi-packs) September 16, 2025 – October 29, 2025 Multi-pack, excluding “Washington” and “Organic” packages

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their peaches for the specified PLU codes and sales dates and avoid consuming products included in this recall. Dispose of or return affected peaches to the retailer. For questions, contact Moonlight Companies at 855-215-5017, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. Eastern Time.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (855) 215-5017

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on November 09, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.

title: ByHeart Recalls Two Batches of Whole Nutrition Infant Formula Due to Potential Clostridium botulinum Contamination
headline: ByHeart Initiates Voluntary Recall of Two Whole Nutrition Infant Formula Batches Following FDA Infant Botulism Investigation
description: ByHeart recalls two batches of Whole Nutrition Infant Formula amid FDA investigation of infant botulism; no direct link to formula found, precautionary measure taken.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
ByHeart Inc. has initiated a voluntary recall of two batches of its Whole Nutrition Infant Formula following a broader investigation by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) into an outbreak of infant botulism. Infant botulism is a rare but serious illness caused by ingestion and colonization of Clostridium botulinum spores in an infant's intestinal tract, leading to potentially severe symptoms including constipation, poor feeding, muscle weakness, and respiratory difficulties.

The FDA reported approximately 83 cases of infant botulism nationwide since August 2025, with 13 of the affected infants having received ByHeart formula at some point. However, the FDA has not found a direct link between any infant formula and these cases, and there is no historical precedent for infant formula causing infant botulism. Botulism is typically associated with environmental sources such as soil and dust rather than dairy products or infant formulas.

ByHeart adheres to strict global and U.S. safety standards and has found no contamination in its products. Despite this, the company is taking the precautionary measure of recalling the specified batches to eliminate any potential risk and uphold the highest safety standards for infants.

Batch Code Use By Date UPC
251261P2 01 Dec 2026 5004496800
251131P2 01 Dec 2026

No product contamination has been found, and no illnesses have been directly linked to these formula batches to date.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the batch codes on ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula products to identify recalled lots 251261P2 and 251131P2, and if affected, stop use immediately, dispose of the product, and contact ByHeart at 1 (833) 429-4327 or hello@byheart.com to request a free replacement.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: hello@byheart.com. | (833) 429-4327 | hello@byheart.com
Media contact: press@byheart.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on November 08, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.
Review consumer support information on the company website.

title: Fresenius Kabi Voluntarily Recalls Three Lots of Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL Due to Out-of-Specification Endotoxin Results
headline: Fresenius Kabi Announces Nationwide Recall of Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL for Endotoxin Testing Failure in Certain Lots
description: Fresenius Kabi recalls three lots of Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL due to out-of-specification endotoxin results in reserve samples. Affected US distribution.
keywords: Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL
productDescription: Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Fresenius Kabi USA, LLC is voluntarily recalling three lots of Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL (10 mg per mL), 2 mL fill in a 2 mL vial. The recall affects products distributed within the United States and is due to out-of-specification endotoxin results found in certain reserve samples from one lot. Two additional lots were included as a precaution following the investigation.

The company has informed distributors and customers and is coordinating the return of the affected products.

Patients experiencing any adverse effects potentially related to this drug should consult their healthcare provider.

Adverse reactions or quality issues may be reported to Fresenius Kabi Medical Affairs or Vigilance departments at 1-800-551-7176 during business hours, or via email at productcomplaint.USA@fresenius-kabi.com or adverse.events.USA@fresenius-kabi.com. Reports can also be submitted to the FDA MedWatch program online or by mail or fax.

Lot Number Expiration Date First Ship Date Last Ship Date
6133156 01/02/2025 02/04/2025 04/15/2025
6133194 Not specified Not specified Not specified
6133388 Not specified Not specified Not specified

facts.product: Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL

title: African Food on Wheels Inc. Recalls Oven Dried Fish (Scomberomorus Cavalla) Due to Botulism Risk
headline: African Food on Wheels Inc. Recalls Oven Dried Fish (Scomberomorus Cavalla) Over Potential Clostridium botulinum Contamination
description: African Food on Wheels Inc. recalls Oven Dried Fish (Scomberomorus cavalla) for potential contamination with Clostridium botulinum, posing serious botulism risk.
keywords: Potential to be contaminated with Clostridium botulinum
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Clostridium botulinum

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
African Food on Wheels Inc., based in Bronx, New York, has announced a recall of its Oven Dried Fish (Scomberomorus cavalla) due to a potential health risk. The recalled product consists of dried, un-eviscerated fish greater than 5 inches in length, which may be contaminated with Clostridium botulinum. This bacterium can cause botulism, a serious and potentially fatal illness.

Consumers should be aware that botulism symptoms include general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing difficulties, muscle weakness, abdominal swelling, and constipation. Anyone experiencing these symptoms should seek immediate medical care.

The affected products were sold only to walk-in customers at African Food on Wheels Inc., located at 1376 Randall Avenue, Bronx, NY 10474. The products are packaged in corrugated brown boxes. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this product.

The recall was initiated following an FDA inspection that found the fish had not been properly eviscerated.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: If you have purchased African Food on Wheels Inc. Oven Dried Fish (Scomberomorus cavalla), return it to the place of purchase for a full refund or contact the company at 646-632-2911 or africanfoodonwheelsinc@gmail.com for further assistance.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (646) 632-2911 | africanfoodonwheelsinc@gmail.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on November 06, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

title: Blue Oven Bakery, Inc. Recalls English Muffins for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Blue Oven Bakery, Inc. Voluntarily Recalls English Muffins Containing Undeclared Milk Allergen
description: Blue Oven Bakery, Inc. recalls English muffins due to undeclared milk allergen on labels affecting products sold in Ohio, Kentucky, and Indiana.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Blue Oven Bakery, Inc. has announced a voluntary recall of their English muffins due to an undeclared milk allergen on the product labels. The recall affects English muffins packaged in 13.04 oz sealed plastic bags, which were sold fresh as baked products. This mislabeling poses a risk to individuals with milk allergies.

The affected English muffins were produced from April 24, 2025, through October 24, 2025. These products were distributed and sold at select retail locations in Ohio, Kentucky, and Indiana.

To date, no illnesses or allergic reactions related to this product have been reported.

Country Fresh Market 8315 Beechmont Ave. #15 Cincinnati, OH 45255
Kremers Market 755 Buttermilk Pike Crescent Springs, KY 41017
Back Ally Mercantile 221 Walnut St. #102 Lawrenceburg, IN 47025
European Cafe 9450 Montgomery Rd. Cincinnati, OH 45242

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should verify if their Blue Oven Bakery English muffins were produced between April 24, 2025, and October 24, 2025. Affected products can be returned at Blue Oven Bakery’s Cincinnati location on November 4 and 5, 2025, for a full refund. For questions, contact Blue Oven Bakery at 513.706.0901 or blueovencustomerservice@gmail.com.

title: SiluetaYa Recalls Tejocote Root Dietary Supplement Due to Toxic Yellow Oleander Contamination
headline: SiluetaYa Issues Recall of Tejocote Root Supplement After Detection of Toxic Yellow Oleander
description: SiluetaYa recalls Tejocote Root dietary supplement due to contamination with toxic yellow oleander, posing serious health risks. No reported illnesses to date.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
SiluetaYa is recalling its Tejocote Root dietary supplement after FDA analysis revealed the product contains Thevetia peruviana, commonly known as yellow oleander. This plant contains cardiac glycosides, which are highly toxic and can cause serious neurological, gastrointestinal, and cardiovascular health issues in humans and animals.

Symptoms of yellow oleander poisoning may include nausea, vomiting, dizziness, diarrhea, abdominal pain, changes in heart rhythm, arrhythmia, and other severe effects. The recalled Tejocote Root product was distributed online in 8-ounce containers. To date, no illnesses related to this product have been reported.

For more information, please refer to the FDA Advisory.

Product Container Size Distribution
Tejocote Root dietary supplement (SiluetaYa brand) 8-ounce container Online distribution

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased the SiluetaYa Tejocote Root dietary supplement should not consume it and must return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional assistance, contact SiluetaYa at contacto@siluetaya.com Monday through Friday, 10:00 am to 4:00 pm Eastern Time.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: contacto@siluetaya.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on November 04, 2025.

title: Dreyer's Grand Ice Cream Recalls Häagen-Dazs Chocolate Dark Chocolate Mini Bars for Undeclared Wheat Allergen
headline: Dreyer’s Grand Ice Cream Initiates Recall of Häagen-Dazs Chocolate Dark Chocolate Mini Bars Due to Undeclared Wheat
description: Dreyer’s Grand Ice Cream recalls Häagen-Dazs Chocolate Dark Chocolate Mini Bars over undeclared wheat allergen in 6-count packs sold at Kroger and Giant Eagle.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On November 3, 2025, Dreyer’s Grand Ice Cream, Inc. initiated a voluntary recall of a limited batch of Häagen-Dazs Chocolate Dark Chocolate Mini Bars due to potential undeclared wheat content. This undeclared allergen presents a serious health risk to individuals with wheat allergies or severe sensitivities, potentially causing life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recall applies specifically to the 6-count package of Häagen-Dazs Chocolate Dark Chocolate Mini Bars with batch code LLA519501 and a Best By date of January 31, 2027. This batch was distributed exclusively to Kroger and Giant Eagle stores across selected states.

No illnesses or injuries related to this recall have been reported to date. Dreyer’s Grand Ice Cream is working in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and investigating the root cause, believed to be repackaging errors early in production.
