IN, MD, OH, PA, WV (Giant Eagle)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased the affected Häagen-Dazs Chocolate Dark Chocolate Mini Bars with batch code LLA519501 and Best By date January 31, 2027, should dispose of the product or return it to the place of purchase for a refund. For questions or further information, contact Dreyer’s Grand Ice Cream customer service at 800-767-0120 (Monday to Friday, 8am–5pm EST) or via email at dreyers@casupport.com.

title: First and Last Bakery Recalls First & Last Brand Tomato Sauces Due to Potential Clostridium botulinum Hazard
headline: First and Last Bakery Recalls Marinara, Puttanesca, and Meat Flavored Pasta Sauces Over Possible Botulism Risk
description: First and Last Bakery recalls First & Last brand tomato sauces due to potential Clostridium botulinum hazard from unapproved manufacturing process.
keywords: Marinara Sauce, Puttanesca Sauce, and Meat Flavored Pasta Sauce
Potential for Clostridium botulinum hazard as the product is manufactured without an approved schedule
productDescription: Marinara Sauce, Puttanesca Sauce, and Meat Flavored Pasta Sauce
reason: Potential for Clostridium botulinum hazard as the product is manufactured without an approved schedule

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
First and Last Bakery, LLC, based in Hartford, CT, has announced a recall of certain First & Last brand tomato sauce products due to a potential health risk. The recall follows an identification by the Connecticut Department of Consumer Protection related to manufacturing without an approved scheduled process, which may allow the growth of Clostridium botulinum bacteria. This bacteria can produce botulinum toxin, a serious and potentially fatal foodborne toxin.

The affected products include Marinara Sauce, Puttanesca Sauce, and Meat Flavored Pasta Sauce. These items were distributed in Connecticut and Massachusetts, primarily through Big Y stores and possibly other retailers. Sales and production of these sauces have been suspended while the company ensures compliance with regulatory requirements. No illnesses associated with these products have been reported at this time.

Botulism poisoning from contaminated foods can cause symptoms such as general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing problems, muscle weakness, abdominal swelling, and constipation. Anyone experiencing these symptoms after consuming these products should seek immediate medical care.

Product Name Package Size UPC Number
First and Last Original Marinara Sauce, Tomato & Basil 26 FL OZ jar Not specified
First and Last Original Traditional Pasta Sauce, Meat Flavored 26 FL OZ jar Not specified
First and Last Original Puttanesca Sauce, Mildly Hot & Spicy 26 FL OZ jar Not specified
  • Recall reason: Potential Clostridium botulinum hazard due to lack of approved scheduled process
  • Distribution areas: Connecticut and Massachusetts
  • Retailers: Big Y and possibly others
  • Distribution dates: September 22, 2025 to October 28, 2025
  • Consumer contact: 860-956-5000, Monday–Friday, 9:00 am – 3:00 pm EST

title: Olympus Updates Labeling for Bronchofiberscopes and Bronchovideoscopes to Address Endobronchial Combustion Risk
headline: Olympus Issues Voluntary Labeling Update for Bronchoscopes to Reduce Endobronchial Combustion Incidents
description: Olympus updates labeling for bronchofiberscopes and bronchovideoscopes used with laser therapy after reported endobronchial combustion injuries and fatality.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Olympus Corporation has announced a voluntary global update to the labeling of its bronchofiberscopes and bronchovideoscopes used with laser therapy, argon plasma coagulation, or high-frequency cauterization equipment. This action aims to provide clearer instructions to ensure the safe and effective use of these bronchoscopes during therapeutic procedures in the tracheobronchial tree.

The update follows a previous corrective action in 2023 after reports of endobronchial combustion incidents involving Olympus bronchoscopes, including one fatality. Since then, Olympus has investigated further and identified the need for additional instructions to reduce the risk of combustion. Since the 2023 communication, four more serious injury incidents have been reported worldwide, including one from the United States.

Key safety recommendations include maintaining a separation of more than 4 centimeters between the endoscope and the endotracheal tube, keeping oxygen levels below 40%, limiting energy output to less than 40 watts, avoiding excessive energy concentration in a single spot, and using suction to evacuate smoke. Endobronchial combustion can cause severe internal burns requiring additional medical interventions, prolonged hospitalization, or even result in death. Device damage during combustion may also lead to injury or necessitate surgical removal of fragments.

Users are advised to carefully review the updated instructions provided in the September 2025 customer letter.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should review the September 2025 updated product instructions carefully and contact Olympus Technical Assistance at 1-800-848-9024 or via email at complaints@olympus.com for support or to report any problems.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (800) 848-9024 | complaints@olympus.com

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Otsuka ICU Medical LLC has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of a specific lot of their 20 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection due to a labeling error. The overwrap of the recalled lot incorrectly identifies the product as 10 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection, which could lead to a potassium overdose if the mislabeled dosage is used. The correct dosage of 20 mEq is printed on the product bag label but is obscured by the incorrect overwrap.

Potassium chloride overdose may cause serious health risks including hyperkalemia, which can result in neuromuscular dysfunction, cardiac dysrhythmias, or cardiac arrest. Certain patient populations such as premature infants, those with kidney issues, or patients on potassium-sparing medications are at higher risk for severe complications. To date, no adverse events related to this issue have been reported.

The affected product lot, manufactured on April 15, 2025, was distributed in the United States between May 23, 2025, and August 26, 2025.

Product Description Lot Number Expiration Date NDC
Potassium Chloride Injection 20 mEq (overwrap labeled as 10 mEq) 1030613 09-30-2026 0990-7077-14 (Product), 0990-7074-26 (Overwrap)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Check product labels to identify the affected lot 1030613 of 20 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection mislabeled as 10 mEq. Stop using or distributing this lot and contact Sedgwick at 1-888-566-2363 (Mon-Fri 8am-5pm ET) or Otsuka ICU Medical LLC Customer Service at 1-800-258-5361 for return instructions and support.

title: Vanguard Enterprises Recalls Monarch Premium Kratom Powders Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination
headline: Vanguard Enterprises, LLC. DBA Bedrock MFG Recalls Monarch Premium Kratom Powders Including Bali Gold, Red Bali, Green Maeng Da, and White Elephant
description: Vanguard Enterprises recalls Monarch Premium Kratom powders due to potential Salmonella contamination, affecting Bali Gold, Red Bali, Green Maeng Da, and White Elephant.
keywords: Vanguard Enterprises, LLC. DBA Bedrock MFG
Bali Gold, Red Bali, Green Maeng Da, and White Elephant Kratom powder
Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella
productDescription: Bali Gold, Red Bali, Green Maeng Da, and White Elephant Kratom powder
companyName: Vanguard Enterprises, LLC. DBA Bedrock MFG
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Vanguard Enterprises, LLC. DBA Bedrock MFG of Boise, Idaho, has announced a recall of its Monarch Premium Kratom powders, including Bali Gold, Red Bali, Green Maeng Da, and White Elephant varieties. The recall is due to the potential contamination of these products with Salmonella, a bacteria that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems.

Symptoms of Salmonella infection can include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream, leading to severe illnesses such as arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis.

The affected Monarch Premium Kratom powders were distributed nationwide in retail stores and through mail orders via the company’s website from April 2023 through September 2023. These products were not sold in Alabama, Arkansas, Indiana, Rhode Island, or Wisconsin. No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall to date.

This recall follows laboratory testing by the Florida Department of Agriculture & Consumer Services, which identified Salmonella in a retail sample.

title: New Hoque & Sons Inc Recalls Uneviscerated Dry Ghoinnya Fish Due to Botulism Risk
headline: New Hoque & Sons Inc Issues Recall of Uneviscerated Dry Ghoinnya Fish Over Botulism Poisoning Risk
description: New Hoque & Sons Inc recalls Dry Ghoinnya Fish packaged uneviscerated, posing a risk of botulism poisoning. No illnesses reported. Check affected products.
keywords: Product was found to be uneviscerated. Uneviscerated fish have been linked to outbreaks of botulism poisoning.
reason: Product was found to be uneviscerated. Uneviscerated fish have been linked to outbreaks of botulism poisoning.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
New Hoque & Sons Inc. of Maspeth, NY, has issued a recall for its “Dry Ghoinnya Fish” due to the product being uneviscerated. The recall affects packages distributed nationwide through retail stores. Uneviscerated fish pose a health risk as they may contain Clostridium botulinum spores concentrated in the viscera, which have been linked to outbreaks of botulism poisoning.

The affected product is packaged in clear plastic bags weighing 10 to 12 pounds and features an expiration date of 5/19/25 stamped on the bottom. The product UPC code is 908172635412. The recall follows routine sampling and testing conducted by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets Food Inspectors and Food Laboratory staff, which identified improper evisceration prior to processing.

Symptoms of botulism poisoning can include dizziness, blurred or double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing difficulties, muscle weakness, abdominal bloating, and constipation. Individuals experiencing any of these symptoms should seek immediate medical attention. To date, no illnesses have been reported in relation to this recall.

Product Package Weight Expiration Date UPC Code
Dry Ghoinnya Fish (uneviscerated) 10-12 pounds 5/19/25 908172635412

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Dry Ghoinnya Fish with the UPC code 908172635412 and expiration date 5/19/25 should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact New Hoque & Sons Inc. at (718) 391-0992. Seek immediate medical attention if experiencing symptoms of botulism poisoning.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (718) 391-0992

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on October 29, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.

title: Teasdale Foods Recalls Martin’s and Giant/Casa Mamita Taco Dinner Kits for Undeclared Milk
headline: Teasdale Foods Voluntary Recall of Martin’s and Giant/Casa Mamita Taco Dinner Kits Due to Undeclared Milk Allergy Risk
description: Teasdale Foods recalls specific Taco Dinner Kits containing undeclared milk posing serious allergy risks. Check affected lots before consumption.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Teasdale Foods, Inc. has issued a voluntary recall of certain Taco Dinner Kits due to the potential presence of undeclared milk. This allergy alert is important for individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities, as consuming the affected products could trigger serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recall involves specific lots of Martin’s and Giant Crunchy Taco Dinner Kits as well as Casa Mamita Soft Taco Dinner Kits. The issue arose after consumer complaints indicated that cocoa mix packets containing milk were mistakenly included instead of taco seasoning packets. This mislabeling by a third-party seasoning supplier led to milk being present in products without proper ingredient disclosure on the packaging.

The affected products were distributed through Giant, Martin’s, and Aldi stores across multiple states including Alabama, Connecticut, Florida, Illinois, New York, Ohio, Pennsylvania, and others. No illnesses have been reported to date in connection with these items.

Brand and Product Packaging Description UPC Lot Code Best If Used By Date
Martin’s and Giant Crunchy Taco Dinner Kit Purple box 68826757516 25257 MAR 13 26
Casa Mamita Soft Taco Dinner Kit Blue and yellow box 4099100318715 25259 MAR 15 26

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the lot codes on Martin’s, Giant, and Casa Mamita Taco Dinner Kits to determine if their product is affected. Affected products should be returned to the retailer for a full refund. For questions, contact Teasdale Foods at teasdalecomplaints@teasdalefoods.com.

content.text: Consumer support: teasdalecomplaints@teasdalefoods.com

title: Hampton Farms Recalls Mixed Nuts 8 oz Packages Due to Undeclared Hazelnut Allergen
headline: Hampton Farms Recalls Mixed Nuts Roasted Unsalted and Roasted Salted 8 oz Packages for Missing Hazelnut Allergen Warning
description: Hampton Farms recalls Mixed Nuts 8 oz packages due to undeclared hazelnut allergen, posing allergy risks. Distributed in several Northeastern states in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hampton Farms has issued a recall for its Mixed Nuts Roasted Unsalted 8 oz and Mixed Nuts Roasted and Salted 8 oz packages due to an undeclared hazelnut allergen. Although hazelnuts are listed in the ingredient statement, they were not included in the allergen warning label, posing a risk to individuals with hazelnut allergies or severe sensitivities. Consumption of these products may result in serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The issue was identified on September 15, 2025, during a routine paperwork review, when the labeling omission was discovered. To date, no illnesses related to this recall have been reported. The affected products were distributed between September 2 and September 9, 2025, and sold in Stop&Shop stores across Massachusetts, Connecticut, New Jersey, New York, and Rhode Island.

This recall is conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Product Lot Number UPC Best If Used By Date
Mixed Nuts Roasted Unsalted 8 oz 23025 8 10111 02237 8 08/18/2026
Mixed Nuts Roasted and Salted 8 oz 23825 8 10111 02236 1 08/26/2026

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their product packaging for the affected lot numbers 23025 and 23825 and the corresponding UPCs. If they have purchased these products, especially if allergic to hazelnuts, they should not consume them and return the items to the place of purchase for a full refund or dispose of them. For further assistance, contact Hampton Farms Customer Service at 1-800-313-2748, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. EST.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (800) 313-2748

title: Pacific International Marketing Recalls Bulk Italian Parsley Due to Salmonella Contamination
headline: Pacific International Marketing Recalls Bulk Italian Parsley Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination
description: Pacific International Marketing recalls bulk Italian Parsley due to Salmonella contamination risk, with no reported illnesses related to the recall.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Pacific International Marketing has issued a recall of 474 cases of bulk Italian Parsley due to potential contamination with Salmonella. This bacterium can cause serious infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of Salmonella infection include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can become severe and lead to complications such as arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The affected Italian Parsley was distributed to wholesalers in Arizona, California, Florida, Minnesota, Michigan, Georgia, Ohio, and Nevada between September 22 and September 25, 2025. The product was sold in bulk cases containing 30 or 60 bunches with a twist tie, and in 24 count bunches in bags, all intended for wholesale distribution. The shelf life of the parsley is 18 days from harvest, with the expiry date listed as October 10, 2025.

Pacific International Marketing was alerted to the issue after a sample taken on October 6 tested positive for Salmonella. No illnesses related to this recall have been reported so far.

Consumers or businesses with questions about the recall can contact Pacific International Marketing by phone, mail, or email. The company is cooperating with the FDA during the ongoing investigation and is conducting its own internal review.

Product Description UPC Packaging Lot/Case Details Expiration Date
Italian Parsley 30 ct bunches with twist tie 40695 80125 Bulk case containing 30 bunches 474 cases recalled October 10, 2025
Italian Parsley 60 ct bunches with twist tie 40695 80125 Bulk case containing 60 bunches 474 cases recalled October 10, 2025
Italian Parsley 24 ct bunches in bags with twist tie 40695 80120 Bulk bags of 24 bunches 474 cases recalled October 10, 2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Pacific International Marketing’s bulk Italian Parsley should check the lot details to verify if their product is affected. If so, they should discard it or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Pacific International Marketing by phone at 831-759-8301 or email customerservice@pim4u.com.

title: Purity Products Recalls MyBladder Dietary Supplement Due to Possible E. coli Contamination
headline: Purity Products Recalls MyBladder Dietary Supplement Over Potential Escherichia coli O7:K1 and 1303 Contamination
description: Purity Products recalls MyBladder dietary supplement due to potential contamination with Escherichia coli O7:K1 and 1303, posing health risks to vulnerable groups.
keywords: Potential contamination with Escherichia coli O7:K1 and 1303
reason: Potential contamination with Escherichia coli O7:K1 and 1303

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Purity Products has announced a recall of its dietary supplement, MyBladder, due to possible contamination with Escherichia coli strains O7:K1 and 1303. This contamination may pose health risks, particularly for vulnerable groups such as neonates, young children, older adults, and people with weakened immune systems. The recall is a precautionary measure to ensure consumer safety.

The affected product was distributed in the United States via direct consumer delivery, Walmart, and Amazon websites. The supplement is packaged in a white HDPE 150 CC bottle with a white cap, containing 60 clear capsules of brown powder. The specific lot under recall is number 030425176.

Purity Products has attributed the contamination to a temporary change in suppliers.

Product Lot Number Description
MyBladder Dietary Supplement 030425176 White HDPE 150 CC bottle with white cap, 60 clear capsules containing brown powder

There have been no confirmed reports of injuries or adverse events related to this recall at this time.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased lot number 030425176 of the MyBladder dietary supplement should immediately discontinue use and return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund or dispose of it. For further assistance, contact Dwater at (516) 316-9486 or email richard.conant@purityproducts.com.

content.text: Consumer support: Richard Conant | (516) 316-9486 | richard.conant@purityproducts.com

title: Twin Sisters Creamery Recalls Whatcom Blue, Farmhouse, Peppercorn, and Mustard Seed Cheeses Due to STEC Contamination
headline: Twin Sisters Creamery Issues Recall of Whatcom Blue, Farmhouse, Peppercorn, and Mustard Seed Cheeses for Potential E. coli O103 Contamination
description: Twin Sisters Creamery recalls Whatcom Blue, Farmhouse, Peppercorn, and Mustard Seed cheeses due to possible contamination with Shiga toxin-producing E. coli (STEC).
keywords: Whatcom Blue, Farmhouse, Peppercorn and Mustard Seed cheese products
Potential Foodborne Illness - Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli (STEC) and Escherichia coli O103
productDescription: Whatcom Blue, Farmhouse, Peppercorn and Mustard Seed cheese products
reason: Potential Foodborne Illness - Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli (STEC) and Escherichia coli O103

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Twin Sisters Creamery, Inc., based in Ferndale, Washington, is voluntarily recalling several cheese products due to potential contamination with Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli (STEC) and Escherichia coli O103. The recalled cheeses include Whatcom Blue, Farmhouse, Peppercorn, and Mustard Seed varieties made from raw and unpasteurized milk and aged for at least 60 days.

These bacterial contaminants can cause severe health risks, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Infection symptoms include severe abdominal cramps, bloody diarrhea, and vomiting, typically appearing 1 to 10 days after exposure. In some cases, the infection may lead to Hemolytic Uremic Syndrome (HUS), a life-threatening condition causing kidney failure.

The recalled products were shipped between July 27, 2025, and October 22, 2025, to distributors in Oregon and Washington. They may have been further distributed to retail outlets and repackaged or sold as pre-cut half-moon slices with different lot numbers or expiration dates. Three cases of STEC infections linked to these products have been reported so far, including one in Oregon involving the Farmhouse cheese.

Product NameBatch Code
Whatcom Blue250527B
Whatcom Blue250610B
Whatcom Blue250618B
Whatcom Blue250624B
Farmhouse250603F
Farmhouse250616B
Peppercorn250603P
Mustard Seed250616M

This recall is conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and the Washington Department of Agriculture.

facts.product: Whatcom Blue, Farmhouse, Peppercorn and Mustard Seed cheese products

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Do not consume the affected Twin Sisters Creamery cheese products. Dispose of any remaining product safely, thoroughly clean and sanitize all surfaces contacted by the cheese, return products to the place of purchase for a full refund, and contact Twin Sisters Creamery at 360-656-5240, Tuesday through Saturday, 11:00 AM to 5:00 PM Pacific Time, for further assistance.

title: Peterson Company Recalls Twin Sisters Creamery Whatcom Blue and Farmhouse Cheeses Due to E. coli Contamination
headline: Peterson Company Issues Recall of Twin Sisters Creamery Whatcom Blue and Farmhouse Cheese Products for E. coli O103 and STEC Risk
description: Peterson Company recalls Twin Sisters Creamery Whatcom Blue and Farmhouse cheeses due to contamination with Shiga toxin-producing E. coli O103, posing health risks.
keywords: Whatcom Blue and Farmhouse cheese products
Potential Foodborne Illness - Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli (STEC) and Escherichia coli O103
productDescription: Whatcom Blue and Farmhouse cheese products
reason: Potential Foodborne Illness - Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli (STEC) and Escherichia coli O103

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On October 26, 2025, Peterson Company of Auburn, Washington, announced a voluntary recall of Twin Sisters Creamery brand Whatcom Blue and Farmhouse cheese products due to potential contamination with Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli (STEC) and Escherichia coli O103. These cheeses, made from raw milk, pose a risk of serious foodborne illness.

STEC infections can cause severe and sometimes fatal complications, particularly in young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Infection symptoms include severe abdominal cramps, bloody diarrhea, and vomiting, typically appearing 1 to 10 days after exposure. E. coli O103 infection may lead to Hemolytic Uremic Syndrome (HUS), a life-threatening condition that can result in kidney failure.

The affected Whatcom Blue (item #28855) and Farmhouse (item #29608) cheeses were distributed to retailers, distributors, caterers, and restaurants in Colorado, Idaho, Oregon, and Washington between August 14, 2025, and October 24, 2025. These products are approximately 5 to 6 ounces in weight, half-moon shaped, and packaged in clear plastic wrap. They are identified by specific manufacturer codes ink jet printed or attached as small tan-to-orange stickers on each piece.

Three confirmed STEC infections linked to E. coli O103 have been reported in Oregon and Washington, with one Oregon case associated with consumption of the Farmhouse cheese. The recall follows supplier Twin Sisters Creamery’s notification after third-party lab testing and government agency analyses confirmed the presence of these bacteria in the affected cheeses.

Whatcom Blue 28855 781511
Whatcom Blue 28855 775511
Whatcom Blue 28855 761511
Farmhouse 29608 765511
Farmhouse 29608 752511

Farmhouse 29608 726511

title: E.A. Sween Company Announces Product Recall Due to Choking Hazard
headline: E.A. Sween Company Announces Product Recall Due to Choking Hazard
description: Deli Express announced a recall involving BBQ Pulled Pork Sandwich after issues linked to due to the potential presence of foreign particles (plastic).. FDA...
keywords: Due to the potential presence of foreign particles (plastic).
reason: Due to the potential presence of foreign particles (plastic).

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
E.A. Sween Company has announced a voluntary recall of its Deli Express® BBQ Pulled Pork sandwich due to the potential presence of plastic foreign particles in the product. These particles could pose a choking hazard if ingested. The affected products were distributed to convenience stores, grocery stores, and mass retail outlets across the Continental U.S., Hawaii, and Guam.

The recall applies to specific lot codes of the Deli Express® BBQ Pulled Pork sandwich, with a UPC of 0-41433-13087-2. The products were available for purchase between January 16 and October 23, 2025. Although there have been no reported injuries or illnesses related to this issue, consumers are strongly advised not to eat the product if they have purchased any from the affected lots.

Retailers have been directed to remove the recalled products from shelves and inventory immediately. Consumers who have the affected product may return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further questions or if the product has been consumed, E.A. Sween Company encourages customers to contact their support line during business hours.

en.title: E.A. Sween Company Announces Product Recall Due to Choking Hazard

en.reason: Due to the potential presence of foreign particles (plastic).
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
E.A. Sween Company has announced a voluntary recall of its Deli Express® BBQ Pulled Pork sandwich due to the potential presence of plastic foreign particles in the product. These particles could pose a choking hazard if ingested. The affected products were distributed to convenience stores, grocery stores, and mass retail outlets across the Continental U.S., Hawaii, and Guam.

The recall applies to specific lot codes of the Deli Express® BBQ Pulled Pork sandwich, with a UPC of 0-41433-13087-2. The products were available for purchase between January 16 and October 23, 2025. Although there have been no reported injuries or illnesses related to this issue, consumers are strongly advised not to eat the product if they have purchased any from the affected lots.

Retailers have been directed to remove the recalled products from shelves and inventory immediately. Consumers who have the affected product may return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further questions or if the product has been consumed, E.A. Sween Company encourages customers to contact their support line during business hours.

title: Haitai, Inc. Recalls Haetae (HT) Brand 8 oz Cinnamon Powder for Potential Lead Contamination
headline: Haitai, Inc. Issues Recall of Haetae (HT) Brand 8 oz Cinnamon Powder Due to Elevated Lead Levels
description: Haitai recalls Haetae (HT) 8 oz cinnamon powder nationwide due to potential lead contamination; no injuries reported. Recall published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Haitai, Inc. of Cerritos, CA, announced a recall on October 15, 2025, for the Haetae (HT) brand Cinnamon powder 8 oz due to potential contamination with lead. Although short-term exposure to low levels of lead may not produce immediate symptoms, increased blood lead levels can be the sole indicator of exposure. High lead intake can cause severe symptoms, prompting medical attention.

Lead exposure poses significant health risks, especially to children, where prolonged exposure may lead to permanent central nervous system damage, including learning disorders and developmental defects. Adults experiencing acute lead poisoning may suffer from abdominal pain, muscle weakness, nausea, and other symptoms. Chronic exposure is linked to kidney problems, hypertension, and neurocognitive effects.

The affected product was distributed nationwide through various supermarkets. It is packaged in a square plastic bottle with a medium brown color and orange-red undertones. The recall covers the product with the UPC 0 20914 81415 9 and a Best By date of 02/09/25. No injuries or illnesses related to the contamination have been reported so far.

The recall was initiated after the FDA detected elevated lead levels in product samples. The source of contamination may be adulterated raw materials or the natural presence of lead in cinnamon.

BrandProductPackage SizeUPCBest By Date
Haetae (HT)Cinnamon Powder8 oz0 20914 81415 902/09/25

Consumers with questions can contact Haitai, Inc. at 1-323-890-0101 from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM Pacific time, Monday through Friday.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have Haetae (HT) brand Cinnamon Powder 8 oz with UPC 0 20914 81415 9 and Best By date 02/09/25, and return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Haitai, Inc. at 1-323-890-0101 between 8:00 AM and 5:00 PM Pacific time, Monday through Friday.

title: Nat’s Nuts Recalls Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans Due to Undeclared Cashews Allergy Risk
headline: Nat’s Nuts Recalls Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans for Undeclared Cashews Posing Allergy Hazard
description: Nat’s Nuts recalls Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans for undeclared cashews, risking serious allergic reactions. Distributed in 2025 across multiple US states and online.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Nat’s Nuts, a Rochester, NY-based company, has issued a recall for their Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans due to the potential presence of undeclared cashews. This recall is critical for individuals allergic or highly sensitive to cashews, as consumption may cause serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

Approximately 600 bags of Nat’s Nuts brand Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans, 4 oz., are involved. These were distributed to 25 retail stores across Connecticut, New York, New Jersey, New Hampshire, Pennsylvania, Ohio, Nevada, Florida, and Wisconsin between October 8 and October 16, 2025. The product was also sold online via Amazon and NatsNuts.com from October 9 to October 16, 2025.

The presence of cashews was initially identified after a retailer discovered cashews in a Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans package during sampling. Investigations attributed the issue to a temporary failure in the company’s production and packaging process. No illnesses have been reported to date related to this recall.

Product Size UPC Lot Code Best By Date
Nat’s Nuts Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans 4 oz. 860002146720 1.22 9/29/2026

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans packaging for the lot code 1.22 and best by date 9/29/2026. If affected, do not consume the product and return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Nat’s Nuts at 585-310-0157, Monday through Friday, 8 AM to 5 PM ET.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (585) 310-0157

title: Jody’s Inc. Recalls Cabot Creamery Sea Salt Caramel Cheddar Popcorn for Undeclared Peanuts
headline: Jody’s Inc. Voluntary Recall of Cabot Creamery Sea Salt Caramel Cheddar Popcorn Due to Undeclared Peanuts
description: Jody’s Inc. recalls Cabot Creamery Sea Salt Caramel Cheddar Popcorn over undeclared peanuts, risking serious allergic reactions. Check affected lots and take action.
keywords: Due to presence of undeclared peanuts
reason: Due to presence of undeclared peanuts

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Jody’s Inc. has issued a voluntary recall of Cabot Creamery Sea Salt Caramel Cheddar Popcorn 6 oz (170 g) bags due to the presence of undeclared peanuts. This poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals allergic or highly sensitive to peanuts.

The affected product, distributed beginning July 23, 2025, was shipped to distribution centers in multiple states, including California, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Massachusetts, Maryland, North Carolina, Oregon, and Texas, before reaching retail locations nationwide. The product is packaged in foil printed bags and sold in six-pack boxes.

Jody’s Inc. initiated the recall following two consumer complaints about the presence of peanuts in the product. As of this announcement, no illnesses have been reported related to this issue.

Product UPC Lot Number Best If Enjoyed By
Cabot Creamery Sea Salt Caramel Cheddar Popcorn 6 oz (170 g) bag 8 50016 94430 6 2519907B1 July 15, 2026

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with peanut allergies should not consume this product. Check your packaging for the lot number 2519907B1 and UPC 8 50016 94430 6. Return the recalled popcorn to the place of purchase for a full refund or discard it. For questions, contact Jody M. Wagner at 757-422-8646 ext. 103 between 9:00 AM and 4:30 PM ET.

title: Kenz Henz Recalls Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs Due to Salmonella Risk
headline: Kenz Henz Recalls Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs Because of Potential Salmonella Contamination
description: Kenz Henz recalls Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs due to possible Salmonella contamination, posing a health risk especially to vulnerable groups.
keywords: Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs
Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella
productDescription: Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Kenz Henz, based in Santa Fe, TX, has issued a recall of its 12 count packages of "Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs" due to the potential presence of Salmonella contamination. Salmonella is a harmful bacterium that can cause severe infections, particularly in young children, older adults, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of Salmonella infection in healthy individuals typically include fever, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

The recalled eggs were sold in retail stores in Houston, TX. Although no illnesses have been reported linked to this recall, Salmonella infections can occasionally lead to more serious conditions such as bloodstream infections, arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

Recent FDA inspections of the Black Sheep Egg Company’s processing facility found multiple environmental samples positive for various strains of Salmonella, some of which are known to cause human illness. However, the FDA has not identified this facility as the source of any ongoing outbreak at this time.

Product Description UPC Code Julian Date Range Best By Date Range Package Size
Kenz Henz Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs 86949400030 241 to 244 and 246 to 247 10/11 to 10/14 and 10/16 to 10/17 12 count carton

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should return recalled Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs in 12 count cartons with affected date and UPC codes to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Kenz Henz at 1-(409) 457-5934, Monday through Friday, 9:00 am to 4:00 pm.

title: AquaStar Corp Recalls Frozen Raw Shrimp Due to Possible Cesium-137 Exposure
headline: AquaStar Corp Initiates Voluntary Recall of Frozen Raw Shrimp Over Potential Cesium-137 Contamination
description: AquaStar Corp recalls frozen raw shrimp imported from Indonesia due to possible exposure to very low levels of cesium-137. No illnesses reported.
keywords: AquaStar, Best Yet, Waterfront Bistro, Publix
Product may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions whereby they may have become exposed to very low levels of cesium-137 (Cs-137)
brandName: AquaStar, Best Yet, Waterfront Bistro, Publix
brandNames: AquaStar, Best Yet, Waterfront Bistro, Publix
reason: Product may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions whereby they may have become exposed to very low levels of cesium-137 (Cs-137)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On October 17, 2025, AquaStar (USA) Corp, based in Seattle, WA, announced a voluntary recall of a limited quantity of frozen raw shrimp imported from Indonesia. The recall was initiated because the products may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions where they could have been exposed to very low levels of cesium-137 (Cs-137), a man-made radioisotope of cesium.

It is important to note that none of the recalled products have tested positive for Cs-137, and no illnesses have been reported to date. The recall is a precautionary measure to ensure consumer safety and is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

Cs-137 can be found in the environment at background levels and may be higher in certain areas with environmental contamination, but the risk is considered very low in this case.

facts.brand: AquaStar, Best Yet, Waterfront Bistro, Publix

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their frozen raw shrimp bearing AquaStar, Best Yet, Waterfront Bistro, or Publix brands are part of the recall and return affected products to the place of purchase for a full refund or dispose of them safely. For questions, contact AquaStar Corp at 1-800-331-3440.

en.reason: Product may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions whereby they may have become exposed to very low levels of cesium-137 (Cs-137)
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Shellfish Foodborne Illness, Food & Beverages, Shellfish, Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On October 17, 2025, AquaStar (USA) Corp, based in Seattle, WA, announced a voluntary recall of a limited quantity of frozen raw shrimp imported from Indonesia. The recall was initiated because the products may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions where they could have been exposed to very low levels of cesium-137 (Cs-137), a man-made radioisotope of cesium.

It is important to note that none of the recalled products have tested positive for Cs-137, and no illnesses have been reported to date. The recall is a precautionary measure to ensure consumer safety and is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

Cs-137 can be found in the environment at background levels and may be higher in certain areas with environmental contamination, but the risk is considered very low in this case.

facts.brand: AquaStar, Best Yet, Waterfront Bistro, Publix

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their frozen raw shrimp bearing AquaStar, Best Yet, Waterfront Bistro, or Publix brands are part of the recall and return affected products to the place of purchase for a full refund or dispose of them safely. For questions, contact AquaStar Corp at 1-800-331-3440.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: AquaStar | 1-800-331-3440

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on October 17, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.
Visit the company website for product details.

whatToDo: Do not consume the recalled frozen raw shrimp.
Check your freezer for any products from AquaStar Corp matching the description.
Follow any additional guidance from health authorities.
eeatMeta.affectedPopulation: Consumers who have purchased or may consume the recalled frozen raw shrimp.
eeatMeta.expertSummary: AquaStar Corp is recalling frozen raw shrimp due to possible exposure to very low levels of cesium-137, a radioactive substance, which could pose a health risk if consumed.
eeatMeta.riskExplanation: The shrimp may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions that led to contamination by cesium-137, which can pose a risk of radiation exposure. The exact risk level and injury likelihood are not specified.
eeatMeta.realWorldContext: Food products contaminated with radioactive materials are a serious concern as they can lead to radiation exposure, which may cause health problems over time. Consumers should be vigilant about recalls involving radioactive contamination.

title: Ben’s Original Voluntary Recall of Select Ready Rice Products Due to Potential Small Stones Contamination
headline: Ben’s Original Recalls Select Long Grain White, Whole Grain Brown, and Long Grain & Wild Ready Rice for Possible Small Stones Contamination
description: Ben’s Original recalls select lots of Long Grain White, Whole Grain Brown, and Long Grain & Wild Ready Rice due to possible small stones contaminant risk.
keywords: Long Grain White, Whole Grain Brown, and Long Grain & Wild Ready Rice
Potential Foreign Body Contaminant – Small Stones
productDescription: Long Grain White, Whole Grain Brown, and Long Grain & Wild Ready Rice
reason: Potential Foreign Body Contaminant – Small Stones

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ben’s Original has announced a voluntary recall of select Ready Rice products in the United States due to the possible presence of small stones naturally occurring from the rice farm. The affected products include certain lots of Long Grain White, Whole Grain Brown, and Long Grain & Wild Ready Rice. These small stones could pose a risk of oral or digestive tract injury if consumed.

The recall applies only to specific batch codes and best by dates, which are printed on the bottom seal of the back of the package. While the distribution involved multiple retailers through distributors Associated Grocers, C&S, and Dot Foods during August through September, this issue is isolated to the batches listed below. No other Ben’s Original products are affected by this recall.

To date, there have been no reported injuries or illnesses related to the recalled products.

Product Batch Code Best By Date UPC
Long Grain White Ready Rice 39280A August 2024 000-28000-02792
Whole Grain Brown Ready Rice 39281A August 2024 000-28000-02797
Long Grain & Wild Ready Rice 39290A September 2024 000-28000-02800

facts.product: Long Grain White, Whole Grain Brown, and Long Grain & Wild Ready Rice

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their packages for the affected batch codes and best by dates listed and stop using those products. For return instructions or questions, contact Ben’s Original Consumer Care at 1-800-548-6253, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. CST, and weekends from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. CST.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 1-800-548-6253

content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on October 14, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

title: Foodynamics Recalls Freeze-Dried Pet Treats Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination
headline: Foodynamics Recalls Specific Lots of Raw Dog Barkery, BellePepper Cats, and Kanu Pets Freeze-Dried Pet Treats for Salmonella Risk
description: Foodynamics recalls freeze-dried pet treats over possible Salmonella contamination, posing health risks to pets and humans. No illnesses reported.
keywords: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Foodynamics of Wales, Wisconsin, has issued a voluntary recall of specific lots of freeze-dried pet treats under the Raw Dog Barkery, BellePepper Cats, Kanu Pets, and What’s In the Bowl brands due to potential contamination with Salmonella. This contamination poses a health risk to both pets consuming the products and humans handling them, especially if proper hand hygiene is not maintained after contact.

Salmonella infection in healthy individuals can cause symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, diarrhea (which may be bloody), abdominal cramps, and fever. More severe complications are rare but can include arterial infections, endocarditis, arthritis, muscle pain, eye irritation, and urinary tract issues. Pets infected with Salmonella may exhibit lethargy, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Infected pets can also be asymptomatic carriers, posing a risk of transmitting the bacteria to other animals and humans. Anyone experiencing symptoms following contact with these products should consult a healthcare professional, and pet owners should contact their veterinarian if their pets show symptoms after consuming the recalled treats.

The recalled products were shipped via UPS from Wisconsin to retailers in Wisconsin, New York, and Florida. A total of eight packages distributed to consumers have been identified. Foodynamics has traced all product distribution, informed all direct customers, and is cooperating with the FDA in the ongoing investigation. No illnesses have been reported to date. Production and distribution of the products have been suspended as a precaution.

Brand Product Description Net Weight Use By Date / Lot #
Raw Dog Barkery Whole Chicken Hearts 16 oz. (1 lb.) 030527
What’s In the Bowl Whole Chicken Hearts 3 oz., 16 oz. (1 lb.) 030527
BellePepper Cats Freeze Dried Chicken Heart Slices 3 oz. 031627
BellePepper Cats Freeze Dried Sample Treats (Unpackaged & Unlabeled) 0.1 oz. 121426, 011526
Kanu Pets Freeze Dried Chicken Heart Raw Treats 3 oz. 031627

facts.productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should stop feeding these products to pets immediately, return affected items to the place of purchase for a full refund, dispose of any recalled products safely, and thoroughly wash and sanitize any bowls, utensils, and surfaces that contacted the treats. For questions, contact Foodynamics at info@foodynamics.com or call (262) 421-5339, Monday through Friday from 9 AM to 5 PM CST.

content.text: Consumer support: Foodynamics | (262) 421-5339 | info@foodynamics.com

title: Tai Foong USA Recalls Fusia Asian Inspirations Veggie Spring Rolls Due to Undeclared Shrimp Allergen
headline: Tai Foong USA Recalls Fusia Asian Inspirations Veggie Spring Rolls for Undeclared Shrimp Allergen Risk
description: Tai Foong USA recalls Fusia Asian Inspirations Veggie Spring Rolls distributed at ALDI for undeclared shrimp allergen posing serious allergic reaction risk.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tai Foong USA of Seattle, Washington, has announced a recall of a limited quantity of Fusia Asian Inspirations Veggie Spring Rolls, net weight 10 oz. The product may contain undeclared shrimp, a known allergen, which poses a risk of a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction to individuals with shrimp or shellfish allergies.

The affected Veggie Spring Rolls were distributed exclusively to ALDI stores nationwide under the Fusia Asian Inspirations brand. The recall involves only the 10-ounce retail package with UPC 4099100222258 and the Best Before date of 05/17/2027 marked on the back panel. No other products from the Fusia Asian Inspirations line are included in this recall.

This action was initiated after it was discovered that certain cases of Shrimp Spring Rolls may have been mistakenly packaged in boxes labeled as Vegetable Spring Rolls. Tai Foong USA is investigating the source of this labeling error. The recall is being conducted in cooperation with ALDI and the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. To date, there have been no confirmed reports of allergic reactions or illnesses related to this issue.

Brand Product Net Weight UPC Best Before Date Retailer
Fusia Asian Inspirations Veggie Spring Rolls 10 oz (283.5 g) 4099100222258 05/17/2027 ALDI (nationwide)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the 10 oz Fusia Asian Inspirations Veggie Spring Rolls packaging for UPC 4099100222258 and Best Before date 05/17/2027. If you have a shrimp or shellfish allergy, do not consume the product. Discard it or return it to ALDI stores for a full refund. For questions, contact Tai Foong USA at (206) 883-2317, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. Pacific Time, or email bcox@northernchef.com.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: TAI FOONG USA | (206) 883-2317 | bcox@northernchef.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on October 08, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.
Visit the company website for product details.

disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.

title: Durra Ground Cinnamon 100 g Recalled Due to Potential Lead Contamination
headline: Durra Ground Cinnamon 100 g Recalled for Possible Lead Contamination Risk
description: Durra Ground Cinnamon 100 g recalled for potential lead contamination. Distributed in CA and MI between Aug 2024 and Oct 2025. No illnesses reported.
keywords: Food & Beverages Spices, Flavors & Salts Contaminants
productType: Food & Beverages Spices, Flavors & Salts Contaminants
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Spices, Flavors & Salts Contaminants

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Eureka Inc of Pomona, CA, announced a recall on October 6, 2025, for Durra Ground Cinnamon 100 gram plastic containers due to potential contamination with lead. Lead exposure can have serious health implications, particularly for children and individuals with prolonged or high-level exposure.

Short-term exposure to low levels of lead may not show symptoms, but increased blood lead levels can be an indicator. High levels of lead exposure can cause severe symptoms such as abdominal pain, muscle weakness, nausea, vomiting, and neurological effects. Chronic exposure may lead to kidney dysfunction, hypertension, and permanent damage to the central nervous system, especially in children. No illnesses have been reported to date in connection with this product.

The affected product was distributed to grocery stores in California and Michigan between August 24, 2024, and October 6, 2025. The recall follows the detection of elevated lead levels during FDA testing of the product.

Brand Product Package Size Lot Code Best By Date UPC Distribution Area Distribution Dates
Durra Ground Cinnamon 100 g Clear Plastic Container Batch No.: 06 B:02 May 2026 6251136 034139 California, Michigan 08/24/2024 – 10/6/2025

facts.productType: Food & Beverages Spices, Flavors & Salts Contaminants

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should verify if their Durra Ground Cinnamon 100 g container matches the lot code Batch No.: 06 B:02 and best by date May 2026 distributed in California or Michigan. If affected, discontinue use and return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. Consumers who have consumed the product should consult a healthcare provider about lead exposure testing. For additional information, contact Eureka Inc at 1-951-444-7779, Monday through Friday, 10 am to 5 pm PST.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 1-951-444-7779

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on October 07, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

title: Trividia Health Voluntary Recall of TRUE METRIX® Blood Glucose Meters Due to Defective LCD Displays
headline: Trividia Health, Inc. Announces Recall of Limited TRUE METRIX® Blood Glucose Meters with Defective LCD Displays Impacting Performance
description: Trividia Health recalls 601 TRUE METRIX® blood glucose meters with defective LCD displays that may cause misinterpretation of blood glucose results.
keywords: Potential for defective LCD displays that can affect product performance
reason: Potential for defective LCD displays that can affect product performance

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Trividia Health, Inc. has announced a voluntary recall of a limited number of TRUE METRIX® self-monitoring blood glucose meters co-branded under the Good Neighbor Pharmacy® brand and distributed in the United States. The recall applies to 601 meters from lot number KD0746, manufactured on September 4, 2025, and distributed between September 8 and September 16, 2025. These units may have defective LCD displays that could affect product performance.

The issue with the LCD display may cause partial or missing numerical segments or characters, or ghosting (fading) of these characters. This defect could potentially lead users to misinterpret test results or experience delays in obtaining results. For individuals with low blood glucose (hypoglycemia), such delays could impact timely treatment or therapy decisions. Trividia Health reports no injuries related to the recall at this time.

Trividia Health is notifying customers, pharmacies, distributors, and mail order companies that sell these meters in the U.S. Consumers may continue using TRUE METRIX® blood glucose meters from other lots not included in this recall. This action is being taken in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration to ensure user safety.

ProductLot NumberManufacture DateDistribution DatesQuantity

en.regulatedProducts: Medical Devices

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Trividia Health, Inc. has announced a voluntary recall of a limited number of TRUE METRIX® self-monitoring blood glucose meters co-branded under the Good Neighbor Pharmacy® brand and distributed in the United States. The recall applies to 601 meters from lot number KD0746, manufactured on September 4, 2025, and distributed between September 8 and September 16, 2025. These units may have defective LCD displays that could affect product performance.

The issue with the LCD display may cause partial or missing numerical segments or characters, or ghosting (fading) of these characters. This defect could potentially lead users to misinterpret test results or experience delays in obtaining results. For individuals with low blood glucose (hypoglycemia), such delays could impact timely treatment or therapy decisions. Trividia Health reports no injuries related to the recall at this time.

Trividia Health is notifying customers, pharmacies, distributors, and mail order companies that sell these meters in the U.S. Consumers may continue using TRUE METRIX® blood glucose meters from other lots not included in this recall. This action is being taken in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration to ensure user safety.

title: Giant Eagle Recalls Smoked Mozzarella Pasta Salad Due to Potential Listeria Contamination
headline: Giant Eagle Issues Voluntary Recall of Smoked Mozzarella Pasta Salad Over Possible Listeria monocytogenes Presence
description: Giant Eagle recalls Smoked Mozzarella Pasta Salad sold in select states for potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination linked to Nate’s Fine Foods recall.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Giant Eagle, Inc. has announced a voluntary recall of its Smoked Mozzarella Pasta Salad sold in the prepared foods departments at Giant Eagle and Market District stores located in western Pennsylvania, Ohio, Maryland, and Indiana. The recall is due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes and follows a nationwide recall of ready-to-eat pasta products from Nate’s Fine Foods.

Listeria monocytogenes can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. While healthy individuals may experience only mild and short-term symptoms such as fever, headache, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea, pregnant women face additional risks including miscarriage and stillbirth. To date, there have been no reported illnesses linked to the Giant Eagle recall.

The recalled Smoked Mozzarella Pasta Salad was sold beginning September 25, 2025.

Product Name Weight Expiration Date UPC
Smoked Mozzarella Penne Salad Varies 9/30/25 through 10/7/25 256616000000
Smoked Mozzarella Penne Salad - Small 4 lbs 8 oz 9/30/25 through 10/3/25 246511000000
Smoked Mozzarella Penne Salad - Large 9 lbs 9/30/25 through 10/3/25 246512000000

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should not eat the recalled Smoked Mozzarella Pasta Salad. Check product details and either safely dispose of the salad or return it to any Giant Eagle or Market District store with a qualifying receipt for a full refund. For questions, contact Giant Eagle Customer Care at 1-800-553-2324, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. EST.

title: Best Buy Bones, Inc. Recalls Nature’s Own Pet Chews Bully Bites Due to Salmonella Contamination Risk
headline: Best Buy Bones, Inc. Issues Recall of Nature’s Own Pet Chews Bully Bites 1 Pound Treats for Potential Salmonella Contamination
description: Best Buy Bones, Inc. recalls Nature’s Own Pet Chews Bully Bites 1 Pound treats due to Salmonella contamination risk affecting pets and humans.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Best Buy Bones, Inc., based in Mount Morris, Michigan, has initiated a recall of four lots of Nature’s Own Pet Chews Bully Bites 1 Pound pet treats due to potential contamination with Salmonella. The affected product comes in 16 oz. resealable bags with an expiration date of September 2027. Salmonella poses a health risk to both animals consuming the treats and humans handling them, particularly if proper handwashing is not observed after contact.

Salmonella infection in healthy individuals can cause symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, abdominal cramps, and fever. In rare cases, more severe illness may occur, including arterial infections, endocarditis, arthritis, muscle pain, eye irritation, and urinary tract symptoms. Those experiencing symptoms after handling the product should seek medical advice promptly.

Pets affected by Salmonella may show lethargy, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Even asymptomatic pets can carry and transmit the bacteria to other animals or humans. Veterinarians should be contacted if pets develop these symptoms after consuming the product.

The recall involves 34 bags distributed primarily in Minnesota and sold through retail locations in Wisconsin after September 23, 2025. The investigation began after FDA testing of product samples on September 11, 2025, confirmed the presence of Salmonella. The company has halted production and distribution of the affected lots while the FDA and Best Buy Bones continue their investigation.

Product Lot Number Expiration Date UPC Package Size
Nature’s Own Pet Chews Bully Bites 19379, 19380, 19381, 19382 September 2027 739598900750 16 oz. resealable pouch

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check packaging for the affected lot numbers 19379, 19380, 19381, and 19382 with September 2027 expiration. If purchased, discontinue use and return the product to the place of purchase or dispose of it safely. Contact Best Buy Bones, Inc. at (810) 687-2106 for further assistance.

title: Demers Food Group Recalls Select Scott & Jon's Shrimp Scampi with Linguini Bowls for Potential Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
headline: Demers Food Group Recalls Select Scott & Jon's Shrimp Scampi with Linguini Bowls Following Supplier Recall for Listeria monocytogenes Risk
description: Demers Food Group recalls select Scott & Jon's Shrimp Scampi with Linguini bowls due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination from a supplier ingredient.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Demers Food Group has voluntarily recalled select Scott & Jon's Shrimp Scampi with Linguini bowls due to a potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This action follows a recall initiated by Nate’s Fine Foods, the supplier of the linguini pasta used in the product, which was found to be contaminated. The recall applies only to specific Scott & Jon's Shrimp Scampi with Linguini products and does not affect other products under the Scott & Jon's brand.

No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall. The product was distributed nationwide to wholesale distributors and operators between September 15, 2025, and September 25, 2025.

Listeria monocytogenes can survive refrigerated temperatures and may spread to other foods and surfaces. The FDA recommends thorough cleaning and sanitizing of any surfaces and containers that might have come into contact with the recalled products to prevent cross-contamination. Listeria infection can cause serious health issues, especially for young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at risk for miscarriage or stillbirth.

title: UPDATE - Albertsons Companies Voluntarily Recalls Select Store-Made Deli Items Containing Bowtie Pasta Supplied by Fresh Creative Foods Due to an Ingredient Recall for Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
headline: UPDATE - Albertsons Companies Voluntarily Recalls Select Store-Made Deli Items Containing Bowtie Pasta Supplied by Fresh Creative Foods Due to an Ingredient Recall for Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
description: Multiple brand names announced a recall involving Bowtie Pasta Salads and Meals after issues linked to possible listeria monocytogenes contamination. FDA...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Albertsons Companies has voluntarily recalled certain store-made deli items containing bowtie pasta supplied by Fresh Creative Foods, following a recall issued by Fresh Creative Foods, a division of Reser’s Fine Foods, Inc. The recall affects products made with a bowtie pasta ingredient produced by Nate’s Fine Foods in Roseville, California, due to possible contamination with Listeria monocytogenes.

Listeria monocytogenes is a harmful bacterium that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of listeriosis include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria risk miscarriages and stillbirths. No illnesses or injuries have been reported so far related to the recalled products.

Consumers who have purchased any of the affected deli items are urged not to consume them. Instead, they should either dispose of the products or return them to the store where purchased for a full refund. The FDA advises extra care in thoroughly cleaning and sanitizing any surfaces or containers that may have come into contact with the recalled products to prevent cross-contamination, as Listeria can survive in refrigerated conditions and spread easily.

The affected deli items were available at a range of stores including Albertsons, Safeway, Vons, Pavilions, Andronico's Community Markets, and others across many states including Alaska, Arizona, California, Texas, and more. For questions or concerns, customers can contact Albertsons Companies’ Customer Service Center at 1-877-723-3929, Monday through Friday, from 5 a.m. to 9 p.m. PST.

en.description: Multiple brand names announced a recall involving Bowtie Pasta Salads and Meals after issues linked to possible listeria monocytogenes contamination. FDA...

en.regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness, Food & Beverages, Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Albertsons Companies has voluntarily recalled certain store-made deli items containing bowtie pasta supplied by Fresh Creative Foods, following a recall issued by Fresh Creative Foods, a division of Reser’s Fine Foods, Inc. The recall affects products made with a bowtie pasta ingredient produced by Nate’s Fine Foods in Roseville, California, due to possible contamination with Listeria monocytogenes.

Listeria monocytogenes is a harmful bacterium that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of listeriosis include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria risk miscarriages and stillbirths. No illnesses or injuries have been reported so far related to the recalled products.

Consumers who have purchased any of the affected deli items are urged not to consume them. Instead, they should either dispose of the products or return them to the store where purchased for a full refund. The FDA advises extra care in thoroughly cleaning and sanitizing any surfaces or containers that may have come into contact with the recalled products to prevent cross-contamination, as Listeria can survive in refrigerated conditions and spread easily.

The affected deli items were available at a range of stores including Albertsons, Safeway, Vons, Pavilions, Andronico's Community Markets, and others across many states including Alaska, Arizona, California, Texas, and more. For questions or concerns, customers can contact Albertsons Companies’ Customer Service Center at 1-877-723-3929, Monday through Friday, from 5 a.m. to 9 p.m. PST.

title: Sea Port Products Corp Recalls Raw Frozen Easy Peel White Shrimp Jumbo 16/20 Due to Potential Cesium-137 Contamination
headline: Sea Port Products Corp Recalls Raw Frozen Easy Peel White Shrimp Jumbo 16/20 for Possible Cesium-137 (Cs-137) Contamination
description: Sea Port Products Corp recalls Raw Frozen Easy Peel White Shrimp Jumbo 16/20 over potential contamination with Cesium-137, distributed in select US states in 2025.
keywords: Raw Frozen Easy Peel White Shrimp Jumbo size 16/20
Product May Have Become Contaminated with Cesium-137 (Cs-137)
productDescription: Raw Frozen Easy Peel White Shrimp Jumbo size 16/20
reason: Product May Have Become Contaminated with Cesium-137 (Cs-137)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sea Port Products Corp of Kirkland, WA is voluntarily recalling certain Sea Port brand Frozen Raw Easy Peel White Shrimp Jumbo size 16/20 count per pound. The recall involves limited quantities of bagged shrimp that may have been exposed to insanitary conditions, potentially leading to contamination with Cesium-137 (Cs-137), a man-made radioactive isotope.

Cesium-137 can be found in the environment at low background levels but may be present at elevated levels in contaminated food or water. Prolonged exposure to Cs-137 through intake of contaminated food poses a health risk, primarily by increasing the risk of cancer due to DNA damage within living cells.

The affected shrimp products were distributed in small amounts to retailers located in Alaska, California, Hawaii, Montana, Oregon, Washington State, and American Samoa between July 10, 2025, and September 22, 2025. Consumers are advised to check their freezers for the recalled Sea Port brand shrimp using the specified lot codes printed on the UPC barcode, located on the back of the bag.

No illnesses related to this contamination have been reported to date. The FDA is investigating Cs-137 contamination reports linked to frozen shrimp products from PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods) in Indonesia. The FDA notes that no products testing positive for Cs-137 have entered U.S. commerce.

For questions, contact Sea Port Products Corp at 425-896-0186, Monday through Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. PST.

title: New Age International Recalls Signature Enoki Mushrooms for Potential Listeria Contamination
headline: New Age International Recalls Signature Enoki Mushrooms Due to Potential Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
description: New Age International recalls Signature Enoki Mushrooms distributed in NYC due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. No illnesses reported.
keywords: Product may be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes
reason: Product may be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
New Age International Inc., located in Brooklyn, NY, has issued a recall for its 200g (7.05 OZ) packaged Signature Enoki Mushrooms due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and potentially fatal infections, especially in vulnerable groups such as young children, the elderly, pregnant women, and individuals with weakened immune systems.

The affected product, distributed to retail stores and distributors in New York City, is packaged in a clear 200g plastic container and is labeled with the code GGN: 4063061794758 on the back. While no illnesses have been reported so far, the contamination was identified following routine testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets Food Inspectors and confirmed by laboratory analysis.

The company confirms that no products from this batch remain in their warehouse.

Product Package Size Lot Code / UPC Distribution Area
Signature Enoki Mushrooms 200g (7.05 OZ) clear plastic package GGN: 4063061794758 New York City

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Signature Enoki Mushrooms from August to September 2025 with lot code GGN: 4063061794758 should not consume the product and must either destroy it or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact New Age International Inc. at 718-808-1018.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 718-808-1018

title: Albertsons Companies Voluntarily Recalls Select Store-Made Deli Items Containing Bowtie Pasta Supplied by Fresh Creative Foods Due to an Ingredient Recall for Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
headline: Albertsons Companies Voluntarily Recalls Select Store-Made Deli Items Containing Bowtie Pasta Supplied by Fresh Creative Foods Due to an Ingredient Recall for Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
description: Albertsons Companies announced a recall involving Bowtie Pasta Salads and Meals after issues linked to possible listeria monocytogenes contamination. FDA...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Albertsons Companies has issued a voluntary recall of select store-made deli items containing bowtie pasta supplied by Fresh Creative Foods, a division of Reser’s Fine Foods, Inc. This recall follows Fresh Creative Foods’ recall of an ingredient due to possible contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. The affected bowtie pasta ingredient was manufactured by Nate’s Fine Foods of Roseville, California.

Listeria monocytogenes can cause severe infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms in healthy people may include fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. For pregnant women, infection can lead to miscarriage or stillbirth. There have been no reported illnesses linked to these recalled products to date.

Consumers who have purchased these deli items are advised not to consume them. They should either dispose of the products or return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) also recommends thorough cleaning and sanitizing of any surfaces or containers that may have come into contact with the recalled products to prevent cross-contamination, as Listeria can survive under refrigerated conditions.

The recalled products were available at Albertsons Companies stores, including Albertsons, Safeway, Vons, Randalls, Tom Thumb, Pavilions, Carrs-Safeway, and other banners, across multiple states including Alaska, Arizona, Arkansas, California, Colorado, Hawaii, Louisiana, Nebraska, Nevada, New Mexico, Oklahoma, South Dakota, Texas, Utah, and Wyoming. For questions, consumers can contact Albertsons Companies’ Customer Service Center at 1-877-723-3929 from 5 a.m. to 9 p.m. PST, Monday through Friday.

title: Wholesale Produce Supply Recalls Fresh Cut Cantaloupe Due to Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination Risk
headline: Wholesale Produce Supply Recalls Fresh Cut and Processed Cantaloupe Over Potential Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination
description: Wholesale Produce Supply recalls fresh cut cantaloupe sold under Harvest Cuts and Fresh and Finest brands due to potential Listeria contamination.
keywords: Food & Beverages Fruit/Fruit Product Foodborne Illness
Potential to be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes
productType: Food & Beverages Fruit/Fruit Product Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Fruit/Fruit Product Foodborne Illness
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Wholesale Produce Supply, LLC. of Minneapolis, Minnesota, is recalling fresh cut and processed cantaloupe due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria may face miscarriage or stillbirth risks.

The affected fresh cut cantaloupe products were sold under the “Harvest Cuts” and “Fresh and Finest” brands. These items were distributed to locations in Nebraska, North Dakota, and Wisconsin, and may have reached other states through grocery distributors. Products were packaged in traditional plastic clamshell containers and included both cantaloupe-only and mixed fruit containers.

This recall follows a routine sampling program by Wholesale Produce Supply that detected the presence of Listeria monocytogenes in the finished products. To address the issue, the company has suspended production and distribution of the identified lots while investigating the source of contamination. No illnesses have been reported related to these products at this time.

Brand Product Description Distribution States Packaging
Harvest Cuts Fresh Cut Cantaloupe (cantaloupe-only and mixed fruit containers) Nebraska, North Dakota, Wisconsin (and possibly other states) Plastic clamshell containers
Fresh and Finest Fresh Cut Cantaloupe (cantaloupe-only and mixed fruit containers) Nebraska, North Dakota, Wisconsin (and possibly other states) Plastic clamshell containers

facts.productType: Food & Beverages Fruit/Fruit Product Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have purchased fresh cut cantaloupe under the "Harvest Cuts" or "Fresh and Finest" brands distributed in Nebraska, North Dakota, or Wisconsin, and return affected products to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional information, contact Wholesale Produce Supply, LLC at (612) 378-2025 or email SFOSTER@WPSFRESH.COM.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Wholesale Produce Supply, Steve Foster VP of Food Safety & QA | (612) 378-2025 | SFOSTER@WPSFRESH.COM
