title: Georgia Nut Company Recalls Specific Tru Fru Freeze Dried Fruit Snacks Due to Potential Metal Contamination
headline: Georgia Nut Company Voluntarily Recalls Select Tru Fru Freeze Dried Fruit Snack Varieties for Possible Metal Fragments
description: Georgia Nut Company recalls select Tru Fru Freeze Dried Fruit snacks across U.S. retail for potential metal contamination. No injuries reported.
keywords: Due to potential presence of metal in the product.
reason: Due to potential presence of metal in the product.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tru Fru, LLC has announced that its third-party manufacturer, Georgia Nut Company (GNC), is voluntarily recalling specific varieties of Tru Fru Freeze Dried products due to the potential presence of metal fragments. The presence of hard or sharp foreign objects in food poses a risk of traumatic injuries, such as lacerations or perforations of the mouth, tongue, throat, stomach, or intestines, as well as possible damage to teeth and gums.

The affected products were distributed across the United States and may have been sold at retail stores and online platforms, including Albertson’s, CVS, Food Lion, H-E-B, Hungryroot, Ingles Markets, Kroger, Stew Leonard’s, and Target. The recall specifically targets certain lots of Tru Fru Freeze Dried Strawberries in Dark & White Chocolate and Tru Fru Freeze Dried Strawberries & Crème.

There have been no reported injuries or illnesses linked to these products to date. The recall was initiated following a consumer report identifying the potential issue. Details to identify the recall include a 10-digit manufacturing code on the back of the package, where the first four digits and/or letters correspond to the impacted products listed below.

Item Number Description UPC First 4 Digits/Letters of Manufacturing Code (Impacted Lots)
10300458 Tru Fru Freeze Dried Strawberries In Dark & White Chocolate 3.4oz 850048358270 517B, 517C, 517D, 517E, 517F, 518D, 518E, 518F, 519A, 519B, 524A, 524B, 524C, 529C, 529D, 529E, 530C, 530D, 530E, 531A, 531B, 531C, 531D, 531E, 532A, 532B
10300442 Tru Fru Freeze Dried Strawberries In Dark & White Chocolate 1.7oz 850048358331 514A, 514B, 514C, 514D, 525A, 525B, 525C, 526B, 526C, 526D, 526E, 526F, 521C, 521D, 521E, 522B, 522C, 522D, 522E, 524D, 524E, 525A, 525B, 525C, 525D, 525E, 526A
10300474 Tru Fru Freeze Dried Strawberries In Dark & White Chocolate 13 oz 850048358379 515A, 516B, 516C
10300455 Tru Fru Freeze Dried Strawberries & Crème 3.4 oz 850048358249 520B, 520C, 520D, 520E, 520F, 521A, 524C, 524D, 524E

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the manufacturing code on their Tru Fru Freeze Dried Fruit snack packages against the list of affected lots and refrain from consuming any recalled product. Dispose of impacted items or request reimbursement via the contact details provided by Tru Fru at (888) 293-7748 or trufru@rqa-inc.com.

title: Gansu Zhaofeng Agricultural Development Recalls G&L Food Dried Bean Curd for Undeclared Wheat Allergen
headline: Gansu Zhaofeng Agricultural Development Co., Ltd. Recalls G&L Food Dried Bean Curd Due to Undeclared Wheat in Soy Sauce
description: G&L Food recalls Dried Bean Curd in 500g bags due to undeclared wheat allergen in soy sauce; products sold in Northern California with no reported illnesses.
keywords: Gansu Zhaofeng Agricultural Development Co., Ltd.
companyName: Gansu Zhaofeng Agricultural Development Co., Ltd.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gansu Zhaofeng Agricultural Development Co., Ltd., located in Hui County Industrial Concentration Zone, Longnan City, Gansu Province, has issued a voluntary recall for its 500g bags of G&L FOOD Dried Bean Curd. The recall is due to the failure to declare wheat as an allergen on the product labeling. Wheat is present as a part of the soy sauce used, which poses a serious risk to individuals with wheat allergies or severe sensitivities, potentially causing life-threatening allergic reactions.

The affected dried bean curd products are primarily sold in restaurants and retail stores in the Bay Area of Northern California. Product packaging consists of 500g food plastic bags with expiration dates of 25/10/2025, 11/06/2026, and 20/06/2026. The allergen information was missing from the label, as identified during an FDA inspection. There have been no reported illnesses or adverse reactions linked to this product to date.

Brand Product Package Size UPC Expiration Dates Container Type Recall Reason
G&L FOOD Dried Bean Curd 500g/bag 6976325560236 25/10/2025, 11/06/2026, 20/06/2026 Food plastic bag Undeclared wheat allergen (in soy sauce)

facts.company: Gansu Zhaofeng Agricultural Development Co., Ltd.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should not eat G&L Food Dried Bean Curd with expiration dates 25/10/2025, 11/06/2026, or 20/06/2026. Return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Mr. Gu at 415-218-2332 Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM.

title: SLR Food Distribution Recalls Wise Wife Ground Cinnamon Due to Elevated Lead Levels
headline: SLR Food Distribution Issues Recall for Wise Wife Ground Cinnamon Because of Potential Lead Contamination
description: SLR Food Distribution recalls Wise Wife Ground Cinnamon powder due to potential contamination with elevated lead levels. No illnesses reported.
keywords: Food & Beverages Spices, Flavors & Salts Contaminants
Potential to be contaminated with elevated levels of lead
productType: Food & Beverages Spices, Flavors & Salts Contaminants
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Spices, Flavors & Salts Contaminants
reason: Potential to be contaminated with elevated levels of lead

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
SLR Food Distribution, Inc., based in Hicksville, NY, has issued a recall for Wise Wife brand Ground Cinnamon due to the potential contamination with elevated levels of lead. This recall involves 1.76 oz (50g) clear plastic jars with black lids, distributed to retailers in New Jersey, New York, Florida, Maryland, Minnesota, Oklahoma, and Ohio between February 15, 2024, and June 28, 2025.

Exposure to lead, even at low levels, can pose health risks. While short-term low-level exposure may not cause immediate symptoms, increased blood lead levels could be the only sign. Prolonged or high levels of lead exposure can lead to serious health issues including learning disorders and developmental defects in children, as well as kidney dysfunction, hypertension, and neurocognitive effects in adults.

No illnesses related to this recall have been reported to date. The recall was initiated after testing by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration detected lead levels above acceptable limits. SLR Food Distribution has ceased sales of the product as of June 28, 2025.

Product Package Size UPC Distribution Dates Retail States
Wise Wife Ground Cinnamon 1.76 oz (50g) jar 0 688474 302853 02/15/2024 – 06/28/2025 NJ, NY, FL, MD, MN, OK, OH

facts.productType: Food & Beverages Spices, Flavors & Salts Contaminants

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have Wise Wife Ground Cinnamon in 1.76 oz jars with UPC 0 688474 302853 distributed between 02/15/2024 and 06/28/2025. If affected, return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund or contact SLR Food Distribution at 516-437-3782 for further information and support.

title: AquaStar (USA) Corp Recalls AquaStar Raw Shrimp Skewers Because of Possible Health Risk
headline: AquaStar (USA) Corp Recalls AquaStar Raw Shrimp Skewers Because of Possible Health Risk
description: AquaStar announced a recall involving Shrimp Skewers, Raw Peeled Tail-on after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - possible radionuclide...
keywords: Potential Foodborne Illness - possible radionuclide (Cesium-137) contamination
reason: Potential Foodborne Illness - possible radionuclide (Cesium-137) contamination

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On September 23, 2025, AquaStar (USA) Corp, based in Seattle, WA, announced a voluntary recall of approximately 8,000 bags of AquaStar Raw Peeled Tail-on Shrimp Skewers due to potential contamination with the radionuclide Cesium-137 (Cs-137). These shrimp skewers, imported from Indonesia, may have been processed under unsanitary conditions, raising concerns about possible radioactive contamination.

Cesium-137 is a man-made radioactive isotope that can be present at varying levels in the environment, particularly in areas affected by contamination. Long-term, repeated exposure to Cs-137 through food consumption can increase the risk of cancer due to DNA damage in living cells.

The recalled shrimp skewers, each weighing 1.25 pounds, were sold in Food Lion stores located in Delaware, Georgia, Kentucky, Maryland, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia, and West Virginia. Sales occurred between July 7, 2025, and September 20, 2025. There have been no reported illnesses linked to this product to date.

Consumers are advised not to consume the affected shrimp skewers. Instead, they should dispose of the product or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further inquiries, AquaStar can be contacted at 1-800-331-3440, Monday through Friday, from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PST. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

UPC Lot Code Best If Used By Date
731149390010 10662 5127 10 11 07 2027
731149390010 10662 5128 11 11 08 2027

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased AquaStar Shrimp Skewers, Raw Peeled Tail-on, should check the product packaging for lot numbers to determine if their item is included in the recall due to possible contamination with radionuclide Cesium-137.

en.title: AquaStar (USA) Corp Recalls AquaStar Raw Shrimp Skewers Because of Possible Health Risk
en.description: AquaStar announced a recall involving Shrimp Skewers, Raw Peeled Tail-on after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - possible radionuclide...
en.reason: Potential Foodborne Illness - possible radionuclide (Cesium-137) contamination
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Shellfish Contaminants, Food & Beverages, Shellfish, Contaminants

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On September 23, 2025, AquaStar (USA) Corp, based in Seattle, WA, announced a voluntary recall of approximately 8,000 bags of AquaStar Raw Peeled Tail-on Shrimp Skewers due to potential contamination with the radionuclide Cesium-137 (Cs-137). These shrimp skewers, imported from Indonesia, may have been processed under unsanitary conditions, raising concerns about possible radioactive contamination.

Cesium-137 is a man-made radioactive isotope that can be present at varying levels in the environment, particularly in areas affected by contamination. Long-term, repeated exposure to Cs-137 through food consumption can increase the risk of cancer due to DNA damage in living cells.

The recalled shrimp skewers, each weighing 1.25 pounds, were sold in Food Lion stores located in Delaware, Georgia, Kentucky, Maryland, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia, and West Virginia. Sales occurred between July 7, 2025, and September 20, 2025. There have been no reported illnesses linked to this product to date.

Consumers are advised not to consume the affected shrimp skewers. Instead, they should dispose of the product or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further inquiries, AquaStar can be contacted at 1-800-331-3440, Monday through Friday, from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PST. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

title: Goot Essa Recalls Der Mutterschaf Cheese Due to Potential Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
headline: Goot Essa Announces Recall of Der Mutterschaf Cheese Over Possible Listeria Contamination
description: Goot Essa recalls Der Mutterschaf Cheese batch 33 for possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No reported illnesses; distributed in NJ, VA, CT, and MT.
keywords: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness
productType: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Goot Essa LLC of Howard, Pennsylvania, is recalling a specific batch of its Der Mutterschaf Cheese due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. While healthy individuals may experience short-term symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea, Listeria infections can also lead to miscarriages and stillbirths in pregnant women.

The recall involves one lot totaling 64 pounds, identified as batch number 33, of Goot Essa brand Der Mutterschaf Cheese. This product was distributed to seven customers across New Jersey, Virginia, Connecticut, and Montana via retail stores and restaurants. The affected cheese comes in clear plastic packages of 4 oz and 8 oz sizes and carries specific UPC codes and a lot number sticker on the back.

The presence of Listeria monocytogenes was identified through routine company testing on a sample from the batch. Subsequent testing of similar batches did not show contamination. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this issue. Goot Essa is actively cooperating with the FDA to complete the investigation.

Product Name Package Size UPC Lot Number
Goot Essa Der Mutterschaf Cheese 4 oz 810154560189 33
Goot Essa Der Mutterschaf Cheese 8 oz 810154560196 33

title: Sprout Organics Expands Recall of Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5 oz Pouch for Potential Lead Contamination
headline: Sprout Organics Expands Recall of Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5 oz Pouch Due to Elevated Lead Levels in Additional Lots
description: Sprout Organics recalls Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5 oz pouches for potential lead contamination in multiple lots sold at Walgreens and select retailers.
keywords: Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach, 3.5-ounce pouch
productDescription: Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach, 3.5-ounce pouch

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sprout Organics has expanded its voluntary recall of the Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5-ounce pouch to include additional lot codes due to potentially elevated lead levels. Lead exposure, even at low levels, may increase blood lead levels, with risks rising based on amount and duration of exposure. Children are especially vulnerable as prolonged exposure to lead can affect learning, development, and cause other long-term health issues.

The recalled product was sold at Walgreens, certain independent retailers in select states, and online. It was not distributed through other major retail chains besides Walgreens.

No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall. No other Sprout Organics products are affected. The recall follows routine internal testing by the company and is conducted with knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

For more information about Sprout Organics’ product testing procedures, visit the company's FAQ page.

content.text: Consumers should check the expiration date and lot code on the back bottom strip of the pouch. If their product matches the recalled lots, they should return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. title: Southwind Foods, LLC Recalls Frozen Shrimp Due to Possible Cesium-137 Contamination
headline: Southwind Foods, LLC Issues Recall of Frozen Shrimp Citing Potential Radioactive Contamination
description: Southwind Foods recalls frozen shrimp from multiple brands due to possible contamination with radioactive Cesium-137, distributed across 35 US states.
keywords: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street
Potential Foodborne Illness - possible radionuclide (Cesium-137) contamination
brandName: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street
brandNames: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street
reason: Potential Foodborne Illness - possible radionuclide (Cesium-137) contamination

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Southwind Foods, LLC of Carson, California, has announced a voluntary recall of a limited quantity of frozen shrimp due to possible contamination with radionuclide Cesium-137 (Cs-137). Cs-137 is a manmade radioactive isotope that can be found at varying levels in the environment, sometimes concentrated in water or food from contaminated areas. Long-term exposure to low doses of Cs-137 through consumption may increase cancer risk by damaging DNA in living cells.

The affected frozen shrimp products were distributed between June 24 and September 16, 2025, to retailers, distributors, and wholesalers across 35 states. This recall follows an FDA investigation into reports of Cs-137 contamination in shipping containers and frozen shrimp processed by PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods) in Indonesia. There have been no reported illnesses linked to this issue so far.

title: Olympus Voluntary Recall of ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA Needles Due to Component Detachment Risk
headline: Olympus Announces Global Recall for ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA Needles Manufactured Before May 12, 2025
description: Olympus recalls ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA needles due to risk of device component detachment during procedures, posing patient safety concerns.
keywords: Device Components May Detach During Procedures
reason: Device Components May Detach During Procedures

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Olympus Corporation has initiated a voluntary, global recall of specific lots of its ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA needles manufactured before May 12, 2025. This recall follows reports that device components may detach during medical procedures, posing potential risks to patient safety.

The ViziShot 2 FLEX needle is designed with a hypotube and sheath to protect and stabilize the device during use. Damage to the sheath, which may occur during manufacturing or use, can cause the hypotube and associated plastic components to detach. These detached components are radiolucent and not visible on X-rays, increasing the risk of retained fragments within the tracheobronchial tree. Such retained parts may require bronchoscopic extraction or surgical intervention.

Olympus has enhanced manufacturing quality controls and implemented automated inspection procedures to detect device damage for needles produced before May 12, 2025. Healthcare providers should ensure that needles in use are from unaffected lots and monitor patients for unusual symptoms following procedures involving these needles.

Patients receiving treatment with affected needles should be monitored with standard post-procedural care protocols, paying close attention to signs of retained device components.

No specific injury or adverse event counts are publicly reported at this time; however, the potential for serious complications exists.

ProductModelLot NumbersManufacture Date
ViziShot 2 FLEX EBUS-TBNA Needle19GAll lots manufactured before May 12, 2025Prior to May 12, 2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should verify if their ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS-TBNA needles are impacted by checking lot numbers against the recall information. Affected needles should be quarantined and returned to Olympus following official instructions. For assistance, contact Olympus Technical Assistance at 1-800-848-9024, Option 1, or email complaints@olympus.com.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Olympus Technical Assistance Center | 1-800-848-9024 | complaints@olympus.com

title: Aquastar (USA) Corp Recalls Kroger Frozen Raw EZ Peel, Kroger Mercado Frozen Cooked Shrimp, and Aquastar Raw Shrimp Skewers Because of Possible Health Risk
headline: Aquastar (USA) Corp Recalls Kroger Frozen Raw EZ Peel, Kroger Mercado Frozen Cooked Shrimp, and Aquastar Raw Shrimp Skewers Because of Possible Health Risk
description: Kroger, Kroger Mercado, AquaStar announced a recall involving Raw Shrimp, Cooked Shrimp, Shrimp Skewers after issues linked to due to possible radionuclide...
keywords: Raw Shrimp, Cooked Shrimp, Shrimp Skewers
productDescription: Raw Shrimp, Cooked Shrimp, Shrimp Skewers

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
{ "content": "

On September 19, 2025, AquaStar (USA) Corp, based in Seattle, WA, announced a recall of certain shrimp products due to potential contamination with the radionuclide Cesium-137 (Cs-137). The affected products include Kroger Raw Colossal EZ Peel Shrimp, Kroger Mercado Cooked Medium Peeled Tail-Off Shrimp, and AquaStar Raw Peeled Tail-on Shrimp Skewers. title: Haifa Smoked Fish Recalls Cold Smoked Salmon and Cold Smoked Seabass for Listeria Risk
headline: Haifa Smoked Fish Issues Recall of Cold Smoked Salmon and Cold Smoked Seabass Due to Potential Listeria Contamination
description: Haifa Smoked Fish recalls Cold Smoked Salmon and Seabass due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination affecting vulnerable groups nationwide.
keywords: Cold Smoked Salmon and Cold Smoked Seabass
productDescription: Cold Smoked Salmon and Cold Smoked Seabass

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Haifa Smoked Fish of Jamaica, NY, has announced a recall of two of its products: “Cold Smoked Salmon” 8 oz. (Lot #219) and “Cold Smoked Seabass” 8 oz. (Lot #212). The recall is due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes, a harmful bacterium that can cause serious infections, especially in young children, the elderly, pregnant women, and individuals with weakened immune systems.

Healthy individuals exposed to Listeria monocytogenes may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Infections can be more severe in pregnant women, potentially leading to miscarriages and stillbirths.

The affected products were distributed nationwide through direct delivery to retail stores and distributors.

The contamination was identified following sampling and testing conducted by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets Food Inspectors and Food Laboratory personnel. Haifa Smoked Fish has not reported any illnesses related to this recall to date.

ProductSizeLot Number
Cold Smoked Salmon8 oz.219
Cold Smoked Seabass8 oz.212
  • Potential hazard: Listeria monocytogenes contamination
  • Risk groups: young children, elderly, pregnant women, immunocompromised individuals
  • Distribution: nationwide via direct delivery to retail stores and distributors
  • Packaging: vacuum packed on a paper board with flexible plastic

facts.product: Cold Smoked Salmon and Cold Smoked Seabass

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should verify the product name, lot number, and size of their Cold Smoked Salmon or Cold Smoked Seabass. If matching the recalled lots, they should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund and contact Haifa Smoked Fish at 718-523-8899 for further assistance.

title: Sprout Organics Recalls Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5 oz Pouch Due to Elevated Lead Levels
headline: Sprout Organics Recalls One Lot of Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5 oz Pouch for Possible Lead Contamination
description: Sprout Organics recalls Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5 oz pouch due to potential elevated lead levels; no illnesses reported to date.
keywords: Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach, 3.5-ounce pouch
Product may contain elevated levels of lead
productDescription: Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach, 3.5-ounce pouch
reason: Product may contain elevated levels of lead

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sprout Organics has voluntarily recalled one lot of its Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5-ounce pouch due to potential elevated levels of lead. Lead exposure, even in small amounts, can raise blood lead levels and may lead to serious health issues, especially in children. Prolonged or high-level exposure to lead can impact learning, development, and cause other long-term health complications.

The recalled product was distributed primarily through Walgreens and some independent stores in the US South region, with most sales occurring between September and December 2024. It was not sold in other major grocery chains.

To date, no illnesses linked to this product have been reported, and no other Sprout Organics products are affected by this recall.

For more details on Sprout Organics’ product testing and safety measures, visit their FAQ page. Questions about the recall can be directed to Sprout Organics by phone at 510-833-6089, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. Pacific Time, or by email at Info@sproutorganics.com.

title: BD Expands Class I Recall of BD Alaris™ Pump Module Model 8100 Compatible Infusion Sets
headline: BD Expands Voluntary Recall of BD Alaris™ Pump Module Model 8100 with Additional Compatible Infusion Sets Due to Infusion Delivery Risks
description: BD updates recall of BD Alaris™ Pump Module model 8100 used with 15 additional compatible infusion sets due to infusion flow rate and alarm issues.
keywords: BD Alaris Pump Module model 8100 used with a subset of compatible pump infusion sets
Pump performance variations compared to the performance described in the user manual could impact infusion delivery
productDescription: BD Alaris Pump Module model 8100 used with a subset of compatible pump infusion sets
reason: Pump performance variations compared to the performance described in the user manual could impact infusion delivery

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) has expanded its Class I voluntary recall announced earlier in 2025 involving the BD Alaris™ Pump Module model 8100 when used with certain compatible pump infusion sets. This recall update includes 15 additional pump infusion sets, some discontinued but possibly still in inventory based on their expiration dates. The recall addresses pump performance variations from user manual specifications that may impact infusion delivery accuracy.

Performance issues include flow rate inaccuracies, delayed occlusion alarms, and over-infusion of post-occlusion bolus volume. These deviations can result in serious adverse events, including patient death, and particularly affect vulnerable populations such as neonates and critically ill patients. Factors contributing to the issues include design features of the infusion sets, such as filters and other in-line components.

BD has issued updated corrective actions and risk mitigation recommendations, including the clinical use of alternative BD Alaris™ Pump infusion sets where appropriate. The recalled products were distributed throughout the United States (including Guam and Puerto Rico), as well as in Canada, Belgium, and South Africa.

Consumers and healthcare providers with questions or complaints are advised to contact BD or report adverse events through the FDA MedWatch Program.

Impacted Pump Infusion Sets (15 Additional Models)
Details of the specific 15 additional pump infusion sets were provided in BD’s customer update (not included specifically in the source text).

facts.product: BD Alaris Pump Module model 8100 used with a subset of compatible pump infusion sets

content.text: Check if your BD Alaris Pump Module model 8100 and compatible infusion sets are included in this recall. Contact BD at 1-844-823-5433 or productcomplaints@bd.com for assistance, and follow BD’s instructions for returning or disposing of affected products.

title: Gooder Foods Recalls Goodles Flavored Pasta Due to Undeclared Milk and Cashews Allergens
headline: Gooder Foods Announces Recall of Goodles Vegan Is Believin’ and Here Comes Truffle Pasta Products for Undeclared Milk and Cashews
description: Gooder Foods recalls selected Goodles pasta lots for undeclared milk and cashew allergens. Risk of severe allergic reactions. National distribution in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gooder Foods, Inc. has announced a recall of selected lots of two flavored pasta products from its Goodles brand due to undeclared allergens. The recall involves 5 lots of Vegan Is Believin’ – Plant Based White Cheddar with Spirals, which may contain milk not listed on the label, and 3 lots of Here Comes Truffle – Creamy Truffle Flavored Cheddar and Shells, which may contain cashew not declared on the packaging.

The affected lots were produced between April 7 and April 15, 2025, and were distributed nationally to wholesale distributors, operators, and online from April 29, 2025, through August 5, 2025. Individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to milk or cashews may be at risk of serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume these products.

There have been a total of eight reported allergic reactions linked to the recalled products—six related to Here Comes Truffle and two related to Vegan Is Believin’.

Gooder Foods is notifying distributors, retailers, and consumers about the recall and is working in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. Customer service representatives are available weekdays from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. EST at 1-888-610-2341 to address any questions.

title: Chetak LLC Group Expands Voluntary Recall of Deep Frozen Fruits and Vegetables Due to Salmonella Risk
headline: Chetak LLC Group Expands Voluntary Recall to Include Additional Deep Frozen Fruits and Vegetables Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination
description: Chetak LLC Group expands recall of Deep frozen fruits and vegetables for Salmonella risk; includes additional lots produced from Dec 2024 to Aug 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Chetak LLC Group has expanded its voluntary recall of Deep-brand frozen vegetable and fruit products due to potential contamination with Salmonella. The recall, originally announced on July 16, 2025, now includes additional lots of frozen items produced on the same equipment between December 18, 2024, and August 18, 2025.

Salmonella infection can cause serious and sometimes fatal illnesses, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and people with weakened immune systems. Symptoms in healthy individuals may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection may spread to the bloodstream causing more severe conditions.

The recalled products were distributed nationwide in retail stores and were packaged in flexible plastic bags. Consumers are advised to check the lot numbers and use-by dates printed on the back panel of the packages. No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall to date.

Production of the affected products has been suspended while Chetak investigates the source of contamination. For questions or more information, consumers may contact the company by email at cservice@deepfoods.com or by phone at (908) 810-7500, Monday through Friday, 9 am to 5 pm EST. This recall is being conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

Product Lot Number(s)
Deep-brand Sprouted Mat (Moth) IN 24353K, IN 24354K, IN 25072K
Deep-brand Sprouted Moong (Mung) IN 25058K
Deep-brand Premium Select Surti Undhiu Mix IN 25158K
Deep-brand Premium Select Singoda IN 25150K
Previously Recalled Sprouted Mung and Moth Lots
24330
24297
24291
24292

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the lot numbers on Deep frozen fruit and vegetable packages against the recalled lots and immediately stop use of any affected products. Dispose of or return recalled items to the place of purchase. For questions or assistance, contact Chetak LLC Group by phone at (908) 810-7500 or email cservice@deepfoods.com.

title: Gina Marie Bakery Recalls Cookies for Undeclared Almonds, Sesame, and Artificial Food Dyes
headline: Gina Marie Bakery Recalls Cookies Due to Undeclared Allergens and Food Dyes Posing Serious Allergy Risks
description: Gina Marie Bakery recalls various cookies for undeclared almonds, sesame, and artificial food dyes posing allergy risks. No illnesses reported to date.
keywords: Undeclared almonds, sesame, Red 40, Red 3, Blue 1, Yellow 5, Red 40, Yellow 6
reason: Undeclared almonds, sesame, Red 40, Red 3, Blue 1, Yellow 5, Red 40, Yellow 6

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gina Marie Bakery of Waterbury, Connecticut, has issued a recall of several cookie products due to the presence of undeclared allergens, including almonds, sesame, and various food dyes. The affected cookies include Italian Mixed Vanilla Cookies, Italian Sesame Cookies, Vanilla Chocolate Dipped Cookies, Vanilla with Apricot Jam Cookies, Toasted Almond & Cherry Biscotti, and Vanilla Cookies with Raspberry Peach Jam. The undeclared allergens pose a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions to individuals with allergies to nuts, sesame seeds, or certain food dyes.

The recalled products were distributed primarily in Connecticut at various grocery and specialty stores, including Big Y, Stop and Shop, Labonne’s Supermarkets, Adam’s Markets, Highland Park Markets, Price Chopper, Kensington Market, and many others. Additionally, the cookies were sold at all Nardelli’s Connecticut stores with labels added by Nardelli’s. The cookies come in 1 lb (454g) clamshell plastic containers, except for the Italian Mixed Vanilla Cookies, which are available in both 1 lb and 2 lb (908g) packages.

No illnesses have been reported in connection with these products to date. The recall was initiated after it was found that the packaging failed to disclose the presence of the mentioned allergens. Sales of the products have been suspended pending verification that the packaging labels are corrected.

title: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Recalls Multiple Cheese Varieties Due to Possible Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination
headline: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Issues Recall for Organic Gouda, Colored Cheddar, Mozzarella/Provolone, Pepper Jack, Swiss, Dilly Pickle, and Monterey Jack Cheeses
description: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op recalls various cheese products due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination affecting multiple states and production dates.
keywords: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op, Sunrise Creamery
Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness
brandName: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op, Sunrise Creamery
brandNames: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op, Sunrise Creamery
productType: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op is recalling several cheese products produced at its Middlefield, Ohio facility due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and potentially fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and immunocompromised persons. Symptoms in healthy individuals may include fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Infection during pregnancy can lead to miscarriage or stillbirth.

The recalled products were manufactured on May 30, 2025, and August 13, 2025, and distributed from July 7, 2025, through August 14, 2025, in Ohio, Minnesota, Wisconsin, and Texas via distributors, dining halls, and retail stores. The affected products include Organic Gouda, Mozzarella/Provolone, Colored Cheddar, Swiss, Pepper Jack, Monterey Jack, and Sunrise Creamery Dilly Pickle Monterey Jack cheeses, with specific package sizes, lot codes, and sell-by dates detailed below.

The recall followed the detection of Listeria monocytogenes in finished products and on cutting equipment during routine company testing. No related illnesses have been reported to date.

Product Package Size Lot Code / Label Sell By Date
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Organic Gouda 8 oz. packages - 2/13/2026
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Mozzarella/Provolone (shredded) 5 lb. bags - 9/3/2025
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Colored (Yellow) Cheddar (Smoked Cheddar and All Natural Mild Cheddar) 5 lb. loaves - 2/13/2026
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Colored (Yellow) Cheddar (Shredded Cheddar Cheese) 5 lb. bags (shredded) - 9/3/2025
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Swiss Cheese 5 lb. loaves - 2/13/2026
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Pepper Jack (All Natural Pepper Jack) 5 lb. loaves - 2/13/2026
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Pepper Jack (shredded) 5 lb. bags - 9/3/2025
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Monterey Jack 5 lb. loaves - 2/13/2026
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Monterey Jack (shredded) 5 lb. bags - 9/3/2025
Sunrise Creamery Dilly Pickle Monterey Jack with Pickles 6 oz. package Lot 253054 1-7-26, 1-11-26, 2-16-26
Sunrise Creamery Dilly Pickle Monterey Jack with Pickles 1.25 lb. package Lot 253054 12-30-25

For questions regarding the recall, contact Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op General Manager Nevin Byler at 440-632-5567, Monday through Friday, 8 AM to 4 PM EST.

More information is available in the original company announcement and FDA recall posting.

The affected products include Organic Gouda, Mozzarella/Provolone, Colored Cheddar, Swiss, Pepper Jack, Monterey Jack, and Sunrise Creamery Dilly Pickle Monterey Jack cheeses, with specific package sizes, lot codes, and sell-by dates detailed below.

The recall followed the detection of Listeria monocytogenes in finished products and on cutting equipment during routine company testing. No related illnesses have been reported to date.

title: Ice Cream Factory Recalls Vanilla G.Nutt Ice Cream for Undeclared Almond Allergen
headline: Recall Alert: Vanilla G.Nutt Ice Cream Contains Undeclared Almond Allergen
description: Ice Cream Factory recalls Vanilla G.Nutt Ice Cream due to undeclared almond allergen. Affects products distributed in New York with March 2027 expiration.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ice Cream Factory, based in Mount Vernon, NY, has announced a recall of certain Vanilla G.Nutt ice cream containers due to the presence of undeclared almond allergens. This recall affects 40 pints, 8 quarts, and 3 half gallons of the product, which were distributed exclusively within New York State. Individuals with tree nut allergies or severe sensitivities are advised not to consume the affected products, as doing so could result in serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The impacted Vanilla G.Nutt ice cream items were available at Golden Krust retail stores located in Mount Vernon and White Plains, NY, as well as Farm Country Supermarket in Queens, NY, through direct delivery. The ice cream is sold in plastic pints and half gallons, and in white paper-coated quart containers. The expiration date on all recalled products is March 2027.

This recall was initiated after the detection of the undeclared almond allergen, traced to a temporary breakdown in the company’s production and packaging processes. Currently, no illnesses have been reported in relation to the consumption of the recalled ice cream.

Product Size UPC Code Expiration Date
Pint 8 56781 00361 3 March 2027
Quart 8 56781 00362 0 March 2027
Half Gallon 8 56781 00363 7 March 2027

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Vanilla G.Nutt Ice Cream with the listed UPC codes and expiration date March 2027 should return the product to their place of purchase for a full refund or exchange. For questions, contact Ice Cream Factory at (914) 441-9598, Monday through Friday, 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. EST.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Rudy Guerrino | (914) 441-9598

title: Hans Kissle Recalls Red Bliss Potato Salad Due to Undeclared Wheat Allergen
headline: Hans Kissle Recalls Red Bliss Potato Salad Mislabeling Causing Undeclared Wheat Allergen Risk
description: Hans Kissle recalls Red Bliss Potato Salad mislabeled Tricolor Twist Pasta Salad due to undeclared wheat allergen, distributed in several Northeast states in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hans Kissle LLC has voluntarily recalled 120 units of Hans Kissle Red Potato Bliss Salad because of an undeclared wheat allergen. The recall addresses the risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with wheat allergies or severe sensitivities. The issue originated when containers of Tricolor Twist Pasta Salad were incorrectly labeled with the Red Bliss Potato Salad label, omitting the wheat allergen declaration.

The recalled product was distributed to Stop and Shop locations in Massachusetts, Rhode Island, Connecticut, New Jersey, and New York. The product is sold in a 16-ounce clear plastic container with a white lid. The incorrect label states Red Bliss Potato Salad, while the container contains Tricolor Twist Pasta Salad, which includes wheat.

No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported in connection with this recall to date.

ProductSizeUPC CodeUse By DatesDistribution Areas

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the product's UPC code and use-by dates to identify recalled items and return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Hans Kissle at 978-556-4500, Monday–Friday, 8:00am–5:00pm EST.

title: Southwind Foods Recalls Frozen Shrimp Due to Possible Cesium-137 Contamination
headline: Southwind Foods Recalls Frozen Shrimp Sold Under Multiple Brands for Potential Radioactive Contamination
description: Southwind Foods recalls frozen shrimp sold under Sand Bar, Arctic Shores, Best Yet, Great American, and First Street brands due to potential Cesium-137 contamination.
keywords: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street
brandName: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street
brandNames: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Southwind Foods, LLC of Carson, California, is voluntarily recalling a limited quantity of frozen shrimp due to possible contamination with radionuclide Cesium-137 (Cs-137). Cs-137 is a man-made radioactive isotope that can occur in the environment, especially in areas with environmental contamination. Prolonged, repeated exposure to low doses of Cs-137 through consumption may increase the risk of cancer by causing DNA damage within living cells.

The recalled frozen shrimp products were distributed between July 17 and August 8, 2025, to retailers, distributors, and wholesalers in Alabama, Arizona, California, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Pennsylvania, Utah, Virginia, and Washington. This recall follows an FDA investigation into Cs-137 contamination in shipping containers and frozen shrimp processed by PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods) of Indonesia. At this time, no illnesses related to this contamination have been reported.

facts.brand: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check product lot details for affected frozen shrimp sold under Sand Bar, Arctic Shores, Best Yet, Great American, and First Street brands on the FDA recall website. If they have purchased these items, they should return them to the place of purchase for a full refund or contact Southwind Foods at 1-323-262-8222 Monday through Friday, 8 am to 4 pm Pacific Time, for further assistance.

content.text:

title: Green Lumber Holding, LLC Issues Consumer Alert on Counterfeit Dietary Supplements Containing Undeclared Tadalafil
headline: Green Lumber Holding, LLC Alerts Consumers to Counterfeit Dietary Supplements Containing Undeclared Prescription Drug Tadalafil
description: Green Lumber warns of counterfeit dietary supplements with undeclared tadalafil, a serious health risk. Verify product authenticity to ensure safety.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Green Lumber Holding, LLC has issued a consumer alert regarding counterfeit dietary supplement products labeled as Green Lumber. This alert follows testing by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), which identified undeclared tadalafil, a prescription drug not included in any authentic Green Lumber products, in these counterfeit items. The presence of tadalafil poses serious health risks, especially for individuals taking nitrates or medications for heart conditions and blood pressure.

The company conducted an internal investigation and found that a former employee had diverted legitimate packaging and customer information to distribute counterfeit and adulterated products. This employee has been terminated. Consumers are urged to verify the authenticity of their Green Lumber products to avoid potential health dangers.

Green Lumber is cooperating fully with the FDA and law enforcement to address this issue and enhance consumer protection measures. Contact details for reporting suspected counterfeit products are provided below.

Product Identifier Lot Number Expiration Date
Green Lumber Dietary Supplement (authentic blister pack) LOTGLU13101b1 09/26

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should verify that their Green Lumber dietary supplement blister packs display the lot number LOTGLU13101b1EXP0926. If the product does not bear this lot number, discontinue use immediately. Report suspected counterfeit products to Green Lumber by phone at 949.426.8622 or email consumer-safety@greenlumber.com, and report any adverse events to the FDA’s MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting Program. Seek immediate medical attention if unexpected side effects occur.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Green Lumber Holding, LLC | 949.426.8622 | consumer-safety@greenlumber.com

title: Aquastar (USA) Corp Recalls Kroger Mercado Frozen Cooked Shrimp Due to Potential Cesium-137 Contamination
headline: Aquastar (USA) Corp Issues Recall for Kroger Mercado Frozen Cooked Shrimp Over Potential Cesium-137 Contamination
description: Aquastar (USA) Corp recalls Kroger Mercado Frozen Cooked Shrimp due to possible contamination with cesium-137, a radioactive substance posing health risks.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On August 27, 2025, AquaStar (USA) Corp of Seattle, WA announced a recall of approximately 18,000 bags of Kroger Mercado Cooked Medium Peeled Tail-Off Shrimp (net weight 2 lbs) due to potential contamination with cesium-137 (Cs-137), a man-made radioisotope. The shrimp may have been prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions, which could have led to contamination.

Cs-137 is present in the environment at varying levels, particularly in areas affected by environmental contamination. Long-term exposure to Cs-137 through consumption of contaminated food or water can increase cancer risk by causing DNA damage in living cells. The affected shrimp products were sold between July 24, 2025 and August 11, 2025 at multiple retailers across various states, including Baker’s, Gerbes, Jay C, Kroger, Mariano’s, Metro Market, Pay Less Supermarkets, and Pick ‘n Save.

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) is investigating Cs-137 contamination reports in shipping containers and frozen shrimp processed by PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods) of Indonesia. To date, no illnesses have been reported. The FDA noted no Cs-137 positive products have entered U.S. commerce but recommends recall of certain shipments linked to this supplier.

UPC Lot Code Best Before Date Product Description
011110626196 10662 5139 11/19/2027 Kroger Mercado Cooked Medium Peeled Tail-Off Shrimp, 2 lbs
011110626196 10662 5140 11/20/2027 Kroger Mercado Cooked Medium Peeled Tail-Off Shrimp, 2 lbs

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Kroger Mercado Frozen Cooked Shrimp should check the product's lot code and best before date to determine if it is affected. If the product matches recalled lots, they should dispose of it safely or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions or additional information, contact AquaStar (USA) Corp at 1-800-331-3440, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PST.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 1-800-331-3440

content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on August 28, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

To date, no illnesses have been reported. The FDA noted no Cs-137 positive products have entered U.S. commerce but recommends recall of certain shipments linked to this supplier.

title: DermaRite Industries Expands Voluntary Nationwide Recall Due to Potential Burkholderia cepacia Contamination
headline: DermaRite Industries Expands Voluntary Nationwide Recall Due to Potential Burkholderia cepacia Contamination
description: DermaRite announced a recall involving Hand Sanitizers, Cleansers, Skin Protectants, Deodorant after issues linked to device & drug safety - potential...
keywords: Hand Sanitizers, Cleansers, Skin Protectants, Deodorant
Device & Drug Safety - Potential Burkholderia cepacian contamination
productDescription: Hand Sanitizers, Cleansers, Skin Protectants, Deodorant
reason: Device & Drug Safety - Potential Burkholderia cepacian contamination

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
DermaRite Industries, LLC has expanded its voluntary nationwide recall due to potential contamination with Burkholderia cepacia complex, a microorganism that can cause serious infections. This expansion follows an initial recall announced on July 16 and now includes additional products and lots. The affected products are used for skin protection, cleansing, deodorizing, and antifungal purposes.

The Burkholderia cepacia complex poses significant risk especially to immunosuppressed individuals, who may suffer life-threatening infections if exposed. People with minor skin lesions could experience local infections, while the infection could potentially enter the bloodstream and lead to sepsis in vulnerable populations. Healthy individuals with intact skin are at a lower risk.

The recall involves a variety of over-the-counter (OTC) products including hand sanitizers, skin protectants, antifungal creams, and antiseptic cleansers. These products were distributed nationwide across the United States and Puerto Rico. DermaRite has informed distributors and customers to immediately check their available inventory and destroy any impacted products according to their facility’s protocols.

Consumers with concerns or questions about this recall can contact Sedgwick by phone at 888-943-5190 (Monday–Friday, 8:00 am–5:00 pm EST) or via email at dermarite5186@sedgwick.com. Any adverse health effects suspected to be related to these products should be reported to a healthcare provider.

title: Aquastar (USA) Corp Recalls Aqua Star Cocktail Shrimp 6oz Due to Potential Cesium-137 Contamination
headline: Aquastar (USA) Corp Recalls Aqua Star Cocktail Shrimp 6oz Over Potential Cesium-137 Contamination
description: Aquastar (USA) Corp recalls Aqua Star Cocktail Shrimp 6oz sold at Walmart in 29 states due to possible contamination with cesium-137 (Cs-137).

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On August 28, 2025, AquaStar (USA) Corp of Seattle, WA announced a recall of approximately 26,460 packages of their Cocktail Shrimp 6oz product imported from Indonesia. The recall concerns potential contamination with cesium-137 (Cs-137), a man-made radioisotope that may pose health risks if consumed over time through contaminated food.

The affected shrimp were sold only at Walmart stores in 29 states, including Alaska, Alabama, Arkansas, Colorado, and others, between July 31 and August 16, 2025. The product was refrigerated with a 12-day shelf life and bears various Best if Use By dates. It comes in a clear plastic tray with a red and white label, identified by UPC 19434612191 and specific lot codes.

Cs-137 contamination can elevate cancer risk due to DNA damage from prolonged low-dose exposure. No illnesses have been reported to date. This recall is conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for consumer safety.

Cocktail Shrimp 6oz 19434612191 10662 5124 AK, AL, AR, CO, GA, IA, ID, IL, IN, KS, KY, LA, MI, MN, MO, MS, MT, ND, NE, OH, OK, OR, SD, TN, TX, WA, WI July 31, 2025 – August 16, 2025
Cocktail Shrimp 6oz 19434612191 10662 5125 AK, AL, AR, CO, GA, IA, ID, IL, IN, KS, KY, LA, MI, MN, MO, MS, MT, ND, NE, OH, OK, OR, SD, TN, TX, WA, WI July 31, 2025 – August 16, 2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do

title: Country Eggs Recalls Large Brown Cage Free Sunshine Yolks Eggs Due to Salmonella Risk
headline: Country Eggs, LLC Recalls Large Brown Cage Free Sunshine Yolks Eggs Because of Potential Salmonella Contamination
description: Country Eggs recalls Large Brown Cage Free Sunshine Yolks eggs due to potential Salmonella contamination linked to 92 illnesses, no reported deaths so far.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Country Eggs, LLC of Lucerne Valley, California has announced a recall of its Large Brown Cage Free "Sunshine Yolks" eggs due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella infection can cause serious and sometimes fatal illnesses, especially in young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of Salmonella infection include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream and cause more severe conditions such as arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The affected eggs were distributed in California and Nevada to grocery stores and food service distributors. The recall includes eggs packed in individual retail cartons under various brand names and sold with specific code dates and produce codes, as well as bulk foodservice packaging. Production has been suspended while the FDA and the company investigate the source of the contamination.

To date, there have been 92 reported illnesses linked to this issue, with no reported deaths.

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. The company is committed to addressing this issue fully and implementing corrective actions to prevent future occurrences.

Product VariantCode Dates (Sell By)Produce Code
Nagatoshi Produce Brand - Large Brown Cage Free Sunshine Yolks7/1/25 through 9/18/25No. CA 7695
Misuho Brand - Large Brown Cage Free Sunshine Yolks7/1/25 through 9/18/25No. CA 7695
Nijiya Markets Brand - Large Brown Cage Free Sunshine Yolks7/1/25 through 9/18/25No. CA 7695
Country Eggs Bulk Pack (1/15 Dozen) - Large Brown Sunshine Yolks for Foodservice7/1/25 through 9/18/25Not specified

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the sell-by dates and brand names of any Large Brown Cage Free "Sunshine Yolks" eggs they have purchased. If the eggs fall within the affected range, they should return the products to the place of purchase for a full refund or dispose of them safely. For further assistance, contact Country Eggs at 1 (800) 722-3447 during business hours or email INFO@countryeggsllc.com.

title: Unichem Pharmaceuticals Voluntary Recall of Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg Due to Mislabeling with Meloxicam
headline: Unichem Pharmaceuticals Recalls Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg Nationwide for Mislabeling Containing Meloxicam 7.5 mg
description: Unichem Pharmaceuticals recalls Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg mislabeled as containing Meloxicam posing risks; no adverse events reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Unichem Pharmaceuticals (USA) Inc. has voluntarily recalled one lot of Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg nationwide due to mislabeling. The affected bottles labeled as Cyclobenzaprine 10 mg (90 count) actually contain Meloxicam 7.5 mg tablets. This labeling error presents a risk to patients who may unknowingly take Meloxicam instead of Cyclobenzaprine.

Meloxicam is a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) used to treat conditions such as osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis. Taking Meloxicam unknowingly can lead to serious adverse events, especially for patients using other NSAIDs, blood thinners, or those with allergies or underlying health conditions. To date, no adverse events related to this recall have been reported to Unichem Pharmaceuticals.

The affected product can be identified by the lot number GMML24026A with an expiry date of September 2027, and NDC 29300-415-19 on the 90-count bottle label. This lot was distributed nationwide to distributors, retailers, and consumers. Unichem Pharmaceuticals is notifying all downstream partners, retailers, and consumers and coordinating the return of the affected product through their third-party recall provider, Inmar.

Product Lot Number Expiration Date NDC Count
Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg (mislabeled, contains Meloxicam 7.5 mg) GMML24026A September 2027 29300-415-19 90

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg lot GMML24026A should return the product to the pharmacy where purchased. Retail pharmacies and distributors must not dispense this lot and should contact Inmar at 1-877-840-5109 or rxrecalls@inmar.com for return instructions.

content.text: Consumer support: 1-877-840-5109 | rxrecalls@inmar.com

content.text:
Unichem Pharmaceuticals (USA) Inc. has voluntarily recalled one lot of Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg nationwide due to mislabeling. The affected bottles labeled as Cyclobenzaprine 10 mg (90 count) actually contain Meloxicam 7.5 mg tablets. This labeling error presents a risk to patients who may unknowingly take Meloxicam instead of Cyclobenzaprine.

Meloxicam is a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) used to treat conditions such as osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis. Taking Meloxicam unknowingly can lead to serious adverse events, especially for patients using other NSAIDs, blood thinners, or those with allergies or underlying health conditions. To date, no adverse events related to this recall have been reported to Unichem Pharmaceuticals.

The affected product can be identified by the lot number GMML24026A with an expiry date of September 2027, and NDC 29300-415-19 on the 90-count bottle label. This lot was distributed nationwide to distributors, retailers, and consumers. Unichem Pharmaceuticals is notifying all downstream partners, retailers, and consumers and coordinating the return of the affected product through their third-party recall provider, Inmar.

Product Lot Number Expiration Date NDC Count
Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg (mislabeled, contains Meloxicam 7.5 mg) GMML24026A September 2027 29300-415-19 90

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg lot GMML24026A should return the product to the pharmacy where purchased. Retail pharmacies and distributors must not dispense this lot and should contact Inmar at 1-877-840-5109 or rxrecalls@inmar.com for return instructions.

title: Viva Raw Recalls Two Lots of Dog and Cat Foods for Salmonella and Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination
headline: Viva Raw Recalls Ground Beef and Ground Chicken Pet Foods Due to Salmonella and Listeria Contamination
description: Viva Raw LLC recalls two lots of dog and cat foods due to contamination with Salmonella and Listeria monocytogenes posing health risks to pets and humans.
keywords: Ground Beef for Dogs and Ground Chicken for Dogs and Cats
Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness
productDescription: Ground Beef for Dogs and Ground Chicken for Dogs and Cats
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On August 25, 2025, Viva Raw LLC, based in Charlotte, NC, announced a voluntary recall of two lots of dog and cat foods due to contamination with Salmonella and Listeria monocytogenes. The recall affects Lot 21495, which includes Viva Ground Beef for Dogs and Viva Beef & Turkey for Puppies, as well as Lot 21975, which includes Viva Ground Chicken for Dogs, Viva Chunked Chicken for Dogs, Viva Chicken for Cats, and Viva Pure Chicken.

Salmonella and Listeria contamination pose health risks to both pets and humans. Animals consuming the affected products may experience symptoms such as lethargy, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Infected healthy pets can also carry and spread the bacteria to other animals or humans. People handling the contaminated products may develop symptoms including nausea, vomiting, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, abdominal cramping, and fever. More serious complications can occur, especially in vulnerable groups such as pregnant women, young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems.

The affected products were distributed frozen in 1 lb. vacuum-sealed bricks between July 2, 2025, and August 21, 2025. Distribution included direct consumer sales nationwide and select retailers in New York, Illinois, South Carolina, Florida, Kansas, California, and Arizona. The lot number is printed on the top of the product packaging. This recall was initiated after routine testing by the North Carolina Department of Agriculture identified the contamination. To date, no illnesses associated with these lots have been reported.

Lot Number Product Names Distribution Dates Packaging Distribution Locations
21495 Viva Ground Beef for Dogs; Viva Beef & Turkey for Puppies July 2, 2025 – August 21, 2025 Frozen 1 lb. bricks, clear vacuum packaging Nationwide (direct to consumers), select retailers in NY, IL, SC, FL, KS, CA, AZ
21975 Viva Ground Chicken for Dogs; Viva Chunked Chicken for Dogs; Viva Chicken for Cats; Viva Pure Chicken July 2, 2025 – August 21, 2025 Frozen 1 lb. bricks, clear vacuum packaging Nationwide (direct to consumers), select retailers in NY, IL, SC, FL, KS, CA, AZ

title: Southwind Foods Recalls Frozen Shrimp Due to Possible Cesium-137 Contamination
headline: Southwind Foods Voluntarily Recalls Multiple Brands of Frozen Shrimp for Potential Cesium-137 Radioactive Contamination
description: Southwind Foods recalls frozen shrimp from Sand Bar, Arctic Shores, Best Yet, Great American, and First Street due to possible Cesium-137 contamination.
keywords: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street
brandName: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street
brandNames: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Southwind Foods, LLC, based in Carson, California, has voluntarily recalled certain frozen shrimp products due to possible contamination with radioactive Cesium-137 (Cs-137). This man-made isotope poses health risks, including an increased cancer risk from long-term exposure.

The recalled shrimp products were distributed from July 17 to August 8, 2025. They were sold under the brands Sand Bar, Arctic Shores, Best Yet, Great American, and First Street across Alabama, Arizona, California, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Pennsylvania, Utah, Virginia, and Washington.

The FDA is investigating Cs-137 contamination linked to frozen shrimp processed by PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods) in Indonesia. No illnesses have been reported to date in association with this recall.

title: B. Braun Recalls Lactated Ringer’s and 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL Due to Particulate Matter
headline: B. Braun Medical Announces Voluntary Recall of Lactated Ringer’s and 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL for Particulate Matter Contamination
description: B. Braun voluntarily recalls Lactated Ringer’s and 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL due to particulate contamination posing patient risks.
keywords: Lactated Ringer’s Injection USP 1000 mL/0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL
Due to the presence of particulate matter.
productDescription: Lactated Ringer’s Injection USP 1000 mL/0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL
reason: Due to the presence of particulate matter.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
B. Braun Medical, Inc. has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of two lots of Lactated Ringer’s Injection USP 1000 mL and 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL. The recall affects products distributed to hospitals due to the presence of particulate matter inside the containers, which poses a potential risk to patients.

Although no serious injuries, deaths, or adverse events have been reported so far, the presence of particulate matter in the solution may cause harm if infused. Possible risks include pulmonary emboli, blood vessel occlusions, phlebitis, systemic immune activation, organ dysfunction, and hemolysis. If particulate matter is detected before use, a delay may occur while obtaining a replacement. If used, medical intervention could be necessary to address potential complications.

Lactated Ringers Injection USP 1000 mL is typically used as a source of electrolytes and water for hydration in adults and pediatric patients. The 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL is indicated for extracellular fluid replacement and treatment of metabolic alkalosis in cases of fluid loss and mild sodium depletion. Both products come packaged in boxes of 12 units and were distributed nationwide through distributors.

B. Braun has notified distributors and customers and is coordinating the return of all recalled products. Facilities holding the affected lots should immediately cease use and distribution and quarantine the products pending further instructions.

Product Catalog Number NDC Number Lot Number Distribution Range Expiration Date Region Distributed
Lactated Ringers Injection USP 1000 mL E7500 0264-7750-07 J4S807 26 DEC 2024 - 10 APR 2025 31 MAY 2027 US
0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL E8000 0264-7800-09 V3K770 15 NOV 2023 - 25 SEP 2024 31 JAN 2026 US

Consumers who have experienced any adverse effects or quality problems with these products are encouraged to report them to the FDA’s MedWatch program.

This recall is conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers and healthcare facilities should check the lot numbers of these products and immediately stop use and isolate any affected lots. Contact B. Braun Medical, Inc. at 844-903-6417 for return instructions and replacement guidance.

title: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Recalls Multiple Cheese Products Due to Potential Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
headline: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Issues Recall on 100% Grass-Fed Pepper Jack, Horseradish, Monterey Jack, and Farmers Cheese for Listeria Risk
description: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op recalls several cheese products including 100% Grass-Fed Pepper Jack and Horseradish Cheese due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination.
keywords: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op/Copia Collective/Farmers Cheese
Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness
brandName: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op/Copia Collective/Farmers Cheese
brandNames: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op/Copia Collective/Farmers Cheese
productType: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op has issued a recall for several cheese products due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, the elderly, pregnant women, and individuals with weakened immune systems. While healthy people may experience short-term symptoms such as fever, headache, nausea, and diarrhea, pregnant women face risks of miscarriage and stillbirth from infection.

The recall includes 100% Grass-Fed Pepper Jack Cheese in various sizes, Horseradish Flavored Cheese, Monterey Jack Cheese, and Farmers Cheese. These products were produced on June 16, June 24, and July 16, 2025, and distributed within Ohio from July 14 through August 7, 2025. The recall was initiated after company testing detected the presence of Listeria monocytogenes. Production has been suspended as the source was traced to environmental contamination affecting food-contact surfaces.

There have been no reported illnesses linked to these products so far.

en.title: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Recalls Multiple Cheese Products Due to Potential Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
en.description: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op recalls several cheese products including 100% Grass-Fed Pepper Jack and Horseradish Cheese due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination.
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness, Food & Beverages, Cheese/Cheese Product, Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op has issued a recall for several cheese products due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, the elderly, pregnant women, and individuals with weakened immune systems. While healthy people may experience short-term symptoms such as fever, headache, nausea, and diarrhea, pregnant women face risks of miscarriage and stillbirth from infection.

The recall includes 100% Grass-Fed Pepper Jack Cheese in various sizes, Horseradish Flavored Cheese, Monterey Jack Cheese, and Farmers Cheese. These products were produced on June 16, June 24, and July 16, 2025, and distributed within Ohio from July 14 through August 7, 2025. The recall was initiated after company testing detected the presence of Listeria monocytogenes. Production has been suspended as the source was traced to environmental contamination affecting food-contact surfaces.

There have been no reported illnesses linked to these products so far.

title: Tandem Diabetes Care Voluntarily Corrects t:slim X2 Insulin Pumps Due to Speaker-Related Malfunction
headline: Tandem Diabetes Care Announces Voluntary Medical Device Correction for t:slim X2 Insulin Pumps Due to Potential Speaker Failure
description: Tandem Diabetes Care issues a voluntary correction for select t:slim X2 insulin pumps due to a speaker defect causing insulin delivery interruption risks.
keywords: Potential speaker-related issue that can trigger an error resulting in a discontinuation of insulin delivery
reason: Potential speaker-related issue that can trigger an error resulting in a discontinuation of insulin delivery

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tandem Diabetes Care, Inc. has initiated a voluntary medical device correction for certain t:slim X2 insulin pumps due to a potential speaker-related issue. This defect can trigger a Malfunction 16 alarm, resulting in the discontinuation of insulin delivery and loss of communication between the pump and continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) device. The interruption in insulin delivery and CGM data may lead to hyperglycemia, which in severe cases might require hospitalization or medical intervention.

As of the announcement, there have been 700 confirmed adverse events involving high blood sugar or the need for medical intervention, along with 59 reported injuries. No deaths have been reported. Customers in the United States affected by this issue were notified directly between July 22 and 24, 2025, with guidance on how to respond if they encounter the Malfunction 16 alarm.

Tandem Diabetes Care plans to release a software update aimed at improving early detection of speaker failure. This update will also add persistent vibration alerts to enhance safety. All pump users will be informed when the update is available and encouraged to install it promptly.

Impacted U.S. customers with questions can contact Tandem’s Technical Support Team 24/7 by email at techsupport@tandemdiabetes.com or phone at (877) 801-6901. International users should contact their local distributors; contact details are available at tandemdiabetes.com. Additional resources, including a searchable list of affected serial numbers and the customer notification letter, are available online.

ResourceLink
Customer Notification LetterPDF document
Searchable List of Impacted Serial Numbershttps://tandemdiabetes.com/mal16-2025
Tandem Diabetes Contact Informationhttps://tandemdiabetes.com

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Check your t:slim X2 insulin pump for notifications about the speaker-related Malfunction 16 alarm, and contact Tandem Diabetes Care at (877) 801-6901 or techsupport@tandemdiabetes.com for support and instructions on the software update.

title: Max Mobility/Permobil Recalls All SpeedControl Dial Components for SmartDrive MX2+ Due to Intermittent Connection Issues
headline: Max Mobility/Permobil Issues Worldwide Expanded Recall of All SpeedControl Dial Components for SmartDrive MX2+ Power Assist Device Citing Safety Risks
description: Max Mobility/Permobil recalls all SpeedControl Dial components for SmartDrive MX2+ due to intermittent electrical connection causing unintended motor activation and injury risks.
keywords: Due to potential safety and performance concerns
reason: Due to potential safety and performance concerns

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Max Mobility, LLC., under the Max Mobility/Permobil brand, has announced a worldwide expanded recall and removal of all SpeedControl Dial components used with the SmartDrive MX2+ Power Assist Device. This voluntary action addresses potential safety and performance concerns related to intermittent electrical connections between the SpeedControl Dial and the SmartDrive MX2+ motor.

The affected SpeedControl Dials were manufactured and distributed between April 25, 2022, and July 8, 2025. Users of the SpeedControl Dial have experienced incidents where the device either continued to drive when the dial was pressed or experienced unintended activation of the motor without user input. These issues may lead to involuntary movement or failure to stop, posing risks of minor to serious injuries, including cuts, bruises, bone fractures, and concussions.

This expanded recall supersedes previous recalls related to a printed circuit board assembly issue affecting units manufactured and distributed between August 17, 2023, and March 10, 2025. Since the introduction of the SpeedControl Dial, Max Mobility/Permobil has received a total of 781 complaints, including seven involving serious injuries.

The SpeedControl Dial will no longer be distributed or offered as a controller option for the SmartDrive MX2+. Alternative control options remain available, including the wired SwitchControl with an optional Buddy Button accessory and app-based controls via wearable devices. Users are encouraged to contact their SmartDrive provider or healthcare professional to determine the best alternative control solution.

Part Number Description Manufacture and Distribution Dates
MX2-3DCK SpeedControl Dial Component April 25, 2022 – July 8, 2025
MX2-3DC SpeedControl Dial Component April 25, 2022 – July 8, 2025
MX2-3DCMC SpeedControl Dial Component April 25, 2022 – July 8, 2025

Max Mobility/Permobil has suspended distribution of the SpeedControl Dial and advises that the SmartDrive MX2+ device itself is unaffected when used with other control methods.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Immediately stop using the SpeedControl Dial component if you have one and switch to an alternate control method such as the wired SwitchControl or app-based controls. Contact Max Mobility/Permobil for replacement options, technical support at 615-257-2930, or email sales.smartdrive@permobil.com.

content.text:

title: Dollar General Recalls Clover Valley® Instant Coffee Due to Potential Glass Fragments
headline: Dollar General Voluntarily Recalls Clover Valley® Instant Coffee Over Glass Contamination Concern
description: Dollar General recalls Clover Valley® Instant Coffee sold between July 9-21, 2025 due to potential glass fragments. No injuries reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Dollar General Corporation has announced a voluntary recall of three lots of its Clover Valley® Instant Coffee due to the potential presence of glass fragments in the product. The recalled items are the 8-ounce jars of Clover Valley® Instant Coffee, which were sold exclusively at Dollar General stores.

The affected lots were distributed between July 9 and July 21, 2025, in multiple states across the U.S. Consumers are advised not to use the product as ingesting glass can cause serious injuries such as damage to teeth, mouth and throat lacerations, or intestinal perforation. To date, no injuries or illnesses have been reported in connection with this issue.

The recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). Dollar General is investigating the source of the contamination and apologizes for the inconvenience.

Product Description UPC Lot Number Best By Date
8-Ounce Clover Valley® Instant Coffee 876941004069 L-5163 12/13/2026
8-Ounce Clover Valley® Instant Coffee 876941004069 L-5164 12/13/2026
8-Ounce Clover Valley® Instant Coffee 876941004069 L-5165 12/14/2026

The product was sold in the following states: Alabama, Arkansas, Arizona, California, Colorado, Connecticut, Delaware, Florida, Georgia, Iowa, Idaho, Illinois, Indiana, Kansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Massachusetts, Maryland, Maine, Michigan, Minnesota, Missouri, Mississippi, Montana, North Carolina, North Dakota, Nebraska, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New Mexico, Nevada, New York, Ohio, Oklahoma, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, South Carolina, South Dakota, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Virginia, Vermont, Washington, Wisconsin, West Virginia, and Wyoming.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: If you have purchased Clover Valley® Instant Coffee from the affected lots, do not consume it. Contact Dollar General for a full refund by emailing customercare@dollargeneral.com or calling 1-888-309-9030, available daily from 6 a.m. to 1 a.m. CST.

content.text:
