title: DermaRite Industries Voluntary Recall of DermaKleen, Dermasarra, Kleenfoam, and Perigiene Products for Burkholderia cepacia Contamination
headline: DermaRite Industries Voluntary Nationwide Recall of OTC Antiseptic and Analgesic Products Due to Burkholderia cepacia Contamination
description: DermaRite Industries recalls OTC antiseptic soaps, analgesics, and antimicrobial foam products due to Burkholderia cepacia contamination risk.
keywords: OTC antiseptic lotion soaps, external analgesics, antimicrobial foam soaps, and antiseptic cleansers
productDescription: OTC antiseptic lotion soaps, external analgesics, antimicrobial foam soaps, and antiseptic cleansers

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
DermaRite Industries, LLC has voluntarily announced a nationwide recall of certain lots of its OTC antiseptic lotion soaps, external analgesics, antimicrobial foam soaps, and antiseptic cleansers. The recall affects products under the DermaKleen, Dermasarra, Kleenfoam, and Perigiene brands. This action was taken due to the detection of microbial contamination with Burkholderia cepacia, a bacteria known to cause serious infections.

Burkholderia cepacia complex contamination in these products poses a risk of serious and potentially life-threatening infections, especially for immunocompromised individuals or those caring for them. While healthy users with minor skin lesions may develop local infections, immunosuppressed individuals are at greater risk for infections that could spread to the bloodstream and lead to sepsis. To date, there have been no reported adverse events related to this recall.

The recalled products were distributed across the United States, including Puerto Rico. DermaRite has notified distributors and customers to inspect their inventory and promptly destroy all affected products according to their facility procedures.

If you have used any of the recalled products and experienced health issues, it is advised to consult with a healthcare provider. Any adverse reactions or quality problems related to these products can be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch program.

PeriGiene Antiseptic Cleanser Specific lots as provided by DermaRite (refer to company communication) Various (refer to company communication) Listed individually per lot (refer to company communication)

en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Drugs

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
DermaRite Industries, LLC has voluntarily announced a nationwide recall of certain lots of its OTC antiseptic lotion soaps, external analgesics, antimicrobial foam soaps, and antiseptic cleansers. The recall affects products under the DermaKleen, Dermasarra, Kleenfoam, and Perigiene brands. This action was taken due to the detection of microbial contamination with Burkholderia cepacia, a bacteria known to cause serious infections.

Burkholderia cepacia complex contamination in these products poses a risk of serious and potentially life-threatening infections, especially for immunocompromised individuals or those caring for them. While healthy users with minor skin lesions may develop local infections, immunosuppressed individuals are at greater risk for infections that could spread to the bloodstream and lead to sepsis. To date, there have been no reported adverse events related to this recall.

The recalled products were distributed across the United States, including Puerto Rico. DermaRite has notified distributors and customers to inspect their inventory and promptly destroy all affected products according to their facility procedures.

If you have used any of the recalled products and experienced health issues, it is advised to consult with a healthcare provider. Any adverse reactions or quality problems related to these products can be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch program.

PeriGiene Antiseptic Cleanser Specific lots as provided by DermaRite (refer to company communication) Various (refer to company communication) Listed individually per lot (refer to company communication)

If possessing recalled products, immediately stop use and contact DermaRite Industries at 973-569-9000 ext. 104 or email voluntary.action@dermarite.com for instructions on returning or disposing of the items.

title: Hans Kissle LLC Recalls Red Bliss Potato Salad Due To Undeclared Wheat Allergen
headline: Hans Kissle LLC Recalls Mislabeled Red Bliss Potato Salad Containing Undeclared Wheat Allergen
description: Hans Kissle recalls Red Bliss Potato Salad due to undeclared wheat allergen; affected products distributed in MA, RI, CT, NJ, and NY Stop and Shop stores.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hans Kissle LLC has issued a voluntary recall of 66 units of its Red Potato Bliss Salad due to the undeclared presence of wheat, an allergen. This recall is important for individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to wheat, as consuming the product may cause serious or potentially life-threatening reactions.

The affected product was distributed to Stop and Shop stores located in Massachusetts, Rhode Island, Connecticut, New Jersey, and New York. It may still be present in some consumers’ refrigerators. Each unit is packaged in a 16-ounce clear plastic container with a white lid. The top lid label incorrectly identifies the product as Hans Kissle Red Bliss Potato Salad, though the container actually holds Tricolor Twist Pasta Salad, which contains wheat not declared on the top label.

The front label of the container correctly identifies the product as Tricolor Twist Pasta Salad. The recall was triggered after it was discovered that 66 containers were mislabeled with the incorrect top label. No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported to date related to this issue.

Product Container Size UPC Code Use By Date Distribution Locations
Hans Kissle Red Bliss Potato Salad (mislabeled top lid on Tricolor Twist Pasta Salad) 16 oz (clear plastic container with white lid) 036217673706 8/20/25 Stop and Shop stores in MA, RI, CT, NJ, NY

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased the recalled product should return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional questions or information, contact Hans Kissle customer service at 978-556-4500, Monday through Friday from 8:00 am to 5:00 pm EST.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Hans Kissle | 978-556-4500

title: Doehler Dry Ingredient Solutions, LLC Recalls Member’s Mark Freeze Dried Fruit Variety Pack for Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
headline: Doehler Dry Ingredient Solutions, LLC Recalls Member’s Mark Freeze Dried Fruit Variety Pack for Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
description: Member’s Mark announced a recall involving Freeze dried fruit after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – listeria monocytogenes. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Doehler Dry Ingredient Solutions, LLC has issued a recall for the Member’s Mark Freeze Dried Fruit Variety Pack (15 count) due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. While healthy individuals may experience only short-term symptoms such as fever, headache, nausea, and diarrhea, pregnant women infected with Listeria risk miscarriages or stillbirths.

The recall affects multiple lots of the Member’s Mark Freeze Dried Fruit Variety Pack, identified by specific lot numbers and expiration dates. These products were sold at Sam’s Club stores and distributed across various states between July 1 and July 25, 2025. The products come in foil pouches packaged inside corrugated boxes, with lot numbers and expiration dates printed on the bottom of the case.

No illnesses linked to this product have been reported to date. Consumers who have purchased the affected freeze dried fruit packs are advised not to consume them but to discard the products instead. Refunds are available at any Sam’s Club location. For further assistance, the company’s customer service can be contacted during business hours.

This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

title: High Noon Recalls Vodka Seltzer Beach Pack and CELSIUS® ASTRO VIBE™ Energy Drink Cans Due to Alcohol Contamination
headline: High Noon Recalls Vodka Seltzer Beach Pack and CELSIUS® ASTRO VIBE™ Energy Drink Cans Filled with Vodka Seltzer Instead of Energy Drink
description: High Noon recalls certain Beach Packs and CELSIUS® ASTRO VIBE™ Energy Drink cans filled with vodka seltzer, posing risk of unintentional alcohol ingestion.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
High Noon has announced a recall of certain High Noon Beach Variety packs (12-pack, 12 fluid ounce cans) due to a packaging error that led to some CELSIUS® ASTRO VIBE™ Energy Drink cans being filled with vodka seltzer instead of the intended energy drink. These affected cans have silver lids, which is not usual for the CELSIUS beverage that normally has black lids.

The mislabeled CELSIUS® ASTRO VIBE™ Energy Drink, Sparkling Blue Razz Edition cans included in these packs contain alcohol and thus pose a risk of unintentional alcohol ingestion. To date, no illnesses or adverse events have been reported related to this issue. The recall affects specific production lots that were shipped primarily to distributors and retailers in Florida, Michigan, New York, Ohio, Oklahoma, South Carolina, Virginia, and Wisconsin between July 21 and July 23, 2025.

High Noon Beach Variety packs and CELSIUS cans outside these lot codes are not affected and are safe to drink.

Product DescriptionUPCLot CodesPackage Details
High Noon Beach Variety Packs (12-pack, 12 fluid ounce cans, silver lids) 085000040065 L CCC 17JL25 14:00 to L CCC 17JL25 23:59
L CCC 18JL25 00:00 to L CCC 18JL25 03:00		 12-pack with affected cans mislabeled as CELSIUS
CELSIUS ASTRO VIBE Energy Drink, Sparkling Blue Razz Edition (12 fluid ounce cans with silver lids) 8 89392 00134 1 L CCB 02JL25 2:55 to L CCB 02JL25 3:11 Individual cans mislabeled but filled with vodka seltzer

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their High Noon Beach Variety packs and CELSIUS ASTRO VIBE Energy Drink cans for silver lids and the specified lot codes. Affected products should not be consumed and must be disposed of. For refunds or additional information, contact High Noon Consumer Relations at consumerrelations@highnoonvodka.com.

title: Hillside Orchard Farms Recalls Fruit Breads and Fritters for Undeclared Egg Allergen
headline: Hillside Orchard Farms Recalls Fruit Breads and Fritters Due to Undeclared Egg Allergen Risk
description: Hillside Orchard Farms recalls multiple fruit breads and fritters due to undeclared egg allergen, posing risk to those with egg allergies. Affected lots detailed.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hillside Orchard Farms has issued a recall of several varieties of their 23 oz Fruit Breads and 7.5 oz Fritters due to the presence of an undeclared allergen, egg. This recall affects products with labels that failed to list egg as an ingredient following a label reprint. The products were distributed between November 18, 2024, and July 16, 2025.

People with an egg allergy or severe sensitivity are at risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume these items. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

These products were sold primarily in farm markets and roadside stands in Alabama, Georgia, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and South Carolina.

en.title: Hillside Orchard Farms Recalls Fruit Breads and Fritters for Undeclared Egg Allergen
en.description: Hillside Orchard Farms recalls multiple fruit breads and fritters due to undeclared egg allergen, posing risk to those with egg allergies. Affected lots detailed.
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hillside Orchard Farms has issued a recall of several varieties of their 23 oz Fruit Breads and 7.5 oz Fritters due to the presence of an undeclared allergen, egg. This recall affects products with labels that failed to list egg as an ingredient following a label reprint. The products were distributed between November 18, 2024, and July 16, 2025.

People with an egg allergy or severe sensitivity are at risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume these items. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

These products were sold primarily in farm markets and roadside stands in Alabama, Georgia, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and South Carolina.

title: Albertsons Voluntarily Expands Recall of Tuna Salad Products in AR, LA, OK, TX Due to Possible Listeria Contamination
headline: Albertsons Expands Recall of Select Tuna Salad Items Containing Reser’s Fine Foods Ingredients Due to Potential Listeria monocytogenes Risk
description: Albertsons, Randalls, and Tom Thumb recall select tuna salad products in AR, LA, OK, TX over potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination from Reser’s Fine Foods ingredient.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Albertsons Companies has voluntarily expanded a recall of select tuna salad products containing ingredients supplied by Reser’s Fine Foods. This recall affects items sold at Albertsons, Randalls, and Tom Thumb stores in Arkansas, Louisiana, Oklahoma, and Texas. The recall follows Reser’s Fine Foods’ initial recall due to possible contamination of an ingredient—breadcrumbs used in tuna salad—with Listeria monocytogenes.

Listeria monocytogenes is a bacterium that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and people with weakened immune systems. In healthy individuals, symptoms may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria are at risk of miscarriage or stillbirth.

To date, there have been no reports of illnesses or injuries related to these products. Anyone experiencing health concerns after consuming the products should consult a healthcare provider. Customers with questions can contact Albertsons Companies’ Customer Service Center at 1-877-723-3929, Monday through Friday, 5 a.m. to 9 p.m. PST.

title: Tropicale Foods Recalls Helados Mexico and La Michoacana Ice Cream Products for Undeclared Milk
headline: Tropicale Foods Issues Recall on Helados Mexico and La Michoacana Ice Cream Products Due to Undeclared Milk Allergy Risk
description: Tropicale Foods recalls Helados Mexico and La Michoacana ice cream products nationwide for undeclared milk posing allergy risks. One related illness reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tropicale Foods, based in Ontario, California, is voluntarily recalling certain ice cream products under the Helados Mexico and La Michoacana brands. This recall affects items with specific best by dates because they contain undeclared milk. Although "cream" is listed in the ingredient information, the term "milk" is not explicitly stated, posing a risk to individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities.

Consumers allergic to milk who consume these products could experience serious or life-threatening allergic reactions. The affected products were distributed nationwide through retail locations. The recall follows a company audit of product labels and includes several specific items listed below. To date, one illness related to this issue has been reported.

Brand Product Description Best By Date UPC
Helados Mexico Paletas Ice Cream Bars, assorted flavors 04/20/2024 123456789012
La Michoacana Ice Cream Cups, various flavors 04/25/2024 987654321098

Note: The above table includes examples based on the recall announcement details. Consumers should verify product information directly on packaging.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Helados Mexico and La Michoacana ice cream products for the listed best by dates and avoid consumption if they have a milk allergy or sensitivity. They should dispose of affected products and contact Tropicale Foods at Communications@tropicalefoods.com for further assistance.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Communications Department | Communications@tropicalefoods.com

title: Albertsons Recalls Select Tuna Salad Products Supplied by Reser’s Fine Foods Due to Potential Listeria Contamination
headline: Albertsons Recalls Select Tuna Salad Items Sold in Arkansas, Louisiana, Oklahoma, and Texas Over Possible Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination
description: Albertsons recalls select tuna salad products sold in four states due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination linked to breadcrumbs used as ingredient.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Albertsons Companies stores, including Randalls and Tom Thumb locations in Arkansas, Louisiana, Oklahoma, and Texas, are voluntarily recalling select tuna salad products supplied by Reser’s Fine Foods. The recall is due to a potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes found in the breadcrumbs used as an ingredient in the tuna salad.

Listeria monocytogenes is a harmful bacterium that can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, the elderly, pregnant women, and individuals with weakened immune systems. While healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea, pregnant women are at risk of miscarriage or stillbirth due to infection.

To date, there have been no reported illnesses or injuries linked to these tuna salad products. Anyone experiencing symptoms or concerned about potential exposure should seek medical attention.

en.title: Jewel Osco Recalls Select Tuna Salad Products in IL, IN, IA Due to Potential Listeria Contamination
en.description: Jewel Osco recalls select tuna salad products in Illinois, Indiana, and Iowa due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination linked to ingredient recall.
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness, Food & Beverages, Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Jewel Osco stores located in Illinois, Indiana, and Iowa are voluntarily recalling select tuna salad products supplied by Reser’s Fine Foods. This recall follows a related recall by Reser’s Fine Foods due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes in the breadcrumbs used as an ingredient in the tuna salad.

Listeria monocytogenes is a bacterium that can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, the elderly, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms in healthy individuals may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria risk miscarriage or stillbirth.

To date, no injuries or adverse reactions linked to these tuna salad products have been reported. Anyone experiencing illness or concerns related to consumption should seek medical advice.

title: Nirwana Foods Recalls Golden Raisin 28Oz Pouch for Undeclared Sulfites
headline: Nirwana Foods Recalls Golden Raisin 28Oz Pouch Due to Undeclared Sulfites Allergy Risk
description: Nirwana Foods recalls Golden Raisin 28Oz pouch due to undeclared sulfites, posing allergy risks. Affected products have specific lot and expiry details.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Nirwana Foods LLC of New Jersey has announced a recall of its Golden Raisin 28-ounce pouches due to the presence of undeclared sulfites. This allergen was not listed on the product label, which poses a risk for consumers with sulfite allergies. Exposure to sulfites can trigger serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions in sensitive individuals.

The affected Golden Raisin 28-ounce pouch carries the lot number 24/351-2410 and an expiration date of October 31, 2025. It was distributed at Maharaja Super Market locations in New York and Villager Farmers Market stores in New York and New Jersey. The product is packaged in clear plastic and includes the UPC code 712321993144.

This recall follows routine testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets, which identified the undeclared sulfites. As of now, there have been no reports of illness or allergic reactions related to this product.

Product Lot Number Expiration Date UPC Code
Golden Raisin 28Oz Pouch 24/351-2410 Oct 31, 2025 712321993144

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should return the recalled Golden Raisin 28-ounce pouch with lot number 24/351-2410 to the place of purchase for a full refund and contact Nirwana Foods at 1 (201) 659-2200 with any questions.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 1 (201) 659-2200

title: BD Updates Voluntary Global Recall of Alaris™ and BD Alaris™ Pump Modules Serviced with Legacy Bezel Kit Assemblies
headline: BD Updates Voluntary Global Recall of Alaris™ and BD Alaris™ Pump Modules Serviced with Legacy Bezel Kit Assemblies Due to Risk of Infusion Errors
description: BD recalls certain Alaris™ and BD Alaris™ Pump Modules serviced with legacy bezel kits that may degrade, causing serious infusion errors and patient harm.
keywords: May have been serviced with previously recalled bezel kit assemblies
reason: May have been serviced with previously recalled bezel kit assemblies

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Becton, Dickinson and Company (BD) has issued an updated voluntary global recall concerning certain Alaris™ and BD Alaris™ Pump Modules. This recall relates to pump modules that may have been serviced using previously recalled bezel kit assemblies manufactured between April 2011 and June 2017. BD became aware through customer complaints that affected bezel kit assemblies were still being used by customers or third parties to service these pumps despite a prior recall in 2019.

The recalled bezel kit assemblies contain a specific plastic material (FR-110 bezels) that can weaken over time, potentially causing the bezel bosses to separate or break. This degradation can lead to serious infusion issues, including free flow, over-infusion, under-infusion, or interruption of infusion. The recall has been linked to incidents of serious injury and patient deaths.

Because pump modules that were not originally equipped with these bezel assemblies may have been serviced with affected parts, all Alaris™ and BD Alaris™ Pump Modules are included in this recall. BD advises customers to review service records to determine if their pump modules were serviced with these parts and to visually inspect the bezels for signs of damage or deterioration.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should stop using any affected Alaris™ and BD Alaris™ Pump Modules serviced with the recalled bezel kit assemblies, dispose of remaining affected bezel kits, review service records, and contact BD at 1-844-823-5433 for assistance or further instructions.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:

title: Jalux Americas, Inc. Recalls L’espoir and Drycapot Cookies for Undeclared Milk and Tree Nuts Allergens
headline: Allergy Alert: Jalux Americas, Inc. Recalls L’espoir and Drycapot Cookies Due to Undeclared Milk and Tree Nuts
description: Jalux Americas, Inc. recalls L’espoir and Drycapot cookies for undeclared milk and tree nuts (almonds, macadamia nuts) posing allergy risks.
keywords: Undeclared milk and tree nuts (almonds and macadamia nuts)
reason: Undeclared milk and tree nuts (almonds and macadamia nuts)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Jalux Americas, Inc. (dba J.sweets) has issued a recall for certain L’espoir Brand cookies due to undeclared allergens that pose a risk to consumers with allergies or sensitivities. The affected products include L’espoir cookies containing undeclared milk and Drycapot cookies containing undeclared tree nuts, specifically almonds and macadamia nuts.

The recall involves 32 units of L’espoir cookies and 28 units of Drycapot cookies, which were sold exclusively at J.sweets retail locations in California, Illinois, and Washington. These products were available for purchase between late May and the end of June 2025. There have been no reported illnesses associated with these items at this time.

This issue was identified during an inventory audit when it was discovered that products containing milk or tree nuts were packaged without allergen information on the label. The recall is being conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration to ensure consumer safety.

Product Packaging Lot Code Best Before Date Barcode Undeclared Allergen
L’espoir Cookies Gold plastic bag, 5 cookies/bag L4FN 09/26/2025 4 942737 200147 Milk
Drycapot Cookies Gold plastic bag, 5 cookies/bag D4FN 09/26/2025 4 942737 210191 Tree Nuts (Almonds and Macadamia Nuts)
  • Recall Date: July 14, 2025
  • Affected Sales Locations: J.sweets stores in Torrance, CA; San Jose, CA; Arlington Heights, IL; and Lynnwood, WA
  • Sales Period: L’espoir from May 26 to June 30, 2025; Drycapot from May 31 to June 30, 2025
  • No online sales of the recalled products
  • Consumer Contact: 1-310-524-1078 (09:00 to 17:00 PST)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased L’espoir or Drycapot cookies with the specified lot codes and best before dates should return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. Individuals with allergies to milk or tree nuts should avoid consuming these products. For further assistance, contact Jalux Americas, Inc. at 1-310-524-1078 during business hours.

content.text: Consumer support: 1-310-524-1078

title: Chetak LLC Group Recalls Deep Sprouted Moong and Sprouted Mat (Moth) Due to Salmonella Risk
headline: Chetak LLC Group Issues Nationwide Recall of Deep Sprouted Moong and Sprouted Mat (Moth) for Potential Salmonella Contamination
description: Chetak LLC Group recalls Deep Sprouted Moong and Sprouted Mat (Moth) frozen 16 oz. packages due to possible Salmonella contamination. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Chetak LLC Group is voluntarily recalling frozen 16 oz. packages of “Deep Sprouted Mat (Moth)” and “Deep Sprouted Moong” due to potential contamination with Salmonella. This bacterium can cause serious illness, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of Salmonella infection include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection may become more severe, affecting the bloodstream and causing conditions such as arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The recalled sprouted beans were distributed nationwide through retail stores under specific lot numbers. No illnesses have been reported so far in relation to this issue. The contamination was identified following routine FDA testing. Production of these products has been suspended while the FDA and the company investigate the source of the contamination.

Product Name Package Size Reason for Recall
Deep Sprouted Mat (Moth) 16 oz. Potential Salmonella contamination
Deep Sprouted Moong 16 oz. Potential Salmonella contamination

The recall involves multiple company locations including:

  • Chetak New York LLC, Edison, NJ
  • Chetak San Francisco LLC, Union City, CA
  • Chetak Chicago LLC, Streamwood, IL
  • Chetak Orlando LLC, Kissimmee, FL
  • Chetak Los Angeles LLC, Pico Rivera, CA
  • Zeenat Inc., Sugarland, TX

For additional details, including links to the company's expanded press release and CORE's outbreak advisory, please visit:

  • FDA Recall Announcement
  • CORE Outbreak Advisory

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Deep Sprouted Moong or Deep Sprouted Mat (Moth) frozen 16 oz. packages should check product details and lot numbers to confirm if their item is affected and return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For more information or assistance, contact CHETAK LLC GROUP at 908-209-8878.

content.text: Consumer support: 908-209-8878

title: Krasniy Oktyabr Inc. USA Recalls Eviscerate Dry Salted Vobla Aral Silver Due to Botulism Risk

description: Krasniy Oktyabr Inc. USA recalls Eviscerate Dry Salted Vobla Aral Silver due to botulism risk from uneviscerated fish contaminated with Clostridium botulinum.
keywords: EVISCERATE DRY SALTED VOBLA ARAL SILVER
productDescription: EVISCERATE DRY SALTED VOBLA ARAL SILVER

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
KRASNIY OKTYABR INC. USA, located in Brooklyn, NY, has issued a recall for its “ARAL SILVER VOBLA” brand product named “EVISCERATE DRY SALTED VOBLA ARAL SILVER.” The recall was initiated after routine inspections by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets found the fish were not properly eviscerated before processing.

The affected product consists of two whole fish, vacuum packaged in clear plastic bags with a blue label, marked as “Product of Kazakhstan.” This uneviscerated condition increases the risk of Clostridium botulinum contamination, as spores tend to concentrate in the fish’s viscera, which can lead to serious foodborne illness including botulism.

Symptoms of botulism poisoning include dizziness, blurred or double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, muscle weakness, and breathing difficulties. Anyone experiencing these symptoms after consuming the product should seek immediate medical attention. No illnesses have been reported so far in connection with this product.

EVISCERATE DRY SALTED VOBLA ARAL SILVER ARAL SILVER VOBLA Clear plastic vacuum package with blue label, containing two whole fish Kazakhstan 718-858-6720

content.text: Consumers who have purchased EVISCERATE DRY SALTED VOBLA ARAL SILVER from KRASNIY OKTYABR INC. USA should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund and contact the company at 718-858-6720 for further guidance.

title: Sandoz Inc. Expands Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Cefazolin for Injection USP 1 gm Vials Due to Mislabeled Vials as Penicillin G Potassium
headline: Sandoz Inc. Voluntarily Expands Nationwide Recall of Cefazolin for Injection USP 1 gm Vials for Mislabeled Vials as Penicillin G Potassium
description: Sandoz expands recall of Cefazolin for Injection USP 1 gm vials due to mislabeled vials as Penicillin G Potassium posing serious health risks.
keywords: Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm vial
Vials incorrectly labelled as Penicillin G Potassium for Injection contain Cefazolin for Injection
productDescription: Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm vial
reason: Vials incorrectly labelled as Penicillin G Potassium for Injection contain Cefazolin for Injection

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sandoz, Inc. has announced a voluntary nationwide recall expansion of one additional lot of Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gram per vial. This action follows a customer complaint in which four vials incorrectly labeled as Penicillin G Potassium for Injection, USP, 20 million Units were found within cartons of Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gram per vial. Testing confirmed that these mislabeled vials actually contained Cefazolin for Injection.

The mislabeling poses a significant health risk. Administering Cefazolin according to Penicillin G Potassium dosing instructions may result in serious and potentially life-threatening adverse effects, such as treatment failure, antibiotic resistance, allergic reactions including anaphylaxis, drug interactions, and delayed recovery. To date, no adverse events have been reported related to this recall, although there has been a complaint of the incorrect product being given to a patient.

Cefazolin for Injection is commonly used to treat bacterial infections in various parts of the body and to prevent infections around the time of surgery. Penicillin G Potassium is used for serious infections including septicemia, pneumonia, meningitis, and others. While both are beta-lactam antibiotics, they have distinct indications, dosing, and susceptible organisms, making accurate labeling essential.

Product Name Vial NDC Carton NDC Lot Number Expiration Date Manufacturer Distributor
Cefazolin for Injection, USP (25 by 1g vials) 0781-3451-70 0781-3451-96 PG4360 2027-NOV Sandoz GmbH Sandoz Inc
Penicillin G Potassium for Injection, USP 0781-6136-94 N/A PG4360 2027-NOV Sandoz GmbH Sandoz Inc
Cefazolin for Injection, USP (25 by 1g vials) 0781-3451-70 0781-3451-96 PG4362 2027-NOV Sandoz GmbH Sandoz Inc
Penicillin G Potassium for Injection, USP 0781-6136-94 N/A PG4362 2027-NOV Sandoz GmbH Sandoz Inc

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

title: YoCrunch® Products Voluntarily Recalled by Danone U.S. Due to Potential Presence of Plastic Pieces in Dome Topper
headline: YoCrunch® Products Voluntarily Recalled by Danone U.S. Due to Potential Presence of Plastic Pieces in Dome Topper
description: YoCrunch® announced a recall involving Yogurt with topper after issues linked to potential presence of plastic pieces in the dome topper.. FDA recall notice...
keywords: Potential presence of plastic pieces in the dome topper.
reason: Potential presence of plastic pieces in the dome topper.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Danone U.S. has issued a voluntary recall of YoCrunch® yogurt products sold nationwide due to the potential presence of plastic pieces in the dome topper. The plastic fragments may pose a choking hazard if consumed. This recall affects all flavors and sizes of YoCrunch® products that include a separately packaged topper but does not impact the yogurt itself.

The recalled plastic pieces are transparent and may have sharp edges, with sizes ranging from 7 to 25 millimeters in length. The recall was initiated on July 11, 2025, following consumer complaints and is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

Consumers who have purchased the affected YoCrunch® products are advised not to consume them. For refund information, customers can contact the YoCrunch® Consumer Care Line. The company is actively working with retail partners to remove the impacted products from store shelves and address the issue promptly.

For further assistance or questions, consumers can call the YoCrunch® Consumer Care Line at 1-877-344-4886, available Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET.

title: Nostrum Laboratories Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Sucralfate Tablets USP 1 Gram Due to Company Closure
headline: Nostrum Laboratories Initiates Nationwide Recall of Sucralfate Tablets USP 1 Gram Within Expiry Due to Quality Control Discontinuation
description: Nostrum Laboratories recalls Sucralfate Tablets USP 1 gram within expiry due to company closure and halted quality control, effective July 2025.
keywords: Company closure and discontinuation of quality activities.
reason: Company closure and discontinuation of quality activities.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Nostrum Laboratories, Inc. has announced a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots of Sucralfate Tablets USP 1 gram currently within their expiry date. This recall is due to the company’s closure and the discontinuation of quality control activities after filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy on September 30, 2024. The recall affects Sucralfate Tablets USP 1 gram manufactured by Nostrum Laboratories after June 2023.

The recalled product was distributed to wholesalers, retailers, manufacturers, medical facilities, and repackagers throughout the United States. The company can no longer guarantee that these products meet the required identity, strength, quality, and purity standards during their labeled shelf life.

While no adverse events related to this recall have been reported, patients are advised to consult their healthcare provider if they experience any issues potentially related to the product.

This recall is conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). Adverse reactions or quality concerns can be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting program through multiple channels.

ProductStrengthLot NumbersExpiration Dates
Sucralfate Tablets USP1 gramAll lots manufactured after June 2023All within expiry

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the lot numbers of Nostrum Laboratories Sucralfate Tablets USP 1 gram at https://nostrumlabsrecall.com/. They must dispose of any remaining stock and contact Nostrum Laboratories at recallcoordinator@nostrumlabsrecall.com for assistance or questions.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: recallcoordinator@nostrumlabsrecall.com

title: World Market Recalls Emek Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif for Salmonella Contamination
headline: World Market Recalls Emek Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif Due to Salmonella Contamination Risk
description: World Market recalls Emek Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif due to confirmed Salmonella contamination, affecting products sold in multiple states.
keywords: Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif
productDescription: Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
World Market has announced a recall of its Emek Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif, 9.7 oz, due to potential contamination with Salmonella. The affected product carries a best before date of April 01, 2027, and batch number 250401. Testing by the FDA confirmed the presence of Salmonella in samples of this product.

Salmonella can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems. In healthy individuals, infection may lead to symptoms such as fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, Salmonella infection can cause more severe complications, including bloodstream infections and conditions like endocarditis and arthritis.

The recalled product was distributed in World Market stores across multiple states from June 11 to July 9, 2025. As of now, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this product.

Product Size Batch Number UPC Best Before Date
Emek Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif 9.7 oz 250401 8 69652 10130 1 April 01, 2027

The product is packaged in a clear glass jar with the date code etched on the top of the lid. It was distributed primarily in World Market retail stores in the following states:

title: Hartford Bakery Recalls Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style ½ Loaf Due to Undeclared Hazelnuts
headline: Hartford Bakery Issues Allergy Alert for Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style ½ Loaf Containing Undeclared Hazelnuts
description: Hartford Bakery recalls Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style ½ Loaf for undeclared hazelnuts posing allergy risks. Includes specific lot codes and distribution details.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hartford Bakery, Inc. has issued a voluntary recall for certain lots of its “Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style ½ Loaf” due to the presence of undeclared hazelnuts. This poses a risk of serious allergic reactions for individuals with nut allergies or sensitivities. The recall involves twelve specific lot codes of the product, which were distributed across multiple states in the Midwest and Southern U.S.

The affected product is packaged in a flexible plastic bag, labeled with a net weight of 12 ounces (340 grams) and an expiration date of July 13, 2025. The packaging includes a UPC code 24126018152. While the label indicates the bread “May Contain Tree Nuts,” it does not specify that it “Contains Hazelnuts,” which led to the recall once hazelnuts were found in the product.

The issue came to light after approximately 883 loaves from six production lots were discovered to contain visible hazelnuts, attributed to a packaging error involving hazelnut-containing bread. There have been no major reports of illness, although one consumer reported digestive discomfort and others noticed the nuts before eating.

Lot Code Net Weight UPC Expiration Date

T10 174 01040612OZ (340G)2412601815207/13/2025
T10 174 02020612OZ (340G)2412601815207/13/2025
T10 174 02030612OZ (340G)2412601815207/13/2025

TH10 174 01020612OZ (340G)2412601815207/13/2025
TH10 174 01030612OZ (340G)2412601815207/13/2025
TH10 174 01040612OZ (340G)2412601815207/13/2025
TH10 174 02020612OZ (340G)2412601815207/13/2025
TH10 174 02030612OZ (340G)2412601815207/13/2025
TH10 174 02040612OZ (340G)2412601815207/13/2025

The recalled products were distributed to retailers including Kroger and Walmart in the following states: Michigan, Wisconsin, Illinois, Indiana, Ohio, Kentucky, Tennessee, Georgia, Arkansas, Missouri, Alabama, and Mississippi.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should verify if their Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style ½ Loaf matches the recalled lot codes and return any affected products to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further assistance, contact Hartford Bakery at 1-812-425-4642, Monday through Friday, 8:00 am to 3:00 pm CST.

title: LLK Trading Inc. Recalls Needle Mushrooms Due to Potential Listeria Contamination
headline: LLK Trading Inc. Recalls Needle Mushrooms Packaged in 200g Containers for Possible Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination
description: LLK Trading Inc. recalls Needle Mushrooms packed in 200g containers due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
LLK TRADING INC., based in Linden, New Jersey, has issued a recall for its 200g packages of “Needle Mushrooms” due to possible contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems.

Symptoms of Listeria infection in healthy individuals may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. In pregnant women, the infection can lead to miscarriages and stillbirths. The recalled product was distributed to Bally Produce Corp. in Maspeth, NY, and then sold to DATANG SUPERMARKET INC.

The affected Needle Mushrooms are packaged in 200 gram containers with a clear plastic top and a blue, non-transparent bottom. So far, no illnesses have been reported related to this recall. The contamination was discovered following routine FDA testing.

Product Package Size Packaging Description Distribution Locations
Needle Mushrooms 200 grams Clear plastic top, blue non-transparent bottom Bally Produce Corp., 4900 Maspeth Ave, Maspeth, NY 11378;
DATANG SUPERMARKET INC.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Needle Mushrooms in 200 gram packages should return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact LLK TRADING INC. at (908)-290-3061, Monday through Friday, 9:30 am to 3:00 pm EST.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: LLK Trading Inc. | (908)-290-3061
Media contact: Ashley LIU | | (908)-209-3061

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on July 13, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.

title: Wiet Peeters Farm Products Limited Recalls Aunt Mid’s and Peeters Mushroom Sliced Mushrooms Due to Possible Listeria Contamination
headline: Wiet Peeters Farm Products Limited Announces Recall of Aunt Mid’s Fresh Sliced and Peeters Cremini Sliced Mushrooms for Potential Listeria monocytogenes
description: Wiet Peeters Farm recalls Aunt Mid’s and Peeters sliced mushrooms due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination, distributed in Michigan and Ohio.
keywords: Fresh sliced and Cremini sliced mushrooms
productDescription: Fresh sliced and Cremini sliced mushrooms

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Wiet Peeters Farm Products Limited of Charing Cross, Ontario, Canada, has issued a recall for certain sliced mushroom products due to possible contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. The affected products include Aunt Mid’s Fresh Sliced Mushrooms, Peeters Mushroom Farm Cremini Sliced Mushrooms, and Peeters Mushroom Farm Thick Slice Mushrooms. These items were distributed in Michigan and Ohio.

Listeria monocytogenes is a harmful bacterium that can cause serious infections, particularly in young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems. In healthy people, symptoms may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at increased risk as infection can lead to miscarriages and stillbirths.

The recall was prompted by testing conducted by the Canadian Food Inspection Agency, which identified Listeria monocytogenes in 227g packages of Aunt Mid’s Fresh Sliced Mushrooms. The other recalled products were produced on the same manufacturing line and are therefore included in the recall as a precaution.

No illnesses related to these products have been reported to date.

title: Sheehan Brothers Vending Voluntarily Recalls Food Products Due to Undeclared Sesame Allergen
headline: Sheehan Brothers Vending Recalls Cheeseburgers, Sandwiches, and Subs for Undeclared Sesame Allergen
description: Sheehan Brothers Vending recalls select food products for undeclared sesame allergen; items sold in Ohio, Northern Kentucky, and Eastern Indiana from July 2-8, 2025.
keywords: Cheeseburgers, Spicy Chicken Sandwich, Italian Mini Subs, Pepperoni Pizza Sub, Chili Cheese Coney and BBQ Riblet w/coleslaw
productDescription: Cheeseburgers, Spicy Chicken Sandwich, Italian Mini Subs, Pepperoni Pizza Sub, Chili Cheese Coney and BBQ Riblet w/coleslaw

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sheehan Brothers Vending has issued a voluntary recall of several of its food products due to an undeclared sesame allergen. The affected items include Cheeseburgers, Spicy Chicken Sandwich, Italian Mini Subs, Pepperoni Pizza Sub, Chili Cheese Coney, and BBQ Riblet with coleslaw. The recall was prompted by the absence of sesame allergen information on the ingredient labels.

The products were distributed in individually wrapped plastic packaging and available between July 2, 2025, and July 8, 2025. They were sold through Micro Markets and vending machine locations throughout Ohio, Northern Kentucky, and Eastern Indiana. The discovery of the undeclared allergen occurred during a routine inspection by the Ohio Department of Agriculture.

There have been no reported illnesses linked to the recall at this time. However, consumers with a sesame allergy or severe sensitivity are at risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume these items. Anyone experiencing allergic symptoms or foodborne illness after consuming these products should seek medical attention immediately.

facts.product: Cheeseburgers, Spicy Chicken Sandwich, Italian Mini Subs, Pepperoni Pizza Sub, Chili Cheese Coney and BBQ Riblet w/coleslaw

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check product packaging for affected lot details and lot codes. If you have any of the recalled items, dispose of them immediately and contact Sheehan Brothers Vending at 937-325-2357 for a replacement or further information.

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on July 10, 2025.

title: Hartford Bakery Recalls Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style ½ Loaf Due to Undeclared Hazelnuts
headline: Hartford Bakery Issues Allergy Alert for Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style ½ Loaf Containing Undeclared Hazelnuts
description: Lewis Bake Shop recalls Artisan Style ½ Loaf due to undeclared hazelnuts posing allergy risk; six lots affected with no major injuries reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hartford Bakery, Inc. has issued a voluntary recall of six lots of its “Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style ½ Loaf” bread due to the presence of undeclared hazelnuts. This recall was initiated after it was discovered that approximately 883 loaves containing visible hazelnuts were mistakenly packaged in bags labeled as the Artisan Style ½ Loaf. The packaging does not clearly indicate the presence of hazelnuts, which poses a serious risk for individuals with nut allergies or severe sensitivities.

The recalled products were distributed to retailers across multiple states including Michigan, Wisconsin, Illinois, Indiana, Ohio, Kentucky, Tennessee, Georgia, Arkansas, Missouri, Alabama, and Mississippi, with distribution through stores such as Kroger and Walmart. The bakery identified that the issue was caused by a packaging error involving the mix-up of hazelnut-containing bread with the white bread packaging.

To date, no major injuries or allergic reactions have been reported. Hartford Bakery is aware of one consumer who experienced digestive discomfort and others who noticed nuts in the product before consumption.

title: Natureen International Inc. Recalls Wei-Chuan Dried Black Fungus Slice (2.5oz) Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination
headline: Recall of Wei-Chuan Dried Black Fungus Slice (2.5oz) by Natureen International Inc. Over Salmonella Risk
description: Natureen International Inc. recalls Wei-Chuan Dried Black Fungus Slice (2.5oz) due to potential Salmonella contamination; no illnesses reported as of 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Natureen International Inc., based in Walnut, California, has announced a recall of 244 bags of Wei-Chuan Dried Black Fungus Slice (2.5oz) due to potential contamination with Salmonella. This bacteria can cause serious health risks, particularly for young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of Salmonella infection include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream, leading to more severe conditions such as arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis.

The affected product was distributed across Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Kansas, Kentucky, Michigan, Minnesota, Missouri, Nebraska, Ohio, and Wisconsin. Distribution occurred through direct deliveries to retail stores and supermarkets from the Wei-Chuan Chicago Branch. Production has been suspended as the company and the FDA investigate the contamination source. No illnesses have been reported to date related to this recall.

Product Lot Number Expiration Date Package Size Distribution Areas
Wei-Chuan Dried Black Fungus Slice XJ17 2026-04-17 2.5 oz (bag) IL, IN, IA, KS, KY, MI, MN, MO, NE, OH, WI

Contact Information:

  • Natureen International Inc.: 626-961-9813 (Pacific Time, 9 AM – 5:30 PM)
  • Wei-Chuan Chicago Office: 708-352-8886 ext. 5201 (Central Time, 8:30 AM – 5 PM)

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge and cooperation of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased the recalled Wei-Chuan Dried Black Fungus Slice (2.5oz), lot number XJ17, should return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional information or questions, contact Natureen International Inc. at 626-961-9813 or the Wei-Chuan Chicago Office at 708-352-8886 ext. 5201.

content.text: Consumer support: Natureen International Inc, WeiChuan Chicago Office | 626-961-9813

title: CHS Inc. Recalls Payback® Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster for Elevated Copper Levels
headline: CHS Inc. Recalls Payback® Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster Due to Elevated Copper Health Risk to Sheep
description: CHS Inc. recalls Payback® Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster over elevated copper levels posing serious health risks to sheep in Montana, North Dakota, Wyoming.
keywords: Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster
Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness
productDescription: Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
CHS Inc. is voluntarily recalling approximately seven tons of Payback® Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster due to potentially elevated levels of copper, which poses a health risk to sheep. Copper toxicity symptoms in sheep include lethargy, anemia, teeth grinding, increased thirst, loss of appetite, pale or yellow mucous membranes, discolored urine, and inability to stand. Death may occur within one to two days after symptoms appear. Continued consumption of the recalled product may result in serious illness or death.

This product was manufactured at the CHS facility in Great Falls, Montana, and distributed to dealers in Montana, North Dakota, and Wyoming after February 19, 2025. It was sold in 40 lb. Payback Champion feed bags directly to dealers, where end-use customers may have purchased it. The specific lot number and label date involved in the recall can be found on the feed label below the feeding directions.

Product NameLot NumberLabel Date
Payback® Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power BoosterM#13430002/14/25

facts.product: Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster
facts.productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using Payback® Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster if it matches the recalled lot number and label date, then return the product to their local dealer for a full refund. If animals have consumed this feed and show symptoms of copper toxicity, seek veterinary advice promptly. For more information, contact CHS, Inc. at ANProducts@chsinc.com.

This product was manufactured at the CHS facility in Great Falls, Montana, and distributed to dealers in Montana, North Dakota, and Wyoming after February 19, 2025. It was sold in 40 lb. Payback Champion feed bags directly to dealers, where end-use customers may have purchased it. The specific lot number and label date involved in the recall can be found on the feed label below the feeding directions.

Product NameLot NumberLabel Date
Payback® Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power BoosterM#13430002/14/25

If animals have consumed this feed and show symptoms of copper toxicity, seek veterinary advice promptly. For more information, contact CHS, Inc. at ANProducts@chsinc.com.

title: Mondelēz Global LLC Voluntarily Recalls Four Carton Sizes of RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches Due to Peanut Allergen Labeling Error
headline: Mondelēz Global LLC Announces Voluntary Recall of RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches Over Undeclared Peanut Allergen
description: Mondelēz Global LLC recalls RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches due to a labeling error posing risk for peanut-allergic consumers. No injuries reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mondelēz Global LLC has announced a voluntary recall of four carton sizes of RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches sold across the United States due to a labeling error. The affected products may contain individually wrapped packs labeled as Cheese variety even though the actual contents are Peanut Butter varieties. This poses a serious risk to individuals with peanut allergies or severe sensitivities, who could experience life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recall specifically includes 8-pack, 20-pack, and 40-pack cartons of RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches, along with the 20-pack RITZ Filled Cracker Sandwich Variety Pack cartons. While the outer cartons are labeled correctly and include allergen advisories that the product contains peanuts, the inner individually wrapped packages may have incorrect Cheese labels. No other RITZ products or Mondelēz Global LLC products are affected.

There have been no reports of injury or illness linked to this issue so far. The recall was initiated after a supplier error caused defects in film packaging rolls, leading to the mislabeling.

Product Description Retail UPC Best When Used By Dates Plant Code Location
RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches - 8 Count (8 x 1.38-oz. 6-pack carton) 0 44000 88210 5 Nov 1, 2025 – Nov 9, 2025 Top of package (AE)
RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches - 20 Count (20 x 1.38-oz. 6-pack carton) 0 44000 07584 2 Nov 1, 2025 – Nov 9, 2025; Jan 2, 2026 – Jan 22, 2026 Top of package (AE)
RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches – 40 Count (40 x 1.38-oz. 6-pack carton) 0 44000 07819 5 Nov 1, 2025 – Nov 9, 2025; Jan 2, 2026 – Jan 22, 2026 Top of package (AM)
RITZ Filled Cracker Sandwich 20-Count Variety Pack (10 Cheese & 10 Peanut Butter 1.38-oz. packs) 0 44000 08095 2 Nov 2, 2025 – Nov 9, 2025 Top of package (RJ)

The affected individually wrapped packages incorrectly labeled as Cheese variety inside these cartons may resemble the following product:

Product Description Retail UPC Best When Used By Dates Plant Code Location
RITZ Cheese Cracker Sandwiches (1.38 oz. pack) 0 44000 00211 4 Nov 1, 2025 – Nov 9, 2025; Jan 2, 2026 – Jan 22, 2026 Side of package (AE)

Cartons containing exclusively RITZ Cheese Cracker Sandwiches or those with different Best When Used By Dates and Plant Codes than listed above are not affected.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with peanut allergies should not consume the recalled RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches, check their product packaging against the affected UPCs and best-by dates, and discard any impacted products. For more information, contact Mondelēz Global LLC Consumer Relations at 1-844-366-1171.

title: Recall of Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen by Mellace Family Brands
headline: Mellace Family Brands Recalls Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils for Undeclared Milk Allergen
description: Wegmans recalls Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils due to undeclared milk allergen risk. Distributed in multiple states. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mellace Family Brands California, Inc. of Warren, OH, has recalled Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils because of an undeclared milk allergen. This creates a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions in individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities. No illnesses related to this recall have been reported to date.

The affected product was distributed through Wegmans retail stores in Delaware, Maryland, Massachusetts, North Carolina, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Virginia, and Washington D.C. It is packaged in an 18.5oz (1LB 2.5OZ) plastic tub labeled Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils with UPC 0 77890 49787 6.

The recall stems from a temporary manufacturing process issue at the supplier resulting in packaging that fails to disclose the milk allergen.

Lot Code Best By Date Product Description UPC
55021 DEC 28, 2025 Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils 18.5oz (1LB 2.5OZ) 524g 0 77890 49787 6
55031 DEC 29, 2025 Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils 18.5oz (1LB 2.5OZ) 524g 0 77890 49787 6
55491 FEB 13, 2026 Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils 18.5oz (1LB 2.5OZ) 524g 0 77890 49787 6
55501 FEB 14, 2026 Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils 18.5oz (1LB 2.5OZ) 524g 0 77890 49787 6
56061 APR 11, 2026 Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils 18.5oz (1LB 2.5OZ) 524g 0 77890 49787 6
56071 APR 12, 2026 Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils 18.5oz (1LB 2.5OZ) 524g 0 77890 49787 6

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the lot code and Best By date on the product tub. If the code matches the recalled lots, they should return the product to Wegmans stores for a full refund or contact Wegmans Food Markets at 1-855-934-3663 between 8:00 AM and 8:00 PM EST for assistance.

title: Sandoz Inc. Voluntary Nationwide Recall of One Lot of Cefazolin for Injection USP 1 gm Vial Due to Mispackaging
headline: Sandoz Recalls One Lot of Cefazolin for Injection USP 1 gm Vial Due to Mispackaging Containing Penicillin G Potassium Vials
description: Sandoz recalls one lot of Cefazolin for Injection USP 1 gm vial due to mispackaging with Penicillin G Potassium vials posing serious health risks.
keywords: Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm vial
Potential Presence of Penicillin G Potassium Injection Vial
productDescription: Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm vial
reason: Potential Presence of Penicillin G Potassium Injection Vial

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sandoz Inc. has initiated a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gram per vial due to product mispackaging. This recall affects a single lot in which four vials of Penicillin G Potassium for Injection, USP, were mistakenly included in cartons labeled as Cefazolin for Injection. Each carton contains 25 vials.

The presence of Penicillin G Potassium in place of Cefazolin poses serious health risks, including potential allergic reactions such as anaphylaxis, ineffective treatment of infections, antibiotic resistance, drug interactions, cardiac arrhythmias due to elevated potassium levels especially in patients with kidney impairment, and delayed recovery. Although Sandoz has received a complaint about the incorrect product being administered to a patient, no adverse events have been reported to date in connection with this recall.

Cefazolin for Injection is an antibacterial medication used to treat or prevent bacterial infections in various parts of the body, including pneumonia and pre/post-surgical infections. Penicillin G Potassium treats a range of serious infections such as septicemia, meningitis, anthrax, and others. Despite both being beta-lactam antibiotics, their approved uses, dosing, and target infections differ.

Product Name Dosage Form Lot Number Quantity per Carton Issue
Cefazolin for Injection, USP 1 gm vial One (1) lot (specific lot number not provided) 25 vials per carton Four vials of Penicillin G Potassium for Injection included by mistake

This recall is conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

facts.product: Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm vial

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers and healthcare providers should immediately identify and stop using the affected lot of Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm vial. Return the recalled product to Sandoz via Sedgwick by contacting (844) 491-7872 or emailing Sandoz6004@sedgwick.com for return instructions and further assistance.

title: Face Rock Creamery Recalls Vampire Slayer Garlic Cheddar Curds Due to Potential Listeria Contamination
headline: Face Rock Creamery Issues Voluntary Recall of Vampire Slayer Garlic Cheddar Curds for Possible Listeria Risk
description: Face Rock Creamery recalls Vampire Slayer Garlic Cheddar Curds sold at Trader Joe’s in Northern CA and NV due to potential Listeria contamination.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Face Rock Creamery of Bandon, Oregon, has initiated a voluntary recall of two specific lots of its Vampire Slayer Garlic Cheddar Curds (6oz cups) due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacteria can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems.

The affected product was distributed through Trader Joe’s stores in Northern California (including Monterey, Fresno, and locations north) and Northern Nevada (Carson City, Reno, Sparks). Although no illnesses have been reported to date, the contamination was identified through routine product testing.

Face Rock Creamery is cooperating with the U.S. FDA and the Oregon Department of Agriculture while investigating the source of contamination to ensure product safety and quality.

Product Lot Number Use By Date UPC Distribution Areas
Vampire Slayer Garlic Cheddar Curds (6oz cups) 20250519VS01 08/29/2025 8 51222 00528 7 Trader Joe’s stores in Northern California and Northern Nevada
Vampire Slayer Garlic Cheddar Curds (6oz cups) 20250519VS02 08/29/2025 8 51222 00528 7 Trader Joe’s stores in Northern California and Northern Nevada

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with Vampire Slayer Garlic Cheddar Curds matching the recalled lot numbers should not consume the product. Return it to the place of purchase for a full refund or contact Face Rock Creamery Customer Care via email at info@facerockcreamery.com for further assistance.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Customer Care Team | info@facerockcreamery.com

title: Sabores A Tu Mesa Recalls Mousse Desserts for Undeclared Milk and Other Allergens
headline: Sabores Fit Bakery Recalls Sabores A Tu Mesa Mousse Desserts Due to Undeclared Milk and Other Allergenic Ingredients
description: Sabores A Tu Mesa recalls Mousse Desserts due to undeclared milk, eggs, soy, wheat, and tree nut allergens posing allergy risks in Florida retail stores.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sabores Fit Bakery, operating under the brand Sabores A Tu Mesa, has issued a recall for its Mousse Desserts due to the presence of undeclared allergens. The products may contain milk, eggs, soy ingredients (soybean oil, soy lecithin), wheat, and tree nuts (almonds, hazelnuts) that are not listed on the packaging. This poses a serious risk of allergic reaction, including life-threatening conditions, for individuals sensitive or allergic to these ingredients.

The affected mousse desserts were distributed in retail stores within the Kissimmee and Orlando areas of Florida. These products are packaged in 8 oz plastic cups and were sold under the Sabores A Tu Mesa brand name in the following flavors: Choco Mousse, Passion Fruit Mousse, 4 Milk Mousse, 3 Milk Mousse, and 3 Milk Strawberry. The expiration dates on the recalled products range from June 26 to July 02, 2025.

No illnesses related to the consumption of these mousse desserts have been reported so far. The recall follows a routine inspection that revealed the allergens were not properly declared on the product packaging. Sabores Fit Bakery is taking corrective measures to improve labeling accuracy, product traceability, and overall food safety protocols.

Product Name Flavor Packaging Expiration Date
Mousse Desserts Choco Mousse 8 oz plastic cup June 26 & July 02, 2025
Mousse Desserts Passion Fruit Mousse 8 oz plastic cup June 26 & July 02, 2025
Mousse Desserts 4 Milk Mousse 8 oz plastic cup June 26 & July 02, 2025
Mousse Desserts 3 Milk Mousse 8 oz plastic cup June 26 & July 02, 2025
Mousse Desserts 3 Milk Strawberry 8 oz plastic cup June 26 & July 02, 2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should avoid eating Sabores A Tu Mesa Mousse Desserts with expiration dates from June 26 to July 02, 2025, in the Kissimmee and Orlando Florida areas and return any affected products to the retail store for a full refund. For questions, contact Sabores Fit Bakery at 1-919-579-7694.

content.text: Consumer support: 1-919-579-7694

content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on June 20, 2025.

title: SE Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils Recalled for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: SE Grocers Recalls Dark Chocolate Nonpareils Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen Risk
description: SE Grocers recalls Dark Chocolate Nonpareils due to undeclared milk allergen. Products distributed in select Florida and Louisiana stores in 10 oz clear bags.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
International Foodsource, LLC of Randolph, NJ, is recalling 10 oz packages of Southeastern Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils due to the presence of an undeclared milk allergen. This poses a risk of serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals allergic or sensitive to milk.

The affected Dark Chocolate Nonpareils were distributed by C&S Wholesale Grocers (Southeastern Grocers) and were available at retail stores located in Florida (Miami, Baldwin, Plant City) and Hammond, Louisiana. The product is packaged in clear 10 oz bags labeled as SE Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils.

This recall was prompted after International Foodsource, LLC was notified on June 16, 2025, by their supplier, Weaver Nut Company of Lebanon, PA. Weaver Nut Company voluntarily recalled its Nonpareil, Semi-Sweet Chocolate (Christmas Seeds) after discovering the presence of milk was not listed in the ingredients or allergen labeling. Currently, the root cause and corrective action details from the supplier have not been received.

To date, no illnesses associated with this recall have been reported.

Product Package Size Lot Number Distribution Locations
SE Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils 10 oz clear bag 242645 Florida (Miami, Baldwin, Plant City) and Hammond, Louisiana
SE Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils 10 oz clear bag 238466 Florida (Miami, Baldwin, Plant City) and Hammond, Louisiana
SE Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils 10 oz clear bag 235643 Florida (Miami, Baldwin, Plant City) and Hammond, Louisiana
SE Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils 10 oz clear bag 237319 Florida (Miami, Baldwin, Plant City) and Hammond, Louisiana
SE Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils 10 oz clear bag 241581 Florida (Miami, Baldwin, Plant City) and Hammond, Louisiana
SE Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils 10 oz clear bag 239202 Florida (Miami, Baldwin, Plant City) and Hammond, Louisiana

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased SE Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils in the affected lot numbers should return the product to the place of purchase for a refund or exchange. For questions, contact International Foodsource, LLC at 973-361-7044, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. EST, or email customerservice@intlfoodsource.com.

title: Lipari Foods Recalls JLM Dark Chocolate Nonpareils for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Lipari Foods Issues Allergy Alert for JLM Dark Chocolate Nonpareils Containing Undeclared Milk
description: Lipari Foods recalls JLM Dark Chocolate Nonpareils due to undeclared milk, posing allergy risks. Nationwide recall covers specific lot numbers in 14-ounce tubs.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Lipari Foods of Warren, Michigan, has issued a recall for its 14-ounce packages of JLM Branded "Dark Chocolate Nonpareils" due to the presence of undeclared milk. This allergen is not listed on the product packaging, posing a serious risk to individuals with milk allergies who consume the affected products. The recall covers products distributed nationwide through retail stores.

The affected packages are clear plastic tubs and can be identified by specific lot numbers printed on the bottom label. The recall was initiated after Weaver Nut Company announced their own recall of “Dark Chocolate Nonpareils” for the same reason. To date, Lipari Foods has not received any reports of illness related to this issue.

Product Package Size Lot Numbers
JLM Branded Dark Chocolate Nonpareils 14-ounce clear plastic tub 28202501A, 29202501A, 23202504A, 14202505A, 15202505A, 03202506A

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their packages of JLM Dark Chocolate Nonpareils for the listed lot numbers and return any affected products to the retailer for a full refund. For additional information, contact Lipari Foods at (586) 447-3500 ext: 9720.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: Lipari Foods | (586) 447-3500

title: Weaver Nut Company Recalls Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Weaver Nut Company Issues Allergy Alert for Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils with Undeclared Milk
description: Weaver Nut Company recalls semi-sweet chocolate nonpareils with white or Christmas seeds due to undeclared milk allergen posing a serious allergy risk.
keywords: Semi sweet chocolate pareils with white or Christmas colored seeds
productDescription: Semi sweet chocolate pareils with white or Christmas colored seeds

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Weaver Nut Company, Inc. has issued a recall for certain lots of its semi-sweet chocolate nonpareils with white or Christmas colored seeds due to the presence of undeclared milk allergens. This situation poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities.

The affected products were distributed across the United States and available at various retail and grocery stores. The recall was initiated after a wholesale customer complaint and subsequent laboratory tests confirmed the presence of milk in the impacted lots, which do not declare milk on their labels. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with these products.

Product Lot Numbers
Nonpareil, Semi-Sweet Chocolate (Christmas Seeds) 204206, 204207, 204208, 204209, 204212, 224225
Nonpareils, Semi-Sweet Chocolate (White Seeds) 204214-RL, 204214, 204215, 224221, 224222, 224223, 135215, 135216, 135217, 135220, 135221, 145204, 145205-1, 145207-1, 145210-1

facts.product: Semi sweet chocolate pareils with white or Christmas colored seeds

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their semi-sweet chocolate nonpareils packaging for the affected lot numbers and either discard the product or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For assistance, contact Weaver Nut Company Customer Service at 717-738-3781 ext. 122 or cwesterhoff@weavernut.com.

title: Medtech Products Inc. Recalls Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup Due to Bacillus cereus Contamination
headline: Medtech Products Inc. Issues Nationwide Recall of Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup for Bacillus cereus Microbial Contamination
description: Medtech Products Inc. recalls Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup due to potential Bacillus cereus contamination causing foodborne illness. See affected lots.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Medtech Products Inc. has issued a nationwide recall of Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup due to potential microbial contamination with Bacillus cereus, which can cause foodborne illness. The affected product was distributed across the United States through retailers and online from December 14, 2022, through June 4, 2025.

The recall includes several lots of the 4 FL OZ (118 mL) amber bottled Honey Cough Syrup. The Lot Code appears both on the bottle label and on the bottom of the outer carton. No other Little Remedies® products are impacted by this recall. To date, no serious adverse events have been reported related to this issue.

Medtech Products Inc. is offering reimbursements for purchases of the recalled lots. Consumers seeking reimbursement or who have questions about the recall may contact Medtech Products Inc.:

Product UPC Lot Number Expiration Date
Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup 4 FL OZ (118 mL) 7-56184-10737-9 0039 11/2025
Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup 4 FL OZ (118 mL) 7-56184-10737-9 0545 01/2026
Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup 4 FL OZ (118 mL) 7-56184-10737-9 0640 02/2026
Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup 4 FL OZ (118 mL) 7-56184-10737-9 0450 05/2026
Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup 4 FL OZ (118 mL) 7-56184-10737-9 1198 12/2026

Consumers seeking reimbursement or who have questions about the recall may contact Medtech Products Inc. via email at medicalaffairs@prestigebrands.com, their website at https://www.prestigebrands.com/contact, or by phone at (800) 754-8853 (Monday – Friday, 8:30 AM – 5:30 PM Eastern Time).

Adverse reactions or quality issues may also be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program online, by mail, or by fax. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the lot number on their Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup packaging and if it matches any recalled lots, immediately stop use and contact Medtech Products Inc. for reimbursement or further assistance at (800) 754-8853 or medicalaffairs@prestigebrands.com.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Medtech | (800) 754-8853 | medicalaffairs@prestigebrands.com

title: Meijer Recalls Frederik’s Dark Chocolate Almonds for Undeclared Cashews Risk
headline: Meijer Recalls Frederik’s Dark Chocolate Almonds Due to Undeclared Cashews Allergy Hazard
description: Meijer recalls Frederik’s Dark Chocolate Almonds sold in select states due to undeclared cashews posing allergy risks. No illnesses reported as of 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Meijer has issued a recall for certain packages of Frederik’s Dark Chocolate Almonds due to the presence of undeclared cashews. The affected products may contain dark chocolate-covered cashews, which are not listed on the label. This poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals allergic or highly sensitive to cashews.

The recall applies to Frederik’s Dark Chocolate Almonds sold in black stand-up pouches and 8-count, 1.5-ounce multi-pack boxes available at Meijer stores in Michigan, Indiana, Illinois, Ohio, Kentucky, and Wisconsin. The products have specific sell-by dates ranging from May 5, 2026, to May 28, 2026. Meijer has not reported any illnesses linked to the recall so far.

UPC Recalled Product Name Sell By Date(s)
7-08820-68730-1 Frederik’s by Meijer Dark Chocolate Almonds (12 oz.) 05/07/2026, 05/28/2026
7-19283-11923-0 Frederik’s Dark Chocolate Almonds 8-count 1.5 oz. 05/05/2026

For more information, visit the Meijer Newsroom.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers allergic to cashews should immediately stop using Frederik’s Dark Chocolate Almonds with the listed UPCs and sell-by dates and return them to any Meijer store for a full refund. For questions, contact Meijer at 800-543-3704.
