title: Fuentes Farms LLC Recalls Fresh Cucumbers Due to Salmonella Contamination Risk
headline: Fuentes Farms LLC Recalls 71 Boxes of Fresh Cucumbers Labeled Lot #357 for Potential Salmonella Contamination
description: Fuentes Farms LLC recalls cucumbers in 40 lb bushel boxes due to Salmonella contamination risk. Distributed in Texas flea markets, no illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Fuentes Farms LLC of McAllen, Texas, has issued a recall of 71 boxes of fresh cucumbers due to a potential contamination with Salmonella. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals infected with Salmonella may experience symptoms such as fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can become more severe, leading to bloodstream infections and other complications.

The recalled cucumbers were distributed in Texas through local vendors at flea markets in the McAllen and Alamo areas. The affected product carries a Fuentes Farms label with Lot number 357. These cucumbers are packaged in 40-pound (1-1/9 bushel) boxes marked "vegetables" on a black background. Although no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall and it is not linked to any ongoing food outbreaks, Fuentes Farms LLC is notifying customers of the potential contamination risk.

The recall follows a routine FDA sample that detected Salmonella in the finished product. Production and distribution have been halted while the company and FDA continue investigating the cause.

Product Lot Number Package Size Distribution Area Recall Dates
Fresh Cucumbers with Fuentes Farms Label 357 40 lbs (1-1/9 bushel boxes) Texas (McAllen and Alamo flea markets) May 31 to June 3

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Fuentes Farms cucumbers labeled with Lot #357 in 40-pound bushel boxes from May 31 to June 3 should not consume the product and are advised to dispose of or return it to the place of purchase. For questions, contact Fuentes Farms LLC at 1-956-340-8653 or via email at sales@fuentesfarmsllc.com.

content.text:
Consumer support: 1-956-340-8653 | sales@fuentesfarmsllc.com
Media contact: Associated Press (AP) | Business News | info@ap.org

title: Vita Warehouse Corp. Recalls ALDI Welby®, Berkley Jensen®, and VitaGlobe™ Vitamin B12 Gummies for Undeclared Peanut Allergen
headline: Vita Warehouse Corp. Recalls Specific Vitamin B12 Gummies Under Welby®, Berkley Jensen®, and VitaGlobe™ Brands Due to Undeclared Peanut Allergen
description: Vita Warehouse Corp. recalls one lot of Welby®, Berkley Jensen®, and VitaGlobe™ Vitamin B12 gummies due to undeclared peanut allergen risk. No injuries reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Vita Warehouse Corp. has issued a voluntary recall for one lot of specific Vitamin B12 gummy products under the Welby®, Berkley Jensen®, and VitaGlobe™ brands. The recall is due to the possible presence of undeclared peanut allergen in these products, which poses a serious risk to individuals allergic or sensitive to peanuts. The affected products were distributed nationwide through ALDI and BJ’s retail stores, as well as online.

The recall concerns products with lot number 248046601 and expiration date October 2026. No allergic reactions or illnesses related to these products have been reported so far. The issue was identified after routine testing by ALDI revealed a potential presence of peanut allergen despite the products being labeled as peanut-free. Vita Warehouse Corp. conducted additional internal testing that did not detect peanuts; however, the recall is being conducted out of caution to protect consumer safety.

This recall is being done with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration to ensure product safety and consumer trust. No other lots or products from Welby®, Berkley Jensen®, or VitaGlobe™ are impacted.

Brand Packaging Description UPC Code Lot Number Expiration Date Notes
Welby® Clear bottle with white cap, green, red, and white labeling 4099100290868 248046601 10/2026 1000 mcg, 140 gummies
Berkley Jensen® Clear bottle with white cap, red and pink labeling 888670132487 248046601 10/2026 1000 mcg, 250 gummies
VitaGlobe™ Clear bottle with white cap, white and red labeling 850005214670 248046601 10/2026 Extra Strength, 60 gummies

No allergic reactions or illnesses related to these products have been reported to date.

For more information, visit the official websites: ALDI, BJ’s, VitaGlobe, or VitaGlobe on Amazon.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Welby®, Berkley Jensen®, or VitaGlobe™ Vitamin B12 gummies with lot number 248046601 and expiration date October 2026 should immediately stop use and return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund or discard it. For questions, contact Vita Warehouse Corp. at 1-855-214-0100 (Monday-Friday, 7:30 am – 4 pm EST).

title: Turkana Food Inc. Recalls Floria Dried Apricots Due to Undeclared Sulfites Posing Health Risk
headline: Turkana Food Inc. Recalls Floria Dried Apricots for Undeclared Sulfites on Label, Risking Consumer Safety
description: Turkana Food Inc. recalls Floria Dried Apricots due to undeclared sulfites allergen risk. Distributed across 20+ states, no illnesses reported as of June 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Turkana Food Inc., based in Kenilworth, NJ, is recalling 352 cases of Floria Dried Apricots due to undeclared sulfites on the product label. The presence of sulfites, a potential allergen, was not disclosed on the packaging, presenting a health risk to sulfite-sensitive consumers.

The affected Floria Dried Apricots were distributed across multiple states including Florida, Kentucky, Virginia, New York, New Jersey, Tennessee, Massachusetts, Texas, Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Rhode Island, Pennsylvania, North Carolina, Maryland, Ohio, Alabama, Missouri, and California. The recall is limited to the 200-gram paper packaging with specific lot and expiration details as noted below.

This action follows a routine sampling by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets that detected sulfites in the finished product which were not listed on the label. As of June 12, 2025, no illnesses have been reported related to this issue. Turkana Food Inc. has halted production and distribution while working with the FDA to resolve the labeling problem.

Product Lot Number UPC Expiration Date
Floria Dried Apricots, 200 Gram Paper Packaging 440090478-15-333 2539560010 11/2026

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should not eat Floria Dried Apricots with lot number 440090478-15-333 and should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Turkana Food Inc. at 908-810-8800, Monday through Friday, 8am to 6pm EST.

content.text: Consumer support: Turkana Foods Inc. | 908-810-8800

title: Medtronic Voluntary Recall of Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus Ventilators and Related Service Parts Due to Safety Defect
headline: Medtronic Initiates Class I Recall of Select Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus Ventilators and Related Service Parts Over Capacitor Failure Risk
description: Medtronic recalls Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus ventilators and related service parts due to potential failure causing sudden shutdown and serious health risks.
keywords: Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus ventilators and certain related service parts
productDescription: Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus ventilators and certain related service parts

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Medtronic has issued a voluntary recall of certain Newport™ HT70 and Newport™ HT70 Plus ventilators, as well as related Newport™ service parts, due to a potential safety defect. The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has classified this recall as a Class I recall, indicating a serious risk to health.

Investigations identified potential failure in two capacitors on the ventilator’s controller Printed Circuit Board Assembly (PCBA), which could cause the device to shut down unexpectedly or cause the shutdown alert alarm to fail. Such failures may result in inadequate ventilation, posing serious risks including reduced oxygen levels, increased carbon dioxide levels, brain injury, or death.

Since May 2025, Medtronic has advised customers to discontinue clinical use of affected devices and replace them with alternative ventilation methods. The company will no longer service these ventilators and related parts. A total of 63 medical device reports have been received, including two serious injuries and one death associated with this issue.

Medtronic serves patients using Newport™ HT70 ventilators in various settings, including home care and hospitals, particularly for infant and pediatric patients. Customers with affected products are urged to contact Medtronic customer service or report any adverse events to the FDA and Medtronic.

Product Name Manufacture Date Use By Date
Newport™ HT70 Ventilator Specific dates not provided in source Not specified
Newport™ HT70 Plus Ventilator Specific dates not provided in source Not specified
Certain Newport™ Service Parts Post-May 2025 manufacture dates assumed Not specified

facts.product: Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus ventilators and certain related service parts

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Immediately remove affected Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus ventilators and related service parts from clinical use. Replace with alternative ventilation methods. Contact Medtronic Technical Support at 800-255-6774 (option 1, then option 1) or Medtronic Customer Service at 800-962-9888 for assistance. Report adverse events or product quality problems to FDA MedWatch online or by phone at (800) FDA-1088.

en.title: Medtronic Voluntary Recall of Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus Ventilators and Related Service Parts Due to Safety Defect
en.description: Medtronic recalls Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus ventilators and related service parts due to potential failure causing sudden shutdown and serious health risks.
en.productDescription: Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus ventilators and certain related service parts
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Medical Devices

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Medtronic has issued a voluntary recall of certain Newport™ HT70 and Newport™ HT70 Plus ventilators, as well as related Newport™ service parts, due to a potential safety defect. The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has classified this recall as a Class I recall, indicating a serious risk to health.

Investigations identified potential failure in two capacitors on the ventilator’s controller Printed Circuit Board Assembly (PCBA), which could cause the device to shut down unexpectedly or cause the shutdown alert alarm to fail. Such failures may result in inadequate ventilation, posing serious risks including reduced oxygen levels, increased carbon dioxide levels, brain injury, or death.

Since May 2025, Medtronic has advised customers to discontinue clinical use of affected devices and replace them with alternative ventilation methods. The company will no longer service these ventilators and related parts. A total of 63 medical device reports have been received, including two serious injuries and one death associated with this issue.

Medtronic serves patients using Newport™ HT70 ventilators in various settings, including home care and hospitals, particularly for infant and pediatric patients. Customers with affected products are urged to contact Medtronic customer service or report any adverse events to the FDA and Medtronic.

Product Name Manufacture Date Use By Date
Newport™ HT70 Ventilator Specific dates not provided in source Not specified
Newport™ HT70 Plus Ventilator Specific dates not provided in source Not specified
Certain Newport™ Service Parts Post-May 2025 manufacture dates assumed Not specified

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Immediately remove affected Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus ventilators and related service parts from clinical use. Replace with alternative ventilation methods. Contact Medtronic Technical Support at 800-255-6774 (option 1, then option 1) or Medtronic Customer Service at 800-962-9888 for assistance. Report adverse events or product quality problems to FDA MedWatch online or by phone at (800) FDA-1088.

title: P. East Trading Corp Distributors Issues Alert on Uneviscerated 'Salted Smoked Split Herring’ Due to Potential Clostridium Botulinum Contamination
headline: P. East Trading Corp Distributors Issues Alert on Uneviscerated 'Salted Smoked Split Herring’ Due to Potential Clostridium Botulinum Contamination
description: No Brand announced a recall involving Uneviscerated Salted Smoked Split Herring after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – clostridium botulinum....

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
P. East Trading Corp. of Bronx, New York, has issued a recall for its Salted Smoked Split Herring due to potential contamination with Clostridium botulinum, a bacterium that can cause severe and potentially fatal foodborne illness. The recalled product was found to be over 5 inches in length and uneviscerated, conditions that increase the risk of botulinum spores concentration in the fish’s viscera. Consumers are strongly advised not to consume the product even if it appears normal in look or smell.

Clostridium botulinum contamination can lead to botulism, a serious illness characterized by symptoms such as general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing difficulties, muscle weakness, abdominal distension, and constipation. Immediate medical attention is recommended for anyone experiencing these symptoms after consuming the product.

The affected Salted Smoked Split Herring was distributed to retail locations across New York, New Jersey, and Connecticut. It was packaged in 18-pound wooden boxes labeled with container code Lot 1 PRC5073 and produced in Canada by Sea Star Seafood Ltd. Retailers may have repackaged the product into deli-style or other retail packaging, with varying codes depending on the place of purchase.

No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall. Consumers who have purchased the product from the listed retail locations are urged to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional information or questions, consumers can contact P. East Trading Corp. at (718) 991-6070 or via email at peastl@gmail.com, attention Jay Hong, Office Manager.

title: Bornstein Seafoods Inc Recalls Cooked & Peeled Ready-To-Eat Coldwater Shrimp Meat Because of Possible Health Risk
headline: Bornstein Seafoods Inc Recalls Cooked & Peeled Ready-To-Eat Coldwater Shrimp Meat Because of Possible Health Risk
description: Bornstein Seafoods announced a recall involving Coldwater Shrimp Meat after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – listeria monocytogenes. FDA...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Bornstein Seafoods Inc., located in Bellingham, Washington, is voluntarily recalling approximately 44,550 pounds of its Cooked & Peeled Ready-To-Eat Coldwater Shrimp Meat. The recall is due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes, a harmful bacterium that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems.

The recalled products were distributed to distributors and retailers in California, Oregon, Washington, and British Columbia, Canada. There is also a possibility that these products were further distributed to retailers nationwide. The shrimp meat is sold under the Bornstein Seafoods brand in 1-pound or 5-pound plastic bags.

Although no illnesses have been reported so far, consuming products contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes can lead to symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at particular risk, as the infection may cause miscarriages and stillbirths.

Consumers who have purchased the affected shrimp products should return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For any inquiries, consumers may contact Bornstein Seafoods via email at Andrew@bornstein.com.

The recall was initiated after routine sampling detected Listeria monocytogenes in a shrimp sample during production. Bornstein Seafoods has ceased distribution of the product while investigating the root cause. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

title: August Egg Company Recalls Brown Cage-Free and Brown Certified Organic Eggs Due to Salmonella Risk
headline: August Egg Company Voluntarily Recalls Brown Cage-Free and Brown Certified Organic Eggs Because of Potential Salmonella Contamination
description: August Egg Company recalls brown cage-free and brown certified organic eggs due to possible Salmonella enteritidis contamination in multiple states.
keywords: Brown cage free and brown certified organic eggs
productDescription: Brown cage free and brown certified organic eggs

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
August Egg Company of Hilmar, California, has announced a voluntary recall of approximately 1,700,000 dozen brown cage-free and brown certified organic eggs due to potential contamination with Salmonella enteritidis. Salmonella infections can cause serious illness, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of infection often include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, it can lead to severe conditions such as bloodstream infections, arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The affected eggs were distributed between February 3, 2025, and May 15, 2025, with sell-by dates ranging from March 4, 2025, to June 4, 2025, in California and Nevada. Additionally, shipments were made to Walmart stores in multiple states—including California, Washington, Nevada, Arizona, Wyoming, New Mexico, Nebraska, Indiana, and Illinois—between February 3, 2025, and May 6, 2025. Those eggs have sell-by dates from March 4, 2025, through June 19, 2025.

The recalled egg cartons have plant code numbers P-6562 or CA5330 and include Julian Dates between 32 and 126 printed on the carton side. Cartons may be made of fiber or plastic.

en.title: August Egg Company Recalls Brown Cage-Free and Brown Certified Organic Eggs Due to Salmonella Risk
en.description: August Egg Company recalls brown cage-free and brown certified organic eggs due to possible Salmonella enteritidis contamination in multiple states.
en.productDescription: Brown cage free and brown certified organic eggs
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
August Egg Company of Hilmar, California, has announced a voluntary recall of approximately 1,700,000 dozen brown cage-free and brown certified organic eggs due to potential contamination with Salmonella enteritidis. Salmonella infections can cause serious illness, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of infection often include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, it can lead to severe conditions such as bloodstream infections, arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The affected eggs were distributed between February 3, 2025, and May 15, 2025, with sell-by dates ranging from March 4, 2025, to June 4, 2025, in California and Nevada. Additionally, shipments were made to Walmart stores in multiple states—including California, Washington, Nevada, Arizona, Wyoming, New Mexico, Nebraska, Indiana, and Illinois—between February 3, 2025, and May 6, 2025. Those eggs have sell-by dates from March 4, 2025, through June 19, 2025.

The recalled egg cartons have plant code numbers P-6562 or CA5330 and include Julian Dates between 32 and 126 printed on the carton side. Cartons may be made of fiber or plastic.

Distribution Dates Sell-By Dates Locations Plant Codes Julian Dates
Feb 3, 2025 – May 15, 2025 Mar 4, 2025 – Jun 4, 2025 California, Nevada (Save Mart, FoodMaxx, Lucky, Smart & Final, Safeway, Raleys, Food 4 Less, Ralphs) P-6562, CA5330 32 to 126
Feb 3, 2025 – May 6, 2025 Mar 4, 2025 – Jun 19, 2025 CA, WA, NV, AZ, WY, NM, NE, IN, IL (Walmart locations) P-6562, CA5330 32 to 126

title: Church & Dwight Recalls Zicam® and Orajel™ Nasal and Baby Teething Swabs for Microbial Contamination
headline: Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Zicam® Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs, Nasal AllClear Swabs, and Orajel™ Baby Teething Swabs Due to Fungal Contamination
description: Church & Dwight recalls specific lots of Zicam® and Orajel™ swabs over fungal contamination that may pose infection risks. No serious injuries reported.
keywords: Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs, Nasal AllClear Swabs, Baby Teething Swabs
productDescription: Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs, Nasal AllClear Swabs, Baby Teething Swabs

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Church & Dwight Co., Inc. is voluntarily recalling certain lots of Zicam® Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs, Zicam® Nasal AllClear Swabs, and Orajel™ Baby Teething Swabs due to potential microbial contamination. The contamination consists of fungi found in the cotton swab components, posing a health risk especially to individuals with compromised immune systems, underlying medical conditions, or damaged nasal mucosa. While no serious adverse events have been reported, the contamination could lead to serious and life-threatening infections.

These affected products were distributed nationwide in the United States and Puerto Rico. This recall applies exclusively to specified Zicam and Orajel swab products; other Zicam and Orajel items, including Zicam RapidMelts, are not affected.

Product Name UPC Lot Numbers Additional Details
Zicam® Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs 732216301205 All lots within expiry Zinc-free, homeopathic cold remedy swab designed to shorten the duration of the common cold
Zicam® Nasal AllClear Swabs 732216301656 All lots within expiry Nasal cleansing swab product (discontinued December 2024)
Orajel™ Baby Teething Swabs 310310400002 All lots within expiry Pre-moistened swabs designed to soothe teething discomfort in infants and toddlers

Consumers who have used these products and experienced any adverse effects are encouraged to report them to the FDA MedWatch program online, by mail, or by fax.

facts.product: Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs, Nasal AllClear Swabs, Baby Teething Swabs

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their Zicam® Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs, Zicam® Nasal AllClear Swabs, or Orajel™ Baby Teething Swabs are included in the recall by verifying lot numbers and expiry. If affected, they should stop using the products immediately and return them for a full refund. For more information or assistance, visit www.churchdwightrecall.com or contact Church & Dwight at (800) 981-4710.

title: TGD Cuts, LLC Voluntary Recall of Fresh Cucumbers from Bedner Growers Inc. Due to Salmonella Risk
headline: TGD Cuts, LLC Recalls Fresh Cucumbers from Bedner Growers Inc. Potentially Contaminated with Salmonella
description: TGD Cuts, LLC recalls fresh cucumbers supplied by Bedner Growers Inc. due to possible Salmonella contamination affecting multiple states.
keywords: Fresh cucumbers and salsa and salads containing fresh cucumbers
Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella
productDescription: Fresh cucumbers and salsa and salads containing fresh cucumbers
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
TGD Cuts, LLC, located in Jessup, Maryland, has voluntarily recalled certain tub and tray items containing cucumbers supplied by Bedner Growers Inc. due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella infection can cause serious and sometimes fatal illnesses, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems.

Symptoms of Salmonella infection include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream and cause more severe conditions such as arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The recall specifically affects products with use-by dates ranging from May 20, 2025, to May 28, 2025. These items were distributed to retail and foodservice locations across Maryland, Virginia, Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and North Carolina. There have been no reported illnesses linked to these products to date. As the affected items are beyond their shelf life, they are no longer expected to be found in commerce.

Product Description Use By Date Distribution Area
Specific tubs and trays containing cucumber from Bedner Growers Inc. May 20, 2025 – May 28, 2025 Maryland, Virginia, Pennsylvania, New Jersey, North Carolina

facts.product: Fresh cucumbers and salsa and salads containing fresh cucumbers

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check for affected fresh cucumbers with use-by dates from May 20 to May 28, 2025, and either discard them or return to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional information, contact TGD Cuts, LLC at (410) 799-5700, Monday through Friday, 6:30 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. EST, or email mediarelations@classproduce.com.

title: Firehook of Virginia Recalls Classic Sea Salt Organic Crackers for Undeclared Sesame Allergy Risk
headline: Firehook of Virginia Recalls Classic Sea Salt Organic Crackers Due to Undeclared Sesame Labeling
description: Firehook of Virginia recalls Classic Sea Salt Organic Crackers with undeclared sesame posing serious allergy risks. Affected product details and distribution states listed.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Firehook of Virginia has issued a recall for one lot of Firehook brand Classic Sea Salt Organic Crackers due to undeclared sesame. This poses a risk of a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction for individuals with sesame allergies or severe sensitivities.

The recalled product is an 8-ounce package of Firehook artisan baked Classic Sea Salt Crackers, sold in a clear package. The specific lot affected has a Best By Date of 09/29/25 and a UPC code of 8 99055 00063 5. The crackers were distributed at retail stores across several states including Connecticut, Massachusetts, Maryland, Maine, North Carolina, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, and Virginia.

The recall was initiated on May 30, 2025, after discovering that sesame-containing crackers were labeled incorrectly due to a temporary breakdown in the company's production and packaging processes. At this time, no illnesses have been reported related to this issue.

Product Package Size Best By Date UPC Code States Sold
Firehook Classic Sea Salt Organic Crackers 8 oz 09/29/25 8 99055 00063 5 CT, MA, MD, ME, NC, NH, NJ, NY, PA, RI, VA

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the product's Best By Date and UPC code on packaging to confirm if their Firehook Classic Sea Salt Organic Crackers are part of the recall, then return the affected product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Firehook of Virginia Customer Service at 1-888-580-0745, Monday through Friday, 8:00 am to 4:00 pm EST.

content.text:
Consumer support: Firehook of Virginia | 888-580-0745
Media contact: Brent Fowler | | 888-580-0745

title: Amneal Recalls Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim Tablets USP 400 mg/80 mg Due to Microbial Contamination
headline: Amneal Pharmaceutical LLC Announces Nationwide Recall of Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim Tablets USP 400 mg/80 mg for Microbial Contamination
description: Amneal recalls Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim Tablets USP 400 mg/80 mg after microbial contamination found; no injuries reported. Check lot numbers for affected products.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Amneal Pharmaceutical LLC has initiated a nationwide recall of three lots of Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim Tablets, USP, 400 mg/80 mg due to microbial contamination. The affected tablets may show black spots on the surface, which were identified following a product quality complaint. This contamination poses a health risk as oral products contaminated with Aspergillus can cause serious and potentially life-threatening infections, especially in patients with weakened immune systems.

The recalled lots were distributed to wholesalers and distributors across the United States between December 4, 2024, and May 15, 2025. This recall only affects the 400 mg/80 mg strength of Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim Tablets and does not impact other lot numbers or strengths. To date, no adverse events, illnesses, or injuries related to this recall have been reported.

Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim Tablets, USP, 400 mg/80 mg are used to treat urinary tract infections, acute otitis media in pediatric patients, acute exacerbations of chronic bronchitis, enteritis caused by Shigella flexneri, and traveler's diarrhea in adults. The product is available in 100 tablet and 500 tablet bottles. Consumers should check the lot number on the bottle label or consult their pharmacy if the tablets came from a pharmacy vial.

Lot NumberDistribution DatesProduct StrengthPackage Size
Specific lot numbers not listed in source12/4/2024 - 5/15/2025400 mg/80 mg100 and 500 tablets per bottle

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the lot number on their Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim Tablets, USP, 400 mg/80 mg packaging to determine if their product is part of the recall and contact Amneal Pharmaceutical LLC at 1-833-582-0812 or via email at sulfamethoxazole-trimethoprim-recall@amneal.com for return instructions and reimbursement.

title: Recall Alert: Undeclared Milk in Aldi Brand Casa Mamita Churro Bites Filled with Chocolate Hazelnut Cream
headline: Camerican International Recalls Aldi Brand Casa Mamita Churro Bites for Undeclared Milk Allergen
description: Camerican International recalls Aldi Casa Mamita Churro Bites due to undeclared milk allergen, distributed in select states. No reported illnesses.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Camerican International, Inc. of Paramus, NJ is recalling 7.05 oz boxes of Aldi brand Casa Mamita Churro Bites Filled with Chocolate Hazelnut Cream due to the presence of undeclared milk. This poses a serious risk for individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities, who could experience life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume the product.

The affected product was distributed in select Aldi stores across Alabama, Arkansas, Florida, Georgia, Iowa, Illinois, Kentucky, Louisiana, Missouri, Mississippi, North Carolina, South Carolina, and Tennessee. The recall was triggered after a consumer reported milk-containing Churro Bites being sold in packaging that did not list milk as an ingredient. An investigation identified a temporary breakdown in production and packaging processes as the cause, which has since been resolved.

No illnesses have been reported related to this issue.

Product Package Size Best If Used By Date Lot Number Distribution States
Aldi Brand Casa Mamita Churro Bites Filled with Chocolate Hazelnut Cream 7.05 oz box JUL/14/2025 01425 AL, AR, FL, GA, IA, IL, KY, LA, MO, MS, NC, SC, TN

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their packaging for Aldi brand Casa Mamita Churro Bites (7.05 oz, lot 01425, best by JUL/14/2025) and return affected products to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Camerican International at 1-201-587-0101 (Monday-Friday, 8:30 am to 5:00 pm EST) or email fsqaincidents@camerican.com.

title: Homegrown Family Foods Recalls Shore Lunch Oven Style Breader & Batter Mix Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Homegrown Family Foods Recalls Shore Lunch Oven Style Breader & Batter Mix for Undeclared Milk Allergens
description: Homegrown Family Foods recalls Shore Lunch Oven Style Breader & Batter Mix due to undeclared milk posing allergy risks. Affected products distributed in 10 states.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Homegrown Family Foods has issued a recall for its Shore Lunch Oven Style Breader & Batter Mix 6-ounce boxes due to the presence of undeclared milk. This undeclared allergen poses a serious risk to individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities, as consumption may result in life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recalled product was primarily distributed in retail stores across Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Minnesota, Nebraska, New York, North Dakota, Ohio, South Dakota, and Wisconsin. Distribution occurred between April 29, 2024, and May 1, 2025. The affected items have specific Best By dates, lot numbers, and UPC codes for easy identification.

To date, one allergic reaction related to this recall has been reported, with the affected individual having recovered. The recall was initiated after a consumer reported an allergic reaction on April 23, 2025, which led to the discovery that milk-containing product was packaged without proper milk allergen labeling.

Product Net Weight Best By Dates Lot Numbers UPC Code Distribution States
Shore Lunch Oven Style Breader & Batter Mix 6 oz (170 g) April 23, 2025 – February 25, 2026 RP117050, RP120012, RP120011, RP120013, RP123249, RP123389, RP129004, RP129005, RP129006 2473912000 IL, IN, IA, MN, NE, NY, ND, OH, SD, WI

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with milk allergies should inspect their Shore Lunch Oven Style Breader & Batter Mix packaging for affected Best By dates and lot numbers. If the product is impacted, they should return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Homegrown Family Foods at 706-403-5768 (Monday–Friday, 8:00 am–4:00 pm ET) or email QAinquiries@homegrownfamilyfood.com.

title: Isabelle’s Kitchen Inc. Recalls Refrigerated Deli Salads Containing Fresh Cucumbers Because of Possible Health Risk
headline: Isabelle’s Kitchen Inc. Recalls Refrigerated Deli Salads Containing Fresh Cucumbers Because of Possible Health Risk
description: Kings, Isabelle’s Kitchen Inc., Maple Avenue Foods announced a recall involving Salads containing fresh cucumbers. after issues linked to potential to be...
keywords: Kings, Isabelle’s Kitchen Inc., Maple Avenue Foods
Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella
brandName: Kings, Isabelle’s Kitchen Inc., Maple Avenue Foods
brandNames: Kings, Isabelle’s Kitchen Inc., Maple Avenue Foods
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Isabelle’s Kitchen, Inc., based in Harleysville, PA, is recalling 946 cases of refrigerated deli salads containing fresh cucumbers supplied by Bedner Grower’s, Inc. due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella infection can cause serious health issues, including fever, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Vulnerable populations such as young children, elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems are at increased risk of severe or potentially fatal infections.

The recalled refrigerated deli salads were distributed to foodservice distributors and local supermarkets in New Hampshire, Florida, Maryland, South Carolina, New Jersey, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Virginia. These products are packaged in white plastic containers inside white or brown corrugated boxes under the brand names "Isabelle’s Kitchen," "Maple Avenue Foods," or "Kings." It is possible that repackaged items sold at the supermarket level may have different sell-by dates, but none will exceed the original manufacture sell-by date.

Consumers are advised not to consume these products. The recall only affects specific deli salad items produced using cucumbers processed on May 14 and May 15, 2025, bearing designated Julian code use-by dates. There have been no reported illnesses linked to these products to date. Retailers have been notified to remove and destroy the recalled products and inform consumers accordingly.

For more information or questions, consumers can contact Isabelle’s Kitchen, Inc. at 800-355-7252 between 8 AM and 5 PM EST. Individuals experiencing health issues after consuming the products should seek medical advice. This recall is being conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

5002BIKIIKI Greek Pasta Salad134June 1, 2025

  • Consumers who have purchased these deli salads should not eat them.

  • Contact Isabelle’s Kitchen, Inc. at 800-355-7252 (8 AM to 5 PM EST) for additional information.
  • Seek medical advice if you experience symptoms consistent with Salmonella infection.

Further details regarding the related cucumber recall by Bedner Grower’s, Inc. can be found on the FDA website: Bedner Growers Inc. Cucumber Recall.

title: Supreme Service Solutions LLC Recalls Fresh Cucumbers and Salad Trays Due to Salmonella Risk
headline: Supreme Produce Recalls Fresh Cucumbers and Salad Trays Containing Cucumbers Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination
description: Supreme Service Solutions LLC recalls fresh cucumbers and salad trays containing cucumbers due to possible Salmonella contamination affecting multiple states.
keywords: Fresh cucumbers and salad and vegetable trays containing fresh cucumbers.
Due to possible contamination with Salmonella
productDescription: Fresh cucumbers and salad and vegetable trays containing fresh cucumbers.
reason: Due to possible contamination with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Supreme Service Solutions LLC, operating as Supreme Produce, has voluntarily recalled fresh cucumbers and salad and vegetable trays containing fresh cucumbers due to possible contamination with Salmonella. This recall involves products purchased from Bedner Growers Inc. and distributed through Kroger and its affiliated retail stores.

Salmonella is a bacteria that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of infection include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Rarely, infection can lead to more severe complications such as bloodstream infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The recall was initiated after the FDA notified Bedner Growers Inc. of a Salmonella outbreak linked to these cucumbers. This outbreak has affected 26 individuals across multiple states, including Alabama, California, Colorado, Florida, Illinois, Kansas, Kentucky, Michigan, North Carolina, New York, Ohio, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, and Virginia. The recalled products were distributed to Kroger stores and affiliates in Indiana, Illinois, Ohio, Kentucky, Tennessee, Mississippi, Missouri, Arkansas, and Michigan.

There have been no reported illnesses or consumer complaints directly linked to products purchased from Supreme Produce to date.

For health-related concerns, consumers should consult their physician.

title: The Coastal Companies Voluntary Recall of Fresh Cucumber Salads and Salsas Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination
headline: The Coastal Companies Voluntarily Recalls 17 Salad and Salsa Products Containing Fresh Cucumbers Due to Salmonella Risk
description: The Coastal Companies recall 17 salad and salsa products with fresh cucumbers due to potential Salmonella contamination, distributed in multiple states.
keywords: East Coast Fresh, TOPS, Wellsley Farms, WEIS, AHOLD, Jack and Olive, Created Fresh, Spring and Sprout
Salads, salsas, and other products containing salsa containing fresh cucumbers

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The Coastal Companies has announced a voluntary recall of 17 products containing fresh cucumbers supplied by Bedner Growers, Inc. and distributed by Fresh Start Produce Sales, Inc. due to potential Salmonella contamination. These products were distributed between May 6, 2025, and May 21, 2025, across several states including Washington D.C., Ohio, Virginia, Michigan, West Virginia, Delaware, Maryland, Massachusetts, Pennsylvania, New Jersey, Vermont, and North Carolina.

The recalled products include various salads, salsas, and other items under brands such as East Coast Fresh, TOPS, Wellsley Farms, WEIS, AHOLD, Jack and Olive, Created Fresh, Spring and Sprout.

Salmonella infection can cause serious health issues, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Those who believe they may have contracted Salmonella should seek medical attention promptly. To date, there have been no reported illnesses linked directly to The Coastal Companies' recalled products.

title: Santa Monica Seafood Recalls Aldi Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing Due to Undeclared Soy Allergen
headline: Santa Monica Seafood Issues Voluntary Recall of Aldi Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing for Undeclared Soy Content
description: Santa Monica Seafood recalls Aldi Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing due to undeclared soy allergen risk. Distributed in CA, NV, AZ by Aldi.
keywords: Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing
productDescription: Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On May 24, 2025, Santa Monica Seafood, located in Rancho Dominguez, California, announced a voluntary recall of its Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing. The recall is due to the presence of undeclared soy in the product, which poses a risk to individuals with soy allergies or severe sensitivities. Consuming the product could cause a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction.

The affected product was distributed in Aldi Stores across California, Nevada, and Arizona. To date, no illnesses related to this issue have been reported. The recall was initiated after the supplier identified a labeling error during a routine inspection, where soy was not listed as an ingredient despite being present in the product.

This recall is being carried out in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Product Name Brand Size Use/Freeze By
Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing Aldi 16 oz Jun. 02, 2025

content.text: Consumers with soy allergies should check for Aldi-branded Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing and return affected products to the store for a full refund or contact Santa Monica Seafood at 1-800-969-8862 for assistance.

title: Spectrum Brands Recalls Single Lot of Tetra ReptoMin 3-In-1 SELECT-A-FOOD Reptile Food for Aquatic Turtles Due to Salmonella Risk
headline: Spectrum Brands Recalls Tetra ReptoMin 3-In-1 SELECT-A-FOOD for Aquatic Turtles Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination
description: Spectrum Brands recalls one lot of Tetra ReptoMin 3-In-1 SELECT-A-FOOD reptile food for aquatic turtles due to potential Salmonella contamination.
keywords: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Spectrum Brands Pet LLC (SPG) is voluntarily recalling a single lot of its Tetra ReptoMin 3-In-1 SELECT-A-FOOD reptile food due to possible contamination with Salmonella. This product is designed for aquatic turtles and is packaged in a 1.56 oz plastic container with a green label. Only the lot with code 951790 is affected by this recall. No other lots or product lines are included, and they may continue to be used.

Salmonella can pose health risks to both animals consuming the product and humans handling it. Symptoms in humans can include nausea, vomiting, diarrhea (including bloody diarrhea), abdominal cramping, and fever. Serious complications, though rare, can involve infections of the arteries, endocarditis, arthritis, muscle pain, eye irritation, and urinary tract symptoms. In pets, particularly aquatic turtles, symptoms may include lethargy, diarrhea, vomiting, fever, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Infected pets that appear healthy can still spread bacteria to other animals and people.

The affected product was distributed nationwide between May 6, 2025, and May 16, 2025, through major retailers including Petco, Walmart, Chewy, Amazon, and Meijer.

Spectrum Brands has not received reports of illness linked to this recall but is cooperating with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration to ensure safety. The recall follows internal testing that suggested a component ingredient of the reptile food may contain Salmonella, despite the final product testing negative.

Product Name Lot Code Package Size UPC Distribution Dates Retailers
Tetra ReptoMin 3-In-1 SELECT-A-FOOD reptile food 951790 1.56 oz plastic container 0 46798 78626 9 May 6, 2025 – May 16, 2025 Petco, Walmart, Chewy, Amazon, Meijer

facts.productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should stop using the recalled Tetra ReptoMin 3-In-1 SELECT-A-FOOD reptile food with lot code 951790, contact the retailer for a full refund, wash and sanitize all surfaces and utensils that came into contact with the product, and contact a veterinarian if pets show symptoms of illness. For questions, contact Spectrum Brands Pet LLC at 1-800-526-0650 or aquaticssupport@spectrumbrands.com, Monday through Friday from 9 am to 5 pm EST.

content.text: Consumer support: SPG | 1-800-526-0650 | aquaticssupport@spectrumbrands.com

title: Albertsons Companies Recall of Ready Meals & Star Market Greek Salad Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination
headline: Albertsons Companies Voluntarily Recalls Three Deli Greek Salad Items Containing Recalled Cucumber Due to Salmonella Risk
description: Albertsons recalls Ready Meals & Star Market Greek Salad items due to possible Salmonella contamination linked to cucumbers from Fresh Creative Foods.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Albertsons Companies has voluntarily recalled three store-made deli items containing cucumbers supplied by Fresh Creative Foods due to possible Salmonella contamination. This action follows a recall initiated by Fresh Creative Foods, a division of Reser’s Fine Foods, Inc., linked to an ongoing Salmonella illness outbreak. The cucumbers involved in the recall were grown by Bedner Growers, Inc. in Boynton Beach, Florida.

Salmonella infection can cause serious and potentially fatal illnesses, especially in young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Symptoms often include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. The affected deli items were sold in several store banners across multiple states in the Northeast and Mid-Atlantic regions.

Product Name UPC Size Sell Thru Dates Store Banners States
SALAD GREEK AUTHENTIC FS 29307000000–00901 LBS May 23, 2025 to May 24, 2025 ACME, Safeway, Kings Food Markets, Balducci's Food Lovers Market CT, DE, DC, MD, NJ, NY, PA, VA
READYMEALS SALAD GREEK SS 29248300000 LBS May 20, 2025 to May 24, 2025 Shaw’s, Star Market MA, ME, NH, RI, VT
SALAD GREEK FS 29232900000 LBS May 20, 2025 to May 24, 2025 Shaw’s, Star Market MA, ME, NH, RI, VT

For more information on this incident, consumers can refer to the FDA investigation notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Ready Meals & Star Market Greek Salad packaging for the affected UPCs and sell-through dates. Do not consume the recalled products; instead, return them to the store for a full refund or dispose of them. Contact Albertsons Companies Customer Service at 1-877-723-3929 for assistance.

title: Walmart Recalls Marketside Fresh Cut Cucumber Slices in Select Texas Stores Due to Salmonella Risk
headline: Walmart Recalls Marketside Fresh Cut Cucumber Slices in Select Texas Stores Over Potential Salmonella Contamination
description: Walmart recalls Marketside Fresh Cut Cucumber Slices sold in select Texas stores due to potential Salmonella contamination. Check affected dates up to 5/24/2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Walmart Inc. announced a voluntary recall of Marketside Fresh Cut Cucumber Slices sold in select Texas stores due to potential Salmonella contamination. The recall follows an FDA notification regarding whole cucumbers supplied by Bedner Growers, Inc., linked to reported salmonellosis illnesses.

Salmonella infection can cause serious health issues, especially in young children, the elderly, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms typically begin 6 hours to 6 days after exposure and include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. No illnesses have been reported from this specific recalled product, but anyone experiencing symptoms after consumption should consult a healthcare provider.

Consumers unsure if they have purchased the affected cucumbers can visit Walmart's help page or contact Walmart's Customer Care Team at 1-800-925-6278.

title: JFE Franchising, Inc. Recalls A Limited Number of Cucumber Products Because Of Possible Health Risk
headline: JFE Franchising, Inc. Recalls A Limited Number of Cucumber Products Because Of Possible Health Risk
description: Snowfruit & Snowfox announced a recall involving Fresh cut cucumber items and sushi products after issues linked to salmonella. FDA recall notice published...
keywords: Fresh cut cucumber items and sushi products
productDescription: Fresh cut cucumber items and sushi products

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
JFE Franchising, Inc. of Houston, TX, is voluntarily recalling a limited number of fresh-cut cucumber products and sushi items containing cucumber due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella bacteria can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and people with weakened immune systems. Symptoms in healthy individuals often include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, typically starting 6 hours to 6 days after exposure and lasting 4 to 7 days.

To date, no illnesses or consumer complaints have been reported related to the recalled products. The recall is in response to a prior recall of cucumbers by Bedner Growers, Inc., whose cucumbers were supplied to retailers and used as ingredients in JFE Franchising’s products. These items were sold through Kroger, Roundy’s (including Pick n Save and Metro Market), and Weis Market locations across several states including Indiana, Kentucky, Illinois, Michigan, Tennessee, Mississippi, Arkansas, Alabama, Wisconsin, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, West Virginia, Delaware, Maryland, Virginia, and New York.

Consumers are advised not to consume the recalled Snowfruit and Snowfox branded products listed below. Instead, they should return the items to the place of purchase for a full refund or dispose of them. For questions, customers can contact JFE Franchising, Inc. via email at hello@snowfruit.com or hello@snowfox.com. The company monitors these email addresses Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. CST.

JFE Franchising, Inc. is actively taking measures to address this recall and ensure consumer safety. More information is available through the FDA’s outbreak advisory.

title: PennRose Farms Recalls Whole Cucumbers Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination
headline: PennRose Farms Recalls 5-Pound Mesh Bags of Whole Cucumbers Over Salmonella Risk
description: PennRose Farms recalls whole cucumbers in 5-pound mesh bags due to possible Salmonella contamination affecting multiple states in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
PennRose Farms, LLC has issued a recall for 5-pound mesh bags of whole cucumbers due to possible contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of Salmonella infection include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream and cause more serious conditions such as arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis.

The cucumbers in question were repacked by PennRose Farms but originally recalled by Bedner Growers, Inc., as notified by their supplier Fresh Start Produce Sales, Inc. The affected products are 5-pound mesh bags bearing the Restaurant Depot and PennRose Farms logos. These were packaged between May 2 and May 5, 2025, and shipped to Restaurant Depot distribution centers across New Jersey, Georgia, Florida, Illinois, and Ohio.

PennRose Farms is coordinating this recall with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). No injuries have been reported to date in connection with this product.

Product Description Packaging UPC Code Packaged Dates Lot Numbers Distribution Locations
Whole Cucumbers 5-pound mesh bags 841214101714 May 2 – May 5, 2025 48-122, 48-123, 48-124, 48-125 Restaurant Depot distribution centers in NJ, GA, FL, IL, OH

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their cucumbers’ packaging for the listed lot numbers and production dates. If you have the recalled product, do not consume it. Dispose of it safely or return it to the place of purchase. For assistance or questions, contact PennRose Farms at 800-804-7254 (8 am to 5 pm EST).

title: UMARY USA Voluntary Nationwide Recall of UNAVY and UMOVY Ácido Hialurónico Caplets Due to Undeclared Dexamethasone, Diclofenac, and Omeprazole
headline: UMARY USA Recalls UNAVY and UMOVY Ácido Hialurónico Caplets After Detection of Undeclared Pharmaceutical Ingredients
description: UMARY USA recalls UNAVY and UMOVY Ácido Hialurónico (30 caplets/850 mg) for undeclared drugs dexamethasone, diclofenac, and omeprazole posing health risks.
keywords: Unavy Acidio Hialuronico (30 caplets/850 mg) and Umovy Acidio Hialuronico (30 caplets/850 mg)
The product contains undeclared Dexamethasone, Diclofenac and Omeprazole
productDescription: Unavy Acidio Hialuronico (30 caplets/850 mg) and Umovy Acidio Hialuronico (30 caplets/850 mg)
reason: The product contains undeclared Dexamethasone, Diclofenac and Omeprazole

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
UMARY USA has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots of Unavy Ácido Hialurónico (30 caplets/850 mg) and Umovy Ácido Hialurónico (30 caplets/850 mg) due to the presence of undeclared drug ingredients: dexamethasone, diclofenac, and omeprazole. These products were marketed as dietary supplements but have been identified as unapproved new drugs by the FDA because they contain active pharmaceutical ingredients not listed on the labels.

Dexamethasone is a corticosteroid that may impair immune function and cause serious side effects such as high blood sugar, muscle injuries, psychiatric conditions, cardiovascular events, and adrenal gland suppression, with potential withdrawal symptoms upon abrupt discontinuation. Diclofenac is a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) that can cause cardiovascular, gastrointestinal, renal, and allergic reactions, especially in individuals taking other NSAIDs or anticoagulants. Omeprazole, a proton pump inhibitor, may mask serious stomach conditions including ulcers and cancer.

No specific reports of adverse events associated with these products have been confirmed at this time; however, the presence of these undeclared drugs poses potential serious health risks.

Brand Product Name Quantity Dosage per Caplet Container Description Lot Numbers Expiration Dates
UNAVY Ácido Hialurónico 30 caplets 850 mg White plastic bottle with black background label, white and yellow writing All lots All expiration dates
UMOVY Ácido Hialurónico 30 caplets 850 mg Black plastic bottle with black label, white and blue lettering All lots All expiration dates

These products were distributed nationwide exclusively via online sales through umary-usa.com.

facts.product: Unavy Acidio Hialuronico (30 caplets/850 mg) and Umovy Acidio Hialuronico (30 caplets/850 mg)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Unavy Ácido Hialurónico or Umovy Ácido Hialurónico (30 caplets/850 mg) should immediately stop using the products and contact UMARY USA at umaryusa2025@gmail.com for return and refund instructions.

Media contact: Hugo Ramirez | | 520-342-7385

Publix Super Markets, Inc. has announced a voluntary recall of GreenWise Pear, Kiwi, Spinach & Pea Baby Food pouches due to potential contamination with elevated levels of lead. The affected product is produced by Bowman Andros LLC and distributed across Publix stores in its eight-state operating area.

The product subject to this recall is sold in 4-ounce plastic pouches and was identified through routine sampling. The recall is being conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to ensure consumer safety.

Publix has removed all potentially impacted products from store shelves. There have been no reported illnesses associated with the consumption of this product to date.

title: Ukrop’s Marinated Cucumber Salad Recalled for Potential Salmonella Contamination
headline: Ukrop’s Issues Recall of Marinated Cucumber Salad Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination
description: Ukrop’s recalls Marinated Cucumber Salad due to potential Salmonella contamination. Products sold with specific sell-by dates in multiple states. FDA recall 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods, LLC, based in Richmond, VA, has issued a recall of three marinated cucumber salad products due to potential contamination with Salmonella. This action follows a related recall by Bedner Growers of cucumbers, which are an ingredient in these products. Salmonella infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems.

Salmonella symptoms often include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can become severe by spreading to the bloodstream, leading to complications such as infected aneurysms, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The recall affects specific sell-by dates of Ukrop’s marinated cucumber salad sold in various sizes, and products have been distributed to multiple retailers across several states.

Product Net Weight Sell-By Date Range
Marinated Cucumber Salad 16 ounces 5/11/25 through 5/26/25
Marinated Cucumber Salad 36 ounces 5/11/25 through 5/26/25
Marinated Cucumber Salad Bulk 5 lbs. 5/11/25 through 5/26/25

The affected products were available at the following retailers:

  • Food Lion Stores in Virginia and North Carolina
  • Harris Teeter, Williamsburg, VA
  • Kroger Stores in Virginia, West Virginia, Ohio, and Kentucky
  • Libbie Market, Richmond, VA
  • Ukrop’s Market Hall, Richmond, VA

All retail locations have been notified and are in the process of removing the recalled products from their shelves.

Additional company and recall information is available from Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods at ukropshomestylefoods.com or by phone at 804-340-3050.

For official updates and more details regarding this recall and related advisories, visit the FDA website at FDA.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the sell-by dates on Ukrop’s Marinated Cucumber Salad products and avoid consuming those dated 5/11/25 through 5/26/25. Return affected products to the place of purchase for a full refund or contact Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods at 804-340-3050 for assistance.

title: Big Y Foods Recalls Made-To-Order Subs, Wraps and Paninis Sold in Massachusetts and Connecticut Because of Possible Health Risk
headline: Big Y Foods Recalls Made-To-Order Subs, Wraps and Paninis Sold in Massachusetts and Connecticut Because of Possible Health Risk
description: Big Y Foods announced a recall involving Made-to-order subs, wraps and paninis after issues linked to salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Big Y Foods of Springfield, MA, is voluntarily recalling certain Made-To-Order Subs, Wraps, and Paninis sold between May 20 and May 21, 2025. This recall affects products sold at select Big Y retail locations in Massachusetts and Connecticut due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella is a bacteria that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and people with weakened immune systems.

Individuals infected with Salmonella may experience symptoms such as fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream, causing severe complications such as arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The recall is linked to sliced cucumbers supplied by Smartcuts, an ingredient option for subs, wraps, and paninis at the sandwich service line in Big Y’s Kitchen Department. The cucumbers are part of a wider Bedner Growers Inc. Cucumber Outbreak investigation. Following notification, Big Y Foods ceased operations at the affected service lines in all listed stores, discarded remaining product, and conducted thorough cleaning and sanitization.

Consumers who purchased Made-To-Order subs, wraps, or paninis on the specified dates at the affected stores are advised to return the product or present their receipt for a full refund. No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall to date. For questions, customers can contact Big Y Foods or Smartcuts as detailed below.

title: New Grains Gluten Free Bakery Recalls Bread, Bagels, Cookies, and Croutons for Undeclared Egg, Tree Nuts, Soy, and Milk Allergens
headline: New Grains Gluten Free Bakery Recalls Bakery Products for Missing Allergen Labels Containing Egg, Tree Nuts, Soy, and Milk
description: New Grains Gluten Free Bakery recalls breads, bagels, cookies, and croutons sold in Utah due to undeclared egg, tree nuts, soy, and milk allergens posing allergy risks.
keywords: Breads, bagels, caramel bars, cookies and croutons
Undeclared Allergen – Egg, tree nuts Soy and Milk
productDescription: Breads, bagels, caramel bars, cookies and croutons
reason: Undeclared Allergen – Egg, tree nuts Soy and Milk

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
New Grains Gluten Free Bakery has issued a recall for several bakery products because of undeclared allergens: eggs, tree nuts, soy, and milk. These allergens are not listed on the product labels, creating a severe allergy risk for sensitive individuals. The affected products include breads, bagels, cookies, and croutons distributed in Utah.

The recalled items were distributed between April 7 and April 21, 2025, across lot numbers 90–107. Products were sold in retail stores in Utah. Breads and croutons are packaged in clear vacuum-sealed plastic bags; cookies in clear regular plastic bags. All products are labeled gluten-free and bear the New Grains brand with label colors of red, purple, orange, blue, green, or pink.

The recall was initiated after discovery that these items contain eggs, tree nuts, soy, and milk but lack proper allergen disclosure on the labels. To date, no illnesses have been reported related to this issue.

Product Name Contains Allergens
Artisan White BreadEgg
Artisan Multigrain BreadEgg
Artisan Sourdough BreadEgg
Artisan Cinnamon Raisin BreadEgg
Blueberry BagelsEgg
Cinnamon Raisin BagelsEgg
Plain BagelsEgg
Multigrain BagelsEgg
Artisan Sourdough Ciabatta RollsEgg
Chocolate Chip CookieEgg, Milk, Soy
Dye-Free Frosted Sugar CookieEgg, Milk, Soy
Frosted Sugar CookieEgg, Milk, Soy
Coconut Macaroon CookieEgg, Milk, Soy
Pecan Caramel BarEgg, Milk, Tree nuts
Brownie Chocolate Chip CookieEgg, Milk, Soy
Artisan Seasoned CroutonsEgg, Milk
Seasoned Bread CrumbsEgg, Milk
  • Recall Dates: Distributed between April 7 and April 21, 2025
  • Lot Numbers: 90–107
  • Distribution Area: Retail stores in Utah
  • Packaging: Breads and croutons in clear vacuum-sealed plastic bags; cookies in clear regular plastic bags
  • Contact Phone: 801-980-5751 (Monday to Friday, 10 AM to 3 PM MST)

eeatMeta.expertSummary: New Grains Gluten Free Bakery recalled a variety of baked goods due to undeclared allergens including egg, tree nuts, soy, and milk, posing serious risks to consumers with allergies.

title: Bedner Growers, Inc. Recalls No Brand Name Cucumbers Due to Salmonella Contamination
headline: Bedner Growers, Inc. Issues Recall of No Brand Name Cucumbers Sold in Florida for Salmonella Risk
description: Bedner Growers, Inc. recalls No Brand Name cucumbers sold April–May 2025 in Florida over Salmonella contamination linked to 26 illnesses across multiple states.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Bedner Growers, Inc., located in Boynton Beach, Florida, is voluntarily recalling cucumbers sold at its Bedner’s Farm Fresh Market stores between April 29, 2025, and May 14, 2025. The recall is due to potential contamination with Salmonella, a bacteria that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems.

Salmonella infection symptoms include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream and cause severe conditions such as infected aneurysms, endocarditis, and arthritis. The recalled cucumbers were sold directly to consumers at three Bedner’s Farm Fresh Market locations in Florida: Boynton Beach, Delray Beach, and West Palm Beach.

The cucumbers involved in this recall do not bear any identifying stickers or labels. The cucumbers were also sold to a wholesale distributor that has been instructed to notify its customers about the recall and provide further instructions.

This recall is linked by the FDA to a Salmonella outbreak resulting in 26 reported illnesses across multiple states: Alabama, California, Colorado, Florida, Illinois, Kansas, Kentucky, Michigan, North Carolina, New York, Ohio, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, and Virginia. The recall does not affect cucumbers sold after May 14, 2025, nor does it include any other agricultural products at Bedner’s Farm Fresh Markets. Bedner Growers, Inc. has ceased production, packing, and sales of cucumbers as the growing season has ended.

Product Retail Locations Recall Dates Reason for Recall
Cucumbers (No brand name, no labels) Bedner’s Farm Fresh Markets in Boynton Beach, Delray Beach, and West Palm Beach, FL April 29, 2025 – May 14, 2025 Potential Salmonella contamination

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased cucumbers sold at Bedner’s Farm Fresh Market locations in Boynton Beach, Delray Beach, or West Palm Beach, FL between April 29, 2025, and May 14, 2025, should discard the cucumbers immediately. Wholesale distributors have been notified to inform customers accordingly. For questions or additional information, contact Bedner Growers, Inc. at 866-222-9180, Monday through Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. EDT.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Bedner Growers, Inc | 866-222-9180

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on May 20, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.
Visit the company website for product details.

title: Madeline’s Pâtisserie Recalls Croissants and Croissant Buns for Undeclared Wheat Allergen
headline: Madeline’s Pâtisserie Voluntarily Recalls Croissants and Croissant Buns Due to Undeclared Wheat
description: Madeline’s Pâtisserie recalls croissants and croissant buns mislabeled for wheat allergen, sold in Michigan and Ohio food service distributors.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Element 112, LLC dba Madeline’s Pâtisserie has announced a voluntary recall of certain ready-to-eat croissants and croissant buns due to an undeclared allergen. The products were incorrectly labeled with “flour” on the ingredient statement instead of specifying “wheat,” which poses a risk for individuals with wheat allergies or sensitivities.

The affected items, sold fully baked and frozen in food service cases of 24 units, were produced between March 10, 2025, and May 7, 2025. These products were distributed to several food distributors in Michigan and Ohio.

No reports of injuries or allergic reactions have been confirmed related to this issue.

Product
2.5oz Chocolate Croissants
3.5oz Chocolate Croissants
2.5oz Raspberry Croissants
3.5oz Raspberry Croissants
Croissant Buns
Production Dates
March 10, 2025 – May 7, 2025
Distribution Locations
Superior Food Distributors - 4243 Broadmoor Ave SE, Grand Rapids, MI 49512
Carmela Food Distributor - 18350 15 mile Rd, Fraser, MI 48026
Euclid Fish Company - 7839 Enterprise Dr, Mentor, OH 44060
Atlantic Food Distributors - 430 Sixth St. SE, Canton, OH 44702

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers and distributors should check their inventory for the affected croissants and croissant buns, avoid consumption if wheat allergy is a concern, and contact Madeline’s Pâtisserie at info@madelinespatisserie.com to arrange product replacement with corrected labeling.

content.text: Consumer support: info@madelinespatisserie.com

title: South Asian Food Inc. Recalls Bengal King Family Pack Vegetable Singara for Undeclared Peanut Allergen
headline: South Asian Food Inc. Announces Recall of Bengal King Family Pack Vegetable Singara Due to Undeclared Peanuts
description: South Asian Food Inc. recalls Bengal King Family Pack Vegetable Singara for undeclared peanuts, posing allergy risks. No reported illnesses. Nationwide distribution.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
South Asian Food Inc., located in Maspeth, NY, has issued a recall for its 1875g packages of Bengal King Family Pack Vegetable Singara. This recall is due to the possible presence of undeclared peanuts, which pose a serious risk for individuals with peanut allergies or severe peanut sensitivities.

The affected product was distributed nationwide at retail stores and through mail orders. The issue was identified when a peanut-containing ingredient was found in the product but not listed on the label, caused by unintentional human error during packaging. Production of this product has been suspended as the company works with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to correct the problem.

To date, no allergic reactions or illnesses related to this recall have been reported.

Product Package Size Lot Number UPC Number Expiration Date
Bengal King Family Pack Vegetable Singara 1875g 007 1824448372340 06/10/2026

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their packaging for Bengal King Family Pack Vegetable Singara, lot number 007, and if affected, return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact South Asian Food Inc. at 718-894-2507, Monday through Friday, 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM ET.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: South Asian Food Inc., Mohammed Khan | 718-894-2507

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on May 16, 2025.

title: Ariana Sweets Inc. Recalls Afghani Corn Bread Due to Undeclared Sesame and Wheat Allergens
headline: Ariana Sweets Inc. Recalls Afghani Corn Bread (‘Doda’) for Undeclared Sesame and Wheat Allergens
description: Ariana Sweets Inc. recalls Afghani Corn Bread containing undeclared sesame and wheat allergens posing risk to allergic consumers in California.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On May 13, 2025, Ariana Sweets Inc., based in Fremont, California, announced a recall of its AFGHANI CORN BREAD “Doda” product manufactured before April 24, 2025. The recall is due to the presence of undeclared allergens, specifically sesame and wheat. These allergens were not listed on the product packaging, which poses a serious risk to individuals with allergies or sensitivities to sesame or wheat.

The affected product is a shelf-stable, 24 oz round sweet bread packaged in a plastic bag and distributed to retail locations within California. This product has a UPC number of 753182432670. No allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported at this time.

The recall was initiated following a routine inspection by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), which found that the product contained sesame and wheat but was labeled without these allergens.

Product Description Manufacture Date UPC Distribution Area
AFGHANI CORN BREAD “Doda” 24 oz round sweet bread, plastic bag packaging Produced before 4/24/2025 753182432670 California retail locations

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with a sesame or wheat allergy or sensitivity should not consume Ariana Afghani Corn Bread (UPC 753182432670) produced before April 24, 2025. Dispose of the product or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Ariana Sweets Inc. at (510) 795-9600 or info@arianasweets.com.

content.text: Consumer support: (510) 795-9600 | info@arianasweets.com

title: NatureMills US Inc. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat, Milk, and Sesame in Rice Mixes, Soups, Spice Mixes, Porridge Mix, Papads and Vadam Products
headline: NatureMills US Inc. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat, Milk, and Sesame in Rice Mixes, Soups, Spice Mixes, Porridge Mix, Papads and Vadam Products
description: Nature Mills announced a recall involving Rice Mixes, Soups, Spice Mixes, Porridge Mix, Papads and Vadam Products after issues linked to undeclared allergen...
keywords: Rice Mixes, Soups, Spice Mixes, Porridge Mix, Papads and Vadam Products
productDescription: Rice Mixes, Soups, Spice Mixes, Porridge Mix, Papads and Vadam Products

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
NatureMills US Inc., located in Prosper, Texas, has issued a recall of select rice mixes, soups, spice mixes, porridge mix, papads, and vadam products due to undeclared allergens including wheat, milk, and sesame. These allergens were not listed on the product labels, posing a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to these ingredients.

The affected products were sold nationwide in the United States through the NatureMills website between December 1, 2023, and May 10, 2025. The recall was initiated after a routine internal audit identified missing allergen information on the labels, traced back to an oversight in the packaging process. No illnesses have been reported in relation to this recall so far.

Consumers who have purchased any of these products and have allergies to wheat, milk, or sesame are advised not to consume them. Instead, they should dispose of the items and contact NatureMills US Inc. for a full refund or replacement. The company can be reached via email at info@naturemills.com or by phone at 1-833-628-8736, available from 9 AM to 5 PM CST. Additional information is also available on the company’s recall webpage at www.naturemills.com/pages/recall.

NatureMills US Inc., located in Prosper, Texas, has issued a recall of select rice mixes, soups, spice mixes, porridge mix, papads, and vadam products due to undeclared allergens including wheat, milk, and sesame. These allergens were not listed on the product labels, posing a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to these ingredients.

The affected products were sold nationwide in the United States through the NatureMills website between December 1, 2023, and May 10, 2025. The recall was initiated after a routine internal audit identified missing allergen information on the labels, traced back to an oversight in the packaging process. No illnesses have been reported in relation to this recall so far.

Consumers who have purchased any of these products and have allergies to wheat, milk, or sesame are advised not to consume them. Instead, they should dispose of the items and contact NatureMills US Inc. for a full refund or replacement. The company can be reached via email at info@naturemills.com or by phone at 1-833-628-8736, available from 9 AM to 5 PM CST. Additional information is also available on the company’s recall webpage at www.naturemills.com/pages/recall.

title: Knockro Recalls Bonya Low Fat Yogurt Parfaits for Undeclared Almond Allergen
headline: Knockro Recalls Bonya Low Fat Yogurt Parfaits Due to Undeclared Almonds in Granola
description: Knockro recalls Bonya Low Fat Yogurt Parfaits nationwide due to undeclared almonds posing allergy risks. No illnesses reported. Expiration May 5, 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Knockro Inc. of Pennsylvania has issued a recall for its Bonya-branded low fat yogurt parfait products due to the presence of undeclared almonds in the granola component. This undeclared tree nut allergen poses a risk of serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions to consumers with almond allergies or severe sensitivities.

The affected yogurt parfaits were distributed nationwide through retail stores. These products are packaged in 12-ounce clear plastic cups and have an expiration date of May 5, 2025. To date, no illnesses related to this recall have been reported.

The recall was initiated after the company discovered that the almond-containing granola was packaged without listing almonds on the label. Investigations revealed that this issue stemmed from a temporary failure in the production and packaging processes at Knockro Inc.
