title: Fresh & Ready Foods Recalls Ready-to-Eat Sandwiches and Snack Items in AZ, CA, NV, and WA for Possible Listeria Contamination
headline: Fresh & Ready Foods Voluntarily Recalls Ready-to-Eat Sandwiches and Snack Items Sold in Arizona, California, Nevada, and Washington Due to Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
description: Fresh & Ready Foods recalls ready-to-eat sandwiches and snack items in four states due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination, distributed April 18-28, 2025.
keywords: Fresh & Ready Foods, Fresh Take Crave Away, City Point Market Fresh Food to Go
Ready to eat sandwiches and snack items
productDescription: Ready to eat sandwiches and snack items
brandName: Fresh & Ready Foods, Fresh Take Crave Away, City Point Market Fresh Food to Go
brandNames: Fresh & Ready Foods, Fresh Take Crave Away, City Point Market Fresh Food to Go

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Fresh & Ready Foods LLC has announced a voluntary recall of certain ready-to-eat sandwiches and snack items sold in Arizona, California, Nevada, and Washington due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. The recalled products were distributed between April 18, 2025, and April 28, 2025, primarily in vending and breakroom areas within corporate offices, medical buildings, and healthcare facilities in the affected states. The recall specifically targets products with “Use By” dates ranging from April 22, 2025, to May 19, 2025.

The issue was identified during an FDA site inspection through environmental monitoring, which detected Listeria monocytogenes on a piece of equipment used in the production process. Fresh & Ready Foods has responded by removing the impacted equipment and implementing corrective actions to maintain food safety and comply with FDA guidance.

Listeria monocytogenes can cause severe and potentially fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, people with weakened immune systems, and pregnant women. Symptoms may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria are at risk of miscarriage or stillbirth. No illnesses have been reported in connection with the recalled products to date, but the recall is a precautionary measure to ensure consumer safety.

Brand Product Description Distributed States Distribution Dates Use By Dates
Fresh & Ready Foods, Fresh Take Crave Away, City Point Market Fresh Food to Go Ready-to-eat sandwiches and snack items Arizona, California, Nevada, Washington 04/18/2025 – 04/28/2025 04/22/2025 – 05/19/2025

facts.brand: Fresh & Ready Foods, Fresh Take Crave Away, City Point Market Fresh Food to Go
facts.product: Ready to eat sandwiches and snack items

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the “Use By” date on the front of the package below the “Net Weight” and discard any sandwiches or snack items with dates between April 22, 2025, and May 19, 2025. For questions or to request a refund, contact Fresh & Ready Foods at RecallConcern@HotLineGlobal.com or call 1-855-424-8390, Monday through Friday, from 5:00 am to 3:00 pm PST.

title: New Grains Gluten Free Bakery Recalls Breads, Bagels, Cookies, and Croutons for Undeclared Eggs, Soy, and Milk Allergens
headline: New Grains Gluten Free Bakery Recalls Products for Undeclared Allergens Including Eggs, Soy, and Milk
description: New Grains Gluten Free Bakery recalls breads, bagels, cookies, and croutons in Utah due to undeclared eggs, soy, and milk posing allergy risks.
keywords: Products may contain undeclared eggs, soy, and milk
reason: Products may contain undeclared eggs, soy, and milk

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
New Grains Gluten Free Bakery has issued a recall for several of its bakery products, including breads, bagels, cookies, and croutons, due to the presence of undeclared allergens: eggs, soy, and milk. These allergens were not disclosed on the product labels, which poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to these ingredients.

The affected items were distributed in Utah through retail stores between April 7 and April 21 under lot numbers 90–107. The breads and croutons were packaged in clear vacuum-sealed plastic bags, while the cookies came in regular clear plastic bags. All recalled products bear the New Grains Gluten Free Bakery brand and are labeled as "gluten-free." The label colors vary and include red, purple, orange, blue, green, and pink.

No illnesses related to this recall have been reported at this time.

This recall is conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

title: Advantage Health Matters Inc Recalls Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination
headline: Advantage Health Matters Inc Issues Recall of Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds for Possible Salmonella Risk
description: Advantage Health Matters Inc recalls Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds due to potential Salmonella contamination in select lots distributed in NY, NJ, and VA.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Advantage Health Matters Inc., located in North York, Ontario, Canada, has issued a recall of its 8-ounce packages of "Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds" due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella infection can cause serious and sometimes fatal illnesses, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms in healthy people typically include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

The recalled product was distributed in the states of New York, New Jersey, and Virginia through retail stores and mail orders. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall. The recall was initiated following a supplier recall from another country. Advantage Health Matters has suspended production of this product while investigating the source of the contamination.

Product Package Size Lot Number Expiration Date Distribution Area
Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds 8 ounces L250320200 05/02/2027 New York, New Jersey, Virginia

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased the recalled Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds with lot number L250320200 and expiration date 05/02/2027 should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional questions, contact the company at info@organictraditions.com.

content.text: Consumer support: info@organictraditions.com

title: Recall of ENDURANCE BOOST WITH HORNY GOAT WEED Capsules Due to Undeclared Propoxyphenylsildenafil and Sildenafil
headline: EnShiShiXiangNiShangMaoYouXianGongSi Voluntary Nationwide Recall of ENDURANCE BOOST WITH HORNY GOAT WEED Capsules for Undeclared Sildenafil Compounds
description: EnShiShiXiangNiShangMaoYouXianGongSi recalls ENDURANCE BOOST capsules due to undeclared sildenafil compounds posing health risks, with no reported injuries.
keywords: Dietary supplement for male performance and energy
productDescription: Dietary supplement for male performance and energy

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
EnShiShiXiangNiShangMaoYouXianGongSi has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of its dietary supplement capsules, ENDURANCE BOOST WITH HORNY GOAT WEED (20 Count). This product, marketed for male performance and energy, was found to contain undeclared Propoxyphenylsildenafil and Sildenafil. Sildenafil is a pharmaceutical ingredient used to treat erectile dysfunction and cannot be legally included in dietary supplements.

The presence of these unlisted drug substances poses health risks, especially for individuals who take nitrates for conditions such as heart disease, diabetes, high blood pressure, or high cholesterol. These interactions can cause dangerously low blood pressure, which may be life threatening. The recalled product lot is identified as LOT: 250214PRO with an expiration date of 02/14/2027.

To date, no adverse events related to this recall have been reported. The company is notifying customers through this announcement and arranging for the return of all recalled products.

Product Name Lot Number Expiration Date Product Description Distribution
ENDURANCE BOOST WITH HORNY GOAT WEED (20 Count) 250214PRO 02/14/2027 Blue and white cardboard carton with white and black lettering, containing a 20-count blister pack Online via US Amazon.com only

content.text: Consumers who have ENDURANCE BOOST WITH HORNY GOAT WEED capsules, lot number 250214PRO, should immediately stop use and return the product to 150-67 Northern Blvd, Flushing, NY 11354. For questions, contact EnShiShiXiangNiShangMaoYouXianGongSi by email at nutriservice2025@outlook.com (Monday-Friday, 9AM-5PM PST) and report any adverse reactions to the FDA MedWatch program.

title: New England Village Foods Recalls 19th Hole Snack Mix for Undeclared Almonds and Sesame Allergens
headline: Recall Alert: New England Village Foods Withdraws 19th Hole Snack Mix Due to Undeclared Almonds and Sesame
description: New England Village Foods recalls 19th Hole Snack Mix in 5-ounce and 10-ounce packages for undeclared almonds and sesame allergens posing allergy risks.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
New England Village Foods of Milford, New Hampshire, has announced a recall of all lots of its “19th Hole Snack Mix” sold under the New England Village Snacks brand. This recall affects 5-ounce and 10-ounce containers due to the presence of undeclared almonds and sesame. The omission of these allergens on the labeling poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals allergic to almonds or sesame seeds.

The affected products were distributed to small independent grocery markets and convenience stores in New England, New York, and Pennsylvania. These products are packaged in clear, reclosable cups and tubs with lids marked by the following UPCs: 609465693477 for the 5-ounce size and 642147152459 for the 10-ounce size. The allergen labeling did not disclose the presence of almonds, and the “Contains” statement failed to include sesame despite its presence in the ingredient list.

The recall was initiated after a customer reported discovering almonds in the product where the packaging did not disclose them. An FDA review confirmed the labeling issue, identifying that a temporary breakdown in the company’s packaging process caused this error. Production of the “19th Hole Snack Mix” has been temporarily suspended as a precautionary measure.

No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

Product Name Package Size UPC
19th Hole Snack Mix 5-ounce 609465693477
19th Hole Snack Mix 10-ounce 642147152459

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased 19th Hole Snack Mix should return the affected products to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions or additional information, contact New England Village Foods at 1.603.554.1873, Monday through Friday from 7 AM to 4 PM Eastern Time.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: New England Village Foods | 1.603.554.1873

title: East CK Trading Recalls President Brand Licorice Plum for Undeclared Sulfites and Unapproved Color Additive
headline: East CK Trading Recalls Licorice Plum Due to Undeclared Sulfites and Unapproved Color Amaranth (E123)
description: East CK Trading recalls 8-ounce Licorice Plum for undeclared sulfites and unapproved color Amaranth (E123), posing allergy risks for sensitive individuals.
keywords: Potential or Undeclared Allergen – sulfites Unapproved color – Amaranth (E123)
reason: Potential or Undeclared Allergen – sulfites Unapproved color – Amaranth (E123)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
East CK Trading, Inc., located in Long Island City, NY, has issued a recall for its 8-ounce packages of “Licorice Plum” food treats. This recall is due to the presence of undeclared sulfites and the use of an unapproved color additive, Amaranth (E123), in the product.

Individuals with severe sensitivity to sulfites may experience serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume this product. Testing revealed that the “Licorice Plum” contains 29.1 milligrams of sulfites per serving, a level significantly above the 10 milligrams per serving reported to cause severe reactions in some asthmatics, including anaphylactic shock in sulfite-sensitive individuals.

The recalled product was distributed nationwide through retail stores and mail orders. To date, no illnesses or allergic reactions related to the consumption of this product have been reported.

The recall was initiated following routine inspection and laboratory analysis by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets Food Inspectors and Food Laboratory personnel, which identified undeclared sulfites on the product label.

Product Package Size UPC Code Reason for Recall
Licorice Plum 8 ounces (clear plastic bottle) 0077-20729 Undeclared sulfites; Unapproved color Amaranth (E123)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have purchased the 8-ounce Licorice Plum with UPC 0077-20729 and return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions or more information, contact East CK Trading, Inc. at 1-718-857-0008.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Jeff Boehner | 1-718-857-0008

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on May 05, 2025.

title: Vietti Food Group Recalls 15-oz Yellowstone Brown Sugar Molasses Baked Beans for Undeclared Soy Allergen
headline: Vietti Food Group Recalls Yellowstone Brown Sugar Molasses Baked Beans (15 oz.) Due to Undeclared Soy Allergen
description: Vietti Food Group recalls 15-oz Yellowstone Brown Sugar Molasses Baked Beans for undeclared soy, posing a risk to soy-allergic consumers. Refunds available.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Vietti Food Group of Nashville, TN has announced a recall of 4,515 cases of Yellowstone Brown Sugar Molasses Baked Beans (15 oz.) due to the presence of undeclared soy. This recall is relevant for individuals who have an allergy or severe sensitivity to soy, as consuming the product could result in serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions.

The affected product was distributed to retailers across multiple states, including Arizona, Colorado, Delaware, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Iowa, Kansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Michigan, Mississippi, Missouri, New Hampshire, New York, North Carolina, Ohio, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, and Virginia. The specific item can be identified by the Lot Code labeled "Best if Used By Feb 17, 2028," which is printed on the bottom of each can.

At present, there have been no reported illnesses or adverse reactions linked to the consumption of this product.

Product Size Lot Code Reason for Recall Distribution States
Yellowstone Brown Sugar Molasses Baked Beans 15 oz. Best if Used By Feb 17, 2028 Undeclared soy allergen AZ, CO, DE, FL, GA, IL, IA, KS, KY, LA, MI, MS, MO, NH, NY, NC, OH, OR, PA, TN, TX, UT, VA

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with the affected Yellowstone Brown Sugar Molasses Baked Beans (15 oz.) bearing the Lot Code "Best if Used By Feb 17, 2028" should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Vietti Food Group at (513) 682-2474, Monday through Friday, 8:00 am to 4:00 pm Central Time, or email recall@zwanfood.com.

title: Ray & Mascari Inc. Recalls 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination
headline: Ray & Mascari Inc. Voluntary Recall of 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes Over Salmonella Risk
description: Ray & Mascari Inc. recalls 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes due to potential Salmonella contamination. No illnesses reported. Sold in select U.S. states in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ray & Mascari Inc. of Indianapolis, Indiana, is voluntarily recalling their 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes packaged in 20 oz. (1 lb. 4 oz) clam shell containers due to a potential risk of Salmonella contamination. The affected products carry UPC# 7 96553 20062 1 and are identified by master case Lot# RM250424 15250B or Lot# RM250427 15250B. This recall follows notification from Hanshaw & Capling Farms, the original supplier, who initiated a recall after detecting possible Salmonella presence in their facility.

Salmonella infections can cause serious health issues, especially for young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms commonly include fever, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, Salmonella can lead to severe complications such as bloodstream infections, arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis.

The recalled 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes were sold in plastic clamshell containers labeled “Packed by Ray & Mascari Inc., Indianapolis, IN 46204” and distributed primarily through Gordon Food Service Stores across Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Missouri, Mississippi, New York, Ohio, Pennsylvania, Tennessee, and Wisconsin.

No illnesses related to this recall have been reported to date.

Product Package Size UPC Lot Number Distribution Areas
4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes 20 oz. (1 lb. 4 oz) Clam shell (567g) 7 96553 20062 1 RM250424 15250B, RM250427 15250B IL, IN, KY, MI, MO, MS, NY, OH, PA, TN, WI

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should not eat the recalled 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes with UPC 7 96553 20062 1 and lot numbers RM250424 15250B or RM250427 15250B. Dispose of these tomatoes immediately and contact Ray & Mascari Inc. at 1-317-637-0234 Monday through Saturday, 6 am to 5 pm EST with any questions or to report illness.

content.text: Consumer support: Ray & Mascari Inc. | 1-317-637-0234

The recalled 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes were sold in plastic clamshell containers labeled “Packed by Ray & Mascari Inc., Indianapolis, IN 46204” and distributed primarily through Gordon Food Service Stores across Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Missouri, Mississippi, New York, Ohio, Pennsylvania, Tennessee, and Wisconsin.

No illnesses related to this recall have been reported to date.

title: Williams Farms Repack LLC Recalls H&C Farms Label Tomatoes Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination
headline: Williams Farms Repack LLC Issues Recall of H&C Farms Label Tomatoes Over Potential Salmonella Risk
description: Williams Farms Repack LLC recalls H&C Farms Label tomatoes due to potential Salmonella contamination. No illnesses reported; distribution limited to GA, NC, SC.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Williams Farms Repack LLC has announced a recall of several tomato products due to potential contamination with Salmonella. This contamination poses a risk of serious illness, particularly for young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Salmonella infection can cause symptoms such as fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream and lead to more severe conditions like arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis.

The recall follows notification from Southeast Tomato Distributors on April 29, 2025, regarding possible Salmonella contamination in tomatoes supplied from H&C Farms. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with these products.

The recalled products were distributed to wholesalers and distributors in Georgia, North Carolina, and South Carolina between April 23, 2025, and April 28, 2025.

title: JJWV Marketing Recalls Ankimo Monkfish Liver for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: JJWV Marketing Recalls Ankimo Monkfish Liver Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen Risk
description: JJWV Marketing Corporation recalls Ankimo Monkfish Liver distributed in California due to undeclared milk allergen posing risk to milk-allergic consumers.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
JJWV Marketing Corporation of Santa Fe Springs, California, has issued a recall for Ankimo Monkfish Liver due to the presence of undeclared milk. This undeclared allergen poses a risk of a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction for individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities.

The affected product was distributed in California through retailers including Little Tokyo Market Place and H Marts, reaching consumers via retail store shelves. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

The recall was initiated after it was found that the packaging did not properly label the presence of milk protein.

This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Ankimo Monkfish Liver should check the product packaging for lot information to confirm if their item is affected and contact JJWV Marketing Corporation at 1-562-906-9988 for assistance.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: 1-562-906-9988
Media contact: Jonathan Park | Victor Pak |, 213-255-8849 | 562-606-4150

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on May 01, 2025.

title: Trader Joe’s Recalls Sesame Miso Salad with Salmon for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Trader Joe’s Recalls Sesame Miso Salad with Salmon Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen
description: Trader Joe’s recalls Sesame Miso Salad with Salmon with use-by dates April 28-29, 2025, due to undeclared milk allergen posing risk to milk-allergic consumers.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Trader Joe’s and Taylor Fresh Foods have initiated a voluntary recall of a limited quantity of Trader Joe’s Sesame Miso Salad with Salmon due to the potential presence of an undeclared milk allergen. Specifically, some packages of the salad were mistakenly included with a sealed packet of parmesan cheese crumbles instead of the intended crispy onion topping.

This issue affects products with Use By dates of April 28, 2025, and April 29, 2025. Approximately 500 units were distributed to Trader Joe’s stores across multiple states. Consumers who have milk allergies or sensitivities may be at risk of an adverse reaction if they consume the affected products.

There have been no reported adverse reactions to date. The recall is limited solely to the mentioned product and dates, with no other Trader Joe’s products or brands involved.

StoreItem DescriptionPackage SizeUse By DateUPCDistribution States
Trader Joe'sSesame Miso Salad with Salmon10.25 ozApril 28, 2025 / April 29, 2025April 29, 2025AL, IA, IL, IN, KS, KY, MI, MN, MO, NC, NE, NJ, NY, OH, PA, SC, TN, WI

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with milk allergies who purchased Trader Joe’s Sesame Miso Salad with Salmon bearing Use By dates April 28 or April 29, 2025, should discard the product immediately. For questions or further information, contact Trader Joe’s customer service at (855) 455-0098, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PST.

title: Health Fixer Recalls Male Ultra, Malextra, Electro Buzz, Ultra Armor, and Male Ultra Pro for Undeclared PDE-5 Inhibitors
headline: Health Fixer Voluntarily Recalls Male Enhancement Supplements Containing Undeclared Chloropretadalafil, Propoxyphenylsildenafil, and Sildenafil
description: Health Fixer recalls male enhancement supplements with undeclared PDE-5 inhibitors posing health risks, distributed nationwide via Amazon.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Health Fixer has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of several male enhancement dietary supplements due to the presence of undeclared substances. Laboratory testing revealed that the products, marketed under the names Male Ultra, Malextra, Electro Buzz, Ultra Armor, and Male Ultra Pro, contain chloropretadalafil, propoxyphenylsildenafil, and sildenafil. These ingredients are phosphodiesterase (PDE-5) inhibitors or analogues, which are not approved for use in dietary supplements and pose potential health risks.

Consumption of these products may lead to serious adverse effects, especially for individuals taking nitrates for heart conditions, as the combination can cause a dangerous drop in blood pressure. People with diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease who use nitrates are particularly at risk. Health Fixer has not received any reports of adverse events related to this recall to date.

The affected dietary supplements are packaged in blister packs containing 10 capsules each and were distributed nationwide through Amazon Online Shopping.

Product Name NDC/UPC Lot Number Expiration Date
Male Ultra B0CMQ4FTHG KT-1ST-43-0110-2025 01/10/2027
Malextra B0CWKZ6ZP3 KT-1ST-43-0104/2026 04/25/2026
Electro Buzz B0DK68LF6J KT-1ST-43-0107/2024 10/15/2026
Ultra Armor B0CYJ7Y5H9 KT-1ST-43-0110/2025 01/10/2027
Male Ultra Pro B0CZN7C6YH KT-1ST-43-0110/2025 03/15/2026

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the lot numbers of their Male Enhancement Supplements and if affected, discontinue use immediately, dispose of the product safely, and contact Health Fixer at 414-888-8818 or ultrahelpbyfixer@gmail.com for return or refund instructions.

title: Mauna Loa Recalls Dark Chocolate Covered Macadamias Due to Undeclared Almond and Cashew Allergens
headline: Mauna Loa Issues Allergy Alert for Dark Chocolate Covered Macadamias with Undeclared Almonds and Cashews in 0.6oz and 4oz Packs
description: Mauna Loa recalls Dark Chocolate Covered Macadamias due to undeclared almonds and cashews, posing allergy risks. Affected products distributed across multiple U.S. states and Guam.
keywords: Mauna Loa Macadamia Nut Company LLC
companyName: Mauna Loa Macadamia Nut Company LLC

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mauna Loa Macadamia Nut Company LLC of Kea’au, HI, has voluntarily issued a recall for certain Mauna Loa Dark Chocolate Covered Macadamias products due to the potential presence of undeclared almonds and cashews. This allergy alert affects consumers who have allergies or severe sensitivities to these nuts, as consuming the product could lead to serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recall involves Mauna Loa Dark Chocolate Covered Macadamias packaged in 0.6oz and 4oz bags. These products were distributed across various states including Arizona, California, Colorado, Florida, Hawaii, Illinois, Maine, Michigan, New Jersey, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Texas, Utah, Virginia, Washington, Wisconsin, and also Guam.

The issue was identified through Mauna Loa’s internal quality control process, which found that the affected batches, manufactured by a third-party co-manufacturer, contained undeclared almonds and cashews. No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported in connection with this recall to date.

Package Size UPC Lot Numbers Best By Date
0.6 oz bag 0 72992 05464 4 K5069C1, K5069C2 10/2026
4 oz bag 0 72992 05556 6 B4339E1, B4340E1 07/2026

facts.company: Mauna Loa Macadamia Nut Company LLC

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with allergies to almonds or cashews should check the lot number on Mauna Loa Dark Chocolate Covered Macadamias packaging and return recalled products to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Mauna Loa Customer Service at 1-888-255-5998, Monday through Friday.

title: Cronus Pharma LLC Recalls Dexmedetomidine Hydrochloride Injection 0.5 mg/mL (Dexased™ and Dexmedvet™) Due to Particulate Matter
headline: Cronus Pharma LLC Initiates Voluntary Recall for Dexased™ and Dexmedvet™ Dexmedetomidine Hydrochloride Injection 0.5 mg/mL Due to Visible Particulate Matter
description: Cronus Pharma LLC recalls Dexmedetomidine Hydrochloride Injection 0.5 mg/mL (Dexased™ and Dexmedvet™) over particulate matter risks in veterinary injectable sedation products.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Cronus Pharma LLC has announced a voluntary recall of two veterinary products containing Dexmedetomidine Hydrochloride Injection 0.5 mg/mL due to the presence of visible particulate matter. The affected products are marketed under the brand names Dexased™ (Aspen) and Dexmedvet™ (Cronus Pharma LLC). These injectable sedative and analgesic medications are used in dogs and cats to facilitate clinical examinations, minor surgical and dental procedures, and as a preanesthetic prior to general anesthesia.

The recall involves one batch consisting of two distinct lot numbers distributed between December 2024 and April 2025. The particulate matter observed includes crystal-like particles, white floating material, or precipitates found during visual inspection of control samples. The presence of particulate matter in an injectable product poses potential risks, such as inflammation, granuloma, fibrosis, or blood vessel blockage that could lead to stroke, life-threatening clots, or death.

If any animal shows signs of adverse reactions—such as pain, weakness, swelling, paralysis, fever, respiratory difficulty, vomiting, decreased activity, vocalization, or loss of consciousness—prompt veterinary consultation is strongly recommended.

Cronus Pharma LLC is working closely with distributors to remove the recalled products from distribution and arrange for their return. Consumers may contact the company for technical questions or product return details via phone, fax, or email. Adverse events can also be reported to the FDA through their dedicated contact channels.

Brand NDC Number Lot Number Manufacturing Date Expiration Date Distribution Dates
Dexased™ (Aspen) 46066-088-13 C24041AV1 Nov 2024 Oct 2026 December 2024 to April 4, 2025
Dexmedvet™ (Cronus Pharma LLC) 69043-038-10 C24041AV2 Nov 2024 Oct 2026 March 2025 to April 4, 2025

For technical questions or to report issues, contact Cronus Pharma LLC:

  • Phone: 1-844-227-6687 (Monday–Friday, 9 a.m. – 5 p.m. CDT)
  • Fax: 732-647-1272
  • Email: pv@cronuspharmausa.com

To report adverse reactions or quality problems to the FDA:

  • Phone: 1-888-FDA-VETS
  • Online: http://www.FDA.gov/reportanimalae

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately check their Dexased™ and Dexmedvet™ Dexmedetomidine Hydrochloride Injection 0.5 mg/mL for the affected lot numbers C24041AV1 and C24041AV2. They should follow the recall notification for product return instructions and contact Cronus Pharma LLC at 1-844-227-6687 or pv@cronuspharmausa.com for assistance.

title: New England Village Foods Recalls 19th Hole Snack Mix for Undeclared Almonds Allergy Risk
headline: New England Village Foods Recalls 19th Hole Snack Mix Due to Undeclared Almonds Posing Allergy Risk
description: New England Village Foods recalls 19th Hole Snack Mix for undeclared almonds, posing severe allergic reaction risk. Sold in New England, NY, and PA.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
New England Village Foods, based in Milford, NH, is recalling all lots of their “19th Hole Snack Mix” sold under the New England Village Snacks brand. The recall affects both 5-ounce and 10-ounce containers due to undeclared almonds in the product. This poses a serious risk to individuals with almond allergies, as consumption could trigger a severe or potentially life-threatening allergic reaction.

The affected products were distributed to small independent grocery markets and convenience stores across New England, New York, and Pennsylvania. The almond-containing snack mix was packaged in clear cups and tubs with re-closable lids, but the packaging failed to disclose the presence of almonds due to a temporary issue in the company’s packaging process. Production of the product has been temporarily suspended as a precaution.

No illnesses have been reported so far.

Product Description Package Size UPC
New England Village Snacks “19th Hole Snack Mix” 5-ounce clear cup with re-closable lid 609465693477
New England Village Snacks “19th Hole Snack Mix” 10-ounce clear tub with re-closable lid 642147152459

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased 5-ounce or 10-ounce 19th Hole Snack Mix should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund and may contact New England Village Foods at 1.603.554.1873 from Monday to Friday, 7 AM to 4 PM ET, for further information.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: New England Village Foods | 1.603.554.1873

title: Amneal Pharmaceuticals LLC Recalls Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection USP 500mg/100mL IV Bag Due to Potential Polypropylene Fiber Contamination
headline: Nationwide Recall of Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection USP 500mg/100mL IV Bag by Amneal Pharmaceuticals LLC for Possible Presence of Polypropylene Fibers
description: Amneal Pharmaceuticals LLC recalls Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection USP 500mg/100mL IV bags due to potential inert polypropylene fiber contamination posing health risks.
keywords: Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL IV bag
Product may contain an inert fiber identified as polypropylene fibers from the IV bag
productDescription: Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL IV bag
reason: Product may contain an inert fiber identified as polypropylene fibers from the IV bag

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Amneal Pharmaceuticals LLC has announced a nationwide recall of two lots of Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL infusion bags. The recall is due to the potential presence of inert polypropylene fiber particulates originating from the IV bag. These fibers could pose serious health risks if introduced into the epidural or intrathecal spaces during administration.

The product is commonly used for local or regional anesthesia in surgery and acute pain management. Exposure to polypropylene particulate matter in the epidural space may trigger inflammatory processes such as meningitis or cause spinal cord damage. If administered intrathecally, these particulates could lead to inflammation, hydrocephalus, embolization, and organ damage. Currently, there have been no reported adverse events or injuries linked to this recall.

The affected lots, AL240003 and AL240004, both expiring in January 2026, were distributed nationwide to wholesalers and distributors between April 23, 2024, and November 8, 2024. Amneal Pharmaceuticals is contacting customers directly to facilitate the return of recalled products and requests that wholesalers and distributors inform hospital and user facilities about the recall.

Healthcare providers and users with questions or requiring further assistance regarding this recall can contact Amneal Pharmaceuticals through the provided phone, fax, or email channels. The recall is conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Product Lot Number Expiration Date NDC Package Size
Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL IV bag AL240003 01/2026 70121-17343 12 x 100mL Single Dose IV bags
Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL IV bag AL240004 01/2026 70121-17343 12 x 100mL Single Dose IV bags

facts.product: Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL IV bag

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers and healthcare providers should immediately check if they have Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL IV bags from lots AL240003 or AL240004. Do not use affected products. Contact Amneal Pharmaceuticals at 1-877-835-5472 for return instructions and further assistance.

title: Recall Reminder: Gerber® Soothe N Chew® Teething Sticks Recalled Over Choking Hazard
headline: Gerber Products Company Recall of Gerber® Soothe N Chew® Teething Sticks Due to Choking Risk
description: Gerber recalled all batches of Gerber® Soothe N Chew® Teething Sticks due to choking hazards affecting babies and young children nationwide.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gerber Products Company has recalled and discontinued all batches of Gerber® Soothe N Chew® Teething Sticks because of a potential choking hazard for babies and young children. This recall was initiated on January 31, 2025, following consumer complaints of choking incidents. The recalled products were distributed nationwide and include specific varieties of the teething sticks.

Gerber is collaborating with the U.S. Food & Drug Administration (FDA) on this recall. Consumers with health concerns related to the product should consult a healthcare provider.

title: Harvest NYC Inc Recalls 200g Enoki Mushrooms Due to Listeria Contamination Risk
headline: Harvest NYC Inc Recalls Enoki Mushrooms Sold in 200g Packages Due to Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
description: Harvest NYC Inc recalls 200g Enoki Mushrooms due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. Distributed nationwide from January 11–31, 2025.
keywords: Due to possibility of contamination with Listeria monocytogenes.
reason: Due to possibility of contamination with Listeria monocytogenes.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Harvest NYC Inc, located in Brooklyn, NY, has issued a recall of its 200g packages of Enoki Mushrooms due to possible contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea.

The affected products were distributed nationwide through retail stores. The recall was initiated after the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets Food Laboratory detected Listeria monocytogenes in samples collected from a store in Buffalo, NY. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with these products.

Product Package Size UPC Barcode Distributor Distribution Period
Enoki Mushrooms 200g (green plastic package) 6975730520101 Hofood99 Inc., 21903 56th Ave, Oakland Gardens, NY 11364 January 11–31, 2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Enoki Mushroom packages for the 200g green plastic packaging with UPC 6975730520101 sold between January 11 and January 31, 2025. If affected, dispose of the product immediately or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Harvest NYC Inc at (718) 596-0777.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Harvest NYC Inc, located in Brooklyn, NY, has issued a recall of its 200g packages of Enoki Mushrooms due to possible contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea.

The affected products were distributed nationwide through retail stores. The recall was initiated after the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets Food Laboratory detected Listeria monocytogenes in samples collected from a store in Buffalo, NY. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with these products.

Product Package Size UPC Barcode Distributor Distribution Period
Enoki Mushrooms 200g (green plastic package) 6975730520101 Hofood99 Inc., 21903 56th Ave, Oakland Gardens, NY 11364 January 11–31, 2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Enoki Mushroom packages for the 200g green plastic packaging with UPC 6975730520101 sold between January 11 and January 31, 2025. If affected, dispose of the product immediately or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Harvest NYC Inc at (718) 596-0777.

title: Q’Apel Medical Recalls 072 Aspiration System (Hippo) Due to Distal Tip Design Outside 510(k) Clearance
headline: FDA Classifies Q’Apel Medical’s Worldwide Recall and Discontinuation of 072 Aspiration System (Hippo) as Class I
description: Q’Apel Medical recalls 072 Aspiration System worldwide due to distal tip design concerns outside 510(k) clearance, involving 1,617 units and reported adverse events.
keywords: Distal Tip Design outside scope of 510k clearance
reason: Distal Tip Design outside scope of 510k clearance

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has classified Q’Apel Medical Inc.’s voluntary recall and discontinuation of its 072 Aspiration System (also known as Hippo with Cheetah Delivery Tool) as a Class I recall. The recall involves a total of 1,617 units and was initiated by Q’Apel on February 26, 2025, following a Warning Letter from the FDA concerning whether the distal tip design of the Hippo aspiration catheter fell within the scope of its 510(k) clearance.

As a result, Q’Apel has chosen to voluntarily remove all affected product lots from distribution and discontinue the 072 Aspiration System line. This decision supports the company’s strategic shift toward newer technologies. The product was distributed in the United States, Qatar, United Arab Emirates, and the Republic of Kazakhstan.

Q’Apel Medical has reported three adverse events related to the Hippo product, including tip detachment (retrieved without patient injury), vessel rupture, and vasospasm. The company’s investigation suggests that factors other than the device’s distal tip likely contributed to these events.

Product DescriptionCatalog NumberUDILot Number(s)
072 Aspiration System (Hippo with Cheetah Delivery Tool) with Aspiration Tubing APT6072-132 00857545008127 FG241008C-03, FG240916C-04, FG240905C-04
072 Aspiration System (Hippo with Cheetah Delivery Tool) AP6072-132 00857545008097 FG241206A-03, FG240917A-01
072 Aspiration Tubing APT-95 00857545008103 FG241206A-04

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately cease use of any affected 072 Aspiration System devices, check their product against the listed catalog and lot numbers, and return all remaining inventory to Q’Apel Medical. For assistance, contact Q’Apel Medical Customer Service by phone at 510-738-6255 or via email at orders@qapelmedical.com.

content.text: Consumer support: Q’Apel Medical Inc. – Customer Service | 510-738-6255 | orders@qapelmedical.com

title: May Flower International Recalls Beijing Soybean Paste Due to Undeclared Wheat Allergy Risk
headline: May Flower International Recalls Beijing Soybean Paste for Undeclared Wheat Allergen
description: May Flower International recalls Beijing Soybean Paste due to undeclared wheat posing allergy risks. Distributed nationwide in retail stores, 2023-2024.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
May Flower International Inc. of Maspeth, NY, has issued a recall for its 8.82-ounce (250g) packages of “Beijing Soybean Paste” due to the presence of undeclared wheat. This poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions to individuals with wheat allergies.

The recalled product was distributed nationwide in retail stores and is packaged in plastic containers labeled with UPC 6917799000385. The packaging shows that the product was packed for Fu Xiang Yuan Trading Inc., New York City, NY 11378. The distribution period ranges from March 25, 2023, to March 5, 2024.

The recall was prompted by a routine FDA inspection that found the product listing flour as an ingredient, but failing to declare wheat, which is a major allergen. No illnesses or allergic reactions have been reported so far in connection with this product.

ProductPackage SizeUPCDistribution DatesPacked For
Beijing Soybean Paste8.82 ounces / 250g691779900038503/25/2023 to 03/05/2024Fu Xiang Yuan Trading Inc., New York City, NY 11378

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should return the recalled Beijing Soybean Paste to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact May Flower International Inc. at 1-718-366-8668, Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM Eastern Time.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 1-718-366-8668

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on April 17, 2025.

title: Supplement Manufacturing Partners, Inc. Recalls Dorado Nutrition Spermidine Supplement for Undeclared Wheat Allergen
headline: Undeclared Wheat Allergen Prompts Recall of Dorado Nutrition Spermidine Supplement by Supplement Manufacturing Partners, Inc.
description: Supplement Manufacturing Partners, Inc. recalls Dorado Nutrition Spermidine supplement due to undeclared wheat posing allergy risks. Sold online and in Germany.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Supplement Manufacturing Partners, Inc. has announced a voluntary recall of Dorado Nutrition brand Spermidine Maximum Strength 10 MG per serving due to the presence of undeclared wheat. This undeclared allergen poses a risk of serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with wheat allergies or severe sensitivities.

The recalled product was sold online through Amazon between May 22, 2024, and April 4, 2025. It was also distributed in Germany under the name Spermidin 60 Kapseln 38g by Deep Green GmbH, also known as Space Garden. The affected Dorado Nutrition Spermidine is packaged in white capsule bottles containing 120 capsules, featuring a blue outlined label.

The recall was initiated following product testing that revealed the presence of wheat not listed on the label. To date, no allergic or adverse reactions have been reported. The mislabeled products have been removed from sale to prevent further risk to consumers.

Brand Product Name Form Lot Number Best By Date Package Size Distribution Dates Retail Platforms
Dorado Nutrition Spermidine Maximum Strength 10 MG per serving Vegetable Capsules 12792402-44, 12792402-44J 04/2026 120 capsules per bottle 05/22/2024 – 04/04/2025 Amazon (online)
Space Garden (Deep Green GmbH) Spermidin 60 Kapseln 38g Capsules Not specified Not specified 60 capsules per bottle Not specified Germany

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Dorado Nutrition or Space Garden Spermidine supplements should return the affected products to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Supplement Manufacturing Partners, Inc. at 833-810-9896 or via email at SMPQuality@smpnutra.com.

title: Blue Ridge Beef Voluntary Recall of Puppy Mix and Kitten Mix Due to Salmonella and Listeria Contamination
headline: Blue Ridge Beef Recalls Puppy Mix and Kitten Mix Following Salmonella and Listeria Contamination
description: Blue Ridge Beef recalls Puppy Mix and Kitten Mix 2 lb logs over Salmonella and Listeria contamination, distributed in nine states from Jan 3-24, 2025.
keywords: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Blue Ridge Beef has issued a voluntary recall of certain lots of its Puppy Mix and Kitten Mix due to potential contamination with Salmonella and Listeria bacteria. The recalled products include 2 lb logs from Lot #N25 1230 of the Puppy Mix and Lot #N26 0114 of the Kitten Mix. These products were distributed between January 3 and January 24, 2025, primarily to retail stores in Virginia, Maryland, Pennsylvania, Connecticut, Massachusetts, New York State, Tennessee, Rhode Island, and Wisconsin.

Salmonella and Listeria can pose health risks to both pets and humans. Pets consuming the contaminated food may experience symptoms such as lethargy, diarrhea (possibly bloody), fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, or abdominal pain. Infected animals can also carry and spread the infections without showing symptoms. Humans handling the products may experience nausea, vomiting, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, abdominal cramps, and fever. More severe complications can occur, including arterial infections and other serious conditions. Anyone experiencing symptoms after contact with the recalled products should seek medical advice, and pet owners should contact their veterinarians if their animals have consumed the affected food and show illness.

The recall was initiated following a customer complaint and subsequent testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets, which confirmed contamination with Salmonella and Listeria.

Product Size Lot Number
Blue Ridge Beef Puppy Mix 2 lb N25 1230
Blue Ridge Beef Kitten Mix 2 lb N26 0114

Consumers are urged to exercise caution due to the serious health risks Salmonella and Listeria pose to humans and animals.

facts.productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should identify if they have the recalled products by checking the lot numbers and 2 lb size; do not feed affected products to pets, and return them to the place of purchase or dispose of them safely. For additional guidance or questions, contact Blue Ridge Beef at 704-873-2072 (Mon–Fri, 8:00 am–5:00 pm EST) or via email at blueridgebeefnc@yahoo.com.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 704-873-2072 | blueridgebeefnc@yahoo.com

title: Duda Farm Fresh Foods Recalls Marketside Celery Sticks Due to Possible Listeria Contamination
headline: Recall of Marketside Celery Sticks 4 in / 1.6 oz Bundle Pack Due to Potential Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
description: Duda Farm Fresh Foods recalls Marketside Celery Sticks sold at Walmart due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination; no illnesses reported.
keywords: Due to possibility of contamination with Listeria monocytogenes.
reason: Due to possibility of contamination with Listeria monocytogenes.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Duda Farm Fresh Foods, Inc. has issued a voluntary recall concerning a specific batch of Marketside Celery Sticks (4 inches / 1.6 oz), washed and ready to eat, with an expiration date of March 23, 2025. Although this product is past its expiration date and no longer available in stores, consumers may have frozen it for later use. The recall was prompted by the possible presence of Listeria monocytogenes, identified through random sampling by the Georgia Department of Agriculture at a store in Georgia, where one of several samples tested positive.

Listeria monocytogenes is a bacterium that can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, people with weakened immune systems, and pregnant women. While healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea, infection during pregnancy can lead to miscarriage or stillbirth. To date, no illnesses linked to this product have been reported.

The recalled product was sold exclusively at Walmart stores in selected states and is identified by the UPC code 6 81131 16151 0 and lot code P047650, both printed on the packaging. This recall applies only to the specified celery sticks and not to any other Marketside or Duda Farm Fresh Foods products. All affected products have expired and have been removed from store shelves.

Product Store States Distributed UPC Code Lot Code Expiration Date Package Size
Marketside Celery Sticks 4 in / 1.6 oz Bundle Pack Walmart AL, CA, CO, DC, DE, FL, GA, HI, IA, IL, IN, KS, KY, MD, MI, MO, MT, NC, NJ, NY, OH, PA, SC, TN, TX, VA, WI, WV, WY 6 81131 16151 0 P047650 03/23/2025 4 / 1.6-ounce, bag

For media inquiries, contact Cathleen Conley at Corpcomm@duda.com.

For more information, the English version of the recall announcement is available here.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Marketside Celery Sticks for the UPC code 6 81131 16151 0 and lot code P047650; if found, they must dispose of the product and contact Duda Farm Fresh Foods at foodsafety@duda.com for assistance.

en.description: Duda Farm Fresh Foods recalls Marketside Celery Sticks sold at Walmart due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination; no illnesses reported.
en.reason: Due to possibility of contamination with Listeria monocytogenes.
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Produce Foodborne Illness, Food & Beverages, Produce, Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Duda Farm Fresh Foods, Inc. has issued a voluntary recall concerning a specific batch of Marketside Celery Sticks (4 inches / 1.6 oz), washed and ready to eat, with an expiration date of March 23, 2025. Although this product is past its expiration date and no longer available in stores, consumers may have frozen it for later use. The recall was prompted by the possible presence of Listeria monocytogenes, identified through random sampling by the Georgia Department of Agriculture at a store in Georgia, where one of several samples tested positive.

Listeria monocytogenes is a bacterium that can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, people with weakened immune systems, and pregnant women. While healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea, infection during pregnancy can lead to miscarriage or stillbirth. To date, no illnesses linked to this product have been reported.

The recalled product was sold exclusively at Walmart stores in selected states and is identified by the UPC code 6 81131 16151 0 and lot code P047650, both printed on the packaging. This recall applies only to the specified celery sticks and not to any other Marketside or Duda Farm Fresh Foods products. All affected products have expired and have been removed from store shelves.

Product Store States Distributed UPC Code Lot Code Expiration Date Package Size
Marketside Celery Sticks 4 in / 1.6 oz Bundle Pack Walmart AL, CA, CO, DC, DE, FL, GA, HI, IA, IL, IN, KS, KY, MD, MI, MO, MT, NC, NJ, NY, OH, PA, SC, TN, TX, VA, WI, WV, WY 6 81131 16151 0 P047650 03/23/2025 4 / 1.6-ounce, bag

For media inquiries, contact Cathleen Conley at Corpcomm@duda.com.

For more information, the English version of the recall announcement is available here.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Marketside Celery Sticks for the UPC code 6 81131 16151 0 and lot code P047650; if found, they must dispose of the product and contact Duda Farm Fresh Foods at foodsafety@duda.com for assistance.

Media contact: Cathleen Conley | | Corpcomm@duda.com

title: Trophy Nut Co. Recalls Heinen’s Honey Roasted Peanuts Due to Undeclared Cashew Allergen
headline: Trophy Nut Company Issues Recall for Heinen’s Honey Roasted Peanuts Contaminated with Undeclared Cashews
description: Trophy Nut Co. recalls Heinen’s Honey Roasted Peanuts due to undeclared cashew allergen; distributed in Ohio and Illinois between Oct 2024 and Apr 2025.
keywords: Food & Beverages Nuts & Nut Products Allergens
productType: Food & Beverages Nuts & Nut Products Allergens
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Nuts & Nut Products Allergens

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Trophy Nut Company of Tipp City, Ohio, is recalling 143 cases of Heinen’s 8.25 oz cans of Honey Roasted Peanuts due to the potential presence of undeclared cashews. These products may pose a serious health risk to individuals with cashew allergies or severe sensitivities, as consuming them could lead to life-threatening allergic reactions.

The affected Honey Roasted Peanuts were distributed exclusively in Ohio and Illinois through Heinen’s Grocery stores between October 24, 2024, and April 4, 2025. The product is packaged in an 8.25 oz composite can with a lid, featuring “best if used by” and “best by” dates stamped on the bottom of the can.

This recall was prompted by a consumer complaint reporting that the Honey Roasted Peanuts contained cashews. An investigation revealed the issue resulted from a temporary breakdown in the company’s production and packaging processes. To date, one allergic reaction related to this issue has been reported.

Product Size Best If Used By Date Best By Date UPC Distribution Area Date Range
Heinen’s Honey Roasted Peanuts 8.25 oz composite can with lid 10-01-2025 10-03-2025 20601401877 Ohio, Illinois (Heinen’s Grocery stores) October 24, 2024 – April 4, 2025

facts.productType: Food & Beverages Nuts & Nut Products Allergens

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Heinen’s Honey Roasted Peanuts 8.25 oz cans for the specified best if used by or best by dates and return affected products to any Heinen’s Grocery store for a full refund. For questions, contact Trophy Nut Company at 1-800-729-6887 or custserv@trophynut.com.

content.text:

title: Recall of Caraluzzi’s Italian Style Seafood Burgers Due to Undeclared Egg Allergen
headline: Caraluzzi’s Markets Issues Recall for Italian Style Seafood Burgers Containing Undeclared Egg Allergen
description: Caraluzzi’s recalls 8 oz Italian Style Seafood Burgers for undeclared egg allergen, posing a serious risk to individuals with egg allergies. Sold in CT stores.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Caraluzzi’s Markets of Bethel, Connecticut, is recalling the 8 oz Caraluzzi’s Italian Style Seafood Burgers due to the presence of undeclared egg. This undeclared allergen poses a serious risk for individuals with an egg allergy or severe sensitivity, potentially causing life-threatening allergic reactions if consumed.

The affected product was sold in-store at Caraluzzi’s Markets locations in Bethel, Wilton, Newtown, and Danbury, Connecticut. The seafood burger comes packaged in 8 oz clear packaging with a blue overwrap label. The expiration date of these products is January 16, 2026, and the package UPC is 0-95864-80008-8. Sales occurred between February 18 and April 2, 2025.

The recall was prompted after a consumer complaint led to an investigation that identified the presence of egg in products with packaging that did not disclose this allergen. To date, one allergic reaction has been reported. The mislabeled seafood burgers have been removed from stores.

Product Weight Packaging Details Expiration Date UPC Sale Dates Store Locations
Caraluzzi’s Italian Style Seafood Burger 8 oz Clear packaging with blue overwrap label 01/16/2026 0-95864-80008-8 February 18 to April 2, 2025 Bethel, Wilton, Newtown, Danbury (CT)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should return the affected 8 oz Italian Style Seafood Burgers to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions or more information, contact Caraluzzi’s Markets at 203-748-3547, email customerservice@caraluzzis.com, or visit the customer service desk at any Caraluzzi’s Markets location.

title: Max Mobility / Permobil Expands Nationwide Recall of SmartDrive Speed Control Dial Due to Motor Unresponsiveness
headline: Max Mobility / Permobil Issues Expanded Nationwide Recall of SmartDrive Speed Control Dial Causing Motor Control Failures
description: Max Mobility/Permobil expands recall of SmartDrive Speed Control Dial due to motor control failures causing injury risks from August 2023 to March 2025 units.
keywords: Expanded recall due to potential safety and performance concerns
reason: Expanded recall due to potential safety and performance concerns

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Max Mobility, LLC., trading under the Max Mobility/Permobil brand, has announced an expansion of the nationwide recall of their Speed Control Dial component used with the SmartDrive MX2+ Power Assist Device. The recall, initially issued on December 20, 2024, now includes all Speed Control Dials manufactured and distributed between August 17, 2023, and March 10, 2025. This expansion addresses products that may have been inadvertently mixed with redesigned units and shipped after the original recall date.

The Speed Control Dial controls the SmartDrive motor, enabling users to power or deactivate the device. However, a material change identified in the printed circuit board assembly (PCBA) has led to performance issues, including unintended motor activation while the dial appears off, failure to start or stop motor movement properly, and unexpected shutdowns.

As of March 27, 2025, Max Mobility/Permobil received 781 complaints concerning the Speed Control Dial and five serious injury reports, which include fractures (hip, tibia, malleolus bone, ribs) and a concussion. Minor injuries such as skin irritation, cuts, and bruises have also been reported. Involuntary motor activation or inability to turn off the device raises risks not only for the user but also for bystanders in close proximity.

Brand Model Number Manufacturing Dates
Max Mobility / Permobil MX2-3DCK / MX2-3DC August 17, 2023 – March 10, 2025

Reported Performance Issues Include:

  • Speed Control Dial does not fully stop the drive unit when set to zero position.
  • Unintended activation of SmartDrive motor while the dial is at zero and flashing in standby mode.
  • Motor unexpectedly shuts down after dial is rotated forward from zero position.
  • No motor activation when dial is rotated forward from zero position.

Reported Injuries:

  • Minor: skin irritation, minor cuts, bruises.
  • Serious: muscle/ligament strains or tears, bone fractures (hip, tibia, malleolus bone, ribs), concussion.
  • Risk to bystanders near the user due to involuntary or persistent motor activation.

Contact Information for Support and Reporting:

  • Max Mobility / Permobil Technical Support: (615) 257-2930, available Monday through Friday, 8:00 am to 5:00 pm CT.
  • To report adverse reactions or quality problems to the FDA:
    • Online: MedWatch Online Reporting
    • Regular mail or fax: Download forms at FDA MedWatch Forms or call 800-332-1088 to request a form; mail to the address on the form or fax to 800-332-0178.

For further assistance or information, contact:

  • Max Mobility/Permobil, 300 Duke Drive, Lebanon, TN 37090
  • Phone: 615-257-2930
  • Website: www.permobil.com
  • Audra Watt, VP Marketing, Permobil Americas - Phone: +1 615 992 4221, Email: audra.watt@permobil.com

facts.reason: Expanded recall due to potential safety and performance concerns

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using the affected Speed Control Dial and switch to alternative wired or wearable controllers. Replacement units can be requested through the company’s field action portal at https://hub.permobil.com/smartdrive-scd-voluntary-field-action. For support, contact Max Mobility at (615) 257-2930.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: Max Mobility / Permobil Technical Support Team | 615-257-2930
Media contact: Audra Watt | VP Marketing, Permobil Americas | | | audra.watt@permobil.com | +1 615 992 4221

title: Bausch + Lomb Voluntarily Recalls enVista Aspire™, enVista Envy™ and enVista Monofocal Intraocular Lenses in U.S. Due to TASS Reports
headline: Bausch + Lomb Announces Voluntary Recall of enVista Aspire™, enVista Envy™ and Certain enVista® Monofocal Intraocular Lenses in the U.S. Due to Toxic Anterior Segment Syndrome
description: Bausch + Lomb recalls enVista Aspire™, enVista Envy™, and certain enVista® Monofocal lenses in the U.S. following increased reports of toxic anterior segment syndrome post cataract surgery.
keywords: enVista Aspire™, enVista Envy™ and Certain enVista® Monofocal Intraocular Lenses
Increased frequency of reports of toxic anterior segment syndrome
productDescription: enVista Aspire™, enVista Envy™ and Certain enVista® Monofocal Intraocular Lenses
reason: Increased frequency of reports of toxic anterior segment syndrome

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Bausch + Lomb Corporation has announced a voluntary recall of certain intraocular lenses (IOLs) within its enVista platform in the United States. This recall is prompted by an increased frequency of reports of toxic anterior segment syndrome (TASS), a rare inflammatory complication that can develop 12 to 48 hours following cataract surgery. The exact cause of these complications is currently under investigation.

The recall includes all lots of the enVista Aspire™, enVista Aspire Toric, enVista Envy™, enVista Envy Toric, as well as enVista monofocal and enVista monofocal Toric intraocular lens models sold in the U.S. According to Bausch + Lomb, reported cases of TASS associated with these lenses have been treated successfully without the need to remove the lenses. These cases represent approximately 1-2% of all implanted enVista lenses, and patients have shown a positive prognosis following treatment.

Eye care professionals are urged to monitor patients carefully for at least 48 hours post-surgery and to instruct patients to report any new or worsening symptoms promptly.

Further communication and instructions regarding the recall and product return are being issued to eye care professionals. Consumers and clinicians seeking more information or wishing to report adverse events can contact Bausch + Lomb Customer Service or the U.S. FDA’s MedWatch program.

Recalled Products
enVista Aspire™
enVista Aspire Toric
enVista Envy™
enVista Envy Toric
enVista monofocal
enVista monofocal Toric

title: T.W. Garner Food Company Recalls Texas Pete® Habanero Buffalo and Sweet CHAbanero Sauces for Undeclared Sulfites and Mislabeling
headline: Recall Issued for Texas Pete® Habanero Buffalo Sauce Due to Undeclared Sulfites and Sweet CHAbanero Sweet Sriracha Habanero Sauce Due to Mislabeling
description: T.W. Garner Food Company recalls select Texas Pete® sauces for undeclared sulfites and labeling errors affecting consumers with allergies. Distributed in multiple states.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
T.W. Garner Food Company has announced a recall of select lots and sizes of Texas Pete® Habanero Buffalo Sauce and Texas Pete® Sweet CHAbanero Sweet Sriracha Habanero Sauce. The recall is due to potential undeclared sulfites in the Habanero Buffalo Sauce and mislabeling issues with the Sweet CHAbanero Sauce. Specifically, some bottles of the Habanero Buffalo Sauce were found to contain Sriracha Sauce, which includes sodium bisulfite (a sulfite preservative) not declared on the label. This poses a risk for consumers with sulfite allergies or sensitivities, who may experience serious or life-threatening reactions if they consume the product.

Additionally, the Sweet CHAbanero Sauce is being recalled because certain lots do not contain the labeled ingredient "Aged Peppers" (Red Habanero Peppers, Salt, Vinegar), meaning the product's contents do not match the label. No illnesses or adverse health events have been reported in connection with these products to date.

The affected products were distributed between February 5, 2025, and March 26, 2025, and were shipped to retail stores and distribution centers in California, Colorado, Connecticut, Illinois, Massachusetts, North Carolina, New Jersey, Ohio, Virginia, and Vermont.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check product labels for the affected lots and sizes, and if they have these Texas Pete® Habanero Buffalo Sauce or Sweet CHAbanero Sweet Sriracha Habanero Sauce products, they should dispose of them or return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Mrs. Katerine Cardoso at (336) 231-6417 (Mon-Fri, 8am-6pm EST) or email ContactUs@garnerfoods.com.

title: Supercan Bulk Recalls Single Lot of Pig Ear Slivers Pet Treats Due to Salmonella Risk
headline: Supercan Bulk Issues Recall on Pig Ear Slivers Pet Treats Over Salmonella Contamination
description: Supercan Bulk recalls one lot of Pig Ear Slivers pet treats due to confirmed Salmonella contamination, posing health risks to pets and humans.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Supercan Bulk, based in Miami, Florida, is recalling one lot of Supercan Pig Ear Slivers - Thick Cut Piggy Ear Slices pet treats due to potential Salmonella contamination. The affected product comes in 500-piece bags, weighing 15.9 oz, with an expiration date of November 9, 2026. The recall was initiated after the Washington State Department of Agriculture collected and tested samples on March 4, 2025, confirming the presence of Salmonella.

Salmonella can cause illness in both pets and humans. Pets exposed to contaminated treats may show signs such as lethargy, diarrhea (including bloody diarrhea), fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Healthy pets can also be carriers of Salmonella and may infect other animals or humans. Humans infected with Salmonella may experience nausea, vomiting, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), abdominal cramps, and fever. More serious complications can occur in rare cases.

The recalled products were distributed on April 11, 2024, exclusively at the North 40 store in Washington State. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

Product Package Size Lot Number Expiration Date Distribution Location Distribution Date
Supercan Pig Ear Slivers - Thick Cut Piggy Ear Slices 500 pieces / 15.9 oz bag Single recalled lot (not specified) 11/09/2026 North 40 store, Washington State 04/11/2024

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check product lot numbers and details to confirm if their Pig Ear Slivers are affected. If so, they should return the product to the North 40 store or dispose of it safely to prevent exposure. For questions or to request a refund, contact Supercan Bulk at (689) 808-5419, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET.

content.text: Consumer support: (689) 808-5419

title: Panaderia Salvadorena Recalls Quesadilla de Queso Bread for Undeclared Milk Allergy Risk
headline: Panaderia Salvadorena Recalls Quesadilla de Queso Bread Due to Undeclared Milk Posing Allergy Risk
description: Panaderia Salvadorena recalls Quesadilla de Queso bread containing undeclared milk, risking allergic reactions. Distributed in Rhode Island and Massachusetts.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Panaderia Salvadorena Inc., located in Providence, Rhode Island, has issued a recall for its 14-ounce packages of Quesadilla de Queso bread due to the presence of undeclared milk. This poses a serious risk to individuals with milk allergies, as consuming the product may trigger life-threatening allergic reactions.

The affected Quesadilla de Queso bread was distributed through direct delivery in Rhode Island and Massachusetts. The product comes in a clear plastic package weighing 14 ounces, labeled with "Quesadilla de Queso" on the top and featuring an expiration date of June 27, 2025, or earlier.

The recall follows an inspection by the Rhode Island Department of Health, which found that the packaging did not disclose the presence of milk, listing only sour cream and butter as ingredients. The issue was traced back to a temporary failure in the company’s packaging process. Panaderia Salvadorena has recalled the affected product and confirmed that the problem has been resolved.

No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall to date.

ProductPackage SizeExpiration DateDistribution Area
Quesadilla de Queso bread14 ounces06/27/2025 or priorRhode Island, Massachusetts

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their 14-ounce Quesadilla de Queso bread packaging for an expiration date of June 27, 2025, or earlier, and return affected products to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Panaderia Salvadorena Inc. at 401-421-8410 Monday through Saturday, 8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m.

content.text:
Consumer support: 401-421-8410

title: Tony's Chocolonely Recalls Dark Chocolate Almond Sea Salt Bar and Everything Bar Due to Possible Small Stones
headline: Tony's Chocolonely Voluntarily Recalls Two Chocolate Bar Products Over Potential Small Stones Contamination
description: Tony’s Chocolonely recalls Dark Chocolate Almond Sea Salt Bar and Everything Bar due to potential small stones found in specific lots distributed nationwide.
keywords: Dark Chocolate Almond Sea Salt Bar and Everything Bar
productDescription: Dark Chocolate Almond Sea Salt Bar and Everything Bar

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tony’s Chocolonely Inc., based in New York, New York, is voluntarily recalling two chocolate products due to the potential presence of small stones. The affected items include the Dark Chocolate Almond Sea Salt Bar and the Everything Bar. These products were distributed nationally across the United States through retail stores and online via the official Tony’s Chocolonely website.

The recall concerns seven specific lots of the 6.35 oz (180g) bars, which were distributed between February 7, 2025, and March 24, 2025. The issue was identified after 12 reports from consumers outside the United States and Canada found small stones in the product. These foreign objects were not removed during the third-party almond harvesting and processing stages. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.

Product Description Weight Lot Code Best Before / Best By Date UPC

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tony’s Chocolonely Inc., based in New York, New York, is voluntarily recalling two chocolate products due to the potential presence of small stones. The affected items include the Dark Chocolate Almond Sea Salt Bar and the Everything Bar. These products were distributed nationally across the United States through retail stores and online via the official Tony’s Chocolonely website.

The recall concerns seven specific lots of the 6.35 oz (180g) bars, which were distributed between February 7, 2025, and March 24, 2025. The issue was identified after 12 reports from consumers outside the United States and Canada found small stones in the product. These foreign objects were not removed during the third-party almond harvesting and processing stages. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.

title: Walker’s Wine Juice LLC Recalls Pumpkin Juice Due to Potential Botulism Risk
headline: Walker’s Wine Juice LLC Issues Recall of Pumpkin Juice Products Over Botulism Concern
description: Walker’s Wine Juice LLC recalls all lots of pumpkin juice due to elevated pH levels risking botulism contamination. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Walker’s Wine Juice LLC of Forestville, NY, has announced a recall of its pumpkin juice products due to the potential presence of botulism, a serious and potentially fatal foodborne illness. Botulism symptoms include general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing problems, muscle weakness, abdominal distension, and constipation. Anyone experiencing these symptoms after consuming the product should seek immediate medical attention.

The recalled products include various packaging sizes labeled as "pumpkin," encompassing 2.5-gallon bag-in-box, 5-gallon hot packs, and bulk containers of 30, 60, and 275 gallons. All lots of these pumpkin juice products are subject to the recall.

Distribution of the pumpkin juice occurred through Walker’s Wine Juice retail store in New York and directly to a limited number of commercial wineries across Illinois, Indiana, Kansas, Kentucky, Maine, Michigan, Minnesota, New Jersey, New York, Ohio, Pennsylvania, and Wisconsin. No illnesses have been reported to date.

The recall followed an inspection by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets Food Inspectors, which revealed that the pumpkin juice's pH level was too high to meet the requirements of Walker’s “hot fill” schedule. Consequently, the product did not undergo an adequate kill step to eliminate possible microbiological hazards such as botulism.

Product Description Packaging Lot Information
Pumpkin Juice 2.5-gallon bag in box All lots
Pumpkin Juice 5-gallon hot pack All lots
Pumpkin Juice 30-, 60-, and 275-gallon bulk containers All lots

For more information or to report concerns, consumers can contact Walker’s Wine Juice LLC directly.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should stop using all lots of Walker’s Wine Juice pumpkin juice products and contact Walker’s Wine Juice LLC at 716-679-1292 for instructions on disposal and reimbursement options.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Matthew Walker | 716-679-1292

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on April 01, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

title: Trader Joe’s Hot Honey Mustard Dressing Recalled by Fresh Creative Foods for Undeclared Peanut, Soy, Sesame, and Wheat Allergens
headline: Fresh Creative Foods Recalls Trader Joe’s Hot Honey Mustard Dressing Use By 05/27/2025 Due to Undeclared Allergens
description: Trader Joe’s Hot Honey Mustard Dressing with Use By 05/27/2025 recalled for undeclared peanut, soy, sesame, and wheat allergens. Affected products distributed in multiple states.
keywords: Undeclared allergen - peanut, soy, sesame, and wheat.
reason: Undeclared allergen - peanut, soy, sesame, and wheat.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Fresh Creative Foods has issued a voluntary recall for a specific batch of Trader Joe’s Hot Honey Mustard Dressing due to an undeclared allergen risk. The product, which carries a Use By Date of 05/27/2025, was mistakenly labeled without allergen information for peanuts, soy, sesame, and wheat.

This recall affects only products distributed to select Trader Joe’s stores in multiple states, including Arkansas, Colorado, and Texas, among others. There have been no reported customer complaints related to this issue as of the recall announcement.

Product Name Use By Date Recall Reason Distribution States
Trader Joe’s Hot Honey Mustard Dressing 05/27/2025 Undeclared allergens: peanuts, soy, sesame, wheat AR, CO, DC, DE, FL, GA, KS, LA, MA, MD, NC, NM, OH, OK, PA, SC, TX, VA

facts.reason: Undeclared allergen - peanut, soy, sesame, and wheat.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Trader Joe’s Hot Honey Mustard Dressing with Use By Date 05/27/2025 for undeclared peanut, soy, sesame, and wheat allergens. Return affected products to Trader Joe’s for a full refund or discard if returns are not possible. For questions, contact Fresh Creative Foods at 888-223-2127, Monday to Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM Pacific Time.

content.text: Consumer support: Customer Inquiries | 888-223-2127
