title: The Bakery Group Recalls Ben E. Keith, Rodeo Goat, Casa Linda Brioche Products for Undeclared Milk, Soy, and Yellow FD&C #5
headline: The Bakery Group Recalls Specific Brioche Loaves and Buns Due to Undeclared Milk, Soy, and Yellow FD&C #5 Allergens
description: The Bakery Group recalls Brioche loaves and buns distributed to Ben E. Keith and Rodeo Goat Casa Linda due to undeclared milk, soy, and Yellow FD&C #5 allergens.
keywords: Ben E. Keith, Rodeo Goat, Casa Linda
May contain undeclared milk, soy and yellow FD&C # 5
brandName: Ben E. Keith, Rodeo Goat, Casa Linda
brandNames: Ben E. Keith, Rodeo Goat, Casa Linda
reason: May contain undeclared milk, soy and yellow FD&C # 5

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The Bakery Group, based in Dallas, Texas, has issued a recall for specific bakery products due to undeclared allergens including milk, soy, and Yellow FD&C #5. This recall addresses potential health risks for individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to these substances.

The affected products include Dense Brioche Pullman loaves and 4.5-inch Brioche Hamburger Buns. These items were distributed to Ben E. Keith in Fort Worth, Texas, and the Rodeo Goat Casa Linda Store in Dallas, Texas. The recall involves cases shipped between December 24, 2024, and March 12, 2025, which may have packaging labels that do not disclose the presence of these allergens.

The Bakery Group identified the labeling error during a routine inspection by the Texas Health and Human Services and confirmed it resulted from human error in their labeling and packaging department. Corrective actions were implemented as of March 19, 2025. No illnesses have been reported related to this recall.

Brioche 4.5-inch Hamburger Buns 54500 Packaged 48 buns per case Brown box with Ben E. Keith label December 24, 2024 – March 12, 2025 Ben E. Keith (Ft. Worth)

title: Cromer Food Services Recalls Chicken Salad on White Sandwich Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Recall of Chicken Salad on White Sandwich by Cromer Food Services for Undeclared Milk Allergen
description: Cromer Food Services recalls Chicken Salad on White Sandwich due to undeclared milk allergen; sold in GA and SC Micro Markets from Dec 2024 to Mar 2025.
keywords: Chicken salad on white bread sandwich
productDescription: Chicken salad on white bread sandwich

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Cromer Food Services, Inc. is voluntarily recalling all lots of their CFS Cromer Food Service brand Chicken Salad on White Sandwich due to undeclared milk present in the bread. The recall was initiated after the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) identified that the product label did not declare milk as an ingredient during a routine inspection. Individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities risk serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume this product.

The affected Chicken Salad on White Sandwich products were distributed from December 26, 2024, through March 24, 2025. These sandwiches were packaged in clear plastic and primarily sold through Micro Markets and Vending Machines in Georgia and South Carolina. No illnesses related to consumption of this product have been reported to date.

Product DescriptionUPCDate Range Distributed
Chicken Salad on White Sandwich3116612/26/2024 – 03/24/2025
Chicken Salad on White Sandwich1317212/26/2024 – 03/24/2025

content.text: Consumers who purchased the affected Chicken Salad on White Sandwich should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund or discard it. For questions or assistance, contact Cromer Food Services, Inc. at 1-800-922-3174, available 24/7.

title: Frito-Lay Recalls Tostitos Cantina Traditional Yellow Corn Tortilla Chips Due to Undeclared Milk
headline: Frito-Lay Announces Limited Recall of Tostitos Cantina Traditional Yellow Corn Tortilla Chips for Undeclared Milk Allergen
description: Frito-Lay recalls select Tostitos Cantina Traditional Yellow Corn Tortilla Chips due to undeclared milk posing allergy risks in 13 states.
keywords: Cantina Traditional Yellow Corn Tortilla Chips
productDescription: Cantina Traditional Yellow Corn Tortilla Chips

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Frito-Lay has announced a recall of a limited number of 13 oz. bags of Tostitos Cantina Traditional Yellow Corn Tortilla Chips due to the potential presence of undeclared milk. This issue arises because some bags may contain nacho cheese tortilla chips, posing a risk to individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities. Consuming the affected product could result in serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recalled products were distributed in retail stores—including grocery, convenience, and drug stores—as well as via e-commerce platforms in thirteen states: Alabama, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Mississippi, North Carolina, Ohio, South Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia, and West Virginia. These products have been available for purchase since March 7, 2025.

Fewer than 1,300 bags are included in this recall. To date, no allergic reactions have been reported in connection with this recall. It is important to note that no other Tostitos products, flavors, sizes, or variety packs are affected by this action.

Product Description Code/Date Information
13 oz. (368.5 grams) Tostitos Cantina Traditional Yellow Corn Tortilla Chips Must have both:
• "Guaranteed Fresh" date of 20 MAY 2025
• One of the following Manufacturing Codes, where "XX" is any number from 30 up to 55:
471106504 18 13:XX
471106505 85 13:XX
471106506 85 13:XX
471106507 85 13:XX

For additional information, contact Frito-Lay Consumer Relations at 1-800-352-4477 (Monday through Friday, 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CST).

title: CAPS Nationwide Recall of Phenylephrine 40 mg in 0.9% Sodium Chloride 250 mL Excel Bags for Visible Black Particulate Matter
headline: CAPS Recalls Phenylephrine 40 mg in 0.9% Sodium Chloride 250 mL Excel Bags Due to Visible Black Particulate Contamination
description: CAPS recalls Phenylephrine 40 mg in 0.9% Sodium Chloride 250 mL Excel Bags due to visible black particulate matter posing risk to patients.
keywords: Phenylephrine 40 mg added to 0.9% Sodium Chloride 250 mL in 250 mL Excel Bag
Due to visible black particulate matter
productDescription: Phenylephrine 40 mg added to 0.9% Sodium Chloride 250 mL in 250 mL Excel Bag
reason: Due to visible black particulate matter

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Central Admixture Pharmacy Services (CAPS) has announced a nationwide recall of three lots of Phenylephrine 40 mg added to 0.9% Sodium Chloride 250 mL in 250 mL Excel Bags. This recall is issued due to the detection of visible black particulate matter found in a single sealed vial of Phenylephrine Hydrochloride, which poses a risk if administered to patients.

The presence of particulate matter in injectable products may cause local irritation or swelling as the body reacts to the foreign material. More seriously, if the particulates enter the bloodstream, they could block blood vessels in vital organs such as the heart, lungs, or brain, potentially leading to complications including stroke or death. CAPS has not received any reports of adverse events related to this recall to date.

The recalled products were distributed to hospitals across the United States, with expiration dates ranging from March 3 to March 10, 2025. CAPS is actively notifying distributors and customers and arranging for the return of affected stock. Customers are requested to complete a “Urgent Pharmaceutical Recall Response Form” to confirm receipt and inventory status.

NDC Product Description Lot Number Expiration Date Distribution Dates Region Distributed
71285-6092-1 Phenylephrine 40 mg added to 0.9% Sodium Chloride 250 mL in 250 mL Excel Bag 37-928390 03MAR2025 17 Dec 2024 United States
71285-6092-1 Phenylephrine 40 mg added to 0.9% Sodium Chloride 250 mL in 250 mL Excel Bag 37-928796 09MAR2025 26 Dec 2024 United States
71285-6092-1 Phenylephrine 40 mg added to 0.9% Sodium Chloride 250 mL in 250 mL Excel Bag 37-928839 10MAR2025 03 Jan 2025 – 08 Jan 2025 United States

facts.product: Phenylephrine 40 mg added to 0.9% Sodium Chloride 250 mL in 250 mL Excel Bag
facts.reason: Due to visible black particulate matter

content.text: Healthcare providers and facilities should check their inventories for the affected Phenylephrine 40 mg in 0.9% Sodium Chloride 250 mL Excel Bags lot numbers and complete the “Urgent Pharmaceutical Recall Response Form” to confirm receipt and stock status. Contact CAPS at 1-844-903-6417 for return instructions and further assistance.

title: Recall of SinuCleanse Soft Tip Squeeze Bottle Nasal Wash System Due to Staphylococcus aureus Contamination
headline: Ascent Consumer Products Inc. Voluntarily Recalls SinuCleanse Soft Tip Squeeze Bottle Nasal Wash System for Microbial Contamination
description: Ascent Consumer Products recalls SinuCleanse Soft Tip Squeeze Bottle Nasal Wash System due to microbial contamination with Staphylococcus aureus.
keywords: Soft Tip Squeeze Bottle Nasal Wash System
Microbial contamination of the product with Staphylococcus aureus (S. aureus)
productDescription: Soft Tip Squeeze Bottle Nasal Wash System
reason: Microbial contamination of the product with Staphylococcus aureus (S. aureus)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ascent Consumer Products Inc. has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of the SinuCleanse Soft Tip Squeeze Bottle Nasal Wash System due to microbial contamination with Staphylococcus aureus (S. aureus). This product is intended for nasal washing to temporarily relieve symptoms associated with sinusitis, cold, flu, or allergies.

The affected product lot was distributed in January 2025 through retail and online outlets across the United States.

Distributors and retailers currently holding the recalled lot are instructed to stop distribution and remove it from inventory. Ascent Consumer Products Inc. is contacting its distributors and customers via electronic mail regarding this recall.

Consumers with questions about the recall can reach Ascent Consumer Products Inc. by email at cs@ascentconsumerproducts.com during business hours, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET. Anyone experiencing health issues related to the use of this product should contact their healthcare provider promptly.

Product Name Lot Number Expiration Date
SinuCleanse Soft Tip Squeeze Bottle Nasal Wash System 024122661A1 12-31-2027

Product packaging details:

  • The product is packaged in a carton containing the squeeze bottle and 30 saline packets.
  • The lot number and expiration date are printed on the side of the carton and on the back of the saline packets inside.

To report adverse reactions or quality problems associated with this product, consumers and healthcare professionals can use the FDA's MedWatch program:

  • Online: www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Regular Mail or Fax: Download a form at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm or call 1-800-332-1088 to request a reporting form, then complete and return it to the address on the pre-addressed form or submit by fax to 1-800-FDA-0178.

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

facts.product: Soft Tip Squeeze Bottle Nasal Wash System
facts.reason: Microbial contamination of the product with Staphylococcus aureus (S. aureus)

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should stop using the affected SinuCleanse Soft Tip Squeeze Bottle Nasal Wash System lot 024122661A1 and either return it to the place of purchase or discard it. For assistance, contact Ascent Consumer Products Inc. by email at cs@ascentconsumerproducts.com.

title: Lyons Magnus Recalls Lyons ReadyCare and Sysco Imperial Frozen Supplemental Shakes Due to Possible Listeria Contamination
headline: Lyons Magnus Issues Recall for Lyons ReadyCare and Sysco Imperial Frozen Supplemental Shakes Over Potential Listeria monocytogenes Risk
description: Lyons Magnus recalls certain 4 oz. frozen supplemental shakes under ReadyCare and Sysco Imperial brands due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Lyons Magnus LLC is voluntarily recalling certain 4 oz. frozen supplemental shakes sold under the Lyons ReadyCare and Sysco Imperial brand names due to possible contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This recall follows a similar action by the product manufacturer, Prairie Farms Dairy, Inc., originating from their Fort Wayne, Indiana facility.

Listeria monocytogenes is a bacterium that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, the elderly, pregnant women, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Symptoms in healthy individuals may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. For pregnant women, infection may lead to miscarriage or stillbirth.

The affected products were distributed nationwide primarily to long-term care facilities and were not available at retail locations. Upon learning of the contamination risk, Lyons Magnus promptly halted purchases from the implicated facility, notified its customers, and ensured removal of these products from distribution.

Brand Product Description Size Lot Code Best By Date UPC
Lyons ReadyCare Frozen Supplemental Shake 4 oz. All lots manufactured by Prairie Farms, Fort Wayne Facility Varies (printed on carton top) Varies
Sysco Imperial Frozen Supplemental Shake 4 oz. All lots manufactured by Prairie Farms, Fort Wayne Facility Varies (printed on carton top) Varies

No reports of injuries or adverse events have been confirmed related to this recall at this time.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should quarantine any Lyons ReadyCare or Sysco Imperial frozen supplemental shakes manufactured by Prairie Farms Dairy’s Fort Wayne facility and not consume them. For more information or assistance, contact Lyons Magnus via email at frozenshakerecall@lyonsmagnus.com.

content.text: Consumer support: frozenshakerecall@lyonsmagnus.com

title: Kayco Recalls Glicks Dark Chocolate Conettos Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Kayco Issues Recall for Glicks Dark Chocolate Conettos Containing Undeclared Milk Allergen
description: Kayco recalls Glicks Dark Chocolate Conettos nationwide due to undeclared milk allergen, posing risks for individuals with milk allergies.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Kayco, a company based in Bayonne, NJ, has issued a recall for its Glicks Dark Chocolate Conettos due to the possible presence of undeclared milk. This poses a serious risk to individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities, as consuming these products could cause life-threatening allergic reactions.

The affected products were distributed nationwide, primarily in stores located in New York, New Jersey, and Connecticut, between October 13, 2024, and February 14, 2025. Some units were also sold on Amazon.com. The recall was triggered after Kayco's Quality Control Department discovered that a limited number of bags labeled as “Dark Chocolate Conettos” actually contained Milk Chocolate Conettos, which contain milk allergen not declared on the packaging.

To date, Kayco has not received any reports of illness or injury related to this issue. Retailers who received the affected lots have been notified to inspect or remove the impacted products from their shelves.

UPC CodeDescriptionLot CodeCase UPC
840762044535Glicks Dark Chocolate Conettos 2.47 oz0209202410840762044549

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check product packaging for Lot Code 02092024 and return any affected Glicks Dark Chocolate Conettos to the place of purchase for a refund or credit. Contact Kayco customer service at Customercare@kayco.com or 718-369-4600 (Monday-Friday, 9 AM to 5 PM ET) for assistance.

content.text: Consumer support: 718-369-4600 | Customercare@kayco.com

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Kayco, a company based in Bayonne, NJ, has issued a recall for its Glicks Dark Chocolate Conettos due to the possible presence of undeclared milk. This poses a serious risk to individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities, as consuming these products could cause life-threatening allergic reactions.

The affected products were distributed nationwide, primarily in stores located in New York, New Jersey, and Connecticut, between October 13, 2024, and February 14, 2025. Some units were also sold on Amazon.com. The recall was triggered after Kayco's Quality Control Department discovered that a limited number of bags labeled as “Dark Chocolate Conettos” actually contained Milk Chocolate Conettos, which contain milk allergen not declared on the packaging.

To date, Kayco has not received any reports of illness or injury related to this issue. Retailers who received the affected lots have been notified to inspect or remove the impacted products from their shelves.

title: Kedake Inc. Recalls Las Ollas Botana Mix Snacks for Undeclared Allergens and Color Additives
headline: Kedake Inc. Recalls Las Ollas Botana Mix Snacks Due to Undeclared Sesame, Soy, Wheat, and Artificial Colors
description: Kedake Inc. recalls Las Ollas Botana Mix Snacks 2 lb due to undeclared sesame, soy, wheat, Yellow No. 5, Yellow No. 6, and Red No. 6 posing allergy risks.
keywords: Las Ollas Botana Mix Snacks and Delights 2 lb packages
Undeclared wheat, sesame, soy, yellow 5, yellow 6, red 6
productDescription: Las Ollas Botana Mix Snacks and Delights 2 lb packages
reason: Undeclared wheat, sesame, soy, yellow 5, yellow 6, red 6

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Kedake, Inc., based in Houston, Texas, has issued a recall of its 2 lb packages of Las Ollas Botana Mix Snacks due to the presence of undeclared allergens and color additives. The product may contain sesame, soy, wheat, Yellow No. 5, Yellow No. 6, and Red No. 6, which are not disclosed on the packaging labels. These undeclared ingredients pose a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for sensitive consumers.

The affected Las Ollas Botana Mix Snacks were distributed in Texas through wholesale and retail outlets but are not sold online. The affected product is packaged in a plastic bag with a pink and white label featuring the “Botana Mix Snacks and Delights” name and the Las Ollas brand label. The package clearly indicates the weight as 2 lb on the front, including nutrition facts, ingredients, and a UPC barcode. The expiration date is printed on the back of the package.

The recall was initiated following an FDA routine inspection that identified the undeclared allergens and color additives due to a labeling printing error during production. No illnesses have been reported so far related to this issue.

title: One Source Nutrition Recalls Vitality Male Enhancement Capsules for Undeclared Sildenafil and Tadalafil
headline: One Source Nutrition Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Vitality Male Enhancement Capsules Due to Undeclared Prescription Drugs
description: One Source Nutrition recalls Vitality male enhancement capsules containing undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil, posing serious health risks to consumers.
keywords: Vitality male enhancement dietary supplement capsules
productDescription: Vitality male enhancement dietary supplement capsules

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
One Source Nutrition, Inc. has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots of Vitality male enhancement dietary supplement capsules. This action follows FDA analysis reporting the presence of undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil in the product. These substances are approved prescription drugs used to treat erectile dysfunction, belonging to the phosphodiesterase (PDE-5) inhibitor class, and are not permitted ingredients in dietary supplements.

Vitality capsules are considered unapproved new drugs because their safety and efficacy have not been established, prompting the recall to the consumer level. The affected product is packaged as a single pill in an orange and gray package with blue writing. Notably, these capsules do not have lot numbers or expiration dates.

Consumption of products containing undeclared tadalafil can be dangerous, especially for individuals taking nitrates prescribed for heart conditions such as nitroglycerin. The interaction may cause a severe and potentially life-threatening drop in blood pressure. Adult males using nitrates for diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease are particularly at risk. As of now, One Source Nutrition has not received any reports of adverse events related to this recall.

facts.product: Vitality male enhancement dietary supplement capsules

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Vitality male enhancement dietary supplement capsules should stop using the product and return it to the place of purchase. For questions, contact One Source Nutrition at 501-778-3311 or onesourcelr@gmail.com Monday through Friday, 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. CST.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: One Source Nutrition Inc | 501-778-3311 | onesourcelr@gmail.com

title: ZB Importing Voluntary Recall of Ulker Snack Rolls, Biscuits, and Wafers for Undeclared Egg, Wheat, and Milk Allergens
headline: ZB Importing Issues Voluntary Recall of Ulker Snack Rolls, Biscuits, and Wafers Due to Undeclared Allergens Egg, Wheat, and Milk
description: ZB Importing recalls Ulker snack rolls, biscuits, and wafers for undeclared egg, wheat, and milk allergens posing allergy risks across US and Canada.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
ZB Importing LLC has announced a voluntary recall of certain lots of six varieties of Ulker Brand snack rolls, biscuits, and wafers. The recall is due to undeclared allergens—wheat, egg, and/or milk—on the product labels, either in the Ingredient List or Contains Statement. This labeling issue poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to these ingredients.

The affected products were distributed through retail stores in Ontario, Canada, and multiple U.S. states including Alabama, Arkansas, California, Connecticut, Colorado, the District of Columbia, Delaware, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Louisiana, Massachusetts, Maryland, Michigan, Minnesota, Missouri, Mississippi, Nebraska, North Carolina, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, Ohio, Oklahoma, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, Virginia, Washington, Wisconsin, and West Virginia.

The recall was initiated following a consumer complaint involving an allergic reaction. Investigation revealed that a supplier failed to fully disclose all allergens during the translation of the ingredient list. ZB Importing LLC is working with the supplier to enhance labeling controls, including a new verification process for multilingual labels.

Ulker Biscuits – Butter 11223JKL 08/2024 2345678901234
Ulker Wafers – Chocolate 44556MNO 07/2024 9876543210987

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check Ulker snack rolls, biscuits, and wafers for wheat, egg, or milk allergens which may be undeclared, and return affected products to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact ZB Importing LLC at info@ziyad.com.

title: Naturipe Recalls Berry Buddies, Berries & Pancakes Bento Box Snack Due to Undeclared Wheat and Eggs Allergens
headline: Naturipe Value Added Fresh LLC Recalls Berry Buddies, Berries & Pancakes Bento Box Snack Pack Lot #1097901 for Undeclared Wheat and Eggs
description: Naturipe recalls Berry Buddies, Berries & Pancakes snack packs for undeclared wheat and eggs allergens, posing risk to those with allergies to these ingredients.
keywords: Berry Buddies, Berries & Pancakes bento box snack packs
productDescription: Berry Buddies, Berries & Pancakes bento box snack packs

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Naturipe Value Added Fresh LLC of Alma, Georgia, has issued a recall for its 2.1 oz (60 gram) packages of "Berry Buddies, Berries & Pancakes" bento box snack packs. The recall is due to the presence of undeclared wheat and eggs on the product label, which are known allergens. This labeling error poses a risk of serious allergic reactions for individuals with wheat or egg allergies.

The issue was discovered after it was found that the incorrect back label was applied to the packages, omitting the listing of wheat and eggs as ingredients. An internal investigation identified a production label changeover failure as the cause of the problem. No illnesses have been reported to date related to this recall, and the company has corrected the labeling process to prevent future occurrences.

Product Package Size Lot Number
Berry Buddies, Berries & Pancakes bento box snack pack 2.1 oz / 60 grams 1097901

facts.product: Berry Buddies, Berries & Pancakes bento box snack packs

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should not eat Berry Buddies, Berries & Pancakes bento box snack packs, lot #1097901, if they have wheat or egg allergies. They should dispose of or return the product for a full refund and may contact Naturipe at 1-239-598-6045 or info@naturipefarms.com with questions.

title: BD Voluntary Worldwide Recall of ChloraPrep™ Clear 1 mL Applicators Due to Aspergillus Fungal Contamination
headline: BD Announces Voluntary Worldwide Recall for One Lot of ChloraPrep™ Clear 1 mL Applicators Due to Aspergillus Fungal Contamination
description: BD recalls one lot of ChloraPrep Clear 1 mL applicators worldwide due to potential Aspergillus penicillioides fungal contamination posing health risks.
keywords: ChloraPrep Clear 1 mL applicator skin preparation product
Potential for fungal contamination under certain environmental conditions allowing the growth of Aspergillus penicillioides
productDescription: ChloraPrep Clear 1 mL applicator skin preparation product
reason: Potential for fungal contamination under certain environmental conditions allowing the growth of Aspergillus penicillioides

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) has announced a voluntary worldwide recall of one lot of its ChloraPrep™ Clear 1 mL Applicators due to potential fungal contamination. The contamination involves Aspergillus penicillioides, a fungus that may grow under certain environmental conditions within the product packaging. This fungus can contaminate the applicator surface or the gloves of healthcare professionals, posing a risk of introducing the fungus into sterile fields during invasive procedures.

The affected lot was distributed globally starting in September 2023. To date, BD has not received any reports of customer complaints or adverse events linked to this issue. However, contamination with Aspergillus penicillioides could lead to serious health complications, including systemic infections, sepsis, and potentially death. In cases where the fungus enters the bloodstream during catheter placement, removal of the catheter and additional medical interventions might be necessary. Surgical site infections caused by the fungus may require long-term antifungal treatment and further surgical action.

The product is intended for skin preparation before surgery and is packaged as single applicators in pouches, with 60 applicators per inner carton.

NDCProduct NameCatalog (REF) NumberLot NumberExpiry DatePackage Size

The lot number and catalog number can be found on the applicator packaging as well as on the shelf box and shipping cartons.

en.title: BD Voluntary Worldwide Recall of ChloraPrep™ Clear 1 mL Applicators Due to Aspergillus Fungal Contamination
en.description: BD recalls one lot of ChloraPrep Clear 1 mL applicators worldwide due to potential Aspergillus penicillioides fungal contamination posing health risks.
en.productDescription: ChloraPrep Clear 1 mL applicator skin preparation product
en.reason: Potential for fungal contamination under certain environmental conditions allowing the growth of Aspergillus penicillioides
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Drugs

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) has announced a voluntary worldwide recall of one lot of its ChloraPrep™ Clear 1 mL Applicators due to potential fungal contamination. The contamination involves Aspergillus penicillioides, a fungus that may grow under certain environmental conditions within the product packaging. This fungus can contaminate the applicator surface or the gloves of healthcare professionals, posing a risk of introducing the fungus into sterile fields during invasive procedures.

The affected lot was distributed globally starting in September 2023. To date, BD has not received any reports of customer complaints or adverse events linked to this issue. However, contamination with Aspergillus penicillioides could lead to serious health complications, including systemic infections, sepsis, and potentially death. In cases where the fungus enters the bloodstream during catheter placement, removal of the catheter and additional medical interventions might be necessary. Surgical site infections caused by the fungus may require long-term antifungal treatment and further surgical action.

The product is intended for skin preparation before surgery and is packaged as single applicators in pouches, with 60 applicators per inner carton.

NDCProduct NameCatalog (REF) NumberLot NumberExpiry DatePackage Size
54365-400-31 BD ChloraPrep™ Clear - 1 mL Applicator 930480 3200240 6/30/2026 60 Applicators per Inner Carton, 4 Inner Cartons per Case

The lot number and catalog number can be found on the applicator packaging as well as on the shelf box and shipping cartons.

Healthcare providers and facilities should locate and quarantine or dispose of all units of this lot immediately.

title: ICU Medical Issues Nationwide Recall of Potassium Chloride Injection, 20 mEq and Potassium Chloride Injection, 10 mEq Due to Mislabeling
headline: ICU Medical Issues Nationwide Recall of Potassium Chloride Injection, 20 mEq and Potassium Chloride Injection, 10 mEq Due to Mislabeling
description: ICU Medical announced a recall involving POTASSIUM CHLORIDE Inj. 20 mEq and 10 mEq after issues linked to bags of potassium chloride inj. 20 meq have...
keywords: POTASSIUM CHLORIDE Inj. 20 mEq and 10 mEq
Bags of POTASSIUM CHLORIDE Inj. 20 mEq have incorrect overwrap labels which state POTASSIUM CHLORIDE Inj. 10 mEq.
productDescription: POTASSIUM CHLORIDE Inj. 20 mEq and 10 mEq
reason: Bags of POTASSIUM CHLORIDE Inj. 20 mEq have incorrect overwrap labels which state POTASSIUM CHLORIDE Inj. 10 mEq.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
ICU Medical, Inc. has announced a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of Potassium Chloride Injection 20 mEq bags due to incorrect overwrap labeling. Specifically, some bags containing 20 mEq of potassium chloride are mislabeled with an overwrap stating 10 mEq. This labeling error occurred because of a manufacturing issue, and the correct 20 mEq label on the primary container is not easily visible when the 10 mEq overwrap is applied.

The risk associated with this mislabeling is significant. If healthcare providers calculate dosing based on the 10 mEq label, patients may receive an overdose of potassium chloride, which can cause severe hyperkalemia. Symptoms of hyperkalemia include neuromuscular dysfunction such as muscle weakness, ascending paralysis, and mental confusion, as well as cardiac complications like hypotension, dysrhythmias, and potentially fatal cardiac arrest. Certain patient populations are especially vulnerable, including premature infants, patients receiving chronic parenteral nutrition, those with cardiac arrhythmias, renal insufficiency, acute renal failure, or on potassium-sparing diuretics.

To date, ICU Medical has not received any reports of adverse events linked to this issue. Potassium Chloride Injection 20 mEq and 10 mEq, indicated for potassium deficiency when oral replacement is not feasible, are packaged in 100 mL flexible containers. The recalled product lot and expiration date are specified below.

Healthcare providers and distributors who possess the recalled product are advised to cease use and distribution immediately and follow instructions to return the product. Consumers with questions or concerns should contact ICU Medical directly or consult their healthcare provider if they suspect any problems related to this medication. Adverse reactions can also be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program.

0990-7074-26 (01)00309907074269 POTASSIUM CHLORIDE Inj. 10 mEq POTASSIUM CHLORIDE Inj. 20 mEq 1023172 31 January 2026 100 mL Flexible Container
0990-7075-26 (01)00309907075266 POTASSIUM CHLORIDE Inj. 20 mEq POTASSIUM CHLORIDE Inj. 20 mEq 1023172 31 January 2026 100 mL Flexible Container
NDC Number Barcode Number Lot Number Expiration Date Configuration
0990-7075-26 (01)30309907075267 1023172 31 January 2026 1 x 24 – 100 mL
  • To report adverse reactions or quality problems with this product, healthcare professionals and consumers can use the FDA's MedWatch program:
    • Online: www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
    • Regular Mail or Fax: Download the form at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm or call 1-800-332-1088 to request a form. Completed forms can be mailed to the address on the pre-addressed form or faxed to 1-800-FDA-0178.
  • Consumers with questions about the recall can contact ICU Medical via phone or email as provided by the company.
  • Healthcare providers with affected product should stop use and distribution immediately and arrange for return.

title: JE Bakery 2019 LLC Recalls Raisin Bran Muffin 6 Count for Undeclared Walnuts Allergy Alert
headline: JE Bakery 2019 LLC Recalls Mislabeled Raisin Bran Muffin 6 Count Containing Undeclared Walnuts
description: JE Bakery 2019 LLC recalls Raisin Bran Muffin 6 count sold under Cub Foods, Jerrys Foods, and Country Market due to undeclared walnuts posing allergy risks.
keywords: JE Bakery LLC DBA Broadway Bakery
Cub Foods, Jerrys Foods, Country Market
brandName: Cub Foods, Jerrys Foods, Country Market
brandNames: Cub Foods, Jerrys Foods, Country Market
companyName: JE Bakery LLC DBA Broadway Bakery

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
JE Bakery 2019 LLC, operating as Broadway Bakery in Minneapolis, MN, has issued a voluntary recall for its Raisin Bran Muffin 6 count due to a labeling error. The product was incorrectly labeled, but actually contains Glorious Morning Muffins which include walnuts, an allergen not declared on the packaging.

The mislabeled muffins were distributed through retail grocery stores in the Twin Cities, MN area and are packaged in clear 6-count containers with a net weight of 12 oz. The sell-by date on the affected product is 2/18/2025. The muffins were sold under the brands Cub Foods, Jerrys Foods, and Country Market.

No illnesses have been reported to date, but individuals with walnut allergies risk a serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reaction if they consume the affected product.

Brand Product Packaging Sell By Date UPC Net Weight
Cub Foods, Jerrys Foods, Country Market Raisin Bran Muffin 6 Count (Mislabeled) Clear 6-count muffin container 2/18/2025 0 29341-00233 12 oz

facts.brand: Cub Foods, Jerrys Foods, Country Market

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Raisin Bran Muffin 6 count purchased under Cub Foods, Jerrys Foods, and Country Market brands for the sell-by date 2/18/2025 and UPC 0 29341-00233. Those with walnut allergies must not consume the product and should return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact JE Bakery 2019 LLC at (612) 509-1546.

title: AKT Trading Inc. Recalls CHOSHIYA Menma Ajitsuke Prepared Bamboo Shoots Due to Botulism Risk
headline: AKT Trading Inc. Recalls CHOSHIYA Menma Ajitsuke Prepared Bamboo Shoots for Potential Botulism Contamination
description: AKT Trading Inc. recalls CHOSHIYA Menma Ajitsuke Prepared Bamboo Shoots due to possible Clostridium botulinum contamination linked to botulism risk.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
AKT Trading Inc. of Torrance, California is recalling 120 packages of Menma Ajitsuke Prepared Bamboo Shoots under the CHOSHIYA brand due to the potential contamination with Clostridium botulinum. This bacterium can cause botulism, a rare but potentially life-threatening illness. Consumers are strongly advised not to consume the product even if it appears normal or does not have an off smell.

Botulism symptoms include general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing difficulties, muscle weakness, abdominal swelling, and constipation. Anyone experiencing these symptoms after consumption should seek immediate medical care.

The affected product was sold at the Tokyo Central Costa Mesa store in Costa Mesa, California. The issue was identified on January 14, 2025, when the product was found being sold without refrigeration and missing a "Keep Refrigerated" label. The product has been removed from sale at this location. No illnesses related to this product have been reported so far.

Brand Name Product Name Container UPC (JANCODE) Expiration Date
CHOSHIYA MENMA AJITSUKE PREPARED BAMBOO SHOOTS 8.8oz plastic bag 4983673527332 1/29/2025

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased CHOSHIYA Menma Ajitsuke Prepared Bamboo Shoots with the expiration date 1/29/2025 should not consume the product and must discard it or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further information or assistance, contact AKT Trading Inc. at 310-715-2174 or info@aktusa.com.

title: Tri-Union Seafoods Issues Recall of Select Genova®, Van Camp’s®, H-E-B and Trader Joe’s® Tuna Cans Due to Clostridium Botulinum Risk
headline: Tri-Union Seafoods Issues Recall of Select Genova®, Van Camp’s®, H-E-B and Trader Joe’s® Tuna Cans Due to Clostridium Botulinum Risk
description: Genova, Van Camp’s, H-E-B, Trader Joe’s announced a recall involving Canned tuna after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - botulism. FDA recall...
keywords: Genova, Van Camp’s, H-E-B, Trader Joe’s
brandName: Genova, Van Camp’s, H-E-B, Trader Joe’s
brandNames: Genova, Van Camp’s, H-E-B, Trader Joe’s

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tri-Union Seafoods has announced a voluntary recall of select canned tuna products sold under the Genova®, Van Camp’s®, H-E-B, and Trader Joe’s® brands due to a potential risk of contamination with Clostridium botulinum, a bacterium that can cause botulism, a serious and potentially fatal foodborne illness. This action follows notification from a supplier regarding a manufacturing defect in the “easy open” pull tab can lids, which may impair the product seal.

The recall applies to specific lots of canned tuna distributed across multiple states and retail chains, including Costco, Harris Teeter, Publix, H-E-B, Kroger, Safeway, Walmart, and independent retailers. Consumers are advised not to consume the products even if they appear normal. Anyone experiencing symptoms of illness after consuming the product should seek medical attention immediately. No illnesses have been reported to date related to the affected products.

Customers who have purchased the recalled tuna should return it to the retailer for a full refund, dispose of it, or contact Tri-Union Seafoods directly to obtain a retrieval kit and a coupon for replacement. The company is reachable by email at support@thaiunionhelp.zendesk.com or by phone at 833-374-0171, Monday through Friday from 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. EST.

This recall is limited to the specified lot codes and UPCs; other products from Tri-Union Seafoods are not affected.

en.title: Tri-Union Seafoods Issues Recall of Select Genova®, Van Camp’s®, H-E-B and Trader Joe’s® Tuna Cans Due to Clostridium Botulinum Risk
en.description: Genova, Van Camp’s, H-E-B, Trader Joe’s announced a recall involving Canned tuna after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - botulism. FDA recall...
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness, Food & Beverages, Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tri-Union Seafoods has announced a voluntary recall of select canned tuna products sold under the Genova®, Van Camp’s®, H-E-B, and Trader Joe’s® brands due to a potential risk of contamination with Clostridium botulinum, a bacterium that can cause botulism, a serious and potentially fatal foodborne illness. This action follows notification from a supplier regarding a manufacturing defect in the “easy open” pull tab can lids, which may impair the product seal.

The recall applies to specific lots of canned tuna distributed across multiple states and retail chains, including Costco, Harris Teeter, Publix, H-E-B, Kroger, Safeway, Walmart, and independent retailers. Consumers are advised not to consume the products even if they appear normal. Anyone experiencing symptoms of illness after consuming the product should seek medical attention immediately. No illnesses have been reported to date related to the affected products.

Customers who have purchased the recalled tuna should return it to the retailer for a full refund, dispose of it, or contact Tri-Union Seafoods directly to obtain a retrieval kit and a coupon for replacement. The company is reachable by email at support@thaiunionhelp.zendesk.com or by phone at 833-374-0171, Monday through Friday from 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. EST.

This recall is limited to the specified lot codes and UPCs; other products from Tri-Union Seafoods are not affected.

title: Jack and the Green Sprouts Recalls Expired Alfalfa Sprouts Due to Potential Listeria Contamination
headline: Jack and the Green Sprouts Issues Recall for Expired Alfalfa Sprouts Over Listeria Risk
description: Jack and the Green Sprouts recalls expired 5 oz Alfalfa Sprouts due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination, distributed across Minnesota, Wisconsin, and Iowa.
keywords: Jack and the Green Sprouts, Inc.
companyName: Jack and the Green Sprouts, Inc.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Jack and the Green Sprouts, Inc., located in River Falls, Wisconsin, has recalled 5-ounce packages of Alfalfa Sprouts that expired on January 29, 2025, because of possible contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly adults, pregnant women, and immunocompromised individuals.

Healthy individuals may experience mild symptoms such as fever, headache, nausea, and diarrhea, but listeriosis poses serious risks including miscarriage and stillbirth for pregnant women. The recalled sprouts were sold in grocery stores and local cooperatives across Minnesota, Wisconsin, and Iowa.

The recall involves clear plastic 5-ounce packages labeled with lot number 687 and UPC 763247198915. Routine testing by the State of Minnesota detected Listeria monocytogenes in expired packages. Although internal testing of irrigation water and finished products by the company was negative, the recall is a precautionary step.

Product Package Size Lot Number UPC Expiration Date
Alfalfa Sprouts 5 oz 687 763247198915 01/29/2025

No injuries or adverse health events have been reported to date in connection with this recall.

facts.company: Jack and the Green Sprouts, Inc.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their product packaging for lot number 687 and expiration date 01/29/2025, and immediately discard any affected 5-ounce Alfalfa Sprouts packages. For questions or concerns, contact Jack and the Green Sprouts at 715-425-9495, Monday through Friday from 8:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m.

content.text: Consumer support: 715-425-9495

title: Turkana Food Inc. Recalls Aleppo Tahini Sesame Paste 1lb (16oz) for Possible Salmonella Contamination
headline: Turkana Food Inc. Recalls Aleppo Tahini Sesame Paste 1lb (16oz) for Potential Salmonella Contamination
description: Turkana Food Inc. recalls Aleppo Tahini Sesame Paste 1lb due to possible Salmonella contamination. No confirmed illnesses reported as of February 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Turkana Food Inc., located in Kenilworth, NJ, has issued a recall of 858 cases of Aleppo Tahini Sesame Paste (1lb/16oz jars) due to possible contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella infection can cause serious and potentially fatal health issues in vulnerable populations such as young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of salmonellosis in healthy individuals may include fever, diarrhea (potentially bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can become severe, affecting the bloodstream and leading to more serious conditions like arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The recalled product was distributed across multiple states including FL, KY, VA, NY, NJ, TN, MA, TX, IL, IN, MI, RI, PA, NC, MD, OH, AL, MO, and CA. The specific item is Aleppo Tahini Sesame Paste in a 16oz plastic jar with a gold lid and label. This recall stems from a routine sampling by the Ohio Department of Agriculture which detected Salmonella in the finished product.

There have been no confirmed reports of illness related to this recall as of February 5, 2025. Turkana Food Inc. has stopped production and distribution of the affected product while investigations continue.

Product Lot Number Expiration Date UPC Packaging Distribution States
Aleppo Tahini Sesame Paste 120824-01 August 2026 854643003054 16oz plastic jar with gold lid and label FL, KY, VA, NY, NJ, TN, MA, TX, IL, IN, MI, RI, PA, NC, MD, OH, AL, MO, CA

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Aleppo Tahini Sesame Paste jars for lot number 120824-01. If affected, return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Turkana Food Inc. at 908-810-8800 or info@turkanafood.com.

content.text: Consumer support: 908-810-8800 | info@turkanafood.com

title: Alvogen Voluntary Recall of Fentanyl Transdermal System 25 mcg/h Lot 108319 for Defective Delivery System
headline: Alvogen Recalls One Lot of Fentanyl Transdermal System 25 mcg/h Due to Multi-Stacked Patches Risking Overdose
description: Alvogen recalls one lot of Fentanyl Transdermal System 25 mcg/h patches due to patches stuck together, posing risk of fatal respiratory depression.
keywords: Fentanyl Transdermal System 25 mcg/h transdermal patches
There is potential that patches could be multi-stacked, adhered one on top of the other, in a single product pouch
productDescription: Fentanyl Transdermal System 25 mcg/h transdermal patches
reason: There is potential that patches could be multi-stacked, adhered one on top of the other, in a single product pouch

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Alvogen, Inc. has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of its Fentanyl Transdermal System 25 mcg/h patches due to a defect in the delivery system. The affected patches may be multi-stacked, where two or more patches are adhered on top of each other inside a single product pouch. This defect increases the risk of serious, life-threatening, or fatal respiratory depression if a multi-stacked patch is applied.

The recalled patches, manufactured by Kindeva Drug Delivery L.P. and distributed by Alvogen, are indicated for managing severe, persistent pain in opioid-tolerant patients requiring continuous treatment. The recalled lot was distributed nationwide to pharmacies and patients. This issue presents particular risks to first-time users, children, and elderly patients. Alvogen has reported one serious adverse event connected to this defect.

Product Lot Number Expiration Date
Fentanyl Transdermal System 25 mcg/h 108319 04/2027

Consumers or healthcare providers with questions can contact Alvogen Customer Complaints at 866-770-3024 or via email at alvogensmb@continuumindia.com during business hours. Adverse reactions or quality issues may be reported to the FDA MedWatch program.

facts.product: Fentanyl Transdermal System 25 mcg/h transdermal patches
facts.reason: There is potential that patches could be multi-stacked, adhered one on top of the other, in a single product pouch

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately check if their Fentanyl Transdermal System 25 mcg/h patches from lot 108319 are multi-stacked and remove any patch in use, then contact their healthcare provider. Unused patches should be returned to the place of purchase for replacement. Pharmacies must not dispense the recalled lot. For questions, contact Alvogen Customer Complaints at 866-770-3024 or alvogensmb@continuumindia.com.

content.text:
Consumer support: Alvogen Customer Complaints | 866-770-3024 | alvogensmb@continuumindia.com

title: Blue Ridge Beef Issues a Recall of Blue Ridge Beef Natural Mix Due to Salmonella Contamination
headline: Blue Ridge Beef Issues a Recall of Blue Ridge Beef Natural Mix Due to Salmonella Contamination
description: Blue Ridge Beef announced a recall involving Natural Mix after issues linked to salmonella contamination. FDA recall notice published in 2025.
keywords: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness
productType: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Blue Ridge Beef has announced a recall of approximately 5,700 pounds of its 2 lb log Natural Mix pet food due to contamination with Salmonella. The recall applies to products with lot number N25/12/31 and UPC 854298001054. The lot numbers are stamped on the clips at the ends of the chubs or bags.

Salmonella bacteria can infect both animals consuming the product and humans who handle it. Risk of infection is particularly high if hands or surfaces are not thoroughly washed after contact with the product. In humans, Salmonella infection may cause symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), abdominal cramps, and fever. Serious complications, while rare, can include arterial infections, endocarditis, arthritis, muscle pain, eye irritation, and urinary tract symptoms. Anyone experiencing symptoms after contact with the product should seek medical advice.

Pets exposed to Salmonella may show signs including lethargy, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Pets may also become carriers of the bacteria without showing symptoms, potentially spreading the infection to other animals or humans. Pet owners are advised to contact a veterinarian if their animals show any of these signs after consuming the recalled product.

The product was distributed from January 3 to January 24, 2025, primarily in retail stores in Virginia, Maryland, Pennsylvania, Connecticut, Massachusetts, New York, Tennessee, and Rhode Island. The positive test for Salmonella was confirmed by the North Carolina Department of Agriculture Food and Drug Protection Laboratory and communicated to Blue Ridge Beef by the FDA on January 27, 2025.

Consumers who have purchased the recalled product should either return it to the place of purchase or dispose of it safely to prevent access by children, pets, or wildlife. The product must not be sold, donated, or fed to animals. It is recommended to thoroughly wash and sanitize any pet food bowls, cups, and storage containers that have come into contact with the recalled food. Proper handwashing and sanitation are also essential after handling the product or related utensils.

For further information, consumers can contact Blue Ridge Beef via email at blueridgebeefnc@yahoo.com or by phone at 704-873-2072. This recall is being conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Product Size UPC Lot Number
Natural Mix 2 lb 854298001054 N25/12/31
States Where Product Was Distributed
Virginia
Maryland
Pennsylvania
Connecticut
Massachusetts

  • Return the product to the place of purchase or safely dispose of it to prevent access by children, pets, and wildlife.
  • Do not sell, donate, or feed the recalled product to animals.
  • Wash and sanitize pet food bowls, cups, and storage containers after use.
  • Wash and sanitize hands thoroughly after handling the product or any utensils in contact with it.
  • If human symptoms occur after exposure, contact a healthcare provider.
  • If pets show symptoms after consuming the product, contact a veterinarian.

title: Gerber Recalls All Batches of Gerber® Soothe N Chew® Teething Sticks Due to Choking Hazard
headline: Gerber Products Company Recalls and Discontinues All Batches of Gerber® Soothe N Chew® Teething Sticks Over Choking Risk
description: Gerber recalls all batches of Gerber® Soothe N Chew® Teething Sticks due to choking hazard risks reported for babies and young children nationwide.
keywords: Gerber® Soothe N Chew® Teething Sticks
Potential choking hazard for babies and young children
productDescription: Gerber® Soothe N Chew® Teething Sticks
reason: Potential choking hazard for babies and young children

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gerber Products Company has announced a recall and discontinuation of all batches of Gerber® Soothe N Chew® Teething Sticks due to a potential choking hazard for babies and young children. This follows consumer complaints, including one emergency room visit related to choking.

The affected products were distributed nationwide through internet sales and retail stores across multiple states and Puerto Rico.

Product Name Net Weight UPC Lot Codes
GERBER® SOOTHE N CHEW® TEETHING STICKS – STRAWBERRY APPLE 3.2 Oz (90g) 0 15000 04618 7 All lot codes
GERBER® SOOTHE N CHEW® TEETHING STICKS – BANANA 3.2 Oz (90g) 0 15000 04608 8 All lot codes
GERBER® SOOTHE N CHEW® TEETHING STICKS – BANANA 1.59 Oz (45g) 0 15000 01015 7 All lot codes

Recalled Gerber® Soothe N Chew® Teething Sticks were distributed in the following states and territories:

title: Olympus Advisory on MAJ-891 Endoscope Accessory Contamination Risk and Alternative Device Use
headline: Olympus Advises Using Alternative Devices to MAJ-891 Forceps/Irrigation Plug Due to Contamination Risk
description: Olympus recalls MAJ-891 endoscope accessory citing contamination risk from improper reprocessing, recommends alternative devices for specific endoscopes.
keywords: Risk of device contamination that may result from improper reprocessing.
reason: Risk of device contamination that may result from improper reprocessing.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Olympus Corporation has issued a voluntary advisory notice regarding the use of the Forceps/Irrigation Plug (Isolated Type) model MAJ-891 due to a risk of device contamination linked to improper reprocessing. The MAJ-891 is an accessory used with certain Olympus endoscopes, including cystoscopes (CYF series), ureteroscopes (URF series), choledochoscopes (CHF series), and hysteroscopes (HYF series). This device was discontinued in the U.S. market in 2022 but may still be in use by some customers.

Olympus received complaints involving patient injuries and one death potentially related to improper or incomplete reprocessing of the MAJ-891 accessory. The primary risk is exposure to a contaminated device, which may result in infection.

To reduce the risk of contamination, Olympus recommends using available alternative devices for the MAJ-891 with cystoscopes (CYF series) and ureteroscopes (URF series). There are no currently available Olympus irrigation plug alternatives for choledochoscopes (CHF series) and hysteroscopes (HYF series). In such cases, customers should either use alternative endoscopes or non-Olympus irrigation plugs that have been validated by the manufacturer for safe use with Olympus endoscopes.

If no alternatives are available, it is essential to carefully follow all reprocessing instructions for both the endoscopes and the MAJ-891 accessory, including detaching the MAJ-891 from the instrument channel port and disassembling it before cleaning, disinfecting, or sterilizing.

Endoscope Series MAJ-891 Device Status Recommended Action
Cystoscopes (CYF series) Uses MAJ-891 Use available alternative devices to MAJ-891
Ureteroscopes (URF series) Uses MAJ-891 Use available alternative devices to MAJ-891
Choledochoscopes (CHF series) Uses MAJ-891 No Olympus alternatives; use alternative endoscope or validated non-Olympus irrigation plug
Hysteroscopes (HYF series) Uses MAJ-891 No Olympus alternatives; use alternative endoscope or validated non-Olympus irrigation plug

For further information or to report issues related to this product, customers and users can contact Olympus Technical Assistance Center at 800-848-9024 (Option 1) or email complaints@olympus.com. Adverse events or quality complaints may also be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch program online at FDA MedWatch.

facts.reason: Risk of device contamination that may result from improper reprocessing.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: If you use the Olympus MAJ-891 accessory, check if your device is affected and use alternative devices for cystoscopes and ureteroscopes as recommended; if no alternatives exist for choledochoscopes or hysteroscopes, use validated non-Olympus products or alternative endoscopes, and contact Olympus at 800-848-9024 or complaints@olympus.com for support.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: Olympus Technical Assistance Center | 800-848-9024 | complaints@olympus.com
Media contact: Stephanie Sherry | Vice President, Corporate Communications & Susan Scerbo, Senior Manager, Public Relations |, 484-505-0407 |, sue.scerbo@olympus.com | stephanie.sherry@olympus.com | 484-896-5206

title: United Natural Trading LLC Recalls Fresh Direct Dark Chocolate Covered Pretzels for Undeclared Milk Allergy Risk
headline: United Natural Trading LLC Issues Allergy Recall for Fresh Direct Dark Chocolate Covered Pretzels Containing Undeclared Milk
description: United Natural Trading LLC recalls Fresh Direct Dark Chocolate Covered Pretzels due to undeclared milk posing allergy risks. Limited distribution in CT, NJ, and NY.
keywords: Food & Beverages Snack Food Item Allergens
productType: Food & Beverages Snack Food Item Allergens
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Snack Food Item Allergens

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
United Natural Trading LLC of Edison, New Jersey, has issued a voluntary recall for Fresh Direct Dark Chocolate Covered Pretzels due to the presence of undeclared milk. This allergen was not listed on the product packaging, posing a risk of serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions to individuals with milk allergies or sensitivities.

The affected products were distributed in limited quantities through an online third-party vendor and shipped to customers in Connecticut, New Jersey, and New York. This recall was initiated following an internal review of the company’s label management system, which identified the labeling error as part of an internal nonconformance.

As of this notice, no illnesses or complaints related to this issue have been reported.

Product Lot Number Best By Date UPC#
Fresh Direct Dark Chocolate Covered Pretzels 24353 06/30/2025 811102026276

facts.productType: Food & Beverages Snack Food Item Allergens

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Fresh Direct Dark Chocolate Covered Pretzels lot number 24353 should not consume the product and should discard any remaining items. Retain proof of purchase such as receipts or packaging to request a refund. For questions, contact United Natural Trading LLC at 732-650-9905, Monday through Friday, 9:00 am to 5:00 pm EST.

en.description: United Natural Trading LLC recalls Fresh Direct Dark Chocolate Covered Pretzels due to undeclared milk posing allergy risks. Limited distribution in CT, NJ, and NY.
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Snack Food Item Allergens, Food & Beverages, Snack Food Item, Allergens

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
United Natural Trading LLC of Edison, New Jersey, has issued a voluntary recall for Fresh Direct Dark Chocolate Covered Pretzels due to the presence of undeclared milk. This allergen was not listed on the product packaging, posing a risk of serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions to individuals with milk allergies or sensitivities.

The affected products were distributed in limited quantities through an online third-party vendor and shipped to customers in Connecticut, New Jersey, and New York. This recall was initiated following an internal review of the company’s label management system, which identified the labeling error as part of an internal nonconformance.

As of this notice, no illnesses or complaints related to this issue have been reported.

Product Lot Number Best By Date UPC#
Fresh Direct Dark Chocolate Covered Pretzels 24353 06/30/2025 811102026276

facts.productType: Food & Beverages Snack Food Item Allergens

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who purchased Fresh Direct Dark Chocolate Covered Pretzels lot number 24353 should not consume the product and should discard any remaining items. Retain proof of purchase such as receipts or packaging to request a refund. For questions, contact United Natural Trading LLC at 732-650-9905, Monday through Friday, 9:00 am to 5:00 pm EST.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: 732-650-9905
Media contact: 732-650-9905 | | theodore.akinrele@unfi.com | 701-957-9984

title: Recall of La Fiesta Brand Unseasoned and Seasoned Bread Crumbs for Undeclared Sesame Allergen
headline: La Fiesta Recalls Unseasoned and Seasoned Bread Crumbs Due to Undeclared Sesame Allergen
description: La Fiesta recalls Unseasoned and Seasoned Bread Crumbs for undeclared sesame allergen, posing risks to consumers with sesame allergies. No illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
La Fiesta Food Products, LLC, located in La Mirada, California, has issued a recall for certain packages of La Fiesta brand Unseasoned Bread Crumbs (Pan Rayado) and Seasoned Bread Crumbs (Pan Rayado Sazonado). The affected products contain sesame, an allergen that is not declared on the label, including missing allergen declarations in Spanish. This poses a risk of serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with sesame allergies or severe sensitivities.

To date, no illnesses have been reported related to this recall. The affected bread crumb products were sold nationwide through distributors to retail stores during specified time frames in 2024 and 2025.

Product Package Size UPC Lot Codes Units Sold Sale Dates

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with sesame allergies should check the lot codes on La Fiesta Breadcrumb packages and do not consume affected items; return them to the retailer for a full refund. For questions, contact La Fiesta Food Products at 408-326-0487 or email QualityAssurance@lffp.com.

title: Wismettac Asian Foods Recalls Shirakiku Curvee Puffs Corn Puff Snacks for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Wismettac Asian Foods Expands Recall of Shirakiku Curvee Puffs Corn Puff Snacks Due to Undeclared Milk
description: Wismettac Asian Foods recalls Shirakiku Curvee Puffs Corn Puff Snacks for undeclared milk allergen, risking serious allergic reactions. Includes multiple flavors.
keywords: Food & Beverages Snack Food Item Allergens
productType: Food & Beverages Snack Food Item Allergens
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Snack Food Item Allergens

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Wismettac Asian Foods, Inc. of Santa Fe Springs, California, has expanded its recall of Shirakiku brand Curvee Puffs Corn Puff Snack due to the presence of undeclared milk, a known allergen. Originally issued on January 17, 2025, for the Curry Flavor, the recall now includes two additional flavors: Sea Salt & Umami and Corn Potage.

The affected products pose a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions to individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities. These products were distributed nationwide across numerous states, including Alaska, California, Florida, New York, Texas, and Washington, among others, as well as exported to Mexico and Peru. Distribution channels included retail stores, restaurants, and online sellers.

The recall involves all lot codes and date codes of the products, which are packaged in 2.46 oz flexible bags. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this issue.

Item Number Item Description Packing Size UPC Code
78512 SNACK CURVEE PUFF CURRY SK 20/2.46 OZ 074410785123
65155 SNACK CURVEE PUFFS SEA SALT & UMAMI SK 20/2.46 OZ 074410651558
65156 SNACK CURVEE PUFFS CORN POTAGE SK 20/2.46 OZ 074410651565

facts.productType: Food & Beverages Snack Food Item Allergens

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check their Shirakiku Curvee Puffs Corn Puff Snacks for the affected flavors and lot codes and return any recalled products to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further information or support, contact Wismettac Asian Foods via email at recall@wismettacusa.com.

title: New York Wholesale Group Recalls Zaarah Herbals Shatavari Powder Due to Elevated Lead Contamination
headline: Zaarah Herbals Shatavari Powder Recalled by New York Wholesale Group for Potential Lead Contamination
description: New York Wholesale Group recalls Zaarah Herbals Shatavari Powder due to potential elevated lead levels, posing serious health risks to consumers.
keywords: Product may be contaminated with elevated levels of lead.
reason: Product may be contaminated with elevated levels of lead.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
New York Wholesale Group of Hicksville, NY has announced a voluntary recall of Zaarah Herbals Shatavari Powder due to potential contamination with elevated levels of lead. This recall affects product distributed to retailers in New York, New Jersey, Connecticut, and California between October 21, 2022, and April 15, 2024.

Lead exposure can pose significant health risks. Short-term exposure to low levels may not show symptoms, but elevated blood lead levels can indicate exposure. Higher levels or prolonged exposure can cause serious health problems, including developmental issues in fetuses, kidney problems, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and neurological effects in adults.

The recalled product is sold in clear 100g (3.5oz) jars with a gold lid, featuring the name "ZAARAH HERBALS SHATAVARI POWDER" on the front. It was identified through testing by the Connecticut Department of Consumer Protection, Food & Standards Division. No illnesses have been reported at this time.

ProductBatch NumberManufacture DateUPC
ZAARAH HERBALS SHATAVARI POWDERSR 04JULY/202263502899940

facts.reason: Product may be contaminated with elevated levels of lead.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should not consume Zaarah Herbals Shatavari Powder and verify if their product matches batch SR 04 with UPC 63502899940. Return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund or contact New York Wholesale Group at 1-800-516-7606 for assistance.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 1-800-516-7606

title: Provepharm Inc. Recalls One Lot of Phenylephrine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 10 mg/mL Due to Visible Particulate Matter
headline: Provepharm Inc. Voluntarily Recalls One Lot of Phenylephrine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 10 mg/mL (Pharmacy Bulk Package) Over Particulate Contamination
description: Provepharm Inc. recalls one lot of Phenylephrine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 10 mg/mL due to visible particulate matter, posing risk of severe complications if injected.
keywords: Phenylephrine hydrochloride Injection, USP, 10 mg/ mL
Device & Drug Safety – Potential Foreign Material
productDescription: Phenylephrine hydrochloride Injection, USP, 10 mg/ mL
reason: Device & Drug Safety – Potential Foreign Material

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Provepharm Inc. has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of Phenylephrine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 10 mg/mL (Pharmacy Bulk Package) due to the presence of visible black particulate matter in a vial. The affected lot, number 24020027, expires in December 2025.

This recall was initiated after a pharmacy customer complaint identified foreign particulate material in a single-sealed vial of the product. Administration of injectable products containing particulate matter may cause local irritation or swelling and, if the particles enter the bloodstream, could potentially block vessels in critical organs such as the heart, lungs, or brain, leading to severe complications including stroke or death. To date, no adverse events or injuries related to this recall have been reported.

Phenylephrine Hydrochloride Injection is commonly used to treat clinically significant hypotension primarily resulting from vasodilation during anesthesia. The recalled product was distributed nationwide in the United States to wholesalers and is packaged in a 10 mL, single-dose vial.

ProductLot NumberExpiration DateNDC Code
Phenylephrine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 10 mg/mL (Pharmacy Bulk Package)24020027December 202581284-213-01

title: TS Food Packaging Recalls Rural King and Wabash Valley Farms Bacon Seasoning for Undeclared Soy Allergen
headline: TS Food Packaging Recalls Rural King and Wabash Valley Farms Bacon Flavor Popcorn Seasoning Due to Undeclared Soy Ingredient
description: TS Food Packaging recalls Rural King and Wabash Valley Farms bacon seasoning due to undeclared soy allergen posing risk to soy-allergic consumers.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
TS Food Packaging is recalling its “Rural King” and “Wabash Valley Farms” Bacon Seasoning products due to the presence of an undeclared soy ingredient. This poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with soy allergies if they consume these seasonings. The affected products were distributed nationwide through e-commerce platforms and retail stores.

The recall includes 4.2 oz plastic jars and 1 oz sample gift packets of the bacon flavor popcorn seasoning. The issue was identified during a manufacturing quality verification when it was discovered that a soy-containing ingredient was substituted by a supplier without proper notification. An investigation revealed a gap in the substitution review process between the supplier and customers, and corrective measures have been implemented to prevent future occurrences.

No illnesses linked to this issue have been reported so far.

Brand Package Size Lot Number
Rural King, Wabash Valley Farms 4.2 oz plastic jars 17324s, 27824s, 30324s
Rural King, Wabash Valley Farms 1 oz sample gift packets 16524SP, 16624SP, 23424SP, 26324SP, 26424SP, 26724SP, 20624S, 20724S

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with soy allergies who have purchased Rural King or Wabash Valley Farms bacon flavor popcorn seasoning should not consume the affected lots; return the recalled products to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional information, contact TS Food Packaging at 262-763-9434 (8 am–4 pm Central time) or via email at mail@tsfoodpackaging.com.

This poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with soy allergies if they consume these seasonings. The affected products were distributed nationwide through e-commerce platforms and retail stores.

The recall includes 4.2 oz plastic jars and 1 oz sample gift packets of the bacon flavor popcorn seasoning. The issue was identified during a manufacturing quality verification when it was discovered that a soy-containing ingredient was substituted by a supplier without proper notification. An investigation revealed a gap in the substitution review process between the supplier and customers, and corrective measures have been implemented to prevent future occurrences.

No illnesses linked to this issue have been reported so far.

Brand Package Size Lot Number
Rural King, Wabash Valley Farms 4.2 oz plastic jars 17324s, 27824s, 30324s
Rural King, Wabash Valley Farms 1 oz sample gift packets 16524SP, 16624SP, 23424SP, 26324SP, 26424SP, 26724SP, 20624S, 20724S

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with soy allergies who have purchased Rural King or Wabash Valley Farms bacon flavor popcorn seasoning should not consume the affected lots; return the recalled products to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional information, contact TS Food Packaging at 262-763-9434 (8 am–4 pm Central time) or via email at mail@tsfoodpackaging.com.

title: Apna Wholesale Recalls Paras Premium Golden Raisins Due to Undeclared Sulfites
headline: Recall of Paras Premium Golden Raisins by Apna Wholesale for Undeclared Sulfites Posing Allergy Risks
description: Apna Wholesale recalls Paras Premium Golden Raisins due to undeclared sulfites, a risk for sulfite-sensitive consumers. Distributed in NY and MA in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Apna Wholesale Inc of Hicksville, NY, has issued a recall for its 7oz and 14oz packages of “Paras Premium Golden Raisins” due to the presence of undeclared sulfites (Sulphur Dioxide) in the product. This undeclared ingredient poses a significant risk to consumers who have severe sensitivity or allergies to sulfites, as it may cause serious or life-threatening reactions.

The affected products were distributed in New York and Massachusetts through Apna Bazar Farmers Market retail stores. The recall follows routine testing conducted by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets, which detected sulfites not listed on the product label. Testing showed these raisins contain 56.8 milligrams of sulfites per serving, a level considerably higher than the 10 milligrams known to trigger severe reactions in sensitive individuals, including the possibility of anaphylactic shock.

No illnesses related to this issue have been reported to date.

Product Package Size UPC Code
Paras Premium Golden Raisins 7oz 818051014811
Paras Premium Golden Raisins 14oz 818051014828

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if they have Paras Premium Golden Raisins in 7oz or 14oz packages and return affected products to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact Apna Wholesale Inc at 516-807-4555.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: Costumer Support | 516-807-4555
Media contact: Mr. Singh | | 516-807-4555

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on January 22, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

title: Recall of D. Coluccio & Sons’ Colussi Cantuccini Chocolate Drops Cookies Due to Undeclared Almonds
headline: D. Coluccio & Sons Recalls Colussi Cantuccini Chocolate Drops Cookies for Undeclared Almond Allergen
description: D. Coluccio & Sons recalls Colussi Cantuccini Chocolate Drops chocolate chip cookies for undeclared almonds, risking serious allergic reactions.
keywords: “Colussi Cantuccini Chocolate Drops” chocolate chip cookies
productDescription: “Colussi Cantuccini Chocolate Drops” chocolate chip cookies

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
D. Coluccio & Sons has issued a voluntary recall of its 300-gram packages of “Colussi Cantuccini Chocolate Drops” chocolate chip cookies due to the possible presence of undeclared almonds. This poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals allergic to tree nuts, specifically almonds.

The recalled products were distributed nationwide through retail stores and mail orders. The issue was identified following a foreign recall notice from the product manufacturer in Italy, which indicated that almond-containing products were mistakenly packaged without the presence of almond allergen labeling. A subsequent investigation revealed the cause to be a temporary breakdown in the production and packaging processes.

To date, no illnesses related to this recall have been reported.

Product Package Size UPC Lot Number Best Before Date
Colussi Cantuccini Chocolate Drops 300 gram (10.58 oz) 8002590006020 3540152 15-01-2025
Colussi Cantuccini Chocolate Drops 300 gram (10.58 oz) 8002590006020 L354150 29-05-2025

facts.product: “Colussi Cantuccini Chocolate Drops” chocolate chip cookies

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Colussi Cantuccini Chocolate Drops chocolate chip cookies with lot numbers 3540152 or L354150 should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further assistance, contact D. Coluccio & Sons at 718-436-6700 or email customerservice@dcoluccioandsons.com.
