title: Wismettac Asian Foods Recalls Shirakiku Curvee Puffs Corn Puff Snack Curry Flavor for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Recall of Shirakiku Curvee Puffs Corn Puff Snack Curry Flavor Due to Undeclared Milk by Wismettac Asian Foods
description: Wismettac Asian Foods recalls Shirakiku Curvee Puffs Corn Puff Snack Curry Flavor due to undeclared milk posing allergy risks. Check lot codes and UPC.
keywords: Food & Beverages Snack Food Item Allergens
productType: Food & Beverages Snack Food Item Allergens
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Snack Food Item Allergens

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Wismettac Asian Foods, Inc., based in Santa Fe Springs, California, has announced a recall of its Shirakiku brand Curvee Puffs Corn Puff Snack Curry Flavor due to the presence of undeclared milk. This allergen was not listed on the product packaging, posing a serious risk of allergic reaction for individuals allergic or highly sensitive to milk.

The affected product comes in a 2.46 oz flexible bag and was distributed nationwide across multiple states as well as exported to Mexico. Distribution channels included retail stores, restaurants, and online vendors. The recall covers all lot codes and expiration dates of this product.

No illnesses have been reported so far related to this product.

The final distribution of the product occurred on January 10, 2025.

Product Package Size UPC Lot/Date Codes Distribution Area
Shirakiku Curvee Puffs Corn Puff Snack Curry Flavor 2.46 oz flexible bag 07441078512 All lot and date codes AK, AL, AR, AZ, CA, CO, CT, FL, GA, HI, IA, IL, IN, KS, KY, LA, MA, MD, MI, MO, MS, NC, NE, NJ, NV, NY, OH, OK, OR, PA, RI, SC, TN, TX, UT, VA, WA, WI; also exported to Mexico

facts.productType: Food & Beverages Snack Food Item Allergens

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately check if they have Shirakiku Curvee Puffs Corn Puff Snack Curry Flavor with the listed UPC and lot codes. Those who have the product are advised to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund and may contact Wismettac Asian Foods via email at recall@wismettacusa.com for further information.

title: Max Mobility / Permobil Recalls SmartDrive Speed Control Dial Due to Motor Malfunction Risks
headline: Max Mobility / Permobil Issues Nationwide Recall of Speed Control Dial Component for SmartDrive MX2+ Due to Motor Unresponsiveness
description: Max Mobility/Permobil recalls Speed Control Dial for SmartDrive MX2+ over safety and performance issues causing motor unresponsiveness and injury risks.
keywords: Due to potential safety and performance concerns.
reason: Due to potential safety and performance concerns.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Max Mobility, LLC., under the Max Mobility and Permobil brands, has initiated a nationwide recall of the Speed Control Dial component used with the SmartDrive MX2+ Power Assist Device. This recall addresses potential safety and performance concerns related to the Speed Control Dial that may impact functionality and user safety.

The recall was prompted by a material change in the printed circuit board assembly of the Speed Control Dial, which can result in issues such as unintended activation of the SmartDrive motor, failure to stop the motor when at the zero position, unexpected shutdowns, or failure to start the motor drive. These performance problems may cause minor injuries including skin irritation, cuts, and bruises, as well as serious injuries such as muscle or ligament strains, bone fractures, and concussions. Additionally, there is a risk of injury to bystanders if the motor activates unintentionally or cannot be powered off.

As of December 12, 2024, Max Mobility has received 646 complaints related to the Speed Control Dial and reports of three serious injuries involving fractures to the hip, tibia, and malleolus bone. Impacted Speed Control Dials were manufactured and distributed through authorized dealers from August 17, 2023, through November 21, 2024.

Brand Model Number Description Manufacture/Distribution Dates
Max Mobility / Permobil MX2-3DCK / MX2-3DC Frame-mounted Speed Control Dial for SmartDrive MX2+ Power Assist Device August 17, 2023 – November 21, 2024

facts.reason: Due to potential safety and performance concerns.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who own the Speed Control Dial model numbers MX2-3DCK or MX2-3DC should request a replacement promptly by visiting https://hub.permobil.com/smartdrive-scd-voluntary-field-action or contacting Max Mobility / Permobil technical support at (800) 736-0925, Monday through Friday from 8:00 am to 5:00 pm CT.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: Max Mobility/Permobil technical support | 800-736-0925
Media contact: Audra Watt | | | audra.watt@permobil.com | 615-992-4221

title: Monkey Spit, LLC Recalls Monkey Spit BBQ Sauces for Undeclared Milk, Soy, and Wheat Allergens
headline: Monkey Spit, LLC Recalls Monkey Spit BBQ Sauces Due to Undeclared Milk, Soy, and Wheat Allergens
description: Monkey Spit, LLC recalls Monkey Spit BBQ Sauces for undeclared milk, soy, and wheat allergens to prevent allergic reactions. FDA recall notice 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Monkey Spit, LLC, based in Nipomo, California, has issued a recall for certain 12-ounce glass bottles of its Monkey Spit BBQ Sauces due to the presence of undeclared allergens including milk, soy, and wheat. These undeclared allergens pose a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with allergies to milk, wheat, or soy.

The affected products were distributed locally in retail stores on the central coast of California and were also available through mail orders via the company’s website, www.monkeyspithotsauce.com. The recall follows an FDA inspection that identified undeclared allergens on product labels, attributed to a temporary breakdown in the company’s packaging process.

Production of the affected sauces has been suspended pending confirmation from the FDA and Monkey Spit that the issue has been resolved. No illnesses have been reported to date in connection with this recall.

Product Name Allergen(s) Undeclared Best By Date
Monkey Mop BBQ Sauce Milk 11/07/2026
Swamp Mob BBQ Sauce Soy 12/13/2025
Atomic Mop BBQ Sauce Milk, Wheat 03/19/2026

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should return any affected Monkey Spit BBQ Sauces to the place of purchase for a full refund and may contact Monkey Spit, LLC at 1-714-514-8445 (Monday–Friday, 9 a.m.–5 p.m. PST) or email karen@monkeyspithotsauce.com with questions.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 1-714-514-8445 | karen@monkeyspithotsauce.com

title: Mutual Trading Co. Recalls Prepared Monkfish Liver for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Mutual Trading Co. Issues Allergy Alert for Prepared Monkfish Liver Containing Undeclared Milk
description: Mutual Trading Co. recalls Prepared Monkfish Liver due to undeclared milk allergen; distributed in FL, GA, MD, NJ, NY. No illnesses reported.
keywords: New York Mutual Trading, Co., Inc.
New York Mutual Trading Co., Inc.
brandName: New York Mutual Trading Co., Inc.
brandNames: New York Mutual Trading Co., Inc.
companyName: New York Mutual Trading, Co., Inc.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
New York Mutual Trading Co., Inc. has issued a recall for its Prepared Monkfish Liver product due to the presence of undeclared milk allergens. The product, packaged in a 7.05 oz polyethylene vacuum pouch, was distributed without listing milk ingredients on the label, posing a risk to individuals with milk allergies or sensitivities.

The affected product was distributed primarily through restaurants and some retail stores in Florida, Georgia, Maryland, New Jersey, and New York. No illnesses related to the product have been reported so far. The recall follows the discovery that milk derived from an ingredient was not included on the product label, which could cause allergic reactions in sensitive consumers.

The last known distribution date of the product was December 14, 2024.

Product Packaging UPC Code Lot Code Distribution Area Last Distribution Date
Prepared Monkfish Liver 7.05 oz polyethylene vacuum pouch 72546611224 2025.6.30 or before FL, GA, MD, NJ, NY December 14, 2024

facts.brand: New York Mutual Trading Co., Inc.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Prepared Monkfish Liver with UPC 72546611224 and lot codes 2025.6.30 or before should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact New York Mutual Trading Co., Inc. at 213-626-9458 or la.foodsafetygrp@mutual.us.

title: Quaker Oats Recalls Pearl Milling Company Original Pancake & Waffle Mix for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Quaker Oats Announces Limited Recall of Pearl Milling Company Original Pancake & Waffle Mix Due to Undeclared Milk
description: Quaker Oats recalls Pearl Milling Company Original Pancake & Waffle Mix sold in 11 states over undeclared milk allergen risk. No injuries reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The Quaker Oats Company has issued a limited recall of Pearl Milling Company Original Pancake & Waffle Mix because it contains undeclared milk. This recall followed notification from a retail partner regarding the labeling issue. The presence of undeclared milk may cause serious or life-threatening allergic reactions in individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities.

The affected pancake and waffle mix was distributed in 11 states: Arkansas, Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Kansas, Kentucky, Minnesota, Mississippi, Nebraska, Utah, and Wisconsin. Purchases could date back to November 18, 2024. To date, no allergic reactions related to this recall have been reported.

This recall only involves the Pearl Milling Company Original Pancake & Waffle Mix packaged in a 32-ounce (2-pound) cardboard box. No other Pearl Milling Company products are impacted.

Product Description Size UPC Code Date & Manufacturing Code
Pearl Milling Company Original Pancake & Waffle Mix 32 OZ (2LB) 907g 30000 65040 BBD SEP 13 25 P

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should examine their Pearl Milling Company Original Pancake & Waffle Mix packaging for the affected lot and date codes and immediately stop using the product if it matches. For assistance, contact Quaker Consumer Relations at 1-800-407-2247, Monday to Friday, 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CST.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Quaker Consumer Relations | 1-800-407-2247

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on January 15, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.

title: My Mochi Ice Cream Company LLC Recalls Peach Mango Sorbet for Undeclared Egg Allergen
headline: My Mochi Recalls Peach Mango Sorbet Due to Undeclared Egg Allergen Risk
description: My Mochi recalls Peach Mango Sorbet nationwide due to undeclared egg, posing allergy risks. Two minor allergic reactions reported. Affected lots: 337-24, 341-24.
keywords: My Mochi Ice Cream Company LLC
companyName: My Mochi Ice Cream Company LLC

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The Mochi Ice Cream Company LLC, based in Vernon, California, has issued a recall for 1,350 cases of My Mochi Peach Mango Sorbet due to the potential presence of undeclared pasteurized cooked egg white. This undeclared allergen poses a risk to individuals with egg allergies or severe sensitivities, as consumption could lead to serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The affected Peach Mango Sorbet was distributed nationwide through retail stores. To date, the company has received two reports of minor allergic reactions linked to the product. The recall was initiated after discovering that products containing pasteurized egg white were packaged without labels disclosing this ingredient. An investigation revealed that the issue was caused by a temporary failure in the packaging process.

Product Lot Number
My Mochi Peach Mango Sorbet 337-24
My Mochi Peach Mango Sorbet 341-24

facts.company: My Mochi Ice Cream Company LLC

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check product packaging for My Mochi Peach Mango Sorbet lot numbers 337-24 and 341-24 and return affected products to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact My Mochi Ice Cream Company LLC at 1-844-696-6244.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 1-844-696-6244

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on January 10, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.

title: Lifestyle Evolution Recalls NuGo Dark Chocolate Chip and Dark Pretzel Nutrition Bars for Undeclared Milk Allergen
headline: Lifestyle Evolution Recalls NuGo Dark Chocolate Chip and Dark Pretzel Nutrition Bars Due to Undeclared Milk
description: Lifestyle Evolution recalls select NuGo Dark Chocolate Chip and Dark Pretzel bars due to undeclared milk, posing risk to milk-allergic consumers.
keywords: NuGo Dark Chocolate Chip Nutrition Bar and NuGo Dark Pretzel Chocolate Nutrition Bar
productDescription: NuGo Dark Chocolate Chip Nutrition Bar and NuGo Dark Pretzel Chocolate Nutrition Bar

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Lifestyle Evolution Inc. has voluntarily recalled select lots of NuGo Dark Chocolate Chip and NuGo Dark Pretzel nutrition bars due to the presence of undeclared milk. This recall affects people with milk allergies or severe sensitivities, as consuming these products could result in serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions.

This recall corresponds to a previously announced notice from December 2, 2024, involving customers and retailers such as Amazon, Costco, Wegmans, and Giant Eagle. The affected products are individually wrapped bars sold in 12- or 18-count multipack boxes distributed nationwide.

The affected products bear specific manufacturing codes and best by dates as detailed below. The recall was prompted after 11 consumer reports of allergic reactions, and subsequent testing confirmed milk was present despite not being listed on the packaging.

Product Flavor Manufacturing Code Best By Date Item Number (Bar) Item Number (Box)
NuGo Dark Chocolate Chip C24234B, C24235A, C24235B, C24236A 12/21/2025, 12/22/2025, 12/23/2025 52101 52102
NuGo Dark Chocolate Pretzel w Sea Salt B24227B 12/14/2025 52901 52902
NuGo Dark Multipack Chocolate Chip and Chocolate Pretzel w Sea Salt B24235A, B24235B, B24236B, C24236A 12/22/2025, 12/23/2025 52901, 52101 52004

facts.product: NuGo Dark Chocolate Chip Nutrition Bar and NuGo Dark Pretzel Chocolate Nutrition Bar

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check product labels of NuGo Dark Chocolate Chip and Dark Pretzel nutrition bars for affected lot codes and best by dates; if purchased, return the product to the place of purchase or contact Lifestyle Evolution at 1-888-421-2032 or contact@nugonutriton.com for assistance.

content.text:
Consumer support: 1-888-421-2032 | contact@nugonutriton.com
Media contact: 1-888-421-2032

title: Dierbergs Kitchen Recalls Premium Home-Style Mashed Potatoes for Undeclared Wheat Allergen
headline: Dierbergs Markets Recalls Premium Home-Style Mashed Potatoes Due to Undeclared Wheat
description: Dierbergs Markets recalls Premium Home-Style Mashed Potatoes for undeclared wheat allergen affecting products with sell-by date 01/09/2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Dierbergs Markets has issued a recall for their 16oz Premium Home-Style Mashed Potatoes due to the presence of undeclared wheat, a known allergen. The affected product carries a sell-by date of January 9, 2025, and includes a label ID number 92 located in the lower left corner above the UPC code. This undeclared allergen poses a risk to individuals with wheat allergies or sensitivities.

The recalled product was distributed to numerous Dierbergs Markets locations across Missouri and Illinois. All affected products have been removed from store shelves to prevent further consumer exposure. No other Dierbergs branded products are impacted by this recall.

Product Name Size UPC Sell By Date Label ID
Premium Home-Style Mashed Potatoes 16oz 231220006538 01/09/2025 92

The recalled product was distributed to the following Dierbergs Markets locations:

  • Southroads
  • Four Seasons
  • Warson Woods
  • Manchester
  • West Oak
  • Clarkson
  • Arnold
  • Lemay
  • Bogey Hills
  • Florissant
  • Market Place
  • Mackenzie Point
  • Telegraph
  • Heritage Place
  • 94 Crossing
  • Deer Creek
  • Fenton
  • Brentwood
  • Green Mount
  • Wildwood
  • 79 Crossing
  • Wentzville
  • Edwardsville
  • Lake Saint Louis
  • Crestwood

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers with wheat allergies or sensitivities should check their Premium Home-Style Mashed Potatoes packages for the sell-by date 01/09/2025 and label ID 92, and return unopened packages to Dierbergs Markets for a full refund. For questions, contact Dierbergs Markets customer service at 1-636-532-8884, Monday through Friday, 8 am to 4:30 pm CDT.

description: Blue Ridge Beef recalls Kitten Mix pet food due to possible Salmonella contamination, affecting products distributed in six U.S. states in July 2024.
keywords: Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Blue Ridge Beef has issued a recall of 1,350 pounds of their 2-pound Kitten Mix pet food due to potential contamination with Salmonella. The affected product includes Lot Number N25-0716, with UPC 8542980013436. These lot numbers are stamped on the clips at the ends of the chubs or bags. The product was distributed from July 18, 2024, to July 26, 2024, primarily in retail stores located in Virginia, Maryland, Pennsylvania, Connecticut, Massachusetts, and New York.

Salmonella contamination poses health risks to both pets and humans. Animals consuming the contaminated food may exhibit symptoms such as lethargy, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Humans handling these pet products are also at risk if exposed without proper handwashing. Symptoms in humans include nausea, vomiting, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, abdominal cramps, and fever. More severe complications, though rare, can occur.

The recall follows a customer complaint and subsequent testing by the Massachusetts Department of Agriculture Resources and the Massachusetts State Public Health Laboratory, which confirmed the presence of Salmonella.

Product Size UPC Lot Number
Kitten Mix 2 lb 8542980013436 N25-0716

facts.reason: Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check for Kitten Mix Lot Number N25-0716 with UPC 8542980013436 and if owned, return the product to the place of purchase or dispose of it securely. Contact Blue Ridge Beef at 704-873-2072 or blueridgebeefnc@yahoo.com for additional information.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 704-873-2072 | blueridgebeefnc@yahoo.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on January 03, 2025.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.
Visit the company website for product details.

title: Abbey Specialty Foods Recalls Wicklow Gold Cheddar Nettle & Chive and Tomato & Herb 5.2 oz for Potential Listeria Contamination
headline: Abbey Specialty Foods Issues Recall for Wicklow Gold Cheddar Nettle & Chive and Tomato & Herb 5.2 oz Due to Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
description: Abbey Specialty Foods recalls Wicklow Gold Cheddar Nettle & Chive and Tomato & Herb 5.2 oz cheeses due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination posing health risks.
keywords: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness
Potential to be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes
productType: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness
regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness
reason: Potential to be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Abbey Specialty Foods, based in Fairfield, NJ, has announced a recall of two Wicklow Gold cheddar-style cheese products due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems.

The affected products include Wicklow Gold Cheddar Nettle & Chive 5.2 oz and Wicklow Gold Cheddar Tomato & Herb 5.2 oz. While healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea, pregnant women are at risk of miscarriages and stillbirths caused by listeria infection.

The recalled products were distributed in limited quantities across select states including Massachusetts, Maine, New Hampshire, Ohio, and Colorado. Distributors and retailers have been notified and have removed the affected items from their shelves. Abbey Specialty Foods reports no confirmed adverse health events related to these products at this time.

The recall stems from a notification by the supplier, Wicklow Farmhouse Cheese LTD., regarding possible contamination.

Item# Description Batch# Lot# Sell By Date UPC GTIN
28472 Wicklow Gold Cheddar Nettle & Chive 8/5.2 oz 24192 1113880 6/2/2025 797776045730 73585016606300
28473 Wicklow Gold Cheddar Tomato & Herb 8/5.2 oz 24175 1113881 6/2/2025 797776045778 73585017737300

facts.productType: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness
facts.reason: Potential to be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the lot and batch numbers of their Wicklow Gold Cheddar Nettle & Chive and Tomato & Herb 5.2 oz products. If affected, they are advised to return the cheese to the place of purchase for a full refund or dispose of it safely. For questions, contact Abbey Specialty Foods at 862-210-8150 or via email at info@abbeyspeciatly.com.

title: Braga Fresh Voluntary Advisory on Marketside Broccoli Florets Due to Possible Listeria Contamination
headline: Braga Fresh Advises Voluntary Recall of Marketside Broccoli Florets Over Potential Listeria Risk
description: Braga Fresh recalls Marketside Broccoli Florets due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination in a specific lot; no illnesses reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Braga Fresh has issued a voluntary precautionary advisory for a specific production lot of Marketside Broccoli Florets due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. The affected product is a 12-ounce bag of washed and ready-to-eat broccoli florets with a best if used by date of December 10, 2024. Although this product is past its expiration date and no longer available for purchase in stores, consumers might have frozen it for future use and are advised not to consume it and to discard any remaining quantities.

The possible contamination was identified through random sampling conducted by Texas Health & Human Services, where one sample from a Texas store location tested positive for Listeria monocytogenes. No illnesses have been reported to date in connection with this product. Listeria monocytogenes can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals might experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at risk of miscarriage or stillbirth if infected.

This advisory only applies to the 12-ounce Marketside Broccoli Florets sold at Walmart stores with the specific UPC and lot code listed below. No other Marketside or Braga Fresh products are affected.

Product Name Store Distribution Regions UPC Code Lot Code Best If Used By Date Pack Size & Packaging
Marketside Broccoli Florets Walmart AK, AR, AZ, CA, CO, ID, IL, IN, KY, LA, MI, MT, NV, OH, OK, OR, TX, UT, WA, WY 6 81131 32884 5 BFFG327A6 Dec 10, 2024 12 oz, bag

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check for the 12-ounce Marketside Broccoli Florets with UPC 6 81131 32884 5 and lot code BFFG327A6 with best if used by date December 10, 2024, and discard any remaining product. For questions, contact Braga Fresh at 877-456-7445 or email WeCare@BragaFresh.com.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text:
Consumer support: 877-456-7445 | WeCare@BragaFresh.com
Media contact: WeCare@BragaFresh.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on December 31, 2024.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.
Visit the company website for product details.

disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.

title: Gardners Candies Allergy Alert for Undeclared Tree Nuts in Cappuccino Meltaway® Bars and Meltaway Treat Boxes

en.title: Gardners Candies Allergy Alert for Undeclared Tree Nuts in Cappuccino Meltaway® Bars and Meltaway Treat Boxes
en.description: Gardners Candies warns of undeclared tree nuts in Cappuccino Meltaway® Bars and Meltaway Treat Boxes, posing allergy risks to sensitive consumers.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gardners Candies has issued an allergy alert due to undeclared tree nuts in certain Cappuccino Meltaway Bars and Gardners Meltaway Treat Boxes containing Cappuccino Meltaway Bars, presenting a risk to consumers with tree nut allergies.

The presence of undeclared tree nuts in these products can trigger severe allergic reactions, including anaphylaxis, in sensitive individuals.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Check the product packaging for possible tree nut presence and avoid consuming affected Cappuccino Meltaway Bars and Meltaway Treat Boxes if you have tree nut allergies. Consult a healthcare professional if you experience any allergic reactions after consumption.

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: This summary references the official FDA recall notice linked in this record.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

title: Astellas Pharma US Recalls One Lot Each of PROGRAF® 0.5mg and ASTAGRAF XL® 0.5mg Tacrolimus Capsules Due to Potential Empty Capsules

en.title: Astellas Pharma US Recalls One Lot Each of PROGRAF® 0.5mg and ASTAGRAF XL® 0.5mg Tacrolimus Capsules Due to Potential Empty Capsules
en.description: Astellas Pharma US recalls specific lots of PROGRAF® and ASTAGRAF XL® 0.5mg capsules shipped in the U.S. that may contain empty capsules.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Astellas Pharma US Inc. has issued a voluntary nationwide recall for one lot of PROGRAF 0.5mg (Tacrolimus) capsules and one lot of ASTAGRAF XL 0.5mg (Tacrolimus Extended-Release Capsules). These bottles shipped to the U.S. may contain empty capsules, posing a risk of subtherapeutic dosing.

No information on injuries or adverse events related to this issue has been reported.

ProductStrengthDosage FormLot NumberContainer Size
PROGRAF0.5mgCapsulesX050672100 Capsules
ASTAGRAF XL0.5mgExtended-Release CapsulesX050670100 Capsules

The recall was initiated after it was discovered that some bottles may contain empty capsules, which could result in patients receiving less than the prescribed dose.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Identify if you possess the recalled lots of PROGRAF 0.5mg or ASTAGRAF XL 0.5mg capsules; if so, follow instructions from your healthcare provider and the manufacturer, including returning affected products for proper handling.

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: This summary references the official FDA recall notice linked in this record.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

title: Alcon Laboratories Recalls Lot 10101 of Systane Lubricant Eye Drops Ultra PF Due to Fungal Contamination

en.title: Alcon Laboratories Recalls Lot 10101 of Systane Lubricant Eye Drops Ultra PF Due to Fungal Contamination
en.description: Alcon Laboratories recalls Systane Lubricant Eye Drops Ultra PF lot 10101 nationwide due to fungal contamination risk. No injuries reported.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Alcon Laboratories has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of Systane Lubricant Eye Drops Ultra PF, Single Vials On-the-Go, 25 count (Lot 10101), due to potential fungal contamination concerns.

This recall involves only Lot 10101 of the product distributed nationwide.

As of the recall announcement, no injuries or adverse events related to this issue have been reported.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Check if you have the affected lot 10101 of Systane Lubricant Eye Drops Ultra PF and stop using it immediately. Dispose of the product properly or contact Alcon Laboratories for return and refund instructions.

title: Orgain Voluntary Allergy Alert for Undeclared Peanut Residue in 30G Protein Organic Plant Based Powder – Chocolate 2.01lb

en.title: Orgain Voluntary Allergy Alert for Undeclared Peanut Residue in 30G Protein Organic Plant Based Powder – Chocolate 2.01lb
en.description: Orgain recalls one batch of 30G Protein Organic Plant Based Powder - Chocolate due to undeclared peanut residue posing allergy risks.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
A single batch of Orgain 30g Protein Organic Plant Based Powder - Chocolate 2.01lb may contain undeclared peanut residue, which could pose a serious allergy risk.

The presence of undeclared peanut residue may trigger allergic reactions in individuals sensitive to peanuts, despite peanuts not being listed as an ingredient on the product label.

As of now, no injuries or adverse events have been reported associated with this issue.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check the product batch number before use, avoid consuming the product if they have a peanut allergy, and contact the company or retailer for further information.

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: This summary references the official FDA recall notice linked in this record.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

title: Shoppers-Plaza Recalls Fouzee Sugarlin Herbal Formula Due to Undeclared Metformin and Glyburide

en.title: Shoppers-Plaza Recalls Fouzee Sugarlin Herbal Formula Due to Undeclared Metformin and Glyburide
en.description: Shoppers-Plaza recalls Fouzee Sugarlin Herbal Formula due to presence of undeclared prescription drugs metformin and glyburide, posing health risks.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Shoppers-Plaza has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of Fouzee Sugarlin Herbal Formula due to the discovery of undeclared pharmaceutical ingredients metformin and glyburide in the product. These prescription drugs are not listed on the product label and may pose serious health risks, particularly to individuals with diabetes or other medical conditions.

The affected product is marketed as a herbal dietary supplement but contains the prescription medications metformin and glyburide, which can cause harmful side effects if consumed unknowingly.

No details on reported injuries or adverse events have been provided in the recall notice.

Product NameLot NumberUPC
Fouzee Sugarlin Herbal FormulaVariousUnavailable

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using Fouzee Sugarlin Herbal Formula, check the lot numbers of their product, and return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional information, contact Shoppers-Plaza customer support.

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: This summary references the official FDA recall notice linked in this record.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

title: Frito-Lay Recalls Lay’s Classic Potato Chips in Oregon for Undeclared Milk Allergen

en.title: Frito-Lay Recalls Lay’s Classic Potato Chips in Oregon for Undeclared Milk Allergen
en.description: Frito-Lay recalls Lay’s Classic Potato Chips sold in Oregon due to undeclared milk allergen, posing health risks for milk-allergic consumers.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Frito-Lay issued a limited recall for Lay's Classic Potato Chips sold in Oregon due to undeclared milk allergen presence posing potential health risks to individuals allergic to milk.

The presence of undeclared milk in the product could cause allergic reactions ranging from mild to severe in sensitive individuals.

No information on injuries or adverse events related to this recall has been reported.

ProductDescriptionLot CodesDistribution
Lay's Classic Potato Chips8.5 oz bagAll Lot CodesSold in Oregon

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: If you purchased Lay's Classic Potato Chips in Oregon, do not consume them if you have a milk allergy or intolerance. Return the product to the place of purchase for a refund or replacement.

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: This summary references the official FDA recall notice linked in this record.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.
